verses.esv: And when the LORD smelled the pleasing aroma, the LORD said in his heart, “I will never again curse the ground because of man, for the intention of man's heart is evil from his youth. Neither will I ever again strike down every living creature as I have done.
verses.kjv: And the Lord smelled a sweet savour; and the Lord said in his heart, I will not again curse the ground any more for man’s sake; for the imagination of man’s heart is evil from his youth; neither will I again smite any more every thing living, as I have done.
verses.bsb: When the LORD smelled the pleasing aroma, He said in His heart, “Never again will I curse the ground because of man, even though every inclination of his heart is evil from his youth. And never again will I destroy all living creatures as I have done.

verses.esv: While the earth remains, seedtime and harvest, cold and heat, summer and winter, day and night, shall not cease.”
verses.kjv: While the earth remaineth, seedtime and harvest, and cold and heat, and summer and winter, and day and night shall not cease.
verses.bsb: As long as the earth endures, seedtime and harvest, cold and heat, summer and winter, day and night shall never cease.”

verses.esv: And God blessed Noah and his sons and said to them, “Be fruitful and multiply and fill the earth.
verses.kjv: AND God blessed Noah and his sons, and said unto them, Be fruitful, and multiply, and replenish the earth.
verses.bsb: And God blessed Noah and his sons and said to them, “Be fruitful and multiply and fill the earth.

verses.esv: The fear of you and the dread of you shall be upon every beast of the earth and upon every bird of the heavens, upon everything that creeps on the ground and all the fish of the sea. Into your hand they are delivered.
verses.kjv: And the fear of you and the dread of you shall be upon every beast of the earth, and upon every fowl of the air, upon all that moveth upon the earth, and upon all the fishes of the sea; into your hand are they delivered.
verses.bsb: The fear and dread of you will fall on every living creature on the earth, every bird of the air, every creature that crawls on the ground, and all the fish of the sea. They are delivered into your hand.

verses.esv: Every moving thing that lives shall be food for you. And as I gave you the green plants, I give you everything.
verses.kjv: Every moving thing that liveth shall be meat for you; even as the green herb have I given you all things.
verses.bsb: Everything that lives and moves will be food for you; just as I gave you the green plants, I now give you all things.

verses.esv: But you shall not eat flesh with its life, that is, its blood.
verses.kjv: But flesh with the life thereof, which is the blood thereof, shall ye not eat.
verses.bsb: But you must not eat meat with its lifeblood still in it.

verses.esv: And for your lifeblood I will require a reckoning: from every beast I will require it and from man. From his fellow man I will require a reckoning for the life of man.
verses.kjv: And surely your blood of your lives will I require; at the hand of every beast will I require it, and at the hand of man; at the hand of every man’s brother will I require the life of man.
verses.bsb: And surely I will require the life of any man or beast by whose hand your lifeblood is shed. I will demand an accounting from anyone who takes the life of his fellow man:

verses.esv: “Whoever sheds the blood of man, by man shall his blood be shed, for God made man in his own image.
verses.kjv: Whoso sheddeth man’s blood, by man shall his blood be shed: for in the image of God made he man.
verses.bsb: Whoever sheds the blood of man, by man his blood will be shed; for in His own image God has made mankind.

verses.esv: And you, be fruitful and multiply, increase greatly on the earth and multiply in it.”
verses.kjv: And you, be ye fruitful, and multiply; bring forth abundantly in the earth, and multiply therein.
verses.bsb: But as for you, be fruitful and multiply; spread out across the earth and multiply upon it.”

verses.esv: Then God said to Noah and to his sons with him,
verses.kjv: ¶ And God spake unto Noah, and to his sons with him, saying,
verses.bsb: Then God said to Noah and his sons with him,

verses.esv: “Behold, I establish my covenant with you and your offspring after you,
verses.kjv: And I, behold, I establish my covenant with you, and with your seed after you;
verses.bsb: “Behold, I now establish My covenant with you and your descendants after you,

verses.esv: and with every living creature that is with you, the birds, the livestock, and every beast of the earth with you, as many as came out of the ark; it is for every beast of the earth.
verses.kjv: And with every living creature that is with you, of the fowl, of the cattle, and of every beast of the earth with you; from all that go out of the ark, to every beast of the earth.
verses.bsb: and with every living creature that was with you—the birds, the livestock, and every beast of the earth—every living thing that came out of the ark.

verses.esv: I establish my covenant with you, that never again shall all flesh be cut off by the waters of the flood, and never again shall there be a flood to destroy the earth.”
verses.kjv: And I will establish my covenant with you; neither shall all flesh be cut off any more by the waters of a flood; neither shall there any more be a flood to destroy the earth.
verses.bsb: And I establish My covenant with you: Never again will all life be cut off by the waters of a flood; never again will there be a flood to destroy the earth.”

verses.esv: And God said, “This is the sign of the covenant that I make between me and you and every living creature that is with you, for all future generations:
verses.kjv: And God said, This is the token of the covenant which I make between me and you and every living creature that is with you, for perpetual generations:
verses.bsb: And God said, “This is the sign of the covenant I am making between Me and you and every living creature with you, a covenant for all generations to come:

verses.esv: I have set my bow in the cloud, and it shall be a sign of the covenant between me and the earth.
verses.kjv: I do set my bow in the cloud, and it shall be for a token of a covenant between me and the earth.
verses.bsb: I have set My rainbow in the clouds, and it will be a sign of the covenant between Me and the earth.

verses.esv: When I bring clouds over the earth and the bow is seen in the clouds,
verses.kjv: And it shall come to pass, when I bring a cloud over the earth, that the bow shall be seen in the cloud:
verses.bsb: Whenever I form clouds over the earth and the rainbow appears in the clouds,

verses.esv: I will remember my covenant that is between me and you and every living creature of all flesh. And the waters shall never again become a flood to destroy all flesh.
verses.kjv: And I will remember my covenant, which is between me and you and every living creature of all flesh; and the waters shall no more become a flood to destroy all flesh.
verses.bsb: I will remember My covenant between Me and you and every living creature of every kind. Never again will the waters become a flood to destroy all life.

verses.esv: When the bow is in the clouds, I will see it and remember the everlasting covenant between God and every living creature of all flesh that is on the earth.”
verses.kjv: And the bow shall be in the cloud; and I will look upon it, that I may remember the everlasting covenant between God and every living creature of all flesh that is upon the earth.
verses.bsb: And whenever the rainbow appears in the clouds, I will see it and remember the everlasting covenant between God and every living creature of every kind that is on the earth.”

verses.esv: God said to Noah, “This is the sign of the covenant that I have established between me and all flesh that is on the earth.”
verses.kjv: And God said unto Noah, This is the token of the covenant, which I have established between me and all flesh that is upon the earth.
verses.bsb: So God said to Noah, “This is the sign of the covenant that I have established between Me and every creature on the earth.”

verses.esv: The sons of Noah who went forth from the ark were Shem, Ham, and Japheth. (Ham was the father of Canaan.)
verses.kjv: ¶ And the sons of Noah, that went forth of the ark, were Shem, and Ham, and Japheth: and Ham is the father of Canaan.
verses.bsb: The sons of Noah who came out of the ark were Shem, Ham, and Japheth. And Ham was the father of Canaan.

verses.esv: These three were the sons of Noah, and from these the people of the whole earth were dispersed.
verses.kjv: These are the three sons of Noah: and of them was the whole earth overspread.
verses.bsb: These three were the sons of Noah, and from them the whole earth was populated.

verses.esv: Noah began to be a man of the soil, and he planted a vineyard.
verses.kjv: And Noah began to be an husbandman, and he planted a vineyard:
verses.bsb: Now Noah, a man of the soil, proceeded to plant a vineyard.

verses.esv: He drank of the wine and became drunk and lay uncovered in his tent.
verses.kjv: And he drank of the wine, and was drunken; and he was uncovered within his tent.
verses.bsb: But when he drank some of its wine, he became drunk and uncovered himself inside his tent.

verses.esv: And Ham, the father of Canaan, saw the nakedness of his father and told his two brothers outside.
verses.kjv: And Ham, the father of Canaan, saw the nakedness of his father, and told his two brethren without.
verses.bsb: And Ham, the father of Canaan, saw his father’s nakedness and told his two brothers outside.

verses.esv: Then Shem and Japheth took a garment, laid it on both their shoulders, and walked backward and covered the nakedness of their father. Their faces were turned backward, and they did not see their father's nakedness.
verses.kjv: And Shem and Japheth took a garment, and laid it upon both their shoulders, and went backward, and covered the nakedness of their father; and their faces were backward, and they saw not their father’s nakedness.
verses.bsb: Then Shem and Japheth took a garment and placed it across their shoulders, and walking backward, they covered their father’s nakedness. Their faces were turned away so that they did not see their father’s nakedness.

verses.esv: When Noah awoke from his wine and knew what his youngest son had done to him,
verses.kjv: And Noah awoke from his wine, and knew what his younger son had done unto him.
verses.bsb: When Noah awoke from his drunkenness and learned what his youngest son had done to him,

verses.esv: he said, “Cursed be Canaan; a servant of servants shall he be to his brothers.”
verses.kjv: And he said, Cursed be Canaan; a servant of servants shall he be unto his brethren.
verses.bsb: he said,  “Cursed be Canaan! A servant of servants shall he be to his brothers.”

verses.esv: He also said, “Blessed be the LORD, the God of Shem; and let Canaan be his servant.
verses.kjv: And he said, Blessed be the Lord God of Shem; and Canaan shall be his servant.
verses.bsb: He also declared:  “Blessed be the LORD, the God of Shem! May Canaan be the servant of Shem.

verses.esv: May God enlarge Japheth, and let him dwell in the tents of Shem, and let Canaan be his servant.”
verses.kjv: God shall enlarge Japheth, and he shall dwell in the tents of Shem; and Canaan shall be his servant.
verses.bsb: May God expand the territory of Japheth; may he dwell in the tents of Shem, and may Canaan be his servant.”

verses.esv: After the flood Noah lived 350 years.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Noah lived after the flood three hundred and fifty years.
verses.bsb: After the flood, Noah lived 350 years.

verses.esv: All the days of Noah were 950 years, and he died.
verses.kjv: And all the days of Noah were nine hundred and fifty years: and he died.
verses.bsb: So Noah lived a total of 950 years, and then he died.

verses.esv: These are the generations of the sons of Noah, Shem, Ham, and Japheth. Sons were born to them after the flood.
verses.kjv: NOW these are the generations of the sons of Noah, Shem, Ham, and Japheth: and unto them were sons born after the flood.
verses.bsb: This is the account of Noah’s sons Shem, Ham, and Japheth, who also had sons after the flood.

verses.esv: The sons of Japheth: Gomer, Magog, Madai, Javan, Tubal, Meshech, and Tiras.
verses.kjv: The sons of Japheth; Gomer, and Magog, and Madai, and Javan, and Tubal, and Meshech, and Tiras.
verses.bsb: The sons of Japheth:  Gomer, Magog, Madai, Javan, Tubal, Meshech, and Tiras.

verses.esv: The sons of Gomer: Ashkenaz, Riphath, and Togarmah.
verses.kjv: And the sons of Gomer; Ashkenaz, and Riphath, and Togarmah.
verses.bsb: The sons of Gomer:  Ashkenaz, Riphath, and Togarmah.

verses.esv: The sons of Javan: Elishah, Tarshish, Kittim, and Dodanim.
verses.kjv: And the sons of Javan; Elishah, and Tarshish, Kittim, and Dodanim.
verses.bsb: And the sons of Javan:  Elishah, Tarshish, the Kittites, and the Rodanites.

verses.esv: From these the coastland peoples spread in their lands, each with his own language, by their clans, in their nations.
verses.kjv: By these were the isles of the Gentiles divided in their lands; every one after his tongue, after their families, in their nations.
verses.bsb: From these, the maritime peoples separated into their territories, according to their languages, by clans within their nations.

verses.esv: The sons of Ham: Cush, Egypt, Put, and Canaan.
verses.kjv: ¶ And the sons of Ham; Cush, and Mizraim, and Phut, and Canaan.
verses.bsb: The sons of Ham:  Cush, Mizraim, Put, and Canaan.

verses.esv: The sons of Cush: Seba, Havilah, Sabtah, Raamah, and Sabteca. The sons of Raamah: Sheba and Dedan.
verses.kjv: And the sons of Cush; Seba, and Havilah, and Sabtah, and Raamah, and Sabtecha: and the sons of Raamah; Sheba, and Dedan.
verses.bsb: The sons of Cush:  Seba, Havilah, Sabtah, Raamah, and Sabteca.  And the sons of Raamah:  Sheba and Dedan.

verses.esv: Cush fathered Nimrod; he was the first on earth to be a mighty man.
verses.kjv: And Cush begat Nimrod: he began to be a mighty one in the earth.
verses.bsb: Cush was the father of Nimrod, who began to be a mighty one on the earth.

verses.esv: He was a mighty hunter before the LORD. Therefore it is said, “Like Nimrod a mighty hunter before the LORD.”
verses.kjv: He was a mighty hunter before the Lord: wherefore it is said, Even as Nimrod the mighty hunter before the Lord.
verses.bsb: He was a mighty hunter before the LORD; so it is said, “Like Nimrod, a mighty hunter before the LORD.”

verses.esv: The beginning of his kingdom was Babel, Erech, Accad, and Calneh, in the land of Shinar.
verses.kjv: And the beginning of his kingdom was Babel, and Erech, and Accad, and Calneh, in the land of Shinar.
verses.bsb: His kingdom began in Babylon, Erech, Accad, and Calneh, in the land of Shinar.

verses.esv: From that land he went into Assyria and built Nineveh, Rehoboth-Ir, Calah, and
verses.kjv: Out of that land went forth Asshur, and builded Nineveh, and the city Rehoboth, and Calah,
verses.bsb: From that land he went forth into Assyria, where he built Nineveh, Rehoboth-Ir, Calah,

verses.esv: Resen between Nineveh and Calah; that is the great city.
verses.kjv: And Resen between Nineveh and Calah: the same is a great city.
verses.bsb: and Resen, which is between Nineveh and the great city of Calah.

verses.esv: Egypt fathered Ludim, Anamim, Lehabim, Naphtuhim,
verses.kjv: And Mizraim begat Ludim, and Anamim, and Lehabim, and Naphtuhim,
verses.bsb: Mizraim was the father of the Ludites, the Anamites, the Lehabites, the Naphtuhites,

verses.esv: Pathrusim, Casluhim (from whom the Philistines came), and Caphtorim.
verses.kjv: And Pathrusim, and Casluhim, (out of whom came Philistim,) and Caphtorim.
verses.bsb: the Pathrusites, the Casluhites (from whom the Philistines came), and the Caphtorites.

verses.esv: Canaan fathered Sidon his firstborn and Heth,
verses.kjv: ¶ And Canaan begat Sidon his firstborn, and Heth,
verses.bsb: And Canaan was the father of Sidon his firstborn, and of the Hittites,

verses.esv: and the Jebusites, the Amorites, the Girgashites,
verses.kjv: And the Jebusite, and the Amorite, and the Girgasite,
verses.bsb: the Jebusites, the Amorites, the Girgashites,

verses.esv: the Hivites, the Arkites, the Sinites,
verses.kjv: And the Hivite, and the Arkite, and the Sinite,
verses.bsb: the Hivites, the Arkites, the Sinites,

verses.esv: the Arvadites, the Zemarites, and the Hamathites. Afterward the clans of the Canaanites dispersed.
verses.kjv: And the Arvadite, and the Zemarite, and the Hamathite: and afterward were the families of the Canaanites spread abroad.
verses.bsb: the Arvadites, the Zemarites, and the Hamathites.  Later the Canaanite clans were scattered,

verses.esv: And the territory of the Canaanites extended from Sidon in the direction of Gerar as far as Gaza, and in the direction of Sodom, Gomorrah, Admah, and Zeboiim, as far as Lasha.
verses.kjv: And the border of the Canaanites was from Sidon, as thou comest to Gerar, unto Gaza; as thou goest, unto Sodom, and Gomorrah, and Admah, and Zeboim, even unto Lasha.
verses.bsb: and the borders of Canaan extended from Sidon toward Gerar as far as Gaza, and then toward Sodom, Gomorrah, Admah, and Zeboiim, as far as Lasha.

verses.esv: These are the sons of Ham, by their clans, their languages, their lands, and their nations.
verses.kjv: These are the sons of Ham, after their families, after their tongues, in their countries, and in their nations.
verses.bsb: These are the sons of Ham according to their clans, languages, lands, and nations.

verses.esv: To Shem also, the father of all the children of Eber, the elder brother of Japheth, children were born.
verses.kjv: ¶ Unto Shem also, the father of all the children of Eber, the brother of Japheth the elder, even to him were children born.
verses.bsb: And sons were also born to Shem, the older brother of Japheth; Shem was the forefather of all the sons of Eber.

verses.esv: The sons of Shem: Elam, Asshur, Arpachshad, Lud, and Aram.
verses.kjv: The children of Shem; Elam, and Asshur, and Arphaxad, and Lud, and Aram.
verses.bsb: The sons of Shem:  Elam, Asshur, Arphaxad, Lud, and Aram.

verses.esv: The sons of Aram: Uz, Hul, Gether, and Mash.
verses.kjv: And the children of Aram; Uz, and Hul, and Gether, and Mash.
verses.bsb: The sons of Aram:  Uz, Hul, Gether, and Mash.

verses.esv: Arpachshad fathered Shelah; and Shelah fathered Eber.
verses.kjv: And Arphaxad begat Salah; and Salah begat Eber.
verses.bsb: Arphaxad was the father of Shelah, and Shelah was the father of Eber.

verses.esv: To Eber were born two sons: the name of the one was Peleg, for in his days the earth was divided, and his brother's name was Joktan.
verses.kjv: And unto Eber were born two sons: the name of one was Peleg; for in his days was the earth divided; and his brother’s name was Joktan.
verses.bsb: Two sons were born to Eber: One was named Peleg, because in his days the earth was divided, and his brother was named Joktan.

verses.esv: Joktan fathered Almodad, Sheleph, Hazarmaveth, Jerah,
verses.kjv: And Joktan begat Almodad, and Sheleph, and Hazarmaveth, and Jerah,
verses.bsb: And Joktan was the father of Almodad, Sheleph, Hazarmaveth, Jerah,

verses.esv: Hadoram, Uzal, Diklah,
verses.kjv: And Hadoram, and Uzal, and Diklah,
verses.bsb: Hadoram, Uzal, Diklah,

verses.esv: Obal, Abimael, Sheba,
verses.kjv: And Obal, and Abimael, and Sheba,
verses.bsb: Obal, Abimael, Sheba,

verses.esv: Ophir, Havilah, and Jobab; all these were the sons of Joktan.
verses.kjv: And Ophir, and Havilah, and Jobab: all these were the sons of Joktan.
verses.bsb: Ophir, Havilah, and Jobab. All these were sons of Joktan.

verses.esv: The territory in which they lived extended from Mesha in the direction of Sephar to the hill country of the east.
verses.kjv: And their dwelling was from Mesha, as thou goest unto Sephar a mount of the east.
verses.bsb: Their territory extended from Mesha to Sephar, in the eastern hill country.

verses.esv: These are the sons of Shem, by their clans, their languages, their lands, and their nations.
verses.kjv: These are the sons of Shem, after their families, after their tongues, in their lands, after their nations.
verses.bsb: These are the sons of Shem, according to their clans, languages, lands, and nations.

verses.esv: These are the clans of the sons of Noah, according to their genealogies, in their nations, and from these the nations spread abroad on the earth after the flood.
verses.kjv: These are the families of the sons of Noah, after their generations, in their nations: and by these were the nations divided in the earth after the flood.
verses.bsb: All these are the clans of Noah’s sons, according to their generations and nations. From these the nations of the earth spread out after the flood.

verses.esv: Now the whole earth had one language and the same words.
verses.kjv: AND the whole earth was of one language, and of one speech.
verses.bsb: Now the whole world had one language and a common form of speech.

verses.esv: And as people migrated from the east, they found a plain in the land of Shinar and settled there.
verses.kjv: And it came to pass, as they journeyed from the east, that they found a plain in the land of Shinar; and they dwelt there.
verses.bsb: And as people journeyed eastward, they found a plain in the land of Shinar and settled there.

verses.esv: And they said to one another, “Come, let us make bricks, and burn them thoroughly.” And they had brick for stone, and bitumen for mortar.
verses.kjv: And they said one to another, Go to, let us make brick, and burn them throughly. And they had brick for stone, and slime had they for morter.
verses.bsb: And they said to one another, “Come, let us make bricks and bake them thoroughly.” So they used brick instead of stone, and tar instead of mortar.

verses.esv: Then they said, “Come, let us build ourselves a city and a tower with its top in the heavens, and let us make a name for ourselves, lest we be dispersed over the face of the whole earth.”
verses.kjv: And they said, Go to, let us build us a city and a tower, whose top may reach unto heaven; and let us make us a name, lest we be scattered abroad upon the face of the whole earth.
verses.bsb: “Come,” they said, “let us build for ourselves a city with a tower that reaches to the heavens, that we may make a name for ourselves and not be scattered over the face of all the earth.”

verses.esv: And the LORD came down to see the city and the tower, which the children of man had built.
verses.kjv: And the Lord came down to see the city and the tower, which the children of men builded.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD came down to see the city and the tower that the sons of men were building.

verses.esv: And the LORD said, “Behold, they are one people, and they have all one language, and this is only the beginning of what they will do. And nothing that they propose to do will now be impossible for them.
verses.kjv: And the Lord said, Behold, the people is one, and they have all one language; and this they begin to do: and now nothing will be restrained from them, which they have imagined to do.
verses.bsb: And the LORD said, “If they have begun to do this as one people speaking the same language, then nothing they devise will be beyond them.

verses.esv: Come, let us go down and there confuse their language, so that they may not understand one another's speech.”
verses.kjv: Go to, let us go down, and there confound their language, that they may not understand one another’s speech.
verses.bsb: Come, let Us go down and confuse their language, so that they will not understand one another’s speech.”

verses.esv: So the LORD dispersed them from there over the face of all the earth, and they left off building the city.
verses.kjv: So the Lord scattered them abroad from thence upon the face of all the earth: and they left off to build the city.
verses.bsb: So the LORD scattered them from there over the face of all the earth, and they stopped building the city.

verses.esv: Therefore its name was called Babel, because there the LORD confused the language of all the earth. And from there the LORD dispersed them over the face of all the earth.
verses.kjv: Therefore is the name of it called Babel; because the Lord did there confound the language of all the earth: and from thence did the Lord scatter them abroad upon the face of all the earth.
verses.bsb: That is why it is called Babel, for there the LORD confused the language of the whole world, and from that place the LORD scattered them over the face of all the earth.

verses.esv: These are the generations of Shem. When Shem was 100 years old, he fathered Arpachshad two years after the flood.
verses.kjv: ¶ These are the generations of Shem: Shem was an hundred years old, and begat Arphaxad two years after the flood:
verses.bsb: This is the account of Shem. Two years after the flood, when Shem was 100 years old, he became the father of Arphaxad.

verses.esv: And Shem lived after he fathered Arpachshad 500 years and had other sons and daughters.
verses.kjv: And Shem lived after he begat Arphaxad five hundred years, and begat sons and daughters.
verses.bsb: And after he had become the father of Arphaxad, Shem lived 500 years and had other sons and daughters.

verses.esv: When Arpachshad had lived 35 years, he fathered Shelah.
verses.kjv: And Arphaxad lived five and thirty years, and begat Salah:
verses.bsb: When Arphaxad was 35 years old, he became the father of Shelah.

verses.esv: And Arpachshad lived after he fathered Shelah 403 years and had other sons and daughters.
verses.kjv: And Arphaxad lived after he begat Salah four hundred and three years, and begat sons and daughters.
verses.bsb: And after he had become the father of Shelah, Arphaxad lived 403 years and had other sons and daughters.

verses.esv: When Shelah had lived 30 years, he fathered Eber.
verses.kjv: And Salah lived thirty years, and begat Eber:
verses.bsb: When Shelah was 30 years old, he became the father of Eber.

verses.esv: And Shelah lived after he fathered Eber 403 years and had other sons and daughters.
verses.kjv: And Salah lived after he begat Eber four hundred and three years, and begat sons and daughters.
verses.bsb: And after he had become the father of Eber, Shelah lived 403 years and had other sons and daughters.

verses.esv: When Eber had lived 34 years, he fathered Peleg.
verses.kjv: And Eber lived four and thirty years, and begat Peleg:
verses.bsb: When Eber was 34 years old, he became the father of Peleg.

verses.esv: And Eber lived after he fathered Peleg 430 years and had other sons and daughters.
verses.kjv: And Eber lived after he begat Peleg four hundred and thirty years, and begat sons and daughters.
verses.bsb: And after he had become the father of Peleg, Eber lived 430 years and had other sons and daughters.

verses.esv: When Peleg had lived 30 years, he fathered Reu.
verses.kjv: And Peleg lived thirty years, and begat Reu:
verses.bsb: When Peleg was 30 years old, he became the father of Reu.

verses.esv: And Peleg lived after he fathered Reu 209 years and had other sons and daughters.
verses.kjv: And Peleg lived after he begat Reu two hundred and nine years, and begat sons and daughters.
verses.bsb: And after he had become the father of Reu, Peleg lived 209 years and had other sons and daughters.

verses.esv: When Reu had lived 32 years, he fathered Serug.
verses.kjv: And Reu lived two and thirty years, and begat Serug:
verses.bsb: When Reu was 32 years old, he became the father of Serug.

verses.esv: And Reu lived after he fathered Serug 207 years and had other sons and daughters.
verses.kjv: And Reu lived after he begat Serug two hundred and seven years, and begat sons and daughters.
verses.bsb: And after he had become the father of Serug, Reu lived 207 years and had other sons and daughters.

verses.esv: When Serug had lived 30 years, he fathered Nahor.
verses.kjv: And Serug lived thirty years, and begat Nahor:
verses.bsb: When Serug was 30 years old, he became the father of Nahor.

verses.esv: And Serug lived after he fathered Nahor 200 years and had other sons and daughters.
verses.kjv: And Serug lived after he begat Nahor two hundred years, and begat sons and daughters.
verses.bsb: And after he had become the father of Nahor, Serug lived 200 years and had other sons and daughters.

verses.esv: When Nahor had lived 29 years, he fathered Terah.
verses.kjv: And Nahor lived nine and twenty years, and begat Terah:
verses.bsb: When Nahor was 29 years old, he became the father of Terah.

verses.esv: And Nahor lived after he fathered Terah 119 years and had other sons and daughters.
verses.kjv: And Nahor lived after he begat Terah an hundred and nineteen years, and begat sons and daughters.
verses.bsb: And after he had become the father of Terah, Nahor lived 119 years and had other sons and daughters.

verses.esv: When Terah had lived 70 years, he fathered Abram, Nahor, and Haran.
verses.kjv: And Terah lived seventy years, and begat Abram, Nahor, and Haran.
verses.bsb: When Terah was 70 years old, he became the father of Abram, Nahor, and Haran.

verses.esv: Now these are the generations of Terah. Terah fathered Abram, Nahor, and Haran; and Haran fathered Lot.
verses.kjv: ¶ Now these are the generations of Terah: Terah begat Abram, Nahor, and Haran; and Haran begat Lot.
verses.bsb: This is the account of Terah. Terah became the father of Abram, Nahor, and Haran. And Haran became the father of Lot.

verses.esv: Haran died in the presence of his father Terah in the land of his kindred, in Ur of the Chaldeans.
verses.kjv: And Haran died before his father Terah in the land of his nativity, in Ur of the Chaldees.
verses.bsb: During his father Terah’s lifetime, Haran died in his native land, in Ur of the Chaldeans.

verses.esv: And Abram and Nahor took wives. The name of Abram's wife was Sarai, and the name of Nahor's wife, Milcah, the daughter of Haran the father of Milcah and Iscah.
verses.kjv: And Abram and Nahor took them wives: the name of Abram’s wife was Sarai; and the name of Nahor’s wife, Milcah, the daughter of Haran, the father of Milcah, and the father of Iscah.
verses.bsb: And Abram and Nahor took wives for themselves. Abram’s wife was named Sarai, and Nahor’s wife was named Milcah; she was the daughter of Haran, who was the father of both Milcah and Iscah.

verses.esv: Now Sarai was barren; she had no child.
verses.kjv: But Sarai was barren; she had no child.
verses.bsb: But Sarai was barren; she had no children.

verses.esv: Terah took Abram his son and Lot the son of Haran, his grandson, and Sarai his daughter-in-law, his son Abram's wife, and they went forth together from Ur of the Chaldeans to go into the land of Canaan, but when they came to Haran, they settled there.
verses.kjv: And Terah took Abram his son, and Lot the son of Haran his son’s son, and Sarai his daughter in law, his son Abram’s wife; and they went forth with them from Ur of the Chaldees, to go into the land of Canaan; and they came unto Haran, and dwelt there.
verses.bsb: And Terah took his son Abram, his grandson Lot son of Haran, and his daughter-in-law Sarai the wife of Abram, and they set out from Ur of the Chaldeans for the land of Canaan. But when they arrived in Haran, they settled there.

verses.esv: The days of Terah were 205 years, and Terah died in Haran.
verses.kjv: And the days of Terah were two hundred and five years: and Terah died in Haran.
verses.bsb: Terah lived 205 years, and he died in Haran.

verses.esv: Now the LORD said to Abram, “Go from your country and your kindred and your father's house to the land that I will show you.
verses.kjv: NOW the Lord had said unto Abram, Get thee out of thy country, and from thy kindred, and from thy father’s house, unto a land that I will shew thee:
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Abram, “Leave your country, your kindred, and your father’s household, and go to the land I will show you.

verses.esv: And I will make of you a great nation, and I will bless you and make your name great, so that you will be a blessing.
verses.kjv: And I will make of thee a great nation, and I will bless thee, and make thy name great; and thou shalt be a blessing:
verses.bsb: I will make you into a great nation, and I will bless you; I will make your name great, so that you will be a blessing.

verses.esv: I will bless those who bless you, and him who dishonors you I will curse, and in you all the families of the earth shall be blessed.”
verses.kjv: And I will bless them that bless thee, and curse him that curseth thee: and in thee shall all families of the earth be blessed.
verses.bsb: I will bless those who bless you and curse those who curse you; and all the families of the earth will be blessed through you.”

verses.esv: So Abram went, as the LORD had told him, and Lot went with him. Abram was seventy-five years old when he departed from Haran.
verses.kjv: So Abram departed, as the Lord had spoken unto him; and Lot went with him: and Abram was seventy and five years old when he departed out of Haran.
verses.bsb: So Abram departed, as the LORD had directed him, and Lot went with him. Abram was seventy-five years old when he left Haran.

verses.esv: And Abram took Sarai his wife, and Lot his brother's son, and all their possessions that they had gathered, and the people that they had acquired in Haran, and they set out to go to the land of Canaan. When they came to the land of Canaan,
verses.kjv: And Abram took Sarai his wife, and Lot his brother’s son, and all their substance that they had gathered, and the souls that they had gotten in Haran; and they went forth to go into the land of Canaan; and into the land of Canaan they came.
verses.bsb: And Abram took his wife Sarai, his nephew Lot, and all the possessions and people they had acquired in Haran, and set out for the land of Canaan.  When they came to the land of Canaan,

verses.esv: Abram passed through the land to the place at Shechem, to the oak of Moreh. At that time the Canaanites were in the land.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Abram passed through the land unto the place of Sichem, unto the plain of Moreh. And the Canaanite was then in the land.
verses.bsb: Abram traveled through the land as far as the site of the Oak of Moreh at Shechem. And at that time the Canaanites were in the land.

verses.esv: Then the LORD appeared to Abram and said, “To your offspring I will give this land.” So he built there an altar to the LORD, who had appeared to him.
verses.kjv: And the Lord appeared unto Abram, and said, Unto thy seed will I give this land: and there builded he an altar unto the Lord, who appeared unto him.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD appeared to Abram and said, “I will give this land to your offspring.” So Abram built an altar there to the LORD, who had appeared to him.

verses.esv: From there he moved to the hill country on the east of Bethel and pitched his tent, with Bethel on the west and Ai on the east. And there he built an altar to the LORD and called upon the name of the LORD.
verses.kjv: And he removed from thence unto a mountain on the east of Beth-el, and pitched his tent, having Beth-el on the west, and Hai on the east: and there he builded an altar unto the Lord, and called upon the name of the Lord.
verses.bsb: From there Abram moved on to the hill country east of Bethel and pitched his tent, with Bethel to the west and Ai to the east. There he built an altar to the LORD, and he called on the name of the LORD.

verses.esv: And Abram journeyed on, still going toward the Negeb.
verses.kjv: And Abram journeyed, going on still toward the south.
verses.bsb: And Abram journeyed on toward the Negev.

verses.esv: Now there was a famine in the land. So Abram went down to Egypt to sojourn there, for the famine was severe in the land.
verses.kjv: ¶ And there was a famine in the land: and Abram went down into Egypt to sojourn there; for the famine was grievous in the land.
verses.bsb: Now there was a famine in the land. So Abram went down to Egypt to live there for a while because the famine was severe.

verses.esv: When he was about to enter Egypt, he said to Sarai his wife, “I know that you are a woman beautiful in appearance,
verses.kjv: And it came to pass, when he was come near to enter into Egypt, that he said unto Sarai his wife, Behold now, I know that thou art a fair woman to look upon:
verses.bsb: As he was about to enter Egypt, he said to his wife Sarai, “Look, I know that you are a beautiful woman,

verses.esv: and when the Egyptians see you, they will say, ‘This is his wife.’ Then they will kill me, but they will let you live.
verses.kjv: Therefore it shall come to pass, when the Egyptians shall see thee, that they shall say, This is his wife: and they will kill me, but they will save thee alive.
verses.bsb: and when the Egyptians see you, they will say, ‘This is his wife.’ Then they will kill me but will let you live.

verses.esv: Say you are my sister, that it may go well with me because of you, and that my life may be spared for your sake.”
verses.kjv: Say, I pray thee, thou art my sister: that it may be well with me for thy sake; and my soul shall live because of thee.
verses.bsb: Please say you are my sister, so that I will be treated well for your sake, and on account of you my life will be spared.”

verses.esv: When Abram entered Egypt, the Egyptians saw that the woman was very beautiful.
verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass, that, when Abram was come into Egypt, the Egyptians beheld the woman that she was very fair.
verses.bsb: So when Abram entered Egypt, the Egyptians saw that the woman was very beautiful.

verses.esv: And when the princes of Pharaoh saw her, they praised her to Pharaoh. And the woman was taken into Pharaoh's house.
verses.kjv: The princes also of Pharaoh saw her, and commended her before Pharaoh: and the woman was taken into Pharaoh’s house.
verses.bsb: When Pharaoh’s officials saw Sarai, they commended her to him, and she was taken into the palace of Pharaoh.

verses.esv: And for her sake he dealt well with Abram; and he had sheep, oxen, male donkeys, male servants, female servants, female donkeys, and camels.
verses.kjv: And he entreated Abram well for her sake: and he had sheep, and oxen, and he asses, and menservants, and maidservants, and she asses, and camels.
verses.bsb: He treated Abram well on her account, and Abram acquired sheep and cattle, male and female donkeys, menservants and maidservants, and camels.

verses.esv: But the LORD afflicted Pharaoh and his house with great plagues because of Sarai, Abram's wife.
verses.kjv: And the Lord plagued Pharaoh and his house with great plagues because of Sarai Abram’s wife.
verses.bsb: The LORD, however, afflicted Pharaoh and his household with severe plagues because of Abram’s wife Sarai.

verses.esv: So Pharaoh called Abram and said, “What is this you have done to me? Why did you not tell me that she was your wife?
verses.kjv: And Pharaoh called Abram, and said, What is this that thou hast done unto me? why didst thou not tell me that she was thy wife?
verses.bsb: So Pharaoh summoned Abram and asked, “What have you done to me? Why didn’t you tell me she was your wife?

verses.esv: Why did you say, ‘She is my sister,’ so that I took her for my wife? Now then, here is your wife; take her, and go.”
verses.kjv: Why saidst thou, She is my sister? so I might have taken her to me to wife: now therefore behold thy wife, take her, and go thy way.
verses.bsb: Why did you say, ‘She is my sister,’ so that I took her as my wife? Now then, here is your wife. Take her and go!”

verses.esv: And Pharaoh gave men orders concerning him, and they sent him away with his wife and all that he had.
verses.kjv: And Pharaoh commanded his men concerning him: and they sent him away, and his wife, and all that he had.
verses.bsb: Then Pharaoh gave his men orders concerning Abram, and they sent him away with his wife and all his possessions.

verses.esv: So Abram went up from Egypt, he and his wife and all that he had, and Lot with him, into the Negeb.
verses.kjv: AND Abram went up out of Egypt, he, and his wife, and all that he had, and Lot with him, into the south.
verses.bsb: So Abram went up out of Egypt into the Negev—he and his wife and all his possessions—and Lot was with him.

verses.esv: Now Abram was very rich in livestock, in silver, and in gold.
verses.kjv: And Abram was very rich in cattle, in silver, and in gold.
verses.bsb: And Abram had become extremely wealthy in livestock and silver and gold.

verses.esv: And he journeyed on from the Negeb as far as Bethel to the place where his tent had been at the beginning, between Bethel and Ai,
verses.kjv: And he went on his journeys from the south even to Beth-el, unto the place where his tent had been at the beginning, between Beth-el and Hai;
verses.bsb: From the Negev he journeyed from place to place toward Bethel, until he came to the place between Bethel and Ai where his tent had formerly been pitched,

verses.esv: to the place where he had made an altar at the first. And there Abram called upon the name of the LORD.
verses.kjv: Unto the place of the altar, which he had made there at the first: and there Abram called on the name of the Lord.
verses.bsb: to the site where he had built the altar. And there Abram called on the name of the LORD.

verses.esv: And Lot, who went with Abram, also had flocks and herds and tents,
verses.kjv: ¶ And Lot also, which went with Abram, had flocks, and herds, and tents.
verses.bsb: Now Lot, who was traveling with Abram, also had flocks and herds and tents.

verses.esv: so that the land could not support both of them dwelling together; for their possessions were so great that they could not dwell together,
verses.kjv: And the land was not able to bear them, that they might dwell together: for their substance was great, so that they could not dwell together.
verses.bsb: But the land was unable to support both of them while they stayed together, for they had so many possessions that they were unable to coexist.

verses.esv: and there was strife between the herdsmen of Abram's livestock and the herdsmen of Lot's livestock. At that time the Canaanites and the Perizzites were dwelling in the land.
verses.kjv: And there was a strife between the herdmen of Abram’s cattle and the herdmen of Lot’s cattle: and the Canaanite and the Perizzite dwelled then in the land.
verses.bsb: And there was discord between the herdsmen of Abram and the herdsmen of Lot. At that time the Canaanites and the Perizzites were also living in the land.

verses.esv: Then Abram said to Lot, “Let there be no strife between you and me, and between your herdsmen and my herdsmen, for we are kinsmen.
verses.kjv: And Abram said unto Lot, Let there be no strife, I pray thee, between me and thee, and between my herdmen and thy herdmen; for we be brethren.
verses.bsb: So Abram said to Lot, “Please let there be no contention between you and me, or between your herdsmen and my herdsmen. After all, we are brothers.

verses.esv: Is not the whole land before you? Separate yourself from me. If you take the left hand, then I will go to the right, or if you take the right hand, then I will go to the left.”
verses.kjv: Is not the whole land before thee? separate thyself, I pray thee, from me: if thou wilt take the left hand, then I will go to the right; or if thou depart to the right hand, then I will go to the left.
verses.bsb: Is not the whole land before you? Now separate yourself from me. If you go to the left, I will go to the right; if you go to the right, I will go to the left.”

verses.esv: And Lot lifted up his eyes and saw that the Jordan Valley was well watered everywhere like the garden of the LORD, like the land of Egypt, in the direction of Zoar. (This was before the LORD destroyed Sodom and Gomorrah.)
verses.kjv: And Lot lifted up his eyes, and beheld all the plain of Jordan, that it was well watered every where, before the Lord destroyed Sodom and Gomorrah, even as the garden of the Lord, like the land of Egypt, as thou comest unto Zoar.
verses.bsb: And Lot looked out and saw that the whole plain of the Jordan, all the way to Zoar, was well watered like the garden of the LORD, like the land of Egypt. (This was before the LORD destroyed Sodom and Gomorrah.)

verses.esv: So Lot chose for himself all the Jordan Valley, and Lot journeyed east. Thus they separated from each other.
verses.kjv: Then Lot chose him all the plain of Jordan; and Lot journeyed east: and they separated themselves the one from the other.
verses.bsb: So Lot chose the whole plain of the Jordan for himself and set out toward the east. And Abram and Lot parted company.

verses.esv: Abram settled in the land of Canaan, while Lot settled among the cities of the valley and moved his tent as far as Sodom.
verses.kjv: Abram dwelled in the land of Canaan, and Lot dwelled in the cities of the plain, and pitched his tent toward Sodom.
verses.bsb: Abram lived in the land of Canaan, but Lot settled in the cities of the plain and pitched his tent toward Sodom.

verses.esv: Now the men of Sodom were wicked, great sinners against the LORD.
verses.kjv: But the men of Sodom were wicked and sinners before the Lord exceedingly.
verses.bsb: But the men of Sodom were wicked, sinning greatly against the LORD.

verses.esv: The LORD said to Abram, after Lot had separated from him, “Lift up your eyes and look from the place where you are, northward and southward and eastward and westward,
verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord said unto Abram, after that Lot was separated from him, Lift up now thine eyes, and look from the place where thou art northward, and southward, and eastward, and westward:
verses.bsb: After Lot had departed, the LORD said to Abram, “Now lift up your eyes from the place where you are, and look to the north and south and east and west,

verses.esv: for all the land that you see I will give to you and to your offspring forever.
verses.kjv: For all the land which thou seest, to thee will I give it, and to thy seed for ever.
verses.bsb: for all the land that you see, I will give to you and your offspring forever.

verses.esv: I will make your offspring as the dust of the earth, so that if one can count the dust of the earth, your offspring also can be counted.
verses.kjv: And I will make thy seed as the dust of the earth: so that if a man can number the dust of the earth, then shall thy seed also be numbered.
verses.bsb: I will make your offspring like the dust of the earth, so that if one could count the dust of the earth, then your offspring could be counted.

verses.esv: Arise, walk through the length and the breadth of the land, for I will give it to you.”
verses.kjv: Arise, walk through the land in the length of it and in the breadth of it; for I will give it unto thee.
verses.bsb: Get up and walk around the land, through its length and breadth, for I will give it to you.”

verses.esv: So Abram moved his tent and came and settled by the oaks of Mamre, which are at Hebron, and there he built an altar to the LORD.
verses.kjv: Then Abram removed his tent, and came and dwelt in the plain of Mamre, which is in Hebron, and built there an altar unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: So Abram moved his tent and went to live near the Oaks of Mamre at Hebron, where he built an altar to the LORD.

verses.esv: In the days of Amraphel king of Shinar, Arioch king of Ellasar, Chedorlaomer king of Elam, and Tidal king of Goiim,
verses.kjv: AND it came to pass in the days of Amraphel king of Shinar, Arioch king of Ellasar, Chedorlaomer king of Elam, and Tidal king of nations;
verses.bsb: In those days Amraphel king of Shinar, Arioch king of Ellasar, Chedorlaomer king of Elam, and Tidal king of Goiim

verses.esv: these kings made war with Bera king of Sodom, Birsha king of Gomorrah, Shinab king of Admah, Shemeber king of Zeboiim, and the king of Bela (that is, Zoar).
verses.kjv: That these made war with Bera king of Sodom, and with Birsha king of Gomorrah, Shinab king of Admah, and Shemeber king of Zeboiim, and the king of Bela, which is Zoar.
verses.bsb: went to war against Bera king of Sodom, Birsha king of Gomorrah, Shinab king of Admah, Shemeber king of Zeboiim, and the king of Bela (that is, Zoar).

verses.esv: And all these joined forces in the Valley of Siddim (that is, the Salt Sea).
verses.kjv: All these were joined together in the vale of Siddim, which is the salt sea.
verses.bsb: The latter five came as allies to the Valley of Siddim (that is, the Salt Sea).

verses.esv: Twelve years they had served Chedorlaomer, but in the thirteenth year they rebelled.
verses.kjv: Twelve years they served Chedorlaomer, and in the thirteenth year they rebelled.
verses.bsb: For twelve years they had been subject to Chedorlaomer, but in the thirteenth year they rebelled.

verses.esv: In the fourteenth year Chedorlaomer and the kings who were with him came and defeated the Rephaim in Ashteroth-karnaim, the Zuzim in Ham, the Emim in Shaveh-kiriathaim,
verses.kjv: And in the fourteenth year came Chedorlaomer, and the kings that were with him, and smote the Rephaims in Ashteroth Karnaim, and the Zuzims in Ham, and the Emims in Shaveh Kiriathaim,
verses.bsb: In the fourteenth year, Chedorlaomer and the kings allied with him went out and defeated the Rephaites in Ashteroth-karnaim, the Zuzites in Ham, the Emites in Shaveh-kiriathaim,

verses.esv: and the Horites in their hill country of Seir as far as El-paran on the border of the wilderness.
verses.kjv: And the Horites in their mount Seir, unto El-paran, which is by the wilderness.
verses.bsb: and the Horites in the area of Mount Seir, as far as El-paran, which is near the desert.

verses.esv: Then they turned back and came to En-mishpat (that is, Kadesh) and defeated all the country of the Amalekites, and also the Amorites who were dwelling in Hazazon-tamar.
verses.kjv: And they returned, and came to En-mishpat, which is Kadesh, and smote all the country of the Amalekites, and also the Amorites, that dwelt in Hazezon-tamar.
verses.bsb: Then they turned back to invade En-mishpat (that is, Kadesh), and they conquered the whole territory of the Amalekites, as well as the Amorites who lived in Hazazon-tamar.

verses.esv: Then the king of Sodom, the king of Gomorrah, the king of Admah, the king of Zeboiim, and the king of Bela (that is, Zoar) went out, and they joined battle in the Valley of Siddim
verses.kjv: And there went out the king of Sodom, and the king of Gomorrah, and the king of Admah, and the king of Zeboiim, and the king of Bela (the same is Zoar;) and they joined battle with them in the vale of Siddim;
verses.bsb: Then the king of Sodom, the king of Gomorrah, the king of Admah, the king of Zeboiim, and the king of Bela (that is, Zoar) marched out and arrayed themselves for battle in the Valley of Siddim

verses.esv: with Chedorlaomer king of Elam, Tidal king of Goiim, Amraphel king of Shinar, and Arioch king of Ellasar, four kings against five.
verses.kjv: With Chedorlaomer the king of Elam, and with Tidal king of nations, and Amraphel king of Shinar, and Arioch king of Ellasar; four kings with five.
verses.bsb: against Chedorlaomer king of Elam, Tidal king of Goiim, Amraphel king of Shinar, and Arioch king of Ellasar—four kings against five.

verses.esv: Now the Valley of Siddim was full of bitumen pits, and as the kings of Sodom and Gomorrah fled, some fell into them, and the rest fled to the hill country.
verses.kjv: And the vale of Siddim was full of slimepits; and the kings of Sodom and Gomorrah fled, and fell there; and they that remained fled to the mountain.
verses.bsb: Now the Valley of Siddim was full of tar pits, and as the kings of Sodom and Gomorrah fled, some men fell into the pits, but the survivors fled to the hill country.

verses.esv: So the enemy took all the possessions of Sodom and Gomorrah, and all their provisions, and went their way.
verses.kjv: And they took all the goods of Sodom and Gomorrah, and all their victuals, and went their way.
verses.bsb: The four kings seized all the goods of Sodom and Gomorrah and all their food, and they went on their way.

verses.esv: They also took Lot, the son of Abram's brother, who was dwelling in Sodom, and his possessions, and went their way.
verses.kjv: And they took Lot, Abram’s brother’s son, who dwelt in Sodom, and his goods, and departed.
verses.bsb: They also carried off Abram’s nephew Lot and his possessions, since Lot was living in Sodom.

verses.esv: Then one who had escaped came and told Abram the Hebrew, who was living by the oaks of Mamre the Amorite, brother of Eshcol and of Aner. These were allies of Abram.
verses.kjv: ¶ And there came one that had escaped, and told Abram the Hebrew; for he dwelt in the plain of Mamre the Amorite, brother of Eshcol, and brother of Aner: and these were confederate with Abram.
verses.bsb: Then an escapee came and reported this to Abram the Hebrew. Now Abram was living near the Oaks of Mamre the Amorite, a brother of Eshcol and Aner, all of whom were bound by treaty to Abram.

verses.esv: When Abram heard that his kinsman had been taken captive, he led forth his trained men, born in his house, 318 of them, and went in pursuit as far as Dan.
verses.kjv: And when Abram heard that his brother was taken captive, he armed his trained servants, born in his own house, three hundred and eighteen, and pursued them unto Dan.
verses.bsb: And when Abram heard that his relative had been captured, he mobilized the 318 trained men born in his household, and they set out in pursuit as far as Dan.

verses.esv: And he divided his forces against them by night, he and his servants, and defeated them and pursued them to Hobah, north of Damascus.
verses.kjv: And he divided himself against them, he and his servants, by night, and smote them, and pursued them unto Hobah, which is on the left hand of Damascus.
verses.bsb: During the night, Abram divided his forces and routed Chedorlaomer’s army, pursuing them as far as Hobah, north of Damascus.

verses.esv: Then he brought back all the possessions, and also brought back his kinsman Lot with his possessions, and the women and the people.
verses.kjv: And he brought back all the goods, and also brought again his brother Lot, and his goods, and the women also, and the people.
verses.bsb: He retrieved all the goods, as well as his relative Lot and his possessions, together with the women and the rest of the people.

verses.esv: After his return from the defeat of Chedorlaomer and the kings who were with him, the king of Sodom went out to meet him at the Valley of Shaveh (that is, the King's Valley).
verses.kjv: ¶ And the king of Sodom went out to meet him after his return from the slaughter of Chedorlaomer, and of the kings that were with him, at the valley of Shaveh, which is the king’s dale.
verses.bsb: After Abram returned from defeating Chedorlaomer and the kings allied with him, the king of Sodom went out to meet him in the Valley of Shaveh (that is, the King’s Valley).

verses.esv: And Melchizedek king of Salem brought out bread and wine. (He was priest of God Most High.)
verses.kjv: And Melchizedek king of Salem brought forth bread and wine: and he was the priest of the most high God.
verses.bsb: Then Melchizedek king of Salem brought out bread and wine—since he was priest of God Most High—

verses.esv: And he blessed him and said, “Blessed be Abram by God Most High, Possessor of heaven and earth;
verses.kjv: And he blessed him, and said, Blessed be Abram of the most high God, possessor of heaven and earth:
verses.bsb: and he blessed Abram and said:  “Blessed be Abram by God Most High, Creator of heaven and earth,

verses.esv: and blessed be God Most High, who has delivered your enemies into your hand!” And Abram gave him a tenth of everything.
verses.kjv: And blessed be the most high God, which hath delivered thine enemies into thy hand. And he gave him tithes of all.
verses.bsb: and blessed be God Most High, who has delivered your enemies into your hand.”   Then Abram gave Melchizedek a tenth of everything.

verses.esv: And the king of Sodom said to Abram, “Give me the persons, but take the goods for yourself.”
verses.kjv: And the king of Sodom said unto Abram, Give me the persons, and take the goods to thyself.
verses.bsb: The king of Sodom said to Abram, “Give me the people, but take the goods for yourself.”

verses.esv: But Abram said to the king of Sodom, “I have lifted my hand to the LORD, God Most High, Possessor of heaven and earth,
verses.kjv: And Abram said to the king of Sodom, I have lift up mine hand unto the Lord, the most high God, the possessor of heaven and earth,
verses.bsb: But Abram replied to the king of Sodom, “I have raised my hand to the LORD God Most High, Creator of heaven and earth,

verses.esv: that I would not take a thread or a sandal strap or anything that is yours, lest you should say, ‘I have made Abram rich.’
verses.kjv: That I will not take from a thread even to a shoelatchet, and that I will not take any thing that is thine, lest thou shouldest say, I have made Abram rich:
verses.bsb: that I will not accept even a thread, or a strap of a sandal, or anything that belongs to you, lest you should say, ‘I have made Abram rich.’

verses.esv: I will take nothing but what the young men have eaten, and the share of the men who went with me. Let Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre take their share.”
verses.kjv: Save only that which the young men have eaten, and the portion of the men which went with me, Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre; let them take their portion.
verses.bsb: I will accept nothing but what my men have eaten and the share for the men who went with me—Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre. They may take their portion.”

verses.esv: After these things the word of the LORD came to Abram in a vision: “Fear not, Abram, I am your shield; your reward shall be very great.”
verses.kjv: AFTER these things the word of the Lord came unto Abram in a vision, saying, Fear not, Abram: I am thy shield, and thy exceeding great reward.
verses.bsb: After these events, the word of the LORD came to Abram in a vision:  “Do not be afraid, Abram. I am your shield, your very great reward.”

verses.esv: But Abram said, “O Lord GOD, what will you give me, for I continue childless, and the heir of my house is Eliezer of Damascus?”
verses.kjv: And Abram said, Lord God, what wilt thou give me, seeing I go childless, and the steward of my house is this Eliezer of Damascus?
verses.bsb: But Abram replied, “O Lord GOD, what can You give me, since I remain childless, and the heir of my house is Eliezer of Damascus?”

verses.esv: And Abram said, “Behold, you have given me no offspring, and a member of my household will be my heir.”
verses.kjv: And Abram said, Behold, to me thou hast given no seed: and, lo, one born in my house is mine heir.
verses.bsb: Abram continued, “Behold, You have given me no offspring, so a servant in my household will be my heir.”

verses.esv: And behold, the word of the LORD came to him: “This man shall not be your heir; your very own son shall be your heir.”
verses.kjv: And, behold, the word of the Lord came unto him, saying, This shall not be thine heir; but he that shall come forth out of thine own bowels shall be thine heir.
verses.bsb: Then the word of the LORD came to Abram, saying, “This one will not be your heir, but one who comes from your own body will be your heir.”

verses.esv: And he brought him outside and said, “Look toward heaven, and number the stars, if you are able to number them.” Then he said to him, “So shall your offspring be.”
verses.kjv: And he brought him forth abroad, and said, Look now toward heaven, and tell the stars, if thou be able to number them: and he said unto him, So shall thy seed be.
verses.bsb: And the LORD took him outside and said, “Now look to the heavens and count the stars, if you are able.” Then He told him, “So shall your offspring be.”

verses.esv: And he believed the LORD, and he counted it to him as righteousness.
verses.kjv: And he believed in the Lord; and he counted it to him for righteousness.
verses.bsb: Abram believed the LORD, and it was credited to him as righteousness.

verses.esv: And he said to him, “I am the LORD who brought you out from Ur of the Chaldeans to give you this land to possess.”
verses.kjv: And he said unto him, I am the Lord that brought thee out of Ur of the Chaldees, to give thee this land to inherit it.
verses.bsb: The LORD also told him, “I am the LORD, who brought you out of Ur of the Chaldeans to give you this land to possess.”

verses.esv: But he said, “O Lord GOD, how am I to know that I shall possess it?”
verses.kjv: And he said, Lord God, whereby shall I know that I shall inherit it?
verses.bsb: But Abram replied, “Lord GOD, how can I know that I will possess it?”

verses.esv: He said to him, “Bring me a heifer three years old, a female goat three years old, a ram three years old, a turtledove, and a young pigeon.”
verses.kjv: And he said unto him, Take me an heifer of three years old, and a she goat of three years old, and a ram of three years old, and a turtledove, and a young pigeon.
verses.bsb: And the LORD said to him, “Bring Me a heifer, a goat, and a ram, each three years old, along with a turtledove and a young pigeon.”

verses.esv: And he brought him all these, cut them in half, and laid each half over against the other. But he did not cut the birds in half.
verses.kjv: And he took unto him all these, and divided them in the midst, and laid each piece one against another: but the birds divided he not.
verses.bsb: So Abram brought all these to Him, split each of them down the middle, and laid the halves opposite each other. The birds, however, he did not cut in half.

verses.esv: And when birds of prey came down on the carcasses, Abram drove them away.
verses.kjv: And when the fowls came down upon the carcases, Abram drove them away.
verses.bsb: And the birds of prey descended on the carcasses, but Abram drove them away.

verses.esv: As the sun was going down, a deep sleep fell on Abram. And behold, dreadful and great darkness fell upon him.
verses.kjv: And when the sun was going down, a deep sleep fell upon Abram; and, lo, an horror of great darkness fell upon him.
verses.bsb: As the sun was setting, Abram fell into a deep sleep, and suddenly great terror and darkness overwhelmed him.

verses.esv: Then the LORD said to Abram, “Know for certain that your offspring will be sojourners in a land that is not theirs and will be servants there, and they will be afflicted for four hundred years.
verses.kjv: And he said unto Abram, Know of a surety that thy seed shall be a stranger in a land that is not theirs, and shall serve them; and they shall afflict them four hundred years;
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Abram, “Know for certain that your descendants will be strangers in a land that is not their own, and they will be enslaved and mistreated four hundred years.

verses.esv: But I will bring judgment on the nation that they serve, and afterward they shall come out with great possessions.
verses.kjv: And also that nation, whom they shall serve, will I judge: and afterward shall they come out with great substance.
verses.bsb: But I will judge the nation they serve as slaves, and afterward they will depart with many possessions.

verses.esv: As for you, you shall go to your fathers in peace; you shall be buried in a good old age.
verses.kjv: And thou shalt go to thy fathers in peace; thou shalt be buried in a good old age.
verses.bsb: You, however, will go to your fathers in peace and be buried at a ripe old age.

verses.esv: And they shall come back here in the fourth generation, for the iniquity of the Amorites is not yet complete.”
verses.kjv: But in the fourth generation they shall come hither again: for the iniquity of the Amorites is not yet full.
verses.bsb: In the fourth generation your descendants will return here, for the iniquity of the Amorites is not yet complete.”

verses.esv: When the sun had gone down and it was dark, behold, a smoking fire pot and a flaming torch passed between these pieces.
verses.kjv: And it came to pass, that, when the sun went down, and it was dark, behold a smoking furnace, and a burning lamp that passed between those pieces.
verses.bsb: When the sun had set and darkness had fallen, behold, a smoking firepot and a flaming torch appeared and passed between the halves of the carcasses.

verses.esv: On that day the LORD made a covenant with Abram, saying, “To your offspring I give this land, from the river of Egypt to the great river, the river Euphrates,
verses.kjv: In the same day the Lord made a covenant with Abram, saying, Unto thy seed have I given this land, from the river of Egypt unto the great river, the river Euphrates:
verses.bsb: On that day the LORD made a covenant with Abram, saying, “To your descendants I have given this land—from the river of Egypt to the great River Euphrates—

verses.esv: the land of the Kenites, the Kenizzites, the Kadmonites,
verses.kjv: The Kenites, and the Kenizzites, and the Kadmonites,
verses.bsb: the land of the Kenites, Kenizzites, Kadmonites,

verses.esv: the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Rephaim,
verses.kjv: And the Hittites, and the Perizzites, and the Rephaims,
verses.bsb: Hittites, Perizzites, Rephaites,

verses.esv: the Amorites, the Canaanites, the Girgashites and the Jebusites.”
verses.kjv: And the Amorites, and the Canaanites, and the Girgashites, and the Jebusites.
verses.bsb: Amorites, Canaanites, Girgashites, and Jebusites.”

verses.esv: Now Sarai, Abram's wife, had borne him no children. She had a female Egyptian servant whose name was Hagar.
verses.kjv: NOW Sarai Abram’s wife bare him no children: and she had an handmaid, an Egyptian, whose name was Hagar.
verses.bsb: Now Abram’s wife Sarai had borne him no children, but she had an Egyptian maidservant named Hagar.

verses.esv: And Sarai said to Abram, “Behold now, the LORD has prevented me from bearing children. Go in to my servant; it may be that I shall obtain children by her.” And Abram listened to the voice of Sarai.
verses.kjv: And Sarai said unto Abram, Behold now, the Lord hath restrained me from bearing: I pray thee, go in unto my maid; it may be that I may obtain children by her. And Abram hearkened to the voice of Sarai.
verses.bsb: So Sarai said to Abram, “Look now, the LORD has prevented me from bearing children. Please go to my maidservant; perhaps I can build a family by her.”  And Abram listened to the voice of Sarai.

verses.esv: So, after Abram had lived ten years in the land of Canaan, Sarai, Abram's wife, took Hagar the Egyptian, her servant, and gave her to Abram her husband as a wife.
verses.kjv: And Sarai Abram’s wife took Hagar her maid the Egyptian, after Abram had dwelt ten years in the land of Canaan, and gave her to her husband Abram to be his wife.
verses.bsb: So after he had lived in Canaan for ten years, his wife Sarai took her Egyptian maidservant Hagar and gave her to Abram to be his wife.

verses.esv: And he went in to Hagar, and she conceived. And when she saw that she had conceived, she looked with contempt on her mistress.
verses.kjv: ¶ And he went in unto Hagar, and she conceived: and when she saw that she had conceived, her mistress was despised in her eyes.
verses.bsb: And he slept with Hagar, and she conceived. But when Hagar realized that she was pregnant, she began to despise her mistress.

verses.esv: And Sarai said to Abram, “May the wrong done to me be on you! I gave my servant to your embrace, and when she saw that she had conceived, she looked on me with contempt. May the LORD judge between you and me!”
verses.kjv: And Sarai said unto Abram, My wrong be upon thee: I have given my maid into thy bosom; and when she saw that she had conceived, I was despised in her eyes: the Lord judge between me and thee.
verses.bsb: Then Sarai said to Abram, “May the wrong done to me be upon you! I delivered my servant into your arms, and ever since she saw that she was pregnant, she has treated me with contempt. May the LORD judge between you and me.”

verses.esv: But Abram said to Sarai, “Behold, your servant is in your power; do to her as you please.” Then Sarai dealt harshly with her, and she fled from her.
verses.kjv: But Abram said unto Sarai, Behold, thy maid is in thy hand; do to her as it pleaseth thee. And when Sarai dealt hardly with her, she fled from her face.
verses.bsb: “Here,” said Abram, “your servant is in your hands. Do whatever you want with her.” Then Sarai treated Hagar so harshly that she fled from her.

verses.esv: The angel of the LORD found her by a spring of water in the wilderness, the spring on the way to Shur.
verses.kjv: ¶ And the angel of the Lord found her by a fountain of water in the wilderness, by the fountain in the way to Shur.
verses.bsb: Now the angel of the LORD found Hagar by a spring of water in the desert—the spring along the road to Shur.

verses.esv: And he said, “Hagar, servant of Sarai, where have you come from and where are you going?” She said, “I am fleeing from my mistress Sarai.”
verses.kjv: And he said, Hagar, Sarai’s maid, whence camest thou? and whither wilt thou go? And she said, I flee from the face of my mistress Sarai.
verses.bsb: “Hagar, servant of Sarai,” he said, “where have you come from, and where are you going?”  “I am running away from my mistress Sarai,” she replied.

verses.esv: The angel of the LORD said to her, “Return to your mistress and submit to her.”
verses.kjv: And the angel of the Lord said unto her, Return to thy mistress, and submit thyself under her hands.
verses.bsb: So the angel of the LORD told her, “Return to your mistress and submit to her authority.”

verses.esv: The angel of the LORD also said to her, “I will surely multiply your offspring so that they cannot be numbered for multitude.”
verses.kjv: And the angel of the Lord said unto her, I will multiply thy seed exceedingly, that it shall not be numbered for multitude.
verses.bsb: Then the angel added, “I will greatly multiply your offspring so that they will be too numerous to count.”

verses.esv: And the angel of the LORD said to her, “Behold, you are pregnant and shall bear a son. You shall call his name Ishmael, because the LORD has listened to your affliction.
verses.kjv: And the angel of the Lord said unto her, Behold, thou art with child, and shalt bear a son, and shalt call his name Ishmael; because the Lord hath heard thy affliction.
verses.bsb: The angel of the LORD proceeded:  “Behold, you have conceived and will bear a son. And you shall name him Ishmael, for the LORD has heard your cry of affliction.

verses.esv: He shall be a wild donkey of a man, his hand against everyone and everyone's hand against him, and he shall dwell over against all his kinsmen.”
verses.kjv: And he will be a wild man; his hand will be against every man, and every man’s hand against him; and he shall dwell in the presence of all his brethren.
verses.bsb: He will be a wild donkey of a man, and his hand will be against everyone, and everyone’s hand against him; he will live in hostility toward all his brothers.”

verses.esv: So she called the name of the LORD who spoke to her, “You are a God of seeing,” for she said, “Truly here I have seen him who looks after me.”
verses.kjv: And she called the name of the Lord that spake unto her, Thou God seest me: for she said, Have I also here looked after him that seeth me?
verses.bsb: So Hagar gave this name to the LORD who had spoken to her: “You are the God who sees me,” for she said, “Here I have seen the One who sees me!”

verses.esv: Therefore the well was called Beer-lahai-roi; it lies between Kadesh and Bered.
verses.kjv: Wherefore the well was called Beer-lahai-roi; behold, it is between Kadesh and Bered.
verses.bsb: Therefore the well was called Beer-lahai-roi. It is located between Kadesh and Bered.

verses.esv: And Hagar bore Abram a son, and Abram called the name of his son, whom Hagar bore, Ishmael.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Hagar bare Abram a son: and Abram called his son’s name, which Hagar bare, Ishmael.
verses.bsb: And Hagar bore Abram a son, and Abram gave the name Ishmael to the son she had borne.

verses.esv: Abram was eighty-six years old when Hagar bore Ishmael to Abram.
verses.kjv: And Abram was fourscore and six years old, when Hagar bare Ishmael to Abram.
verses.bsb: Abram was eighty-six years old when Hagar bore Ishmael to him.

verses.esv: When Abram was ninety-nine years old the LORD appeared to Abram and said to him, “I am God Almighty; walk before me, and be blameless,
verses.kjv: AND when Abram was ninety years old and nine, the Lord appeared to Abram, and said unto him, I am the Almighty God; walk before me, and be thou perfect.
verses.bsb: When Abram was ninety-nine years old, the LORD appeared to him and said, “I am God Almighty. Walk before Me and be blameless.

verses.esv: that I may make my covenant between me and you, and may multiply you greatly.”
verses.kjv: And I will make my covenant between me and thee, and will multiply thee exceedingly.
verses.bsb: I will establish My covenant between Me and you, and I will multiply you exceedingly.”

verses.esv: Then Abram fell on his face. And God said to him,
verses.kjv: And Abram fell on his face: and God talked with him, saying,
verses.bsb: Then Abram fell facedown, and God said to him,

verses.esv: “Behold, my covenant is with you, and you shall be the father of a multitude of nations.
verses.kjv: As for me, behold, my covenant is with thee, and thou shalt be a father of many nations.
verses.bsb: “As for Me, this is My covenant with you: You will be the father of many nations.

verses.esv: No longer shall your name be called Abram, but your name shall be Abraham, for I have made you the father of a multitude of nations.
verses.kjv: Neither shall thy name any more be called Abram, but thy name shall be Abraham; for a father of many nations have I made thee.
verses.bsb: No longer will you be called Abram, but your name will be Abraham, for I have made you a father of many nations.

verses.esv: I will make you exceedingly fruitful, and I will make you into nations, and kings shall come from you.
verses.kjv: And I will make thee exceeding fruitful, and I will make nations of thee, and kings shall come out of thee.
verses.bsb: I will make you exceedingly fruitful; I will make nations of you, and kings will descend from you.

verses.esv: And I will establish my covenant between me and you and your offspring after you throughout their generations for an everlasting covenant, to be God to you and to your offspring after you.
verses.kjv: And I will establish my covenant between me and thee and thy seed after thee in their generations for an everlasting covenant, to be a God unto thee, and to thy seed after thee.
verses.bsb: I will establish My covenant as an everlasting covenant between Me and you and your descendants after you, to be your God and the God of your descendants after you.

verses.esv: And I will give to you and to your offspring after you the land of your sojournings, all the land of Canaan, for an everlasting possession, and I will be their God.”
verses.kjv: And I will give unto thee, and to thy seed after thee, the land wherein thou art a stranger, all the land of Canaan, for an everlasting possession; and I will be their God.
verses.bsb: And to you and your descendants I will give the land where you are residing—all the land of Canaan—as an eternal possession; and I will be their God.”

verses.esv: And God said to Abraham, “As for you, you shall keep my covenant, you and your offspring after you throughout their generations.
verses.kjv: ¶ And God said unto Abraham, Thou shalt keep my covenant therefore, thou, and thy seed after thee in their generations.
verses.bsb: God also said to Abraham, “You must keep My covenant—you and your descendants in the generations after you.

verses.esv: This is my covenant, which you shall keep, between me and you and your offspring after you: Every male among you shall be circumcised.
verses.kjv: This is my covenant, which ye shall keep, between me and you and thy seed after thee; Every man child among you shall be circumcised.
verses.bsb: This is My covenant with you and your descendants after you, which you are to keep: Every male among you must be circumcised.

verses.esv: You shall be circumcised in the flesh of your foreskins, and it shall be a sign of the covenant between me and you.
verses.kjv: And ye shall circumcise the flesh of your foreskin; and it shall be a token of the covenant betwixt me and you.
verses.bsb: You are to circumcise the flesh of your foreskin, and this will be a sign of the covenant between Me and you.

verses.esv: He who is eight days old among you shall be circumcised. Every male throughout your generations, whether born in your house or bought with your money from any foreigner who is not of your offspring,
verses.kjv: And he that is eight days old shall be circumcised among you, every man child in your generations, he that is born in the house, or bought with money of any stranger, which is not of thy seed.
verses.bsb: Generation after generation, every male must be circumcised when he is eight days old, including those born in your household and those purchased from a foreigner—even those who are not your offspring.

verses.esv: both he who is born in your house and he who is bought with your money, shall surely be circumcised. So shall my covenant be in your flesh an everlasting covenant.
verses.kjv: He that is born in thy house, and he that is bought with thy money, must needs be circumcised: and my covenant shall be in your flesh for an everlasting covenant.
verses.bsb: Whether they are born in your household or purchased, they must be circumcised. My covenant in your flesh will be an everlasting covenant.

verses.esv: Any uncircumcised male who is not circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin shall be cut off from his people; he has broken my covenant.”
verses.kjv: And the uncircumcised man child whose flesh of his foreskin is not circumcised, that soul shall be cut off from his people; he hath broken my covenant.
verses.bsb: But if any male is not circumcised, he will be cut off from his people; he has broken My covenant.”

verses.esv: And God said to Abraham, “As for Sarai your wife, you shall not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall be her name.
verses.kjv: ¶ And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall her name be.
verses.bsb: Then God said to Abraham, “As for Sarai your wife, do not call her Sarai, for her name is to be Sarah.

verses.esv: I will bless her, and moreover, I will give you a son by her. I will bless her, and she shall become nations; kings of peoples shall come from her.”
verses.kjv: And I will bless her, and give thee a son also of her: yea, I will bless her, and she shall be a mother of nations; kings of people shall be of her.
verses.bsb: And I will bless her and will surely give you a son by her. I will bless her, and she will be the mother of nations; kings of peoples will descend from her.”

verses.esv: Then Abraham fell on his face and laughed and said to himself, “Shall a child be born to a man who is a hundred years old? Shall Sarah, who is ninety years old, bear a child?”
verses.kjv: Then Abraham fell upon his face, and laughed, and said in his heart, Shall a child be born unto him that is an hundred years old? and shall Sarah, that is ninety years old, bear?
verses.bsb: Abraham fell facedown. Then he laughed and said to himself, “Can a child be born to a man who is a hundred years old? Can Sarah give birth at the age of ninety?”
