verses.esv: And Abraham said to God, “Oh that Ishmael might live before you!”
verses.kjv: And Abraham said unto God, O that Ishmael might live before thee!
verses.bsb: And Abraham said to God, “O that Ishmael might live under Your blessing!”

verses.esv: God said, “No, but Sarah your wife shall bear you a son, and you shall call his name Isaac. I will establish my covenant with him as an everlasting covenant for his offspring after him.
verses.kjv: And God said, Sarah thy wife shall bear thee a son indeed; and thou shalt call his name Isaac: and I will establish my covenant with him for an everlasting covenant, and with his seed after him.
verses.bsb: But God replied, “Your wife Sarah will indeed bear you a son, and you are to name him Isaac. I will establish My covenant with him as an everlasting covenant for his descendants after him.

verses.esv: As for Ishmael, I have heard you; behold, I have blessed him and will make him fruitful and multiply him greatly. He shall father twelve princes, and I will make him into a great nation.
verses.kjv: And as for Ishmael, I have heard thee: Behold, I have blessed him, and will make him fruitful, and will multiply him exceedingly; twelve princes shall he beget, and I will make him a great nation.
verses.bsb: As for Ishmael, I have heard you, and I will surely bless him; I will make him fruitful and multiply him greatly. He will become the father of twelve rulers, and I will make him into a great nation.

verses.esv: But I will establish my covenant with Isaac, whom Sarah shall bear to you at this time next year.”
verses.kjv: But my covenant will I establish with Isaac, which Sarah shall bear unto thee at this set time in the next year.
verses.bsb: But I will establish My covenant with Isaac, whom Sarah will bear to you at this time next year.”

verses.esv: When he had finished talking with him, God went up from Abraham.
verses.kjv: And he left off talking with him, and God went up from Abraham.
verses.bsb: When He had finished speaking with Abraham, God went up from him.

verses.esv: Then Abraham took Ishmael his son and all those born in his house or bought with his money, every male among the men of Abraham's house, and he circumcised the flesh of their foreskins that very day, as God had said to him.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Abraham took Ishmael his son, and all that were born in his house, and all that were bought with his money, every male among the men of Abraham’s house; and circumcised the flesh of their foreskin in the selfsame day, as God had said unto him.
verses.bsb: On that very day Abraham took his son Ishmael and all those born in his household or purchased with his money—every male among the members of Abraham’s household—and he circumcised them, just as God had told him.

verses.esv: Abraham was ninety-nine years old when he was circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin.
verses.kjv: And Abraham was ninety years old and nine, when he was circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin.
verses.bsb: So Abraham was ninety-nine years old when he was circumcised,

verses.esv: And Ishmael his son was thirteen years old when he was circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin.
verses.kjv: And Ishmael his son was thirteen years old, when he was circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin.
verses.bsb: and his son Ishmael was thirteen;

verses.esv: That very day Abraham and his son Ishmael were circumcised.
verses.kjv: In the selfsame day was Abraham circumcised, and Ishmael his son.
verses.bsb: Abraham and his son Ishmael were circumcised on the same day.

verses.esv: And all the men of his house, those born in the house and those bought with money from a foreigner, were circumcised with him.
verses.kjv: And all the men of his house, born in the house, and bought with money of the stranger, were circumcised with him.
verses.bsb: And all the men of Abraham’s household—both servants born in his household and those purchased from foreigners—were circumcised with him.

verses.esv: And the LORD appeared to him by the oaks of Mamre, as he sat at the door of his tent in the heat of the day.
verses.kjv: AND the Lord appeared unto him in the plains of Mamre: and he sat in the tent door in the heat of the day;
verses.bsb: Then the LORD appeared to Abraham by the Oaks of Mamre in the heat of the day, while he was sitting at the entrance of his tent.

verses.esv: He lifted up his eyes and looked, and behold, three men were standing in front of him. When he saw them, he ran from the tent door to meet them and bowed himself to the earth
verses.kjv: And he lift up his eyes and looked, and, lo, three men stood by him: and when he saw them, he ran to meet them from the tent door, and bowed himself toward the ground,
verses.bsb: And Abraham looked up and saw three men standing nearby. When he saw them, he ran from the entrance of his tent to meet them and bowed low to the ground.

verses.esv: and said, “O Lord, if I have found favor in your sight, do not pass by your servant.
verses.kjv: And said, My Lord, if now I have found favour in thy sight, pass not away, I pray thee, from thy servant:
verses.bsb: “My lord,” said Abraham, “if I have found favor in your sight, please do not pass your servant by.

verses.esv: Let a little water be brought, and wash your feet, and rest yourselves under the tree,
verses.kjv: Let a little water, I pray you, be fetched, and wash your feet, and rest yourselves under the tree:
verses.bsb: Let a little water be brought, that you may wash your feet and rest yourselves under the tree.

verses.esv: while I bring a morsel of bread, that you may refresh yourselves, and after that you may pass on—since you have come to your servant.” So they said, “Do as you have said.”
verses.kjv: And I will fetch a morsel of bread, and comfort ye your hearts; after that ye shall pass on: for therefore are ye come to your servant. And they said, So do, as thou hast said.
verses.bsb: And I will bring a bit of bread so that you may refresh yourselves. This is why you have passed your servant’s way. After that, you may continue on your way.”  “Yes,” they replied, “you may do as you have said.”

verses.esv: And Abraham went quickly into the tent to Sarah and said, “Quick! Three seahs of fine flour! Knead it, and make cakes.”
verses.kjv: And Abraham hastened into the tent unto Sarah, and said, Make ready quickly three measures of fine meal, knead it, and make cakes upon the hearth.
verses.bsb: So Abraham hurried into the tent and said to Sarah, “Quick! Prepare three seahs of fine flour, knead it, and bake some bread.”

verses.esv: And Abraham ran to the herd and took a calf, tender and good, and gave it to a young man, who prepared it quickly.
verses.kjv: And Abraham ran unto the herd, and fetcht a calf tender and good, and gave it unto a young man; and he hasted to dress it.
verses.bsb: Meanwhile, Abraham ran to the herd, selected a tender and choice calf, and gave it to a servant, who hurried to prepare it.

verses.esv: Then he took curds and milk and the calf that he had prepared, and set it before them. And he stood by them under the tree while they ate.
verses.kjv: And he took butter, and milk, and the calf which he had dressed, and set it before them; and he stood by them under the tree, and they did eat.
verses.bsb: Then Abraham brought curds and milk and the calf that had been prepared, and he set them before the men and stood by them under the tree as they ate.

verses.esv: They said to him, “Where is Sarah your wife?” And he said, “She is in the tent.”
verses.kjv: ¶ And they said unto him, Where is Sarah thy wife? And he said, Behold, in the tent.
verses.bsb: “Where is your wife Sarah?” they asked.  “There, in the tent,” he replied.

verses.esv: The LORD said, “I will surely return to you about this time next year, and Sarah your wife shall have a son.” And Sarah was listening at the tent door behind him.
verses.kjv: And he said, I will certainly return unto thee according to the time of life; and, lo, Sarah thy wife shall have a son. And Sarah heard it in the tent door, which was behind him.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said, “I will surely return to you at this time next year, and your wife Sarah will have a son!”  Now Sarah was behind him, listening at the entrance to the tent.

verses.esv: Now Abraham and Sarah were old, advanced in years. The way of women had ceased to be with Sarah.
verses.kjv: Now Abraham and Sarah were old and well stricken in age; and it ceased to be with Sarah after the manner of women.
verses.bsb: And Abraham and Sarah were already old and well along in years; Sarah had passed the age of childbearing.

verses.esv: So Sarah laughed to herself, saying, “After I am worn out, and my lord is old, shall I have pleasure?”
verses.kjv: Therefore Sarah laughed within herself, saying, After I am waxed old shall I have pleasure, my lord being old also?
verses.bsb: So she laughed to herself, saying, “After I am worn out and my master is old, will I now have this pleasure?”

verses.esv: The LORD said to Abraham, “Why did Sarah laugh and say, ‘Shall I indeed bear a child, now that I am old?’
verses.kjv: And the Lord said unto Abraham, Wherefore did Sarah laugh, saying, Shall I of a surety bear a child, which am old?
verses.bsb: And the LORD asked Abraham, “Why did Sarah laugh and say, ‘Can I really bear a child when I am old?’

verses.esv: Is anything too hard for the LORD? At the appointed time I will return to you, about this time next year, and Sarah shall have a son.”
verses.kjv: Is any thing too hard for the Lord? At the time appointed I will return unto thee, according to the time of life, and Sarah shall have a son.
verses.bsb: Is anything too difficult for the LORD? At the appointed time I will return to you—in about a year—and Sarah will have a son.”

verses.esv: But Sarah denied it, saying, “I did not laugh,” for she was afraid. He said, “No, but you did laugh.”
verses.kjv: Then Sarah denied, saying, I laughed not; for she was afraid. And he said, Nay; but thou didst laugh.
verses.bsb: But Sarah was afraid, so she denied it and said, “I did not laugh.”  “No,” replied the LORD, “but you did laugh.”

verses.esv: Then the men set out from there, and they looked down toward Sodom. And Abraham went with them to set them on their way.
verses.kjv: ¶ And the men rose up from thence, and looked toward Sodom: and Abraham went with them to bring them on the way.
verses.bsb: When the men got up to leave, they looked out over Sodom, and Abraham walked along with them to see them off.

verses.esv: The LORD said, “Shall I hide from Abraham what I am about to do,
verses.kjv: And the Lord said, Shall I hide from Abraham that thing which I do;
verses.bsb: And the LORD said, “Shall I hide from Abraham what I am about to do?

verses.esv: seeing that Abraham shall surely become a great and mighty nation, and all the nations of the earth shall be blessed in him?
verses.kjv: Seeing that Abraham shall surely become a great and mighty nation, and all the nations of the earth shall be blessed in him?
verses.bsb: Abraham will surely become a great and powerful nation, and through him all the nations of the earth will be blessed.

verses.esv: For I have chosen him, that he may command his children and his household after him to keep the way of the LORD by doing righteousness and justice, so that the LORD may bring to Abraham what he has promised him.”
verses.kjv: For I know him, that he will command his children and his household after him, and they shall keep the way of the Lord, to do justice and judgment; that the Lord may bring upon Abraham that which he hath spoken of him.
verses.bsb: For I have chosen him, so that he will command his children and his household after him to keep the way of the LORD by doing what is right and just, in order that the LORD may bring upon Abraham what He has promised.”

verses.esv: Then the LORD said, “Because the outcry against Sodom and Gomorrah is great and their sin is very grave,
verses.kjv: And the Lord said, Because the cry of Sodom and Gomorrah is great, and because their sin is very grievous;
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said, “The outcry against Sodom and Gomorrah is great. Because their sin is so grievous,

verses.esv: I will go down to see whether they have done altogether according to the outcry that has come to me. And if not, I will know.”
verses.kjv: I will go down now, and see whether they have done altogether according to the cry of it, which is come unto me; and if not, I will know.
verses.bsb: I will go down to see if their actions fully justify the outcry that has reached Me. If not, I will find out.”

verses.esv: So the men turned from there and went toward Sodom, but Abraham still stood before the LORD.
verses.kjv: And the men turned their faces from thence, and went toward Sodom: but Abraham stood yet before the Lord.
verses.bsb: And the two men turned away and went toward Sodom, but Abraham remained standing before the LORD.

verses.esv: Then Abraham drew near and said, “Will you indeed sweep away the righteous with the wicked?
verses.kjv: ¶ And Abraham drew near, and said, Wilt thou also destroy the righteous with the wicked?
verses.bsb: Abraham stepped forward and said, “Will You really sweep away the righteous with the wicked?

verses.esv: Suppose there are fifty righteous within the city. Will you then sweep away the place and not spare it for the fifty righteous who are in it?
verses.kjv: Peradventure there be fifty righteous within the city: wilt thou also destroy and not spare the place for the fifty righteous that are therein?
verses.bsb: What if there are fifty righteous ones in the city? Will You really sweep it away and not spare the place for the sake of the fifty righteous ones who are there?

verses.esv: Far be it from you to do such a thing, to put the righteous to death with the wicked, so that the righteous fare as the wicked! Far be that from you! Shall not the Judge of all the earth do what is just?”
verses.kjv: That be far from thee to do after this manner, to slay the righteous with the wicked: and that the righteous should be as the wicked, that be far from thee: Shall not the Judge of all the earth do right?
verses.bsb: Far be it from You to do such a thing—to kill the righteous with the wicked, so that the righteous and the wicked are treated alike. Far be it from You! Will not the Judge of all the earth do what is right?”

verses.esv: And the LORD said, “If I find at Sodom fifty righteous in the city, I will spare the whole place for their sake.”
verses.kjv: And the Lord said, If I find in Sodom fifty righteous within the city, then I will spare all the place for their sakes.
verses.bsb: So the LORD replied, “If I find fifty righteous ones within the city of Sodom, on their account I will spare the whole place.”

verses.esv: Abraham answered and said, “Behold, I have undertaken to speak to the Lord, I who am but dust and ashes.
verses.kjv: And Abraham answered and said, Behold now, I have taken upon me to speak unto the Lord, which am but dust and ashes:
verses.bsb: Then Abraham answered, “Now that I have ventured to speak to the Lord—though I am but dust and ashes—

verses.esv: Suppose five of the fifty righteous are lacking. Will you destroy the whole city for lack of five?” And he said, “I will not destroy it if I find forty-five there.”
verses.kjv: Peradventure there shall lack five of the fifty righteous: wilt thou destroy all the city for lack of five? And he said, If I find there forty and five, I will not destroy it.
verses.bsb: suppose the fifty righteous ones lack five. Will You destroy the whole city for the lack of five?”  He replied, “If I find forty-five there, I will not destroy it.”

verses.esv: Again he spoke to him and said, “Suppose forty are found there.” He answered, “For the sake of forty I will not do it.”
verses.kjv: And he spake unto him yet again, and said, Peradventure there shall be forty found there. And he said, I will not do it for forty’s sake.
verses.bsb: Once again Abraham spoke to the LORD, “Suppose forty are found there?”  He answered, “On account of the forty, I will not do it.”

verses.esv: Then he said, “Oh let not the Lord be angry, and I will speak. Suppose thirty are found there.” He answered, “I will not do it, if I find thirty there.”
verses.kjv: And he said unto him, Oh let not the Lord be angry, and I will speak: Peradventure there shall thirty be found there. And he said, I will not do it, if I find thirty there.
verses.bsb: Then Abraham said, “May the Lord not be angry, but let me speak further. Suppose thirty are found there?”  He replied, “If I find thirty there, I will not do it.”

verses.esv: He said, “Behold, I have undertaken to speak to the Lord. Suppose twenty are found there.” He answered, “For the sake of twenty I will not destroy it.”
verses.kjv: And he said, Behold now, I have taken upon me to speak unto the Lord: Peradventure there shall be twenty found there. And he said, I will not destroy it for twenty’s sake.
verses.bsb: And Abraham said, “Now that I have ventured to speak to the Lord, suppose twenty are found there?”  He answered, “On account of the twenty, I will not destroy it.”

verses.esv: Then he said, “Oh let not the Lord be angry, and I will speak again but this once. Suppose ten are found there.” He answered, “For the sake of ten I will not destroy it.”
verses.kjv: And he said, Oh let not the Lord be angry, and I will speak yet but this once: Peradventure ten shall be found there. And he said, I will not destroy it for ten’s sake.
verses.bsb: Finally, Abraham said, “May the Lord not be angry, but let me speak once more. Suppose ten are found there?”  And He answered, “On account of the ten, I will not destroy it.”

verses.esv: And the LORD went his way, when he had finished speaking to Abraham, and Abraham returned to his place.
verses.kjv: And the Lord went his way, as soon as he had left communing with Abraham: and Abraham returned unto his place.
verses.bsb: When the LORD had finished speaking with Abraham, He departed, and Abraham returned home.

verses.esv: The two angels came to Sodom in the evening, and Lot was sitting in the gate of Sodom. When Lot saw them, he rose to meet them and bowed himself with his face to the earth
verses.kjv: AND there came two angels to Sodom at even; and Lot sat in the gate of Sodom: and Lot seeing them rose up to meet them; and he bowed himself with his face toward the ground;
verses.bsb: Now the two angels arrived at Sodom in the evening, and Lot was sitting in the gateway of the city. When Lot saw them, he got up to meet them, bowed facedown,

verses.esv: and said, “My lords, please turn aside to your servant's house and spend the night and wash your feet. Then you may rise up early and go on your way.” They said, “No; we will spend the night in the town square.”
verses.kjv: And he said, Behold now, my lords, turn in, I pray you, into your servant’s house, and tarry all night, and wash your feet, and ye shall rise up early, and go on your ways. And they said, Nay; but we will abide in the street all night.
verses.bsb: and said, “My lords, please turn aside into the house of your servant; wash your feet and spend the night. Then you can rise early and go on your way.”  “No,” they answered, “we will spend the night in the square.”

verses.esv: But he pressed them strongly; so they turned aside to him and entered his house. And he made them a feast and baked unleavened bread, and they ate.
verses.kjv: And he pressed upon them greatly; and they turned in unto him, and entered into his house; and he made them a feast, and did bake unleavened bread, and they did eat.
verses.bsb: But Lot insisted so strongly that they followed him into his house. He prepared a feast for them and baked unleavened bread, and they ate.

verses.esv: But before they lay down, the men of the city, the men of Sodom, both young and old, all the people to the last man, surrounded the house.
verses.kjv: ¶ But before they lay down, the men of the city, even the men of Sodom, compassed the house round, both old and young, all the people from every quarter:
verses.bsb: Before they had gone to bed, all the men of the city of Sodom, both young and old, surrounded the house.

verses.esv: And they called to Lot, “Where are the men who came to you tonight? Bring them out to us, that we may know them.”
verses.kjv: And they called unto Lot, and said unto him, Where are the men which came in to thee this night? bring them out unto us, that we may know them.
verses.bsb: They called out to Lot, saying, “Where are the men who came to you tonight? Send them out to us so we can have relations with them!”

verses.esv: Lot went out to the men at the entrance, shut the door after him,
verses.kjv: And Lot went out at the door unto them, and shut the door after him,
verses.bsb: Lot went outside to meet them, shutting the door behind him.

verses.esv: and said, “I beg you, my brothers, do not act so wickedly.
verses.kjv: And said, I pray you, brethren, do not so wickedly.
verses.bsb: “Please, my brothers,” he pleaded, “don’t do such a wicked thing!

verses.esv: Behold, I have two daughters who have not known any man. Let me bring them out to you, and do to them as you please. Only do nothing to these men, for they have come under the shelter of my roof.”
verses.kjv: Behold now, I have two daughters which have not known man; let me, I pray you, bring them out unto you, and do ye to them as is good in your eyes: only unto these men do nothing; for therefore came they under the shadow of my roof.
verses.bsb: Look, I have two daughters who have never slept with a man. Let me bring them to you, and you can do to them as you please. But do not do anything to these men, for they have come under the protection of my roof.”

verses.esv: But they said, “Stand back!” And they said, “This fellow came to sojourn, and he has become the judge! Now we will deal worse with you than with them.” Then they pressed hard against the man Lot, and drew near to break the door down.
verses.kjv: And they said, Stand back. And they said again, This one fellow came in to sojourn, and he will needs be a judge: now will we deal worse with thee, than with them. And they pressed sore upon the man, even Lot, and came near to break the door.
verses.bsb: “Get out of the way!” they replied. And they declared, “This one came here as a foreigner, and he is already acting like a judge! Now we will treat you worse than them.” And they pressed in on Lot and moved in to break down the door.

verses.esv: But the men reached out their hands and brought Lot into the house with them and shut the door.
verses.kjv: But the men put forth their hand, and pulled Lot into the house to them, and shut to the door.
verses.bsb: But the men inside reached out, pulled Lot into the house with them, and shut the door.

verses.esv: And they struck with blindness the men who were at the entrance of the house, both small and great, so that they wore themselves out groping for the door.
verses.kjv: And they smote the men that were at the door of the house with blindness, both small and great: so that they wearied themselves to find the door.
verses.bsb: And they struck the men at the entrance, young and old, with blindness, so that they wearied themselves trying to find the door.

verses.esv: Then the men said to Lot, “Have you anyone else here? Sons-in-law, sons, daughters, or anyone you have in the city, bring them out of the place.
verses.kjv: ¶ And the men said unto Lot, Hast thou here any besides? son in law, and thy sons, and thy daughters, and whatsoever thou hast in the city, bring them out of this place:
verses.bsb: Then the two men said to Lot, “Do you have anyone else here—a son-in-law, your sons or daughters, or anyone else in the city who belongs to you? Get them out of here,

verses.esv: For we are about to destroy this place, because the outcry against its people has become great before the LORD, and the LORD has sent us to destroy it.”
verses.kjv: For we will destroy this place, because the cry of them is waxen great before the face of the Lord; and the Lord hath sent us to destroy it.
verses.bsb: because we are about to destroy this place. For the outcry to the LORD against its people is so great that He has sent us to destroy it.”

verses.esv: So Lot went out and said to his sons-in-law, who were to marry his daughters, “Up! Get out of this place, for the LORD is about to destroy the city.” But he seemed to his sons-in-law to be jesting.
verses.kjv: And Lot went out, and spake unto his sons in law, which married his daughters, and said, Up, get you out of this place; for the Lord will destroy this city. But he seemed as one that mocked unto his sons in law.
verses.bsb: So Lot went out and spoke to the sons-in-law who were pledged in marriage to his daughters. “Get up,” he said. “Get out of this place, for the LORD is about to destroy the city!” But his sons-in-law thought he was joking.

verses.esv: As morning dawned, the angels urged Lot, saying, “Up! Take your wife and your two daughters who are here, lest you be swept away in the punishment of the city.”
verses.kjv: ¶ And when the morning arose, then the angels hastened Lot, saying, Arise, take thy wife, and thy two daughters, which are here; lest thou be consumed in the iniquity of the city.
verses.bsb: At daybreak the angels hurried Lot along, saying, “Get up! Take your wife and your two daughters who are here, or you will be swept away in the punishment of the city.”

verses.esv: But he lingered. So the men seized him and his wife and his two daughters by the hand, the LORD being merciful to him, and they brought him out and set him outside the city.
verses.kjv: And while he lingered, the men laid hold upon his hand, and upon the hand of his wife, and upon the hand of his two daughters; the Lord being merciful unto him: and they brought him forth, and set him without the city.
verses.bsb: But when Lot hesitated, the men grabbed his hand and the hands of his wife and his two daughters. And they led them safely out of the city, because of the LORD’s compassion for them.

verses.esv: And as they brought them out, one said, “Escape for your life. Do not look back or stop anywhere in the valley. Escape to the hills, lest you be swept away.”
verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass, when they had brought them forth abroad, that he said, Escape for thy life; look not behind thee, neither stay thou in all the plain; escape to the mountain, lest thou be consumed.
verses.bsb: As soon as the men had brought them out, one of them said, “Run for your lives! Do not look back, and do not stop anywhere on the plain! Flee to the mountains, or you will be swept away!”

verses.esv: And Lot said to them, “Oh, no, my lords.
verses.kjv: And Lot said unto them, Oh, not so, my Lord:
verses.bsb: But Lot replied, “No, my lords, please!

verses.esv: Behold, your servant has found favor in your sight, and you have shown me great kindness in saving my life. But I cannot escape to the hills, lest the disaster overtake me and I die.
verses.kjv: Behold now, thy servant hath found grace in thy sight, and thou hast magnified thy mercy, which thou hast shewed unto me in saving my life; and I cannot escape to the mountain, lest some evil take me, and I die:
verses.bsb: Your servant has indeed found favor in your sight, and you have shown me great kindness by sparing my life. But I cannot run to the mountains; the disaster will overtake me, and I will die.

verses.esv: Behold, this city is near enough to flee to, and it is a little one. Let me escape there—is it not a little one?—and my life will be saved!”
verses.kjv: Behold now, this city is near to flee unto, and it is a little one: Oh, let me escape thither, (is it not a little one?) and my soul shall live.
verses.bsb: Look, there is a town nearby where I can flee, and it is a small place. Please let me flee there—is it not a small place? Then my life will be saved.”

verses.esv: He said to him, “Behold, I grant you this favor also, that I will not overthrow the city of which you have spoken.
verses.kjv: And he said unto him, See, I have accepted thee concerning this thing also, that I will not overthrow this city, for the which thou hast spoken.
verses.bsb: “Very well,” he answered, “I will grant this request as well, and will not demolish the town you indicate.

verses.esv: Escape there quickly, for I can do nothing till you arrive there.” Therefore the name of the city was called Zoar.
verses.kjv: Haste thee, escape thither; for I cannot do any thing till thou be come thither. Therefore the name of the city was called Zoar.
verses.bsb: Hurry! Run there quickly, for I cannot do anything until you reach it.” That is why the town was called Zoar.

verses.esv: The sun had risen on the earth when Lot came to Zoar.
verses.kjv: ¶ The sun was risen upon the earth when Lot entered into Zoar.
verses.bsb: And by the time the sun had risen over the land, Lot had reached Zoar.

verses.esv: Then the LORD rained on Sodom and Gomorrah sulfur and fire from the LORD out of heaven.
verses.kjv: Then the Lord rained upon Sodom and upon Gomorrah brimstone and fire from the Lord out of heaven;
verses.bsb: Then the LORD rained down sulfur and fire on Sodom and Gomorrah—from the LORD out of the heavens.

verses.esv: And he overthrew those cities, and all the valley, and all the inhabitants of the cities, and what grew on the ground.
verses.kjv: And he overthrew those cities, and all the plain, and all the inhabitants of the cities, and that which grew upon the ground.
verses.bsb: Thus He destroyed these cities and the entire plain, including all the inhabitants of the cities and everything that grew on the ground.

verses.esv: But Lot's wife, behind him, looked back, and she became a pillar of salt.
verses.kjv: ¶ But his wife looked back from behind him, and she became a pillar of salt.
verses.bsb: But Lot’s wife looked back, and she became a pillar of salt.

verses.esv: And Abraham went early in the morning to the place where he had stood before the LORD.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Abraham gat up early in the morning to the place where he stood before the Lord:
verses.bsb: Early the next morning, Abraham got up and returned to the place where he had stood before the LORD.

verses.esv: And he looked down toward Sodom and Gomorrah and toward all the land of the valley, and he looked and, behold, the smoke of the land went up like the smoke of a furnace.
verses.kjv: And he looked toward Sodom and Gomorrah, and toward all the land of the plain, and beheld, and, lo, the smoke of the country went up as the smoke of a furnace.
verses.bsb: He looked down toward Sodom and Gomorrah and all the land of the plain, and he saw the smoke rising from the land like smoke from a furnace.

verses.esv: So it was that, when God destroyed the cities of the valley, God remembered Abraham and sent Lot out of the midst of the overthrow when he overthrew the cities in which Lot had lived.
verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass, when God destroyed the cities of the plain, that God remembered Abraham, and sent Lot out of the midst of the overthrow, when he overthrew the cities in the which Lot dwelt.
verses.bsb: So when God destroyed the cities of the plain, He remembered Abraham, and He brought Lot out of the catastrophe that destroyed the cities where he had lived.

verses.esv: Now Lot went up out of Zoar and lived in the hills with his two daughters, for he was afraid to live in Zoar. So he lived in a cave with his two daughters.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Lot went up out of Zoar, and dwelt in the mountain, and his two daughters with him; for he feared to dwell in Zoar: and he dwelt in a cave, he and his two daughters.
verses.bsb: Lot and his two daughters left Zoar and settled in the mountains—for he was afraid to stay in Zoar—where they lived in a cave.

verses.esv: And the firstborn said to the younger, “Our father is old, and there is not a man on earth to come in to us after the manner of all the earth.
verses.kjv: And the firstborn said unto the younger, Our father is old, and there is not a man in the earth to come in unto us after the manner of all the earth:
verses.bsb: One day the older daughter said to the younger, “Our father is old, and there is no man in the land to sleep with us, as is the custom over all the earth.

verses.esv: Come, let us make our father drink wine, and we will lie with him, that we may preserve offspring from our father.”
verses.kjv: Come, let us make our father drink wine, and we will lie with him, that we may preserve seed of our father.
verses.bsb: Come, let us get our father drunk with wine so we can sleep with him and preserve his line.”

verses.esv: So they made their father drink wine that night. And the firstborn went in and lay with her father. He did not know when she lay down or when she arose.
verses.kjv: And they made their father drink wine that night: and the firstborn went in, and lay with her father; and he perceived not when she lay down, nor when she arose.
verses.bsb: So that night they got their father drunk with wine, and the firstborn went in and slept with her father; he was not aware when she lay down or when she got up.

verses.esv: The next day, the firstborn said to the younger, “Behold, I lay last night with my father. Let us make him drink wine tonight also. Then you go in and lie with him, that we may preserve offspring from our father.”
verses.kjv: And it came to pass on the morrow, that the firstborn said unto the younger, Behold, I lay yesternight with my father: let us make him drink wine this night also; and go thou in, and lie with him, that we may preserve seed of our father.
verses.bsb: The next day the older daughter said to the younger, “Look, I slept with my father last night. Let us get him drunk with wine again tonight so you can go in and sleep with him and we can preserve our father’s line.”

verses.esv: So they made their father drink wine that night also. And the younger arose and lay with him, and he did not know when she lay down or when she arose.
verses.kjv: And they made their father drink wine that night also: and the younger arose, and lay with him; and he perceived not when she lay down, nor when she arose.
verses.bsb: So again that night they got their father drunk with wine, and the younger daughter went in and slept with him; he was not aware when she lay down or when she got up.

verses.esv: Thus both the daughters of Lot became pregnant by their father.
verses.kjv: Thus were both the daughters of Lot with child by their father.
verses.bsb: Thus both of Lot’s daughters became pregnant by their father.

verses.esv: The firstborn bore a son and called his name Moab. He is the father of the Moabites to this day.
verses.kjv: And the firstborn bare a son, and called his name Moab: the same is the father of the Moabites unto this day.
verses.bsb: The older daughter gave birth to a son and named him Moab. He is the father of the Moabites of today.

verses.esv: The younger also bore a son and called his name Ben-ammi. He is the father of the Ammonites to this day.
verses.kjv: And the younger, she also bare a son, and called his name Ben-ammi: the same is the father of the children of Ammon unto this day.
verses.bsb: The younger daughter also gave birth to a son, and she named him Ben-ammi. He is the father of the Ammonites of today.

verses.esv: From there Abraham journeyed toward the territory of the Negeb and lived between Kadesh and Shur; and he sojourned in Gerar.
verses.kjv: AND Abraham journeyed from thence toward the south country, and dwelled between Kadesh and Shur, and sojourned in Gerar.
verses.bsb: Now Abraham journeyed from there to the region of the Negev and settled between Kadesh and Shur. While he was staying in Gerar,

verses.esv: And Abraham said of Sarah his wife, “She is my sister.” And Abimelech king of Gerar sent and took Sarah.
verses.kjv: And Abraham said of Sarah his wife, She is my sister: and Abimelech king of Gerar sent, and took Sarah.
verses.bsb: Abraham said of his wife Sarah, “She is my sister.” So Abimelech king of Gerar had Sarah brought to him.

verses.esv: But God came to Abimelech in a dream by night and said to him, “Behold, you are a dead man because of the woman whom you have taken, for she is a man's wife.”
verses.kjv: But God came to Abimelech in a dream by night, and said to him, Behold, thou art but a dead man, for the woman which thou hast taken; for she is a man’s wife.
verses.bsb: One night, however, God came to Abimelech in a dream and told him, “You are as good as dead because of the woman you have taken, for she is a married woman.”

verses.esv: Now Abimelech had not approached her. So he said, “Lord, will you kill an innocent people?
verses.kjv: But Abimelech had not come near her: and he said, Lord, wilt thou slay also a righteous nation?
verses.bsb: Now Abimelech had not gone near her, so he replied, “Lord, would You destroy a nation even though it is innocent?

verses.esv: Did he not himself say to me, ‘She is my sister’? And she herself said, ‘He is my brother.’ In the integrity of my heart and the innocence of my hands I have done this.”
verses.kjv: Said he not unto me, She is my sister? and she, even she herself said, He is my brother: in the integrity of my heart and innocency of my hands have I done this.
verses.bsb: Didn’t Abraham tell me, ‘She is my sister’? And she herself said, ‘He is my brother.’ I have done this in the integrity of my heart and the innocence of my hands.”

verses.esv: Then God said to him in the dream, “Yes, I know that you have done this in the integrity of your heart, and it was I who kept you from sinning against me. Therefore I did not let you touch her.
verses.kjv: And God said unto him in a dream, Yea, I know that thou didst this in the integrity of thy heart; for I also withheld thee from sinning against me: therefore suffered I thee not to touch her.
verses.bsb: Then God said to Abimelech in the dream, “Yes, I know that you did this with a clear conscience, and so I have kept you from sinning against Me. That is why I did not let you touch her.

verses.esv: Now then, return the man's wife, for he is a prophet, so that he will pray for you, and you shall live. But if you do not return her, know that you shall surely die, you and all who are yours.”
verses.kjv: Now therefore restore the man his wife; for he is a prophet, and he shall pray for thee, and thou shalt live: and if thou restore her not, know thou that thou shalt surely die, thou, and all that are thine.
verses.bsb: Now return the man’s wife, for he is a prophet; he will pray for you and you will live. But if you do not restore her, be aware that you will surely die—you and all who belong to you.”

verses.esv: So Abimelech rose early in the morning and called all his servants and told them all these things. And the men were very much afraid.
verses.kjv: Therefore Abimelech rose early in the morning, and called all his servants, and told all these things in their ears: and the men were sore afraid.
verses.bsb: Early the next morning Abimelech got up and summoned all his servants; and when he described to them all that had happened, the men were terrified.

verses.esv: Then Abimelech called Abraham and said to him, “What have you done to us? And how have I sinned against you, that you have brought on me and my kingdom a great sin? You have done to me things that ought not to be done.”
verses.kjv: Then Abimelech called Abraham, and said unto him, What hast thou done unto us? and what have I offended thee, that thou hast brought on me and on my kingdom a great sin? thou hast done deeds unto me that ought not to be done.
verses.bsb: Then Abimelech called Abraham and asked, “What have you done to us? How have I sinned against you, that you have brought such tremendous guilt upon me and my kingdom? You have done things to me that should not be done.”

verses.esv: And Abimelech said to Abraham, “What did you see, that you did this thing?”
verses.kjv: And Abimelech said unto Abraham, What sawest thou, that thou hast done this thing?
verses.bsb: Abimelech also asked Abraham, “What prompted you to do such a thing?”

verses.esv: Abraham said, “I did it because I thought, ‘There is no fear of God at all in this place, and they will kill me because of my wife.’
verses.kjv: And Abraham said, Because I thought, Surely the fear of God is not in this place; and they will slay me for my wife’s sake.
verses.bsb: Abraham replied, “I thought to myself, ‘Surely there is no fear of God in this place. They will kill me on account of my wife.’

verses.esv: Besides, she is indeed my sister, the daughter of my father though not the daughter of my mother, and she became my wife.
verses.kjv: And yet indeed she is my sister; she is the daughter of my father, but not the daughter of my mother; and she became my wife.
verses.bsb: Besides, she really is my sister, the daughter of my father—though not the daughter of my mother—and she became my wife.

verses.esv: And when God caused me to wander from my father's house, I said to her, ‘This is the kindness you must do me: at every place to which we come, say of me, “He is my brother.”’”
verses.kjv: And it came to pass, when God caused me to wander from my father’s house, that I said unto her, This is thy kindness which thou shalt shew unto me; at every place whither we shall come, say of me, He is my brother.
verses.bsb: So when God had me journey from my father’s house, I said to Sarah, ‘This is how you can show your loyalty to me: Wherever we go, say of me, “He is my brother.”’”

verses.esv: Then Abimelech took sheep and oxen, and male servants and female servants, and gave them to Abraham, and returned Sarah his wife to him.
verses.kjv: And Abimelech took sheep, and oxen, and menservants, and womenservants, and gave them unto Abraham, and restored him Sarah his wife.
verses.bsb: So Abimelech brought sheep and cattle, menservants and maidservants, and he gave them to Abraham and restored his wife Sarah to him.

verses.esv: And Abimelech said, “Behold, my land is before you; dwell where it pleases you.”
verses.kjv: And Abimelech said, Behold, my land is before thee: dwell where it pleaseth thee.
verses.bsb: And Abimelech said, “Look, my land is before you. Settle wherever you please.”

verses.esv: To Sarah he said, “Behold, I have given your brother a thousand pieces of silver. It is a sign of your innocence in the eyes of all who are with you, and before everyone you are vindicated.”
verses.kjv: And unto Sarah he said, Behold, I have given thy brother a thousand pieces of silver: behold, he is to thee a covering of the eyes, unto all that are with thee, and with all other: thus she was reproved.
verses.bsb: And he said to Sarah, “See, I am giving your brother a thousand pieces of silver. It is your vindication before all who are with you; you are completely cleared.”

verses.esv: Then Abraham prayed to God, and God healed Abimelech, and also healed his wife and female slaves so that they bore children.
verses.kjv: ¶ So Abraham prayed unto God: and God healed Abimelech, and his wife, and his maidservants; and they bare children.
verses.bsb: Then Abraham prayed to God, and God healed Abimelech and his wife and his maidservants, so that they could again bear children—

verses.esv: For the LORD had closed all the wombs of the house of Abimelech because of Sarah, Abraham's wife.
verses.kjv: For the Lord had fast closed up all the wombs of the house of Abimelech, because of Sarah Abraham’s wife.
verses.bsb: for on account of Abraham’s wife Sarah, the LORD had completely closed all the wombs in Abimelech’s household.

verses.esv: The LORD visited Sarah as he had said, and the LORD did to Sarah as he had promised.
verses.kjv: AND the Lord visited Sarah as he had said, and the Lord did unto Sarah as he had spoken.
verses.bsb: Now the LORD attended to Sarah as He had said, and the LORD did for Sarah what He had promised.

verses.esv: And Sarah conceived and bore Abraham a son in his old age at the time of which God had spoken to him.
verses.kjv: For Sarah conceived, and bare Abraham a son in his old age, at the set time of which God had spoken to him.
verses.bsb: So Sarah conceived and bore a son to Abraham in his old age, at the very time God had promised.

verses.esv: Abraham called the name of his son who was born to him, whom Sarah bore him, Isaac.
verses.kjv: And Abraham called the name of his son that was born unto him, whom Sarah bare to him, Isaac.
verses.bsb: And Abraham gave the name Isaac to the son Sarah bore to him.

verses.esv: And Abraham circumcised his son Isaac when he was eight days old, as God had commanded him.
verses.kjv: And Abraham circumcised his son Isaac being eight days old, as God had commanded him.
verses.bsb: When his son Isaac was eight days old, Abraham circumcised him, as God had commanded him.

verses.esv: Abraham was a hundred years old when his son Isaac was born to him.
verses.kjv: And Abraham was an hundred years old, when his son Isaac was born unto him.
verses.bsb: Abraham was a hundred years old when his son Isaac was born to him.

verses.esv: And Sarah said, “God has made laughter for me; everyone who hears will laugh over me.”
verses.kjv: ¶ And Sarah said, God hath made me to laugh, so that all that hear will laugh with me.
verses.bsb: Then Sarah said, “God has made me laugh, and everyone who hears of this will laugh with me.”

verses.esv: And she said, “Who would have said to Abraham that Sarah would nurse children? Yet I have borne him a son in his old age.”
verses.kjv: And she said, Who would have said unto Abraham, that Sarah should have given children suck? for I have born him a son in his old age.
verses.bsb: She added, “Who would have told Abraham that Sarah would nurse children? Yet I have borne him a son in his old age.”

verses.esv: And the child grew and was weaned. And Abraham made a great feast on the day that Isaac was weaned.
verses.kjv: And the child grew, and was weaned: and Abraham made a great feast the same day that Isaac was weaned.
verses.bsb: So the child grew and was weaned, and Abraham held a great feast on the day Isaac was weaned.

verses.esv: But Sarah saw the son of Hagar the Egyptian, whom she had borne to Abraham, laughing.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Sarah saw the son of Hagar the Egyptian, which she had born unto Abraham, mocking.
verses.bsb: But Sarah saw that the son whom Hagar the Egyptian had borne to Abraham was mocking her son,

verses.esv: So she said to Abraham, “Cast out this slave woman with her son, for the son of this slave woman shall not be heir with my son Isaac.”
verses.kjv: Wherefore she said unto Abraham, Cast out this bondwoman and her son: for the son of this bondwoman shall not be heir with my son, even with Isaac.
verses.bsb: and she said to Abraham, “Expel the slave woman and her son, for the slave woman’s son will never share in the inheritance with my son Isaac!”

verses.esv: And the thing was very displeasing to Abraham on account of his son.
verses.kjv: And the thing was very grievous in Abraham’s sight because of his son.
verses.bsb: Now this matter distressed Abraham greatly because it concerned his son Ishmael.

verses.esv: But God said to Abraham, “Be not displeased because of the boy and because of your slave woman. Whatever Sarah says to you, do as she tells you, for through Isaac shall your offspring be named.
verses.kjv: ¶ And God said unto Abraham, Let it not be grievous in thy sight because of the lad, and because of thy bondwoman; in all that Sarah hath said unto thee, hearken unto her voice; for in Isaac shall thy seed be called.
verses.bsb: But God said to Abraham, “Do not be distressed about the boy and your maidservant. Listen to everything that Sarah tells you, for through Isaac your offspring will be reckoned.

verses.esv: And I will make a nation of the son of the slave woman also, because he is your offspring.”
verses.kjv: And also of the son of the bondwoman will I make a nation, because he is thy seed.
verses.bsb: But I will also make a nation of the slave woman’s son, because he is your offspring.”

verses.esv: So Abraham rose early in the morning and took bread and a skin of water and gave it to Hagar, putting it on her shoulder, along with the child, and sent her away. And she departed and wandered in the wilderness of Beersheba.
verses.kjv: And Abraham rose up early in the morning, and took bread, and a bottle of water, and gave it unto Hagar, putting it on her shoulder, and the child, and sent her away: and she departed, and wandered in the wilderness of Beer-sheba.
verses.bsb: Early in the morning, Abraham got up, took bread and a skin of water, put them on Hagar’s shoulders, and sent her away with the boy. She left and wandered in the Wilderness of Beersheba.

verses.esv: When the water in the skin was gone, she put the child under one of the bushes.
verses.kjv: And the water was spent in the bottle, and she cast the child under one of the shrubs.
verses.bsb: When the water in the skin was gone, she left the boy under one of the bushes.

verses.esv: Then she went and sat down opposite him a good way off, about the distance of a bowshot, for she said, “Let me not look on the death of the child.” And as she sat opposite him, she lifted up her voice and wept.
verses.kjv: And she went, and sat her down over against him a good way off, as it were a bowshot: for she said, Let me not see the death of the child. And she sat over against him, and lift up her voice, and wept.
verses.bsb: Then she went off and sat down nearby, about a bowshot away, for she said, “I cannot bear to watch the boy die!” And as she sat nearby, she lifted up her voice and wept.

verses.esv: And God heard the voice of the boy, and the angel of God called to Hagar from heaven and said to her, “What troubles you, Hagar? Fear not, for God has heard the voice of the boy where he is.
verses.kjv: And God heard the voice of the lad; and the angel of God called to Hagar out of heaven, and said unto her, What aileth thee, Hagar? fear not; for God hath heard the voice of the lad where he is.
verses.bsb: Then God heard the voice of the boy, and the angel of God called to Hagar from heaven, “What is wrong, Hagar? Do not be afraid, for God has heard the voice of the boy where he lies.

verses.esv: Up! Lift up the boy, and hold him fast with your hand, for I will make him into a great nation.”
verses.kjv: Arise, lift up the lad, and hold him in thine hand; for I will make him a great nation.
verses.bsb: Get up, lift up the boy, and take him by the hand, for I will make him into a great nation.”

verses.esv: Then God opened her eyes, and she saw a well of water. And she went and filled the skin with water and gave the boy a drink.
verses.kjv: And God opened her eyes, and she saw a well of water; and she went, and filled the bottle with water, and gave the lad drink.
verses.bsb: Then God opened her eyes, and she saw a well of water. So she went and filled the skin with water and gave the boy a drink.

verses.esv: And God was with the boy, and he grew up. He lived in the wilderness and became an expert with the bow.
verses.kjv: And God was with the lad; and he grew, and dwelt in the wilderness, and became an archer.
verses.bsb: And God was with the boy, and he grew up and settled in the wilderness and became a great archer.

verses.esv: He lived in the wilderness of Paran, and his mother took a wife for him from the land of Egypt.
verses.kjv: And he dwelt in the wilderness of Paran: and his mother took him a wife out of the land of Egypt.
verses.bsb: And while he was dwelling in the Wilderness of Paran, his mother got a wife for him from the land of Egypt.

verses.esv: At that time Abimelech and Phicol the commander of his army said to Abraham, “God is with you in all that you do.
verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass at that time, that Abimelech and Phichol the chief captain of his host spake unto Abraham, saying, God is with thee in all that thou doest:
verses.bsb: At that time Abimelech and Phicol the commander of his army said to Abraham, “God is with you in all that you do.

verses.esv: Now therefore swear to me here by God that you will not deal falsely with me or with my descendants or with my posterity, but as I have dealt kindly with you, so you will deal with me and with the land where you have sojourned.”
verses.kjv: Now therefore swear unto me here by God that thou wilt not deal falsely with me, nor with my son, nor with my son’s son: but according to the kindness that I have done unto thee, thou shalt do unto me, and to the land wherein thou hast sojourned.
verses.bsb: Now, therefore, swear to me here before God that you will not deal falsely with me or my children or descendants. Show to me and to the country in which you reside the same kindness that I have shown to you.”

verses.esv: And Abraham said, “I will swear.”
verses.kjv: And Abraham said, I will swear.
verses.bsb: And Abraham replied, “I swear it.”

verses.esv: When Abraham reproved Abimelech about a well of water that Abimelech's servants had seized,
verses.kjv: And Abraham reproved Abimelech because of a well of water, which Abimelech’s servants had violently taken away.
verses.bsb: But when Abraham complained to Abimelech about a well that Abimelech’s servants had seized,

verses.esv: Abimelech said, “I do not know who has done this thing; you did not tell me, and I have not heard of it until today.”
verses.kjv: And Abimelech said, I wot not who hath done this thing: neither didst thou tell me, neither yet heard I of it, but to day.
verses.bsb: Abimelech replied, “I do not know who has done this. You did not tell me, so I have not heard about it until today.”

verses.esv: So Abraham took sheep and oxen and gave them to Abimelech, and the two men made a covenant.
verses.kjv: And Abraham took sheep and oxen, and gave them unto Abimelech; and both of them made a covenant.
verses.bsb: So Abraham brought sheep and cattle and gave them to Abimelech, and the two men made a covenant.

verses.esv: Abraham set seven ewe lambs of the flock apart.
verses.kjv: And Abraham set seven ewe lambs of the flock by themselves.
verses.bsb: Abraham separated seven ewe lambs from the flock,

verses.esv: And Abimelech said to Abraham, “What is the meaning of these seven ewe lambs that you have set apart?”
verses.kjv: And Abimelech said unto Abraham, What mean these seven ewe lambs which thou hast set by themselves?
verses.bsb: and Abimelech asked him, “Why have you set apart these seven ewe lambs?”

verses.esv: He said, “These seven ewe lambs you will take from my hand, that this may be a witness for me that I dug this well.”
verses.kjv: And he said, For these seven ewe lambs shalt thou take of my hand, that they may be a witness unto me, that I have digged this well.
verses.bsb: He replied, “You are to accept the seven ewe lambs from my hand as my witness that I dug this well.”

verses.esv: Therefore that place was called Beersheba, because there both of them swore an oath.
verses.kjv: Wherefore he called that place Beer-sheba; because there they sware both of them.
verses.bsb: So that place was called Beersheba, because it was there that the two of them swore an oath.

verses.esv: So they made a covenant at Beersheba. Then Abimelech and Phicol the commander of his army rose up and returned to the land of the Philistines.
verses.kjv: Thus they made a covenant at Beer-sheba: then Abimelech rose up, and Phichol the chief captain of his host, and they returned into the land of the Philistines.
verses.bsb: After they had made the covenant at Beersheba, Abimelech and Phicol the commander of his army got up and returned to the land of the Philistines.

verses.esv: Abraham planted a tamarisk tree in Beersheba and called there on the name of the LORD, the Everlasting God.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Abraham planted a grove in Beer-sheba, and called there on the name of the Lord, the everlasting God.
verses.bsb: And Abraham planted a tamarisk tree in Beersheba, and there he called upon the name of the LORD, the Eternal God.

verses.esv: And Abraham sojourned many days in the land of the Philistines.
verses.kjv: And Abraham sojourned in the Philistines’ land many days.
verses.bsb: And Abraham resided in the land of the Philistines for a long time.

verses.esv: After these things God tested Abraham and said to him, “Abraham!” And he said, “Here I am.”
verses.kjv: AND it came to pass after these things, that God did tempt Abraham, and said unto him, Abraham: and he said, Behold, here I am.
verses.bsb: Some time later God tested Abraham and said to him, “Abraham!”  “Here I am,” he answered.

verses.esv: He said, “Take your son, your only son Isaac, whom you love, and go to the land of Moriah, and offer him there as a burnt offering on one of the mountains of which I shall tell you.”
verses.kjv: And he said, Take now thy son, thine only son Isaac, whom thou lovest, and get thee into the land of Moriah; and offer him there for a burnt offering upon one of the mountains which I will tell thee of.
verses.bsb: “Take your son,” God said, “your only son Isaac, whom you love, and go to the land of Moriah. Offer him there as a burnt offering on one of the mountains, which I will show you.”

verses.esv: So Abraham rose early in the morning, saddled his donkey, and took two of his young men with him, and his son Isaac. And he cut the wood for the burnt offering and arose and went to the place of which God had told him.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Abraham rose up early in the morning, and saddled his ass, and took two of his young men with him, and Isaac his son, and clave the wood for the burnt offering, and rose up, and went unto the place of which God had told him.
verses.bsb: So Abraham got up early the next morning, saddled his donkey, and took along two of his servants and his son Isaac. He split the wood for a burnt offering and set out for the place God had designated.

verses.esv: On the third day Abraham lifted up his eyes and saw the place from afar.
verses.kjv: Then on the third day Abraham lifted up his eyes, and saw the place afar off.
verses.bsb: On the third day Abraham looked up and saw the place in the distance.

verses.esv: Then Abraham said to his young men, “Stay here with the donkey; I and the boy will go over there and worship and come again to you.”
verses.kjv: And Abraham said unto his young men, Abide ye here with the ass; and I and the lad will go yonder and worship, and come again to you.
verses.bsb: “Stay here with the donkey,” Abraham told his servants. “The boy and I will go over there to worship, and then we will return to you.”

verses.esv: And Abraham took the wood of the burnt offering and laid it on Isaac his son. And he took in his hand the fire and the knife. So they went both of them together.
verses.kjv: And Abraham took the wood of the burnt offering, and laid it upon Isaac his son; and he took the fire in his hand, and a knife; and they went both of them together.
verses.bsb: Abraham took the wood for the burnt offering and placed it on his son Isaac. He himself carried the fire and the sacrificial knife, and the two of them walked on together.

verses.esv: And Isaac said to his father Abraham, “My father!” And he said, “Here I am, my son.” He said, “Behold, the fire and the wood, but where is the lamb for a burnt offering?”
verses.kjv: And Isaac spake unto Abraham his father, and said, My father: and he said, Here am I, my son. And he said, Behold the fire and the wood: but where is the lamb for a burnt offering?
verses.bsb: Then Isaac said to his father Abraham, “My father!”  “Here I am, my son,” he replied.  “The fire and the wood are here,” said Isaac, “but where is the lamb for the burnt offering?”

verses.esv: Abraham said, “God will provide for himself the lamb for a burnt offering, my son.” So they went both of them together.
verses.kjv: And Abraham said, My son, God will provide himself a lamb for a burnt offering: so they went both of them together.
verses.bsb: Abraham answered, “God Himself will provide the lamb for the burnt offering, my son.” And the two walked on together.

verses.esv: When they came to the place of which God had told him, Abraham built the altar there and laid the wood in order and bound Isaac his son and laid him on the altar, on top of the wood.
verses.kjv: And they came to the place which God had told him of; and Abraham built an altar there, and laid the wood in order, and bound Isaac his son, and laid him on the altar upon the wood.
verses.bsb: When they arrived at the place God had designated, Abraham built the altar there and arranged the wood. He bound his son Isaac and placed him on the altar, atop the wood.

verses.esv: Then Abraham reached out his hand and took the knife to slaughter his son.
verses.kjv: And Abraham stretched forth his hand, and took the knife to slay his son.
verses.bsb: Then Abraham reached out his hand and took the knife to slaughter his son.

verses.esv: But the angel of the LORD called to him from heaven and said, “Abraham, Abraham!” And he said, “Here I am.”
verses.kjv: And the angel of the Lord called unto him out of heaven, and said, Abraham, Abraham: and he said, Here am I.
verses.bsb: Just then the angel of the LORD called out to him from heaven, “Abraham, Abraham!”  “Here I am,” he replied.

verses.esv: He said, “Do not lay your hand on the boy or do anything to him, for now I know that you fear God, seeing you have not withheld your son, your only son, from me.”
verses.kjv: And he said, Lay not thine hand upon the lad, neither do thou any thing unto him: for now I know that thou fearest God, seeing thou hast not withheld thy son, thine only son from me.
verses.bsb: “Do not lay a hand on the boy or do anything to him,” said the angel, “for now I know that you fear God, since you have not withheld your only son from me.”

verses.esv: And Abraham lifted up his eyes and looked, and behold, behind him was a ram, caught in a thicket by his horns. And Abraham went and took the ram and offered it up as a burnt offering instead of his son.
verses.kjv: And Abraham lifted up his eyes, and looked, and behold behind him a ram caught in a thicket by his horns: and Abraham went and took the ram, and offered him up for a burnt offering in the stead of his son.
verses.bsb: Then Abraham looked up and saw behind him a ram in a thicket, caught by its horns. So he went and took the ram and offered it as a burnt offering in place of his son.

verses.esv: So Abraham called the name of that place, “The LORD will provide”; as it is said to this day, “On the mount of the LORD it shall be provided.”
verses.kjv: And Abraham called the name of that place Jehovah-jireh: as it is said to this day, In the mount of the Lord it shall be seen.
verses.bsb: And Abraham called that place The LORD Will Provide. So to this day it is said, “On the mountain of the LORD it will be provided.”

verses.esv: And the angel of the LORD called to Abraham a second time from heaven
verses.kjv: ¶ And the angel of the Lord called unto Abraham out of heaven the second time,
verses.bsb: And the angel of the LORD called to Abraham from heaven a second time,

verses.esv: and said, “By myself I have sworn, declares the LORD, because you have done this and have not withheld your son, your only son,
verses.kjv: And said, By myself have I sworn, saith the Lord, for because thou hast done this thing, and hast not withheld thy son, thine only son:
verses.bsb: saying, “By Myself I have sworn, declares the LORD, that because you have done this and have not withheld your only son,

verses.esv: I will surely bless you, and I will surely multiply your offspring as the stars of heaven and as the sand that is on the seashore. And your offspring shall possess the gate of his enemies,
verses.kjv: That in blessing I will bless thee, and in multiplying I will multiply thy seed as the stars of the heaven, and as the sand which is upon the sea shore; and thy seed shall possess the gate of his enemies;
verses.bsb: I will surely bless you, and I will multiply your descendants like the stars in the sky and the sand on the seashore. Your descendants will possess the gates of their enemies.

verses.esv: and in your offspring shall all the nations of the earth be blessed, because you have obeyed my voice.”
verses.kjv: And in thy seed shall all the nations of the earth be blessed; because thou hast obeyed my voice.
verses.bsb: And through your offspring all nations of the earth will be blessed, because you have obeyed My voice.”

verses.esv: So Abraham returned to his young men, and they arose and went together to Beersheba. And Abraham lived at Beersheba.
verses.kjv: So Abraham returned unto his young men, and they rose up and went together to Beer-sheba; and Abraham dwelt at Beer-sheba.
verses.bsb: Abraham went back to his servants, and they got up and set out together for Beersheba. And Abraham settled in Beersheba.

verses.esv: Now after these things it was told to Abraham, “Behold, Milcah also has borne children to your brother Nahor:
verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass after these things, that it was told Abraham, saying, Behold, Milcah, she hath also born children unto thy brother Nahor;
verses.bsb: Some time later, Abraham was told, “Milcah has also borne sons to your brother Nahor:

verses.esv: Uz his firstborn, Buz his brother, Kemuel the father of Aram,
verses.kjv: Huz his firstborn, and Buz his brother, and Kemuel the father of Aram,
verses.bsb: Uz the firstborn, his brother Buz, Kemuel (the father of Aram),

verses.esv: Chesed, Hazo, Pildash, Jidlaph, and Bethuel.”
verses.kjv: And Chesed, and Hazo, and Pildash, and Jidlaph, and Bethuel.
verses.bsb: Chesed, Hazo, Pildash, Jidlaph, and Bethuel.”

verses.esv: (Bethuel fathered Rebekah.) These eight Milcah bore to Nahor, Abraham's brother.
verses.kjv: And Bethuel begat Rebekah: these eight Milcah did bear to Nahor, Abraham’s brother.
verses.bsb: And Bethuel became the father of Rebekah. Milcah bore these eight sons to Abraham’s brother Nahor.

verses.esv: Moreover, his concubine, whose name was Reumah, bore Tebah, Gaham, Tahash, and Maacah.
verses.kjv: And his concubine, whose name was Reumah, she bare also Tebah, and Gaham, and Thahash, and Maachah.
verses.bsb: Moreover, Nahor’s concubine, whose name was Reumah, bore Tebah, Gaham, Tahash, and Maacah.

verses.esv: Sarah lived 127 years; these were the years of the life of Sarah.
verses.kjv: AND Sarah was an hundred and seven and twenty years old: these were the years of the life of Sarah.
verses.bsb: Now Sarah lived to be 127 years old.

verses.esv: And Sarah died at Kiriath-arba (that is, Hebron) in the land of Canaan, and Abraham went in to mourn for Sarah and to weep for her.
verses.kjv: And Sarah died in Kirjath-arba; the same is Hebron in the land of Canaan: and Abraham came to mourn for Sarah, and to weep for her.
verses.bsb: She died in Kiriath-arba (that is, Hebron) in the land of Canaan, and Abraham went out to mourn and to weep for her.

verses.esv: And Abraham rose up from before his dead and said to the Hittites,
verses.kjv: ¶ And Abraham stood up from before his dead, and spake unto the sons of Heth, saying,
verses.bsb: Then Abraham got up from beside his dead wife and said to the Hittites,

verses.esv: “I am a sojourner and foreigner among you; give me property among you for a burying place, that I may bury my dead out of my sight.”
verses.kjv: I am a stranger and a sojourner with you: give me a possession of a buryingplace with you, that I may bury my dead out of my sight.
verses.bsb: “I am a foreigner and an outsider among you. Give me a burial site among you so that I can bury my dead.”

verses.esv: The Hittites answered Abraham,
verses.kjv: And the children of Heth answered Abraham, saying unto him,
verses.bsb: The Hittites replied to Abraham,

verses.esv: “Hear us, my lord; you are a prince of God among us. Bury your dead in the choicest of our tombs. None of us will withhold from you his tomb to hinder you from burying your dead.”
verses.kjv: Hear us, my lord: thou art a mighty prince among us: in the choice of our sepulchres bury thy dead; none of us shall withhold from thee his sepulchre, but that thou mayest bury thy dead.
verses.bsb: “Listen to us, sir. You are God’s chosen one among us. Bury your dead in the finest of our tombs. None of us will withhold his tomb for burying your dead.”

verses.esv: Abraham rose and bowed to the Hittites, the people of the land.
verses.kjv: And Abraham stood up, and bowed himself to the people of the land, even to the children of Heth.
verses.bsb: Then Abraham rose and bowed down before the people of the land, the Hittites.

verses.esv: And he said to them, “If you are willing that I should bury my dead out of my sight, hear me and entreat for me Ephron the son of Zohar,
verses.kjv: And he communed with them, saying, If it be your mind that I should bury my dead out of my sight; hear me, and intreat for me to Ephron the son of Zohar,
verses.bsb: “If you are willing for me to bury my dead,” he said to them, “listen to me, and approach Ephron son of Zohar on my behalf

verses.esv: that he may give me the cave of Machpelah, which he owns; it is at the end of his field. For the full price let him give it to me in your presence as property for a burying place.”
verses.kjv: That he may give me the cave of Machpelah, which he hath, which is in the end of his field; for as much money as it is worth he shall give it me for a possession of a buryingplace amongst you.
verses.bsb: to sell me the cave of Machpelah that belongs to him; it is at the end of his field. Let him sell it to me in your presence for full price, so that I may have a burial site.”

verses.esv: Now Ephron was sitting among the Hittites, and Ephron the Hittite answered Abraham in the hearing of the Hittites, of all who went in at the gate of his city,
verses.kjv: And Ephron dwelt among the children of Heth: and Ephron the Hittite answered Abraham in the audience of the children of Heth, even of all that went in at the gate of his city, saying,
verses.bsb: Now Ephron was sitting among the sons of Heth. So in the presence of all the Hittites who had come to the gate of his city, Ephron the Hittite answered Abraham,

verses.esv: “No, my lord, hear me: I give you the field, and I give you the cave that is in it. In the sight of the sons of my people I give it to you. Bury your dead.”
verses.kjv: Nay, my lord, hear me: the field give I thee, and the cave that is therein, I give it thee; in the presence of the sons of my people give I it thee: bury thy dead.
verses.bsb: “No, my lord. Listen to me. I give you the field, and I give you the cave that is in it. I give it to you in the presence of my people. Bury your dead.”

verses.esv: Then Abraham bowed down before the people of the land.
verses.kjv: And Abraham bowed down himself before the people of the land.
verses.bsb: Again Abraham bowed down before the people of the land

verses.esv: And he said to Ephron in the hearing of the people of the land, “But if you will, hear me: I give the price of the field. Accept it from me, that I may bury my dead there.”
verses.kjv: And he spake unto Ephron in the audience of the people of the land, saying, But if thou wilt give it, I pray thee, hear me: I will give thee money for the field; take it of me, and I will bury my dead there.
verses.bsb: and said to Ephron in their presence, “If you will please listen to me, I will pay you the price of the field. Accept it from me, so that I may bury my dead there.”

verses.esv: Ephron answered Abraham,
verses.kjv: And Ephron answered Abraham, saying unto him,
verses.bsb: Ephron answered Abraham,

verses.esv: “My lord, listen to me: a piece of land worth four hundred shekels of silver, what is that between you and me? Bury your dead.”
verses.kjv: My lord, hearken unto me: the land is worth four hundred shekels of silver; what is that betwixt me and thee? bury therefore thy dead.
verses.bsb: “Listen to me, my lord. The land is worth four hundred shekels of silver, but what is that between you and me? Bury your dead.”

verses.esv: Abraham listened to Ephron, and Abraham weighed out for Ephron the silver that he had named in the hearing of the Hittites, four hundred shekels of silver, according to the weights current among the merchants.
verses.kjv: And Abraham hearkened unto Ephron; and Abraham weighed to Ephron the silver, which he had named in the audience of the sons of Heth, four hundred shekels of silver, current money with the merchant.
verses.bsb: Abraham agreed to Ephron’s terms and weighed out for him the price he had named in the hearing of the Hittites: four hundred shekels of silver, according to the standard of the merchants.

verses.esv: So the field of Ephron in Machpelah, which was to the east of Mamre, the field with the cave that was in it and all the trees that were in the field, throughout its whole area, was made over
verses.kjv: ¶ And the field of Ephron, which was in Machpelah, which was before Mamre, the field, and the cave which was therein, and all the trees that were in the field, that were in all the borders round about, were made sure
verses.bsb: So Ephron’s field at Machpelah near Mamre, the cave that was in it, and all the trees within the boundaries of the field were deeded over

verses.esv: to Abraham as a possession in the presence of the Hittites, before all who went in at the gate of his city.
verses.kjv: Unto Abraham for a possession in the presence of the children of Heth, before all that went in at the gate of his city.
verses.bsb: to Abraham’s possession in the presence of all the Hittites who had come to the gate of his city.

verses.esv: After this, Abraham buried Sarah his wife in the cave of the field of Machpelah east of Mamre (that is, Hebron) in the land of Canaan.
verses.kjv: And after this, Abraham buried Sarah his wife in the cave of the field of Machpelah before Mamre: the same is Hebron in the land of Canaan.
verses.bsb: After this, Abraham buried his wife Sarah in the cave of the field at Machpelah near Mamre (that is, Hebron) in the land of Canaan.

verses.esv: The field and the cave that is in it were made over to Abraham as property for a burying place by the Hittites.
verses.kjv: And the field, and the cave that is therein, were made sure unto Abraham for a possession of a buryingplace by the sons of Heth.
verses.bsb: So the field and its cave were deeded by the Hittites to Abraham as a burial site.

verses.esv: Now Abraham was old, well advanced in years. And the LORD had blessed Abraham in all things.
verses.kjv: AND Abraham was old, and well stricken in age: and the Lord had blessed Abraham in all things.
verses.bsb: By now Abraham was old and well along in years, and the LORD had blessed him in every way.

verses.esv: And Abraham said to his servant, the oldest of his household, who had charge of all that he had, “Put your hand under my thigh,
verses.kjv: And Abraham said unto his eldest servant of his house, that ruled over all that he had, Put, I pray thee, thy hand under my thigh:
verses.bsb: So Abraham instructed the chief servant of his household, who managed all he owned, “Place your hand under my thigh,

verses.esv: that I may make you swear by the LORD, the God of heaven and God of the earth, that you will not take a wife for my son from the daughters of the Canaanites, among whom I dwell,
verses.kjv: And I will make thee swear by the Lord, the God of heaven, and the God of the earth, that thou shalt not take a wife unto my son of the daughters of the Canaanites, among whom I dwell:
verses.bsb: and I will have you swear by the LORD, the God of heaven and the God of earth, that you will not take a wife for my son from the daughters of the Canaanites among whom I am dwelling,

verses.esv: but will go to my country and to my kindred, and take a wife for my son Isaac.”
verses.kjv: But thou shalt go unto my country, and to my kindred, and take a wife unto my son Isaac.
verses.bsb: but will go to my country and my kindred to take a wife for my son Isaac.”

verses.esv: The servant said to him, “Perhaps the woman may not be willing to follow me to this land. Must I then take your son back to the land from which you came?”
verses.kjv: And the servant said unto him, Peradventure the woman will not be willing to follow me unto this land: must I needs bring thy son again unto the land from whence thou camest?
verses.bsb: The servant asked him, “What if the woman is unwilling to follow me to this land? Shall I then take your son back to the land from which you came?”

verses.esv: Abraham said to him, “See to it that you do not take my son back there.
verses.kjv: And Abraham said unto him, Beware thou that thou bring not my son thither again.
verses.bsb: Abraham replied, “Make sure that you do not take my son back there.

verses.esv: The LORD, the God of heaven, who took me from my father's house and from the land of my kindred, and who spoke to me and swore to me, ‘To your offspring I will give this land,’ he will send his angel before you, and you shall take a wife for my son from there.
verses.kjv: ¶ The Lord God of heaven, which took me from my father’s house, and from the land of my kindred, and which spake unto me, and that sware unto me, saying, Unto thy seed will I give this land; he shall send his angel before thee, and thou shalt take a wife unto my son from thence.
verses.bsb: The LORD, the God of heaven, who brought me from my father’s house and my native land, who spoke to me and promised me on oath, saying, ‘To your offspring I will give this land’—He will send His angel before you so that you can take a wife for my son from there.

verses.esv: But if the woman is not willing to follow you, then you will be free from this oath of mine; only you must not take my son back there.”
verses.kjv: And if the woman will not be willing to follow thee, then thou shalt be clear from this my oath: only bring not my son thither again.
verses.bsb: And if the woman is unwilling to follow you, then you are released from this oath of mine. Only do not take my son back there.”

verses.esv: So the servant put his hand under the thigh of Abraham his master and swore to him concerning this matter.
verses.kjv: And the servant put his hand under the thigh of Abraham his master, and sware to him concerning that matter.
verses.bsb: So the servant placed his hand under the thigh of his master Abraham and swore an oath to him concerning this matter.

verses.esv: Then the servant took ten of his master's camels and departed, taking all sorts of choice gifts from his master; and he arose and went to Mesopotamia to the city of Nahor.
verses.kjv: ¶ And the servant took ten camels of the camels of his master, and departed; for all the goods of his master were in his hand: and he arose, and went to Mesopotamia, unto the city of Nahor.
verses.bsb: Then the servant took ten of his master’s camels and departed with all manner of good things from his master in hand. And he set out for Nahor’s hometown in Aram-naharaim.

verses.esv: And he made the camels kneel down outside the city by the well of water at the time of evening, the time when women go out to draw water.
verses.kjv: And he made his camels to kneel down without the city by a well of water at the time of the evening, even the time that women go out to draw water.
verses.bsb: As evening approached, he made the camels kneel down near the well outside the town at the time when the women went out to draw water.

verses.esv: And he said, “O LORD, God of my master Abraham, please grant me success today and show steadfast love to my master Abraham.
verses.kjv: And he said, O Lord God of my master Abraham, I pray thee, send me good speed this day, and shew kindness unto my master Abraham.
