verses.bsb: “O LORD, God of my master Abraham,” he prayed, “please grant me success today, and show kindness to my master Abraham.

verses.esv: Behold, I am standing by the spring of water, and the daughters of the men of the city are coming out to draw water.
verses.kjv: Behold, I stand here by the well of water; and the daughters of the men of the city come out to draw water:
verses.bsb: Here I am, standing beside the spring, and the daughters of the townspeople are coming out to draw water.

verses.esv: Let the young woman to whom I shall say, ‘Please let down your jar that I may drink,’ and who shall say, ‘Drink, and I will water your camels’—let her be the one whom you have appointed for your servant Isaac. By this I shall know that you have shown steadfast love to my master.”
verses.kjv: And let it come to pass, that the damsel to whom I shall say, Let down thy pitcher, I pray thee, that I may drink; and she shall say, Drink, and I will give thy camels drink also: let the same be she that thou hast appointed for thy servant Isaac; and thereby shall I know that thou hast shewed kindness unto my master.
verses.bsb: Now may it happen that the girl to whom I say, ‘Please let down your jar that I may drink,’ and who responds, ‘Drink, and I will water your camels as well’—let her be the one You have appointed for Your servant Isaac. By this I will know that You have shown kindness to my master.”

verses.esv: Before he had finished speaking, behold, Rebekah, who was born to Bethuel the son of Milcah, the wife of Nahor, Abraham's brother, came out with her water jar on her shoulder.
verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass, before he had done speaking, that, behold, Rebekah came out, who was born to Bethuel, son of Milcah, the wife of Nahor, Abraham’s brother, with her pitcher upon her shoulder.
verses.bsb: Before the servant had finished praying, Rebekah came out with her jar on her shoulder. She was the daughter of Bethuel son of Milcah, the wife of Abraham’s brother Nahor.

verses.esv: The young woman was very attractive in appearance, a maiden whom no man had known. She went down to the spring and filled her jar and came up.
verses.kjv: And the damsel was very fair to look upon, a virgin, neither had any man known her: and she went down to the well, and filled her pitcher, and came up.
verses.bsb: Now the girl was very beautiful, a virgin who had not had relations with any man. She went down to the spring, filled her jar, and came up again.

verses.esv: Then the servant ran to meet her and said, “Please give me a little water to drink from your jar.”
verses.kjv: And the servant ran to meet her, and said, Let me, I pray thee, drink a little water of thy pitcher.
verses.bsb: So the servant ran to meet her and said, “Please let me have a little water from your jar.”

verses.esv: She said, “Drink, my lord.” And she quickly let down her jar upon her hand and gave him a drink.
verses.kjv: And she said, Drink, my lord: and she hasted, and let down her pitcher upon her hand, and gave him drink.
verses.bsb: “Drink, my lord,” she replied, and she quickly lowered her jar to her hands and gave him a drink.

verses.esv: When she had finished giving him a drink, she said, “I will draw water for your camels also, until they have finished drinking.”
verses.kjv: And when she had done giving him drink, she said, I will draw water for thy camels also, until they have done drinking.
verses.bsb: After she had given him a drink, she said, “I will also draw water for your camels, until they have had enough to drink.”

verses.esv: So she quickly emptied her jar into the trough and ran again to the well to draw water, and she drew for all his camels.
verses.kjv: And she hasted, and emptied her pitcher into the trough, and ran again unto the well to draw water, and drew for all his camels.
verses.bsb: And she quickly emptied her jar into the trough and ran back to the well to draw water, until she had drawn water for all his camels.

verses.esv: The man gazed at her in silence to learn whether the LORD had prospered his journey or not.
verses.kjv: And the man wondering at her held his peace, to wit whether the Lord had made his journey prosperous or not.
verses.bsb: Meanwhile, the man watched her silently to see whether or not the LORD had made his journey a success.

verses.esv: When the camels had finished drinking, the man took a gold ring weighing a half shekel, and two bracelets for her arms weighing ten gold shekels,
verses.kjv: And it came to pass, as the camels had done drinking, that the man took a golden earring of half a shekel weight, and two bracelets for her hands of ten shekels weight of gold;
verses.bsb: And after the camels had finished drinking, he took out a gold ring weighing a beka, and two gold bracelets for her wrists weighing ten shekels.

verses.esv: and said, “Please tell me whose daughter you are. Is there room in your father's house for us to spend the night?”
verses.kjv: And said, Whose daughter art thou? tell me, I pray thee: is there room in thy father’s house for us to lodge in?
verses.bsb: “Whose daughter are you?” he asked. “Please tell me, is there room in your father’s house for us to spend the night?”

verses.esv: She said to him, “I am the daughter of Bethuel the son of Milcah, whom she bore to Nahor.”
verses.kjv: And she said unto him, I am the daughter of Bethuel the son of Milcah, which she bare unto Nahor.
verses.bsb: She replied, “I am the daughter of Bethuel, the son that Milcah bore to Nahor.”

verses.esv: She added, “We have plenty of both straw and fodder, and room to spend the night.”
verses.kjv: She said moreover unto him, We have both straw and provender enough, and room to lodge in.
verses.bsb: Then she added, “We have plenty of straw and feed, as well as a place for you to spend the night.”

verses.esv: The man bowed his head and worshiped the LORD
verses.kjv: And the man bowed down his head, and worshipped the Lord.
verses.bsb: Then the man bowed down and worshiped the LORD,

verses.esv: and said, “Blessed be the LORD, the God of my master Abraham, who has not forsaken his steadfast love and his faithfulness toward my master. As for me, the LORD has led me in the way to the house of my master's kinsmen.”
verses.kjv: And he said, Blessed be the Lord God of my master Abraham, who hath not left destitute my master of his mercy and his truth: I being in the way, the Lord led me to the house of my master’s brethren.
verses.bsb: saying, “Blessed be the LORD, the God of my master Abraham, who has not withheld His kindness and faithfulness from my master. As for me, the LORD has led me on the journey to the house of my master’s relatives.”

verses.esv: Then the young woman ran and told her mother's household about these things.
verses.kjv: And the damsel ran, and told them of her mother’s house these things.
verses.bsb: The girl ran and told her mother’s household about these things.

verses.esv: Rebekah had a brother whose name was Laban. Laban ran out toward the man, to the spring.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Rebekah had a brother, and his name was Laban: and Laban ran out unto the man, unto the well.
verses.bsb: Now Rebekah had a brother named Laban, and he rushed out to the man at the spring.

verses.esv: As soon as he saw the ring and the bracelets on his sister's arms, and heard the words of Rebekah his sister, “Thus the man spoke to me,” he went to the man. And behold, he was standing by the camels at the spring.
verses.kjv: And it came to pass, when he saw the earring and bracelets upon his sister’s hands, and when he heard the words of Rebekah his sister, saying, Thus spake the man unto me; that he came unto the man; and, behold, he stood by the camels at the well.
verses.bsb: As soon as he saw the ring, and the bracelets on his sister’s wrists, and heard Rebekah’s words, “The man said this to me,” he went and found the man standing by the camels near the spring.

verses.esv: He said, “Come in, O blessed of the LORD. Why do you stand outside? For I have prepared the house and a place for the camels.”
verses.kjv: And he said, Come in, thou blessed of the Lord; wherefore standest thou without? for I have prepared the house, and room for the camels.
verses.bsb: “Come, you who are blessed by the LORD,” said Laban. “Why are you standing out here? I have prepared the house and a place for the camels.”

verses.esv: So the man came to the house and unharnessed the camels, and gave straw and fodder to the camels, and there was water to wash his feet and the feet of the men who were with him.
verses.kjv: ¶ And the man came into the house: and he ungirded his camels, and gave straw and provender for the camels, and water to wash his feet, and the men’s feet that were with him.
verses.bsb: So the man came to the house, and the camels were unloaded. Straw and feed were brought to the camels, and water to wash his feet and the feet of his companions.

verses.esv: Then food was set before him to eat. But he said, “I will not eat until I have said what I have to say.” He said, “Speak on.”
verses.kjv: And there was set meat before him to eat: but he said, I will not eat, until I have told mine errand. And he said, Speak on.
verses.bsb: Then a meal was set before the man, but he said, “I will not eat until I have told you what I came to say.”  So Laban said, “Please speak.”

verses.esv: So he said, “I am Abraham's servant.
verses.kjv: And he said, I am Abraham’s servant.
verses.bsb: “I am Abraham’s servant,” he replied.

verses.esv: The LORD has greatly blessed my master, and he has become great. He has given him flocks and herds, silver and gold, male servants and female servants, camels and donkeys.
verses.kjv: And the Lord hath blessed my master greatly; and he is become great: and he hath given him flocks, and herds, and silver, and gold, and menservants, and maidservants, and camels, and asses.
verses.bsb: “The LORD has greatly blessed my master, and he has become rich. He has given him sheep and cattle, silver and gold, menservants and maidservants, camels and donkeys.

verses.esv: And Sarah my master's wife bore a son to my master when she was old, and to him he has given all that he has.
verses.kjv: And Sarah my master’s wife bare a son to my master when she was old: and unto him hath he given all that he hath.
verses.bsb: My master’s wife Sarah has borne him a son in her old age, and my master has given him everything he owns.

verses.esv: My master made me swear, saying, ‘You shall not take a wife for my son from the daughters of the Canaanites, in whose land I dwell,
verses.kjv: And my master made me swear, saying, Thou shalt not take a wife to my son of the daughters of the Canaanites, in whose land I dwell:
verses.bsb: My master made me swear an oath and said, ‘You shall not take a wife for my son from the daughters of the Canaanites in whose land I dwell,

verses.esv: but you shall go to my father's house and to my clan and take a wife for my son.’
verses.kjv: But thou shalt go unto my father’s house, and to my kindred, and take a wife unto my son.
verses.bsb: but you shall go to my father’s house and to my kindred to take a wife for my son.’

verses.esv: I said to my master, ‘Perhaps the woman will not follow me.’
verses.kjv: And I said unto my master, Peradventure the woman will not follow me.
verses.bsb: Then I asked my master, ‘What if the woman will not come back with me?’

verses.esv: But he said to me, ‘The LORD, before whom I have walked, will send his angel with you and prosper your way. You shall take a wife for my son from my clan and from my father's house.
verses.kjv: And he said unto me, The Lord, before whom I walk, will send his angel with thee, and prosper thy way; and thou shalt take a wife for my son of my kindred, and of my father’s house:
verses.bsb: And he told me, ‘The LORD, before whom I have walked, will send His angel with you and make your journey a success, so that you may take a wife for my son from my kindred and from my father’s house.

verses.esv: Then you will be free from my oath, when you come to my clan. And if they will not give her to you, you will be free from my oath.’
verses.kjv: Then shalt thou be clear from this my oath, when thou comest to my kindred; and if they give not thee one, thou shalt be clear from my oath.
verses.bsb: And when you go to my kindred, if they refuse to give her to you, then you will be released from my oath.’

verses.esv: “I came today to the spring and said, ‘O LORD, the God of my master Abraham, if now you are prospering the way that I go,
verses.kjv: And I came this day unto the well, and said, O Lord God of my master Abraham, if now thou do prosper my way which I go:
verses.bsb: So when I came to the spring today, I prayed: O LORD, God of my master Abraham, if only You would make my journey a success!

verses.esv: behold, I am standing by the spring of water. Let the virgin who comes out to draw water, to whom I shall say, “Please give me a little water from your jar to drink,”
verses.kjv: Behold, I stand by the well of water; and it shall come to pass, that when the virgin cometh forth to draw water, and I say to her, Give me, I pray thee, a little water of thy pitcher to drink;
verses.bsb: Here I am, standing beside this spring. Now if a maiden comes out to draw water and I say to her, ‘Please let me drink a little water from your jar,’

verses.esv: and who will say to me, “Drink, and I will draw for your camels also,” let her be the woman whom the LORD has appointed for my master's son.’
verses.kjv: And she say to me, Both drink thou, and I will also draw for thy camels: let the same be the woman whom the Lord hath appointed out for my master’s son.
verses.bsb: and she replies, ‘Drink, and I will draw water for your camels as well,’ may she be the woman the LORD has appointed for my master’s son.

verses.esv: “Before I had finished speaking in my heart, behold, Rebekah came out with her water jar on her shoulder, and she went down to the spring and drew water. I said to her, ‘Please let me drink.’
verses.kjv: And before I had done speaking in mine heart, behold, Rebekah came forth with her pitcher on her shoulder; and she went down unto the well, and drew water: and I said unto her, Let me drink, I pray thee.
verses.bsb: And before I had finished praying in my heart, there was Rebekah coming out with her jar on her shoulder, and she went down to the spring and drew water. So I said to her, ‘Please give me a drink.’

verses.esv: She quickly let down her jar from her shoulder and said, ‘Drink, and I will give your camels drink also.’ So I drank, and she gave the camels drink also.
verses.kjv: And she made haste, and let down her pitcher from her shoulder, and said, Drink, and I will give thy camels drink also: so I drank, and she made the camels drink also.
verses.bsb: She quickly lowered her jar from her shoulder and said, ‘Drink, and I will water your camels as well.’ So I drank, and she also watered the camels.

verses.esv: Then I asked her, ‘Whose daughter are you?’ She said, ‘The daughter of Bethuel, Nahor's son, whom Milcah bore to him.’ So I put the ring on her nose and the bracelets on her arms.
verses.kjv: And I asked her, and said, Whose daughter art thou? And she said, The daughter of Bethuel, Nahor’s son, whom Milcah bare unto him: and I put the earring upon her face, and the bracelets upon her hands.
verses.bsb: Then I asked her, ‘Whose daughter are you?’  She replied, ‘The daughter of Bethuel son of Nahor, whom Milcah bore to him.’ So I put the ring on her nose and the bracelets on her wrists.

verses.esv: Then I bowed my head and worshiped the LORD and blessed the LORD, the God of my master Abraham, who had led me by the right way to take the daughter of my master's kinsman for his son.
verses.kjv: And I bowed down my head, and worshipped the Lord, and blessed the Lord God of my master Abraham, which had led me in the right way to take my master’s brother’s daughter unto his son.
verses.bsb: Then I bowed down and worshiped the LORD; and I blessed the LORD, the God of my master Abraham, who led me on the right road to take the granddaughter of my master’s brother for his son.

verses.esv: Now then, if you are going to show steadfast love and faithfulness to my master, tell me; and if not, tell me, that I may turn to the right hand or to the left.”
verses.kjv: And now if ye will deal kindly and truly with my master, tell me: and if not, tell me; that I may turn to the right hand, or to the left.
verses.bsb: Now if you will show kindness and faithfulness to my master, tell me; but if not, let me know, so that I may go elsewhere.”

verses.esv: Then Laban and Bethuel answered and said, “The thing has come from the LORD; we cannot speak to you bad or good.
verses.kjv: Then Laban and Bethuel answered and said, The thing proceedeth from the Lord: we cannot speak unto thee bad or good.
verses.bsb: Laban and Bethuel answered, “This is from the LORD; we have no choice in the matter.

verses.esv: Behold, Rebekah is before you; take her and go, and let her be the wife of your master's son, as the LORD has spoken.”
verses.kjv: Behold, Rebekah is before thee, take her, and go, and let her be thy master’s son’s wife, as the Lord hath spoken.
verses.bsb: Rebekah is here before you. Take her and go, and let her become the wife of your master’s son, just as the LORD has decreed.”

verses.esv: When Abraham's servant heard their words, he bowed himself to the earth before the LORD.
verses.kjv: And it came to pass, that, when Abraham’s servant heard their words, he worshipped the Lord, bowing himself to the earth.
verses.bsb: When Abraham’s servant heard their words, he bowed down to the ground before the LORD.

verses.esv: And the servant brought out jewelry of silver and of gold, and garments, and gave them to Rebekah. He also gave to her brother and to her mother costly ornaments.
verses.kjv: And the servant brought forth jewels of silver, and jewels of gold, and raiment, and gave them to Rebekah: he gave also to her brother and to her mother precious things.
verses.bsb: Then he brought out jewels of silver and gold, and articles of clothing, and he gave them to Rebekah. He also gave precious gifts to her brother and her mother.

verses.esv: And he and the men who were with him ate and drank, and they spent the night there. When they arose in the morning, he said, “Send me away to my master.”
verses.kjv: And they did eat and drink, he and the men that were with him, and tarried all night; and they rose up in the morning, and he said, Send me away unto my master.
verses.bsb: Then he and the men with him ate and drank and spent the night there.  When they got up the next morning, he said, “Send me on my way to my master.”

verses.esv: Her brother and her mother said, “Let the young woman remain with us a while, at least ten days; after that she may go.”
verses.kjv: And her brother and her mother said, Let the damsel abide with us a few days, at the least ten; after that she shall go.
verses.bsb: But her brother and mother said, “Let the girl remain with us ten days or so. After that, she may go.”

verses.esv: But he said to them, “Do not delay me, since the LORD has prospered my way. Send me away that I may go to my master.”
verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Hinder me not, seeing the Lord hath prospered my way; send me away that I may go to my master.
verses.bsb: But he replied, “Do not delay me, since the LORD has made my journey a success. Send me on my way so that I may go to my master.”

verses.esv: They said, “Let us call the young woman and ask her.”
verses.kjv: And they said, We will call the damsel, and inquire at her mouth.
verses.bsb: So they said, “We will call the girl and ask her opinion.”

verses.esv: And they called Rebekah and said to her, “Will you go with this man?” She said, “I will go.”
verses.kjv: And they called Rebekah, and said unto her, Wilt thou go with this man? And she said, I will go.
verses.bsb: They called Rebekah and asked her, “Will you go with this man?”  “I will go,” she replied.

verses.esv: So they sent away Rebekah their sister and her nurse, and Abraham's servant and his men.
verses.kjv: And they sent away Rebekah their sister, and her nurse, and Abraham’s servant, and his men.
verses.bsb: So they sent their sister Rebekah on her way, along with her nurse and Abraham’s servant and his men.

verses.esv: And they blessed Rebekah and said to her, “Our sister, may you become thousands of ten thousands, and may your offspring possess the gate of those who hate him!”
verses.kjv: And they blessed Rebekah, and said unto her, Thou art our sister, be thou the mother of thousands of millions, and let thy seed possess the gate of those which hate them.
verses.bsb: And they blessed Rebekah and said to her,  “Our sister, may you become the mother of thousands upon thousands. May your offspring possess the gates of their enemies.”

verses.esv: Then Rebekah and her young women arose and rode on the camels and followed the man. Thus the servant took Rebekah and went his way.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Rebekah arose, and her damsels, and they rode upon the camels, and followed the man: and the servant took Rebekah, and went his way.
verses.bsb: Then Rebekah and her servant girls got ready, mounted the camels, and followed the man. So the servant took Rebekah and left.

verses.esv: Now Isaac had returned from Beer-lahai-roi and was dwelling in the Negeb.
verses.kjv: And Isaac came from the way of the well Lahai-roi; for he dwelt in the south country.
verses.bsb: Now Isaac had just returned from Beer-lahai-roi, for he was living in the Negev.

verses.esv: And Isaac went out to meditate in the field toward evening. And he lifted up his eyes and saw, and behold, there were camels coming.
verses.kjv: And Isaac went out to meditate in the field at the eventide: and he lifted up his eyes, and saw, and, behold, the camels were coming.
verses.bsb: Early in the evening, Isaac went out to the field to meditate, and looking up, he saw the camels approaching.

verses.esv: And Rebekah lifted up her eyes, and when she saw Isaac, she dismounted from the camel
verses.kjv: And Rebekah lifted up her eyes, and when she saw Isaac, she lighted off the camel.
verses.bsb: And when Rebekah looked up and saw Isaac, she got down from her camel

verses.esv: and said to the servant, “Who is that man, walking in the field to meet us?” The servant said, “It is my master.” So she took her veil and covered herself.
verses.kjv: For she had said unto the servant, What man is this that walketh in the field to meet us? And the servant had said, It is my master: therefore she took a vail, and covered herself.
verses.bsb: and asked the servant, “Who is that man in the field coming to meet us?”  “It is my master,” the servant answered. So she took her veil and covered herself.

verses.esv: And the servant told Isaac all the things that he had done.
verses.kjv: And the servant told Isaac all things that he had done.
verses.bsb: Then the servant told Isaac all that he had done.

verses.esv: Then Isaac brought her into the tent of Sarah his mother and took Rebekah, and she became his wife, and he loved her. So Isaac was comforted after his mother's death.
verses.kjv: And Isaac brought her into his mother Sarah’s tent, and took Rebekah, and she became his wife; and he loved her: and Isaac was comforted after his mother’s death.
verses.bsb: And Isaac brought her into the tent of his mother Sarah and took Rebekah as his wife. And Isaac loved her and was comforted after his mother’s death.

verses.esv: Abraham took another wife, whose name was Keturah.
verses.kjv: THEN again Abraham took a wife, and her name was Keturah.
verses.bsb: Now Abraham had taken another wife, named Keturah,

verses.esv: She bore him Zimran, Jokshan, Medan, Midian, Ishbak, and Shuah.
verses.kjv: And she bare him Zimran, and Jokshan, and Medan, and Midian, and Ishbak, and Shuah.
verses.bsb: and she bore him Zimran, Jokshan, Medan, Midian, Ishbak, and Shuah.

verses.esv: Jokshan fathered Sheba and Dedan. The sons of Dedan were Asshurim, Letushim, and Leummim.
verses.kjv: And Jokshan begat Sheba, and Dedan. And the sons of Dedan were Asshurim, and Letushim, and Leummim.
verses.bsb: Jokshan was the father of Sheba and Dedan. And the sons of Dedan were the Asshurites, the Letushites, and the Leummites.

verses.esv: The sons of Midian were Ephah, Epher, Hanoch, Abida, and Eldaah. All these were the children of Keturah.
verses.kjv: And the sons of Midian; Ephah, and Epher, and Hanoch, and Abida, and Eldaah. All these were the children of Keturah.
verses.bsb: The sons of Midian were Ephah, Epher, Hanoch, Abida, and Eldaah.  All these were descendants of Keturah.

verses.esv: Abraham gave all he had to Isaac.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Abraham gave all that he had unto Isaac.
verses.bsb: Abraham left everything he owned to Isaac.

verses.esv: But to the sons of his concubines Abraham gave gifts, and while he was still living he sent them away from his son Isaac, eastward to the east country.
verses.kjv: But unto the sons of the concubines, which Abraham had, Abraham gave gifts, and sent them away from Isaac his son, while he yet lived, eastward, unto the east country.
verses.bsb: But while he was still alive, Abraham gave gifts to the sons of his concubines and sent them away from his son Isaac to the land of the east.

verses.esv: These are the days of the years of Abraham's life, 175 years.
verses.kjv: And these are the days of the years of Abraham’s life which he lived, an hundred threescore and fifteen years.
verses.bsb: Abraham lived a total of 175 years.

verses.esv: Abraham breathed his last and died in a good old age, an old man and full of years, and was gathered to his people.
verses.kjv: Then Abraham gave up the ghost, and died in a good old age, an old man, and full of years; and was gathered to his people.
verses.bsb: And at a ripe old age he breathed his last and died, old and contented, and was gathered to his people.

verses.esv: Isaac and Ishmael his sons buried him in the cave of Machpelah, in the field of Ephron the son of Zohar the Hittite, east of Mamre,
verses.kjv: And his sons Isaac and Ishmael buried him in the cave of Machpelah, in the field of Ephron the son of Zohar the Hittite, which is before Mamre;
verses.bsb: His sons Isaac and Ishmael buried him in the cave of Machpelah near Mamre, in the field of Ephron son of Zohar the Hittite.

verses.esv: the field that Abraham purchased from the Hittites. There Abraham was buried, with Sarah his wife.
verses.kjv: The field which Abraham purchased of the sons of Heth: there was Abraham buried, and Sarah his wife.
verses.bsb: This was the field that Abraham had bought from the Hittites. Abraham was buried there with his wife Sarah.

verses.esv: After the death of Abraham, God blessed Isaac his son. And Isaac settled at Beer-lahai-roi.
verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass after the death of Abraham, that God blessed his son Isaac; and Isaac dwelt by the well Lahai-roi.
verses.bsb: After Abraham’s death, God blessed his son Isaac, who lived near Beer-lahai-roi.

verses.esv: These are the generations of Ishmael, Abraham's son, whom Hagar the Egyptian, Sarah's servant, bore to Abraham.
verses.kjv: ¶ Now these are the generations of Ishmael, Abraham’s son, whom Hagar the Egyptian, Sarah’s handmaid, bare unto Abraham:
verses.bsb: This is the account of Abraham’s son Ishmael, whom Hagar the Egyptian, Sarah’s maidservant, bore to Abraham.

verses.esv: These are the names of the sons of Ishmael, named in the order of their birth: Nebaioth, the firstborn of Ishmael; and Kedar, Adbeel, Mibsam,
verses.kjv: And these are the names of the sons of Ishmael, by their names, according to their generations: the firstborn of Ishmael, Nebajoth; and Kedar, and Adbeel, and Mibsam,
verses.bsb: These are the names of the sons of Ishmael in the order of their birth: Nebaioth the firstborn of Ishmael, then Kedar, Adbeel, Mibsam,

verses.esv: Mishma, Dumah, Massa,
verses.kjv: And Mishma, and Dumah, and Massa,
verses.bsb: Mishma, Dumah, Massa,

verses.esv: Hadad, Tema, Jetur, Naphish, and Kedemah.
verses.kjv: Hadar, and Tema, Jetur, Naphish, and Kedemah:
verses.bsb: Hadad, Tema, Jetur, Naphish, and Kedemah.

verses.esv: These are the sons of Ishmael and these are their names, by their villages and by their encampments, twelve princes according to their tribes.
verses.kjv: These are the sons of Ishmael, and these are their names, by their towns, and by their castles; twelve princes according to their nations.
verses.bsb: These were the sons of Ishmael, and these were their names by their villages and encampments—twelve princes of their tribes.

verses.esv: (These are the years of the life of Ishmael: 137 years. He breathed his last and died, and was gathered to his people.)
verses.kjv: And these are the years of the life of Ishmael, an hundred and thirty and seven years: and he gave up the ghost and died; and was gathered unto his people.
verses.bsb: Ishmael lived a total of 137 years. Then he breathed his last and died, and was gathered to his people.

verses.esv: They settled from Havilah to Shur, which is opposite Egypt in the direction of Assyria. He settled over against all his kinsmen.
verses.kjv: And they dwelt from Havilah unto Shur, that is before Egypt, as thou goest toward Assyria: and he died in the presence of all his brethren.
verses.bsb: Ishmael’s descendants settled from Havilah to Shur, which is near the border of Egypt as you go toward Asshur. And they lived in hostility toward all their brothers.

verses.esv: These are the generations of Isaac, Abraham's son: Abraham fathered Isaac,
verses.kjv: ¶ And these are the generations of Isaac, Abraham’s son: Abraham begat Isaac:
verses.bsb: This is the account of Abraham’s son Isaac. Abraham became the father of Isaac,

verses.esv: and Isaac was forty years old when he took Rebekah, the daughter of Bethuel the Aramean of Paddan-aram, the sister of Laban the Aramean, to be his wife.
verses.kjv: And Isaac was forty years old when he took Rebekah to wife, the daughter of Bethuel the Syrian of Padan-aram, the sister to Laban the Syrian.
verses.bsb: and Isaac was forty years old when he married Rebekah, the daughter of Bethuel the Aramean from Paddan-aram and the sister of Laban the Aramean.

verses.esv: And Isaac prayed to the LORD for his wife, because she was barren. And the LORD granted his prayer, and Rebekah his wife conceived.
verses.kjv: And Isaac intreated the Lord for his wife, because she was barren: and the Lord was intreated of him, and Rebekah his wife conceived.
verses.bsb: Later, Isaac prayed to the LORD on behalf of his wife, because she was barren. And the LORD heard his prayer, and his wife Rebekah conceived.

verses.esv: The children struggled together within her, and she said, “If it is thus, why is this happening to me?” So she went to inquire of the LORD.
verses.kjv: And the children struggled together within her; and she said, If it be so, why am I thus? And she went to inquire of the Lord.
verses.bsb: But the children inside her struggled with each other, and she said, “Why is this happening to me?” So Rebekah went to inquire of the LORD,

verses.esv: And the LORD said to her, “Two nations are in your womb, and two peoples from within you shall be divided; the one shall be stronger than the other, the older shall serve the younger.”
verses.kjv: And the Lord said unto her, Two nations are in thy womb, and two manner of people shall be separated from thy bowels; and the one people shall be stronger than the other people; and the elder shall serve the younger.
verses.bsb: and He declared to her:  “Two nations are in your womb, and two peoples from within you will be separated; one people will be stronger than the other, and the older will serve the younger.”

verses.esv: When her days to give birth were completed, behold, there were twins in her womb.
verses.kjv: ¶ And when her days to be delivered were fulfilled, behold, there were twins in her womb.
verses.bsb: When her time came to give birth, there were indeed twins in her womb.

verses.esv: The first came out red, all his body like a hairy cloak, so they called his name Esau.
verses.kjv: And the first came out red, all over like an hairy garment; and they called his name Esau.
verses.bsb: The first one came out red, covered with hair like a fur coat; so they named him Esau.

verses.esv: Afterward his brother came out with his hand holding Esau's heel, so his name was called Jacob. Isaac was sixty years old when she bore them.
verses.kjv: And after that came his brother out, and his hand took hold on Esau’s heel; and his name was called Jacob: and Isaac was threescore years old when she bare them.
verses.bsb: After this, his brother came out grasping Esau’s heel; so he was named Jacob. And Isaac was sixty years old when the twins were born.

verses.esv: When the boys grew up, Esau was a skillful hunter, a man of the field, while Jacob was a quiet man, dwelling in tents.
verses.kjv: And the boys grew: and Esau was a cunning hunter, a man of the field; and Jacob was a plain man, dwelling in tents.
verses.bsb: When the boys grew up, Esau became a skillful hunter, a man of the field, while Jacob was a quiet man who stayed at home.

verses.esv: Isaac loved Esau because he ate of his game, but Rebekah loved Jacob.
verses.kjv: And Isaac loved Esau, because he did eat of his venison: but Rebekah loved Jacob.
verses.bsb: Because Isaac had a taste for wild game, he loved Esau; but Rebekah loved Jacob.

verses.esv: Once when Jacob was cooking stew, Esau came in from the field, and he was exhausted.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Jacob sod pottage: and Esau came from the field, and he was faint:
verses.bsb: One day, while Jacob was cooking some stew, Esau came in from the field and was famished.

verses.esv: And Esau said to Jacob, “Let me eat some of that red stew, for I am exhausted!” (Therefore his name was called Edom.)
verses.kjv: And Esau said to Jacob, Feed me, I pray thee, with that same red pottage; for I am faint: therefore was his name called Edom.
verses.bsb: He said to Jacob, “Let me eat some of that red stew, for I am famished.” (That is why he was also called Edom.)

verses.esv: Jacob said, “Sell me your birthright now.”
verses.kjv: And Jacob said, Sell me this day thy birthright.
verses.bsb: “First sell me your birthright,” Jacob replied.

verses.esv: Esau said, “I am about to die; of what use is a birthright to me?”
verses.kjv: And Esau said, Behold, I am at the point to die: and what profit shall this birthright do to me?
verses.bsb: “Look,” said Esau, “I am about to die, so what good is a birthright to me?”

verses.esv: Jacob said, “Swear to me now.” So he swore to him and sold his birthright to Jacob.
verses.kjv: And Jacob said, Swear to me this day; and he sware unto him: and he sold his birthright unto Jacob.
verses.bsb: “Swear to me first,” Jacob said.  So Esau swore to Jacob and sold him the birthright.

verses.esv: Then Jacob gave Esau bread and lentil stew, and he ate and drank and rose and went his way. Thus Esau despised his birthright.
verses.kjv: Then Jacob gave Esau bread and pottage of lentiles; and he did eat and drink, and rose up, and went his way: thus Esau despised his birthright.
verses.bsb: Then Jacob gave some bread and lentil stew to Esau, who ate and drank and then got up and went away. Thus Esau despised his birthright.

verses.esv: Now there was a famine in the land, besides the former famine that was in the days of Abraham. And Isaac went to Gerar to Abimelech king of the Philistines.
verses.kjv: AND there was a famine in the land, beside the first famine that was in the days of Abraham. And Isaac went unto Abimelech king of the Philistines unto Gerar.
verses.bsb: Now there was another famine in the land, subsequent to the one that had occurred in Abraham’s time. And Isaac went to Abimelech king of the Philistines at Gerar.

verses.esv: And the LORD appeared to him and said, “Do not go down to Egypt; dwell in the land of which I shall tell you.
verses.kjv: And the Lord appeared unto him, and said, Go not down into Egypt; dwell in the land which I shall tell thee of:
verses.bsb: The LORD appeared to Isaac and said, “Do not go down to Egypt. Settle in the land where I tell you.

verses.esv: Sojourn in this land, and I will be with you and will bless you, for to you and to your offspring I will give all these lands, and I will establish the oath that I swore to Abraham your father.
verses.kjv: Sojourn in this land, and I will be with thee, and will bless thee; for unto thee, and unto thy seed, I will give all these countries, and I will perform the oath which I sware unto Abraham thy father;
verses.bsb: Stay in this land as a foreigner, and I will be with you and bless you. For I will give all these lands to you and your offspring, and I will confirm the oath that I swore to your father Abraham.

verses.esv: I will multiply your offspring as the stars of heaven and will give to your offspring all these lands. And in your offspring all the nations of the earth shall be blessed,
verses.kjv: And I will make thy seed to multiply as the stars of heaven, and will give unto thy seed all these countries; and in thy seed shall all the nations of the earth be blessed;
verses.bsb: I will make your descendants as numerous as the stars in the sky, and I will give them all these lands, and through your offspring all nations of the earth will be blessed,

verses.esv: because Abraham obeyed my voice and kept my charge, my commandments, my statutes, and my laws.”
verses.kjv: Because that Abraham obeyed my voice, and kept my charge, my commandments, my statutes, and my laws.
verses.bsb: because Abraham listened to My voice and kept My charge, My commandments, My statutes, and My laws.”

verses.esv: So Isaac settled in Gerar.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Isaac dwelt in Gerar:
verses.bsb: So Isaac settled in Gerar.

verses.esv: When the men of the place asked him about his wife, he said, “She is my sister,” for he feared to say, “My wife,” thinking, “lest the men of the place should kill me because of Rebekah,” because she was attractive in appearance.
verses.kjv: And the men of the place asked him of his wife; and he said, She is my sister: for he feared to say, She is my wife; lest, said he, the men of the place should kill me for Rebekah; because she was fair to look upon.
verses.bsb: But when the men of that place asked about his wife, he said, “She is my sister.” For he was afraid to say, “She is my wife,” since he thought to himself, “The men of this place will kill me on account of Rebekah, because she is so beautiful.”

verses.esv: When he had been there a long time, Abimelech king of the Philistines looked out of a window and saw Isaac laughing with Rebekah his wife.
verses.kjv: And it came to pass, when he had been there a long time, that Abimelech king of the Philistines looked out at a window, and saw, and, behold, Isaac was sporting with Rebekah his wife.
verses.bsb: When Isaac had been there a long time, Abimelech king of the Philistines looked down from the window and was surprised to see Isaac caressing his wife Rebekah.

verses.esv: So Abimelech called Isaac and said, “Behold, she is your wife. How then could you say, ‘She is my sister’?” Isaac said to him, “Because I thought, ‘Lest I die because of her.’”
verses.kjv: And Abimelech called Isaac, and said, Behold, of a surety she is thy wife: and how saidst thou, She is my sister? And Isaac said unto him, Because I said, Lest I die for her.
verses.bsb: Abimelech sent for Isaac and said, “So she is really your wife! How could you say, ‘She is my sister’?”  Isaac replied, “Because I thought I might die on account of her.”

verses.esv: Abimelech said, “What is this you have done to us? One of the people might easily have lain with your wife, and you would have brought guilt upon us.”
verses.kjv: And Abimelech said, What is this thou hast done unto us? one of the people might lightly have lien with thy wife, and thou shouldest have brought guiltiness upon us.
verses.bsb: “What is this you have done to us?” asked Abimelech. “One of the people could easily have slept with your wife, and you would have brought guilt upon us.”

verses.esv: So Abimelech warned all the people, saying, “Whoever touches this man or his wife shall surely be put to death.”
verses.kjv: And Abimelech charged all his people, saying, He that toucheth this man or his wife shall surely be put to death.
verses.bsb: So Abimelech warned all the people, saying, “Whoever harms this man or his wife will surely be put to death.”

verses.esv: And Isaac sowed in that land and reaped in the same year a hundredfold. The LORD blessed him,
verses.kjv: Then Isaac sowed in that land, and received in the same year an hundredfold: and the Lord blessed him.
verses.bsb: Now Isaac sowed seed in the land, and that very year he reaped a hundredfold. And the LORD blessed him,

verses.esv: and the man became rich, and gained more and more until he became very wealthy.
verses.kjv: And the man waxed great, and went forward, and grew until he became very great:
verses.bsb: and he became richer and richer, until he was exceedingly wealthy.

verses.esv: He had possessions of flocks and herds and many servants, so that the Philistines envied him.
verses.kjv: For he had possession of flocks, and possession of herds, and great store of servants: and the Philistines envied him.
verses.bsb: He owned so many flocks and herds and servants that the Philistines envied him.

verses.esv: (Now the Philistines had stopped and filled with earth all the wells that his father's servants had dug in the days of Abraham his father.)
verses.kjv: For all the wells which his father’s servants had digged in the days of Abraham his father, the Philistines had stopped them, and filled them with earth.
verses.bsb: So the Philistines took dirt and stopped up all the wells that his father’s servants had dug in the days of his father Abraham.

verses.esv: And Abimelech said to Isaac, “Go away from us, for you are much mightier than we.”
verses.kjv: And Abimelech said unto Isaac, Go from us; for thou art much mightier than we.
verses.bsb: Then Abimelech said to Isaac, “Depart from us, for you are much too powerful for us.”

verses.esv: So Isaac departed from there and encamped in the Valley of Gerar and settled there.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Isaac departed thence, and pitched his tent in the valley of Gerar, and dwelt there.
verses.bsb: So Isaac left that place and encamped in the Valley of Gerar and settled there.

verses.esv: And Isaac dug again the wells of water that had been dug in the days of Abraham his father, which the Philistines had stopped after the death of Abraham. And he gave them the names that his father had given them.
verses.kjv: And Isaac digged again the wells of water, which they had digged in the days of Abraham his father; for the Philistines had stopped them after the death of Abraham: and he called their names after the names by which his father had called them.
verses.bsb: Isaac reopened the wells that had been dug in the days of his father Abraham, which the Philistines had stopped up after Abraham died. And he gave these wells the same names his father had given them.

verses.esv: But when Isaac's servants dug in the valley and found there a well of spring water,
verses.kjv: And Isaac’s servants digged in the valley, and found there a well of springing water.
verses.bsb: Then Isaac’s servants dug in the valley and found a well of fresh water there.

verses.esv: the herdsmen of Gerar quarreled with Isaac's herdsmen, saying, “The water is ours.” So he called the name of the well Esek, because they contended with him.
verses.kjv: And the herdmen of Gerar did strive with Isaac’s herdmen, saying, The water is ours: and he called the name of the well Esek; because they strove with him.
verses.bsb: But the herdsmen of Gerar quarreled with Isaac’s herdsmen and said, “The water is ours!” So he named the well Esek, because they contended with him.

verses.esv: Then they dug another well, and they quarreled over that also, so he called its name Sitnah.
verses.kjv: And they digged another well, and strove for that also: and he called the name of it Sitnah.
verses.bsb: Then they dug another well and quarreled over that one also; so he named it Sitnah.

verses.esv: And he moved from there and dug another well, and they did not quarrel over it. So he called its name Rehoboth, saying, “For now the LORD has made room for us, and we shall be fruitful in the land.”
verses.kjv: And he removed from thence, and digged another well; and for that they strove not: and he called the name of it Rehoboth; and he said, For now the Lord hath made room for us, and we shall be fruitful in the land.
verses.bsb: He moved on from there and dug another well, and they did not quarrel over it. He named it Rehoboth and said, “At last the LORD has made room for us, and we will be fruitful in the land.”

verses.esv: From there he went up to Beersheba.
verses.kjv: And he went up from thence to Beer-sheba.
verses.bsb: From there Isaac went up to Beersheba,

verses.esv: And the LORD appeared to him the same night and said, “I am the God of Abraham your father. Fear not, for I am with you and will bless you and multiply your offspring for my servant Abraham's sake.”
verses.kjv: And the Lord appeared unto him the same night, and said, I am the God of Abraham thy father: fear not, for I am with thee, and will bless thee, and multiply thy seed for my servant Abraham’s sake.
verses.bsb: and that night the LORD appeared to him and said, “I am the God of your father Abraham. Do not be afraid, for I am with you. I will bless you and multiply your descendants for the sake of My servant Abraham.”

verses.esv: So he built an altar there and called upon the name of the LORD and pitched his tent there. And there Isaac's servants dug a well.
verses.kjv: And he builded an altar there, and called upon the name of the Lord, and pitched his tent there: and there Isaac’s servants digged a well.
verses.bsb: So Isaac built an altar there and called on the name of the LORD, and he pitched his tent there. His servants also dug a well there.

verses.esv: When Abimelech went to him from Gerar with Ahuzzath his adviser and Phicol the commander of his army,
verses.kjv: ¶ Then Abimelech went to him from Gerar, and Ahuzzath one of his friends, and Phichol the chief captain of his army.
verses.bsb: Later, Abimelech came to Isaac from Gerar, with Ahuzzath his adviser and Phicol the commander of his army.

verses.esv: Isaac said to them, “Why have you come to me, seeing that you hate me and have sent me away from you?”
verses.kjv: And Isaac said unto them, Wherefore come ye to me, seeing ye hate me, and have sent me away from you?
verses.bsb: “Why have you come to me?” Isaac asked them. “You hated me and sent me away.”

verses.esv: They said, “We see plainly that the LORD has been with you. So we said, let there be a sworn pact between us, between you and us, and let us make a covenant with you,
verses.kjv: And they said, We saw certainly that the Lord was with thee: and we said, Let there be now an oath betwixt us, even betwixt us and thee, and let us make a covenant with thee;
verses.bsb: “We can plainly see that the LORD has been with you,” they replied. “We recommend that there should now be an oath between us and you. Let us make a covenant with you

verses.esv: that you will do us no harm, just as we have not touched you and have done to you nothing but good and have sent you away in peace. You are now the blessed of the LORD.”
verses.kjv: That thou wilt do us no hurt, as we have not touched thee, and as we have done unto thee nothing but good, and have sent thee away in peace: thou art now the blessed of the Lord.
verses.bsb: that you will not harm us, just as we have not harmed you but have done only good to you, sending you on your way in peace. And now you are blessed by the LORD.”

verses.esv: So he made them a feast, and they ate and drank.
verses.kjv: And he made them a feast, and they did eat and drink.
verses.bsb: So Isaac prepared a feast for them, and they ate and drank.

verses.esv: In the morning they rose early and exchanged oaths. And Isaac sent them on their way, and they departed from him in peace.
verses.kjv: And they rose up betimes in the morning, and sware one to another: and Isaac sent them away, and they departed from him in peace.
verses.bsb: And they got up early the next morning and swore an oath to each other. Then Isaac sent them on their way, and they left him in peace.

verses.esv: That same day Isaac's servants came and told him about the well that they had dug and said to him, “We have found water.”
verses.kjv: And it came to pass the same day, that Isaac’s servants came, and told him concerning the well which they had digged, and said unto him, We have found water.
verses.bsb: On that same day, Isaac’s servants came and told him about the well they had dug. “We have found water!” they told him.

verses.esv: He called it Shibah; therefore the name of the city is Beersheba to this day.
verses.kjv: And he called it Shebah: therefore the name of the city is Beer-sheba unto this day.
verses.bsb: So he called it Shibah, and to this day the name of the city is Beersheba.

verses.esv: When Esau was forty years old, he took Judith the daughter of Beeri the Hittite to be his wife, and Basemath the daughter of Elon the Hittite,
verses.kjv: ¶ And Esau was forty years old when he took to wife Judith the daughter of Beeri the Hittite, and Bashemath the daughter of Elon the Hittite:
verses.bsb: When Esau was forty years old, he took as his wives Judith daughter of Beeri the Hittite and Basemath daughter of Elon the Hittite.

verses.esv: and they made life bitter for Isaac and Rebekah.
verses.kjv: Which were a grief of mind unto Isaac and to Rebekah.
verses.bsb: And they brought grief to Isaac and Rebekah.

verses.esv: When Isaac was old and his eyes were dim so that he could not see, he called Esau his older son and said to him, “My son”; and he answered, “Here I am.”
verses.kjv: AND it came to pass, that when Isaac was old, and his eyes were dim, so that he could not see, he called Esau his eldest son, and said unto him, My son: and he said unto him, Behold, here am I.
verses.bsb: When Isaac was old and his eyes were so weak that he could no longer see, he called his older son Esau and said to him, “My son.”  “Here I am,” Esau replied.

verses.esv: He said, “Behold, I am old; I do not know the day of my death.
verses.kjv: And he said, Behold now, I am old, I know not the day of my death:
verses.bsb: “Look,” said Isaac, “I am now old, and I do not know the day of my death.

verses.esv: Now then, take your weapons, your quiver and your bow, and go out to the field and hunt game for me,
verses.kjv: Now therefore take, I pray thee, thy weapons, thy quiver and thy bow, and go out to the field, and take me some venison;
verses.bsb: Take your weapons—your quiver and bow—and go out into the field to hunt some game for me.

verses.esv: and prepare for me delicious food, such as I love, and bring it to me so that I may eat, that my soul may bless you before I die.”
verses.kjv: And make me savoury meat, such as I love, and bring it to me, that I may eat; that my soul may bless thee before I die.
verses.bsb: Then prepare a tasty dish that I love and bring it to me to eat, so that I may bless you before I die.”

verses.esv: Now Rebekah was listening when Isaac spoke to his son Esau. So when Esau went to the field to hunt for game and bring it,
verses.kjv: And Rebekah heard when Isaac spake to Esau his son. And Esau went to the field to hunt for venison, and to bring it.
verses.bsb: Now Rebekah was listening to what Isaac told his son Esau. So when Esau went into the field to hunt game and bring it back,

verses.esv: Rebekah said to her son Jacob, “I heard your father speak to your brother Esau,
verses.kjv: ¶ And Rebekah spake unto Jacob her son, saying, Behold, I heard thy father speak unto Esau thy brother, saying,
verses.bsb: Rebekah said to her son Jacob, “Behold, I overheard your father saying to your brother Esau,

verses.esv: ‘Bring me game and prepare for me delicious food, that I may eat it and bless you before the LORD before I die.’
verses.kjv: Bring me venison, and make me savoury meat, that I may eat, and bless thee before the Lord before my death.
verses.bsb: ‘Bring me some game and prepare me a tasty dish to eat, so that I may bless you in the presence of the LORD before I die.’

verses.esv: Now therefore, my son, obey my voice as I command you.
verses.kjv: Now therefore, my son, obey my voice according to that which I command thee.
verses.bsb: Now, my son, listen to my voice and do exactly as I tell you.

verses.esv: Go to the flock and bring me two good young goats, so that I may prepare from them delicious food for your father, such as he loves.
verses.kjv: Go now to the flock, and fetch me from thence two good kids of the goats; and I will make them savoury meat for thy father, such as he loveth:
verses.bsb: Go out to the flock and bring me two choice young goats, so that I can make them into a tasty dish for your father—the kind he loves.

verses.esv: And you shall bring it to your father to eat, so that he may bless you before he dies.”
verses.kjv: And thou shalt bring it to thy father, that he may eat, and that he may bless thee before his death.
verses.bsb: Then take it to your father to eat, so that he may bless you before he dies.”

verses.esv: But Jacob said to Rebekah his mother, “Behold, my brother Esau is a hairy man, and I am a smooth man.
verses.kjv: And Jacob said to Rebekah his mother, Behold, Esau my brother is a hairy man, and I am a smooth man:
verses.bsb: Jacob answered his mother Rebekah, “Look, my brother Esau is a hairy man, but I am smooth-skinned.

verses.esv: Perhaps my father will feel me, and I shall seem to be mocking him and bring a curse upon myself and not a blessing.”
verses.kjv: My father peradventure will feel me, and I shall seem to him as a deceiver; and I shall bring a curse upon me, and not a blessing.
verses.bsb: What if my father touches me? Then I would be revealed to him as a deceiver, and I would bring upon myself a curse rather than a blessing.”

verses.esv: His mother said to him, “Let your curse be on me, my son; only obey my voice, and go, bring them to me.”
verses.kjv: And his mother said unto him, Upon me be thy curse, my son: only obey my voice, and go fetch me them.
verses.bsb: His mother replied, “Your curse be on me, my son. Just obey my voice and go get them for me.”

verses.esv: So he went and took them and brought them to his mother, and his mother prepared delicious food, such as his father loved.
verses.kjv: And he went, and fetched, and brought them to his mother: and his mother made savoury meat, such as his father loved.
verses.bsb: So Jacob went and got two goats and brought them to his mother, who made the tasty food his father loved.

verses.esv: Then Rebekah took the best garments of Esau her older son, which were with her in the house, and put them on Jacob her younger son.
verses.kjv: And Rebekah took goodly raiment of her eldest son Esau, which were with her in the house, and put them upon Jacob her younger son:
verses.bsb: And Rebekah took the finest clothes in the house that belonged to her older son Esau, and she put them on her younger son Jacob.

verses.esv: And the skins of the young goats she put on his hands and on the smooth part of his neck.
verses.kjv: And she put the skins of the kids of the goats upon his hands, and upon the smooth of his neck:
verses.bsb: She also put the skins of the young goats on his hands and on the smooth part of his neck.

verses.esv: And she put the delicious food and the bread, which she had prepared, into the hand of her son Jacob.
verses.kjv: And she gave the savoury meat and the bread, which she had prepared, into the hand of her son Jacob.
verses.bsb: Then she handed her son Jacob the tasty food and bread she had made.

verses.esv: So he went in to his father and said, “My father.” And he said, “Here I am. Who are you, my son?”
verses.kjv: ¶ And he came unto his father, and said, My father: and he said, Here am I; who art thou, my son?
verses.bsb: So Jacob went to his father and said, “My father.”  “Here I am!” he answered. “Which one are you, my son?”

verses.esv: Jacob said to his father, “I am Esau your firstborn. I have done as you told me; now sit up and eat of my game, that your soul may bless me.”
verses.kjv: And Jacob said unto his father, I am Esau thy firstborn; I have done according as thou badest me: arise, I pray thee, sit and eat of my venison, that thy soul may bless me.
verses.bsb: Jacob said to his father, “I am Esau, your firstborn. I have done as you told me. Please sit up and eat some of my game, so that you may bless me.”

verses.esv: But Isaac said to his son, “How is it that you have found it so quickly, my son?” He answered, “Because the LORD your God granted me success.”
verses.kjv: And Isaac said unto his son, How is it that thou hast found it so quickly, my son? And he said, Because the Lord thy God brought it to me.
verses.bsb: But Isaac asked his son, “How did you ever find it so quickly, my son?”  “Because the LORD your God brought it to me,” he replied.

verses.esv: Then Isaac said to Jacob, “Please come near, that I may feel you, my son, to know whether you are really my son Esau or not.”
verses.kjv: And Isaac said unto Jacob, Come near, I pray thee, that I may feel thee, my son, whether thou be my very son Esau or not.
verses.bsb: Then Isaac said to Jacob, “Please come closer so I can touch you, my son. Are you really my son Esau, or not?”

verses.esv: So Jacob went near to Isaac his father, who felt him and said, “The voice is Jacob's voice, but the hands are the hands of Esau.”
verses.kjv: And Jacob went near unto Isaac his father; and he felt him, and said, The voice is Jacob’s voice, but the hands are the hands of Esau.
verses.bsb: So Jacob came close to his father Isaac, who touched him and said, “The voice is the voice of Jacob, but the hands are the hands of Esau.”

verses.esv: And he did not recognize him, because his hands were hairy like his brother Esau's hands. So he blessed him.
verses.kjv: And he discerned him not, because his hands were hairy, as his brother Esau’s hands: so he blessed him.
verses.bsb: Isaac did not recognize him, because his hands were hairy like those of his brother Esau; so he blessed him.

verses.esv: He said, “Are you really my son Esau?” He answered, “I am.”
verses.kjv: And he said, Art thou my very son Esau? And he said, I am.
verses.bsb: Again he asked, “Are you really my son Esau?”  And he replied, “I am.”

verses.esv: Then he said, “Bring it near to me, that I may eat of my son's game and bless you.” So he brought it near to him, and he ate; and he brought him wine, and he drank.
verses.kjv: And he said, Bring it near to me, and I will eat of my son’s venison, that my soul may bless thee. And he brought it near to him, and he did eat: and he brought him wine, and he drank.
verses.bsb: “Serve me,” said Isaac, “and let me eat some of my son’s game, so that I may bless you.”  Jacob brought it to him, and he ate; then he brought him wine, and he drank.

verses.esv: Then his father Isaac said to him, “Come near and kiss me, my son.”
verses.kjv: And his father Isaac said unto him, Come near now, and kiss me, my son.
verses.bsb: Then his father Isaac said to him, “Please come near and kiss me, my son.”

verses.esv: So he came near and kissed him. And Isaac smelled the smell of his garments and blessed him and said, “See, the smell of my son is as the smell of a field that the LORD has blessed!
verses.kjv: And he came near, and kissed him: and he smelled the smell of his raiment, and blessed him, and said, See, the smell of my son is as the smell of a field which the Lord hath blessed:
verses.bsb: So he came near and kissed him. When Isaac smelled his clothing, he blessed him and said:  “Ah, the smell of my son is like the smell of a field that the LORD has blessed.

verses.esv: May God give you of the dew of heaven and of the fatness of the earth and plenty of grain and wine.
verses.kjv: Therefore God give thee of the dew of heaven, and the fatness of the earth, and plenty of corn and wine:
verses.bsb: May God give to you the dew of heaven and the richness of the earth— an abundance of grain and new wine.

verses.esv: Let peoples serve you, and nations bow down to you. Be lord over your brothers, and may your mother's sons bow down to you. Cursed be everyone who curses you, and blessed be everyone who blesses you!”
verses.kjv: Let people serve thee, and nations bow down to thee: be lord over thy brethren, and let thy mother’s sons bow down to thee: cursed be every one that curseth thee, and blessed be he that blesseth thee.
verses.bsb: May peoples serve you and nations bow down to you. May you be the master of your brothers, and may the sons of your mother bow down to you. May those who curse you be cursed, and those who bless you be blessed.”

verses.esv: As soon as Isaac had finished blessing Jacob, when Jacob had scarcely gone out from the presence of Isaac his father, Esau his brother came in from his hunting.
verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass, as soon as Isaac had made an end of blessing Jacob, and Jacob was yet scarce gone out from the presence of Isaac his father, that Esau his brother came in from his hunting.
verses.bsb: As soon as Isaac had finished blessing him and Jacob had left his father’s presence, his brother Esau returned from the hunt.

verses.esv: He also prepared delicious food and brought it to his father. And he said to his father, “Let my father arise and eat of his son's game, that you may bless me.”
verses.kjv: And he also had made savoury meat, and brought it unto his father, and said unto his father, Let my father arise, and eat of his son’s venison, that thy soul may bless me.
verses.bsb: He too made some tasty food, brought it to his father, and said to him, “My father, sit up and eat of your son’s game, so that you may bless me.”

verses.esv: His father Isaac said to him, “Who are you?” He answered, “I am your son, your firstborn, Esau.”
verses.kjv: And Isaac his father said unto him, Who art thou? And he said, I am thy son, thy firstborn Esau.
verses.bsb: But his father Isaac replied, “Who are you?”  “I am Esau, your firstborn son,” he answered.

verses.esv: Then Isaac trembled very violently and said, “Who was it then that hunted game and brought it to me, and I ate it all before you came, and I have blessed him? Yes, and he shall be blessed.”
verses.kjv: And Isaac trembled very exceedingly, and said, Who? where is he that hath taken venison, and brought it me, and I have eaten of all before thou camest, and have blessed him? yea, and he shall be blessed.
verses.bsb: Isaac began to tremble violently and said, “Who was it, then, who hunted the game and brought it to me? Before you came in, I ate it all and blessed him—and indeed, he will be blessed!”

verses.esv: As soon as Esau heard the words of his father, he cried out with an exceedingly great and bitter cry and said to his father, “Bless me, even me also, O my father!”
verses.kjv: And when Esau heard the words of his father, he cried with a great and exceeding bitter cry, and said unto his father, Bless me, even me also, O my father.
verses.bsb: When Esau heard his father’s words, he let out a loud and bitter cry and said to his father, “Bless me too, O my father!”

verses.esv: But he said, “Your brother came deceitfully, and he has taken away your blessing.”
verses.kjv: And he said, Thy brother came with subtilty, and hath taken away thy blessing.
verses.bsb: But Isaac replied, “Your brother came deceitfully and took your blessing.”

verses.esv: Esau said, “Is he not rightly named Jacob? For he has cheated me these two times. He took away my birthright, and behold, now he has taken away my blessing.” Then he said, “Have you not reserved a blessing for me?”
verses.kjv: And he said, Is not he rightly named Jacob? for he hath supplanted me these two times: he took away my birthright; and, behold, now he hath taken away my blessing. And he said, Hast thou not reserved a blessing for me?
verses.bsb: So Esau declared, “Is he not rightly named Jacob? For he has cheated me twice. He took my birthright, and now he has taken my blessing.” Then he asked, “Haven’t you saved a blessing for me?”

verses.esv: Isaac answered and said to Esau, “Behold, I have made him lord over you, and all his brothers I have given to him for servants, and with grain and wine I have sustained him. What then can I do for you, my son?”
verses.kjv: And Isaac answered and said unto Esau, Behold, I have made him thy lord, and all his brethren have I given to him for servants; and with corn and wine have I sustained him: and what shall I do now unto thee, my son?
verses.bsb: But Isaac answered Esau: “Look, I have made him your master and given him all his relatives as servants; I have sustained him with grain and new wine. What is left that I can do for you, my son?”

verses.esv: Esau said to his father, “Have you but one blessing, my father? Bless me, even me also, O my father.” And Esau lifted up his voice and wept.
verses.kjv: And Esau said unto his father, Hast thou but one blessing, my father? bless me, even me also, O my father. And Esau lifted up his voice, and wept.
verses.bsb: Esau said to his father, “Do you have only one blessing, my father? Bless me too, O my father!” Then Esau wept aloud.

verses.esv: Then Isaac his father answered and said to him: “Behold, away from the fatness of the earth shall your dwelling be, and away from the dew of heaven on high.
verses.kjv: And Isaac his father answered and said unto him, Behold, thy dwelling shall be the fatness of the earth, and of the dew of heaven from above;
verses.bsb: His father Isaac answered him:  “Behold, your dwelling place shall be away from the richness of the land, away from the dew of heaven above.

verses.esv: By your sword you shall live, and you shall serve your brother; but when you grow restless you shall break his yoke from your neck.”
verses.kjv: And by thy sword shalt thou live, and shalt serve thy brother; and it shall come to pass when thou shalt have the dominion, that thou shalt break his yoke from off thy neck.
verses.bsb: You shall live by the sword and serve your brother. But when you rebel, you will tear his yoke from your neck.”

verses.esv: Now Esau hated Jacob because of the blessing with which his father had blessed him, and Esau said to himself, “The days of mourning for my father are approaching; then I will kill my brother Jacob.”
verses.kjv: ¶ And Esau hated Jacob because of the blessing wherewith his father blessed him: and Esau said in his heart, The days of mourning for my father are at hand; then will I slay my brother Jacob.
verses.bsb: Esau held a grudge against Jacob because of the blessing his father had given him. And Esau said in his heart, “The days of mourning for my father are at hand; then I will kill my brother Jacob.”

verses.esv: But the words of Esau her older son were told to Rebekah. So she sent and called Jacob her younger son and said to him, “Behold, your brother Esau comforts himself about you by planning to kill you.
verses.kjv: And these words of Esau her elder son were told to Rebekah: and she sent and called Jacob her younger son, and said unto him, Behold, thy brother Esau, as touching thee, doth comfort himself, purposing to kill thee.
verses.bsb: When the words of her older son Esau were relayed to Rebekah, she sent for her younger son Jacob and told him, “Look, your brother Esau is consoling himself by plotting to kill you.

verses.esv: Now therefore, my son, obey my voice. Arise, flee to Laban my brother in Haran
verses.kjv: Now therefore, my son, obey my voice; and arise, flee thou to Laban my brother to Haran;
verses.bsb: So now, my son, obey my voice and flee at once to my brother Laban in Haran.

verses.esv: and stay with him a while, until your brother's fury turns away—
verses.kjv: And tarry with him a few days, until thy brother’s fury turn away;
verses.bsb: Stay with him for a while, until your brother’s fury subsides—

verses.esv: until your brother's anger turns away from you, and he forgets what you have done to him. Then I will send and bring you from there. Why should I be bereft of you both in one day?”
verses.kjv: Until thy brother’s anger turn away from thee, and he forget that which thou hast done to him: then I will send, and fetch thee from thence: why should I be deprived also of you both in one day?
verses.bsb: until your brother’s rage against you wanes and he forgets what you have done to him. Then I will send for you and bring you back from there. Why should I lose both of you in one day?”

verses.esv: Then Rebekah said to Isaac, “I loathe my life because of the Hittite women. If Jacob marries one of the Hittite women like these, one of the women of the land, what good will my life be to me?”
verses.kjv: And Rebekah said to Isaac, I am weary of my life because of the daughters of Heth: if Jacob take a wife of the daughters of Heth, such as these which are of the daughters of the land, what good shall my life do me?
verses.bsb: Then Rebekah said to Isaac, “I am weary of my life because of these Hittite women. If Jacob takes a Hittite wife from among them, what good is my life?”

verses.esv: Then Isaac called Jacob and blessed him and directed him, “You must not take a wife from the Canaanite women.
verses.kjv: AND Isaac called Jacob, and blessed him, and charged him, and said unto him, Thou shalt not take a wife of the daughters of Canaan.
verses.bsb: So Isaac called for Jacob and blessed him. “Do not take a wife from the Canaanite women,” he commanded.

verses.esv: Arise, go to Paddan-aram to the house of Bethuel your mother's father, and take as your wife from there one of the daughters of Laban your mother's brother.
verses.kjv: Arise, go to Padan-aram, to the house of Bethuel thy mother’s father; and take thee a wife from thence of the daughters of Laban thy mother’s brother.
verses.bsb: “Go at once to Paddan-aram, to the house of your mother’s father Bethuel, and take a wife from among the daughters of Laban, your mother’s brother.

verses.esv: God Almighty bless you and make you fruitful and multiply you, that you may become a company of peoples.
verses.kjv: And God Almighty bless thee, and make thee fruitful, and multiply thee, that thou mayest be a multitude of people;
verses.bsb: May God Almighty bless you and make you fruitful and multiply you, so that you may become a company of peoples.

verses.esv: May he give the blessing of Abraham to you and to your offspring with you, that you may take possession of the land of your sojournings that God gave to Abraham!”
verses.kjv: And give thee the blessing of Abraham, to thee, and to thy seed with thee; that thou mayest inherit the land wherein thou art a stranger, which God gave unto Abraham.
verses.bsb: And may He give the blessing of Abraham to you and your descendants, so that you may possess the land where you dwell as a foreigner, the land God gave to Abraham.”

verses.esv: Thus Isaac sent Jacob away. And he went to Paddan-aram, to Laban, the son of Bethuel the Aramean, the brother of Rebekah, Jacob's and Esau's mother.
verses.kjv: And Isaac sent away Jacob: and he went to Padan-aram unto Laban, son of Bethuel the Syrian, the brother of Rebekah, Jacob’s and Esau’s mother.
verses.bsb: So Isaac sent Jacob to Paddan-aram, to Laban son of Bethuel the Aramean, the brother of Rebekah, who was the mother of Jacob and Esau.

verses.esv: Now Esau saw that Isaac had blessed Jacob and sent him away to Paddan-aram to take a wife from there, and that as he blessed him he directed him, “You must not take a wife from the Canaanite women,”
verses.kjv: ¶ When Esau saw that Isaac had blessed Jacob, and sent him away to Padan-aram, to take him a wife from thence; and that as he blessed him he gave him a charge, saying, Thou shalt not take a wife of the daughters of Canaan;
verses.bsb: Now Esau learned that Isaac had blessed Jacob and sent him to Paddan-aram to take a wife there, commanding him, “Do not marry a Canaanite woman,”

verses.esv: and that Jacob had obeyed his father and his mother and gone to Paddan-aram.
verses.kjv: And that Jacob obeyed his father and his mother, and was gone to Padan-aram;
verses.bsb: and that Jacob had obeyed his father and mother and gone to Paddan-aram.

verses.esv: So when Esau saw that the Canaanite women did not please Isaac his father,
verses.kjv: And Esau seeing that the daughters of Canaan pleased not Isaac his father;
verses.bsb: And seeing that his father Isaac disapproved of the Canaanite women,

verses.esv: Esau went to Ishmael and took as his wife, besides the wives he had, Mahalath the daughter of Ishmael, Abraham's son, the sister of Nebaioth.
verses.kjv: Then went Esau unto Ishmael, and took unto the wives which he had Mahalath the daughter of Ishmael Abraham’s son, the sister of Nebajoth, to be his wife.
verses.bsb: Esau went to Ishmael and married Mahalath, the sister of Nebaioth and daughter of Abraham’s son Ishmael, in addition to the wives he already had.

verses.esv: Jacob left Beersheba and went toward Haran.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Jacob went out from Beer-sheba, and went toward Haran.
verses.bsb: Meanwhile Jacob left Beersheba and set out for Haran.

verses.esv: And he came to a certain place and stayed there that night, because the sun had set. Taking one of the stones of the place, he put it under his head and lay down in that place to sleep.
verses.kjv: And he lighted upon a certain place, and tarried there all night, because the sun was set; and he took of the stones of that place, and put them for his pillows, and lay down in that place to sleep.
verses.bsb: On reaching a certain place, he spent the night there because the sun had set. And taking one of the stones from that place, he put it under his head and lay down to sleep.

verses.esv: And he dreamed, and behold, there was a ladder set up on the earth, and the top of it reached to heaven. And behold, the angels of God were ascending and descending on it!
verses.kjv: And he dreamed, and behold a ladder set up on the earth, and the top of it reached to heaven: and behold the angels of God ascending and descending on it.
verses.bsb: And Jacob had a dream about a ladder that rested on the earth with its top reaching up to heaven, and God’s angels were going up and down the ladder.

verses.esv: And behold, the LORD stood above it and said, “I am the LORD, the God of Abraham your father and the God of Isaac. The land on which you lie I will give to you and to your offspring.
verses.kjv: And, behold, the Lord stood above it, and said, I am the Lord God of Abraham thy father, and the God of Isaac: the land whereon thou liest, to thee will I give it, and to thy seed;
verses.bsb: And there at the top the LORD was standing and saying, “I am the LORD, the God of your father Abraham and the God of Isaac. I will give you and your descendants the land on which you now lie.

verses.esv: Your offspring shall be like the dust of the earth, and you shall spread abroad to the west and to the east and to the north and to the south, and in you and your offspring shall all the families of the earth be blessed.
verses.kjv: And thy seed shall be as the dust of the earth, and thou shalt spread abroad to the west, and to the east, and to the north, and to the south: and in thee and in thy seed shall all the families of the earth be blessed.
verses.bsb: Your descendants will be like the dust of the earth, and you will spread out to the west and east and north and south. All the families of the earth will be blessed through you and your offspring.

verses.esv: Behold, I am with you and will keep you wherever you go, and will bring you back to this land. For I will not leave you until I have done what I have promised you.”
verses.kjv: And, behold, I am with thee, and will keep thee in all places whither thou goest, and will bring thee again into this land; for I will not leave thee, until I have done that which I have spoken to thee of.
verses.bsb: Look, I am with you, and I will watch over you wherever you go, and I will bring you back to this land. For I will not leave you until I have done what I have promised you.”

verses.esv: Then Jacob awoke from his sleep and said, “Surely the LORD is in this place, and I did not know it.”
verses.kjv: ¶ And Jacob awaked out of his sleep, and he said, Surely the Lord is in this place; and I knew it not.
verses.bsb: When Jacob woke up, he thought, “Surely the LORD is in this place, and I was unaware of it.”

verses.esv: And he was afraid and said, “How awesome is this place! This is none other than the house of God, and this is the gate of heaven.”
verses.kjv: And he was afraid, and said, How dreadful is this place! this is none other but the house of God, and this is the gate of heaven.
verses.bsb: And he was afraid and said, “How awesome is this place! This is none other than the house of God; this is the gate of heaven!”

verses.esv: So early in the morning Jacob took the stone that he had put under his head and set it up for a pillar and poured oil on the top of it.
verses.kjv: And Jacob rose up early in the morning, and took the stone that he had put for his pillows, and set it up for a pillar, and poured oil upon the top of it.
verses.bsb: Early the next morning, Jacob took the stone that he had placed under his head, and he set it up as a pillar. He poured oil on top of it,

verses.esv: He called the name of that place Bethel, but the name of the city was Luz at the first.
verses.kjv: And he called the name of that place Beth-el: but the name of that city was called Luz at the first.
verses.bsb: and he called that place Bethel, though previously the city had been named Luz.

verses.esv: Then Jacob made a vow, saying, “If God will be with me and will keep me in this way that I go, and will give me bread to eat and clothing to wear,
verses.kjv: And Jacob vowed a vow, saying, If God will be with me, and will keep me in this way that I go, and will give me bread to eat, and raiment to put on,
verses.bsb: Then Jacob made a vow, saying, “If God will be with me and watch over me on this journey, and if He will provide me with food to eat and clothes to wear,

verses.esv: so that I come again to my father's house in peace, then the LORD shall be my God,
verses.kjv: So that I come again to my father’s house in peace; then shall the Lord be my God:
verses.bsb: so that I may return safely to my father’s house, then the LORD will be my God.

verses.esv: and this stone, which I have set up for a pillar, shall be God's house. And of all that you give me I will give a full tenth to you.”
verses.kjv: And this stone, which I have set for a pillar, shall be God’s house: and of all that thou shalt give me I will surely give the tenth unto thee.
verses.bsb: And this stone I have set up as a pillar will be God’s house, and of all that You give me I will surely give You a tenth.”

verses.esv: Then Jacob went on his journey and came to the land of the people of the east.
verses.kjv: THEN Jacob went on his journey, and came into the land of the people of the east.
verses.bsb: Jacob resumed his journey and came to the land of the people of the east.

verses.esv: As he looked, he saw a well in the field, and behold, three flocks of sheep lying beside it, for out of that well the flocks were watered. The stone on the well's mouth was large,
