verses.kjv: And he looked, and behold a well in the field, and, lo, there were three flocks of sheep lying by it; for out of that well they watered the flocks: and a great stone was upon the well’s mouth.
verses.bsb: He looked and saw a well in the field, and near it lay three flocks of sheep, because the sheep were watered from this well. And a large stone covered the mouth of the well.

verses.esv: and when all the flocks were gathered there, the shepherds would roll the stone from the mouth of the well and water the sheep, and put the stone back in its place over the mouth of the well.
verses.kjv: And thither were all the flocks gathered: and they rolled the stone from the well’s mouth, and watered the sheep, and put the stone again upon the well’s mouth in his place.
verses.bsb: When all the flocks had been gathered there, the shepherds would roll away the stone from the mouth of the well and water the sheep. Then they would return the stone to its place over the mouth of the well.

verses.esv: Jacob said to them, “My brothers, where do you come from?” They said, “We are from Haran.”
verses.kjv: And Jacob said unto them, My brethren, whence be ye? And they said, Of Haran are we.
verses.bsb: “My brothers,” Jacob asked the shepherds, “where are you from?”  “We are from Haran,” they answered.

verses.esv: He said to them, “Do you know Laban the son of Nahor?” They said, “We know him.”
verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Know ye Laban the son of Nahor? And they said, We know him.
verses.bsb: “Do you know Laban the grandson of Nahor?” Jacob asked.  “We know him,” they replied.

verses.esv: He said to them, “Is it well with him?” They said, “It is well; and see, Rachel his daughter is coming with the sheep!”
verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Is he well? And they said, He is well: and, behold, Rachel his daughter cometh with the sheep.
verses.bsb: “Is he well?” Jacob inquired.  “Yes,” they answered, “and here comes his daughter Rachel with his sheep.”

verses.esv: He said, “Behold, it is still high day; it is not time for the livestock to be gathered together. Water the sheep and go, pasture them.”
verses.kjv: And he said, Lo, it is yet high day, neither is it time that the cattle should be gathered together: water ye the sheep, and go and feed them.
verses.bsb: “Look,” said Jacob, “it is still broad daylight; it is not yet time to gather the livestock. Water the sheep and take them back to pasture.”

verses.esv: But they said, “We cannot until all the flocks are gathered together and the stone is rolled from the mouth of the well; then we water the sheep.”
verses.kjv: And they said, We cannot, until all the flocks be gathered together, and till they roll the stone from the well’s mouth; then we water the sheep.
verses.bsb: But they replied, “We cannot, until all the flocks have been gathered and the stone has been rolled away from the mouth of the well. Then we will water the sheep.”

verses.esv: While he was still speaking with them, Rachel came with her father's sheep, for she was a shepherdess.
verses.kjv: ¶ And while he yet spake with them, Rachel came with her father’s sheep: for she kept them.
verses.bsb: While he was still speaking with them, Rachel arrived with her father’s sheep, for she was a shepherdess.

verses.esv: Now as soon as Jacob saw Rachel the daughter of Laban his mother's brother, and the sheep of Laban his mother's brother, Jacob came near and rolled the stone from the well's mouth and watered the flock of Laban his mother's brother.
verses.kjv: And it came to pass, when Jacob saw Rachel the daughter of Laban his mother’s brother, and the sheep of Laban his mother’s brother, that Jacob went near, and rolled the stone from the well’s mouth, and watered the flock of Laban his mother’s brother.
verses.bsb: As soon as Jacob saw Rachel, the daughter of his mother’s brother Laban, with Laban’s sheep, he went up and rolled the stone away from the mouth of the well and watered his uncle’s sheep.

verses.esv: Then Jacob kissed Rachel and wept aloud.
verses.kjv: And Jacob kissed Rachel, and lifted up his voice, and wept.
verses.bsb: Then Jacob kissed Rachel and wept aloud.

verses.esv: And Jacob told Rachel that he was her father's kinsman, and that he was Rebekah's son, and she ran and told her father.
verses.kjv: And Jacob told Rachel that he was her father’s brother, and that he was Rebekah’s son: and she ran and told her father.
verses.bsb: He told Rachel that he was Rebekah’s son, a relative of her father, and she ran and told her father.

verses.esv: As soon as Laban heard the news about Jacob, his sister's son, he ran to meet him and embraced him and kissed him and brought him to his house. Jacob told Laban all these things,
verses.kjv: And it came to pass, when Laban heard the tidings of Jacob his sister’s son, that he ran to meet him, and embraced him, and kissed him, and brought him to his house. And he told Laban all these things.
verses.bsb: When Laban heard the news about his sister’s son Jacob, he ran out to meet him. He embraced him and kissed him and brought him to his home, where Jacob told him all that had happened.

verses.esv: and Laban said to him, “Surely you are my bone and my flesh!” And he stayed with him a month.
verses.kjv: And Laban said to him, Surely thou art my bone and my flesh. And he abode with him the space of a month.
verses.bsb: Then Laban declared, “You are indeed my own flesh and blood.”  After Jacob had stayed with him a month,

verses.esv: Then Laban said to Jacob, “Because you are my kinsman, should you therefore serve me for nothing? Tell me, what shall your wages be?”
verses.kjv: ¶ And Laban said unto Jacob, Because thou art my brother, shouldest thou therefore serve me for nought? tell me, what shall thy wages be?
verses.bsb: Laban said to him, “Just because you are my relative, should you work for nothing? Tell me what your wages should be.”

verses.esv: Now Laban had two daughters. The name of the older was Leah, and the name of the younger was Rachel.
verses.kjv: And Laban had two daughters: the name of the elder was Leah, and the name of the younger was Rachel.
verses.bsb: Now Laban had two daughters; the older was named Leah, and the younger was named Rachel.

verses.esv: Leah's eyes were weak, but Rachel was beautiful in form and appearance.
verses.kjv: Leah was tender eyed; but Rachel was beautiful and well favoured.
verses.bsb: Leah had weak eyes, but Rachel was shapely and beautiful.

verses.esv: Jacob loved Rachel. And he said, “I will serve you seven years for your younger daughter Rachel.”
verses.kjv: And Jacob loved Rachel; and said, I will serve thee seven years for Rachel thy younger daughter.
verses.bsb: Since Jacob loved Rachel, he answered, “I will serve you seven years for your younger daughter Rachel.”

verses.esv: Laban said, “It is better that I give her to you than that I should give her to any other man; stay with me.”
verses.kjv: And Laban said, It is better that I give her to thee, than that I should give her to another man: abide with me.
verses.bsb: Laban replied, “Better that I give her to you than to another. Stay here with me.”

verses.esv: So Jacob served seven years for Rachel, and they seemed to him but a few days because of the love he had for her.
verses.kjv: And Jacob served seven years for Rachel; and they seemed unto him but a few days, for the love he had to her.
verses.bsb: So Jacob served seven years for Rachel, yet it seemed but a few days because of his love for her.

verses.esv: Then Jacob said to Laban, “Give me my wife that I may go in to her, for my time is completed.”
verses.kjv: ¶ And Jacob said unto Laban, Give me my wife, for my days are fulfilled, that I may go in unto her.
verses.bsb: Finally Jacob said to Laban, “Grant me my wife, for my time is complete, and I want to sleep with her.”

verses.esv: So Laban gathered together all the people of the place and made a feast.
verses.kjv: And Laban gathered together all the men of the place, and made a feast.
verses.bsb: So Laban invited all the men of that place and prepared a feast.

verses.esv: But in the evening he took his daughter Leah and brought her to Jacob, and he went in to her.
verses.kjv: And it came to pass in the evening, that he took Leah his daughter, and brought her to him; and he went in unto her.
verses.bsb: But when evening came, Laban took his daughter Leah and gave her to Jacob, and he slept with her.

verses.esv: (Laban gave his female servant Zilpah to his daughter Leah to be her servant.)
verses.kjv: And Laban gave unto his daughter Leah Zilpah his maid for an handmaid.
verses.bsb: And Laban gave his servant girl Zilpah to his daughter Leah as her maidservant.

verses.esv: And in the morning, behold, it was Leah! And Jacob said to Laban, “What is this you have done to me? Did I not serve with you for Rachel? Why then have you deceived me?”
verses.kjv: And it came to pass, that in the morning, behold, it was Leah: and he said to Laban, What is this thou hast done unto me? did not I serve with thee for Rachel? wherefore then hast thou beguiled me?
verses.bsb: When morning came, there was Leah! “What have you done to me?” Jacob said to Laban. “Wasn’t it for Rachel that I served you? Why have you deceived me?”

verses.esv: Laban said, “It is not so done in our country, to give the younger before the firstborn.
verses.kjv: And Laban said, It must not be so done in our country, to give the younger before the firstborn.
verses.bsb: Laban replied, “It is not our custom here to give the younger daughter in marriage before the older.

verses.esv: Complete the week of this one, and we will give you the other also in return for serving me another seven years.”
verses.kjv: Fulfil her week, and we will give thee this also for the service which thou shalt serve with me yet seven other years.
verses.bsb: Finish this week’s celebration, and we will give you the younger one in return for another seven years of work.”

verses.esv: Jacob did so, and completed her week. Then Laban gave him his daughter Rachel to be his wife.
verses.kjv: And Jacob did so, and fulfilled her week: and he gave him Rachel his daughter to wife also.
verses.bsb: And Jacob did just that. He finished the week’s celebration, and Laban gave him his daughter Rachel as his wife.

verses.esv: (Laban gave his female servant Bilhah to his daughter Rachel to be her servant.)
verses.kjv: And Laban gave to Rachel his daughter Bilhah his handmaid to be her maid.
verses.bsb: Laban also gave his servant girl Bilhah to his daughter Rachel as her maidservant.

verses.esv: So Jacob went in to Rachel also, and he loved Rachel more than Leah, and served Laban for another seven years.
verses.kjv: And he went in also unto Rachel, and he loved also Rachel more than Leah, and served with him yet seven other years.
verses.bsb: Jacob slept with Rachel as well, and indeed, he loved Rachel more than Leah. So he worked for Laban another seven years.

verses.esv: When the LORD saw that Leah was hated, he opened her womb, but Rachel was barren.
verses.kjv: ¶ And when the Lord saw that Leah was hated, he opened her womb: but Rachel was barren.
verses.bsb: When the LORD saw that Leah was unloved, He opened her womb; but Rachel was barren.

verses.esv: And Leah conceived and bore a son, and she called his name Reuben, for she said, “Because the LORD has looked upon my affliction; for now my husband will love me.”
verses.kjv: And Leah conceived, and bare a son, and she called his name Reuben: for she said, Surely the Lord hath looked upon my affliction; now therefore my husband will love me.
verses.bsb: And Leah conceived and gave birth to a son, and she named him Reuben, for she said, “The LORD has seen my affliction. Surely my husband will love me now.”

verses.esv: She conceived again and bore a son, and said, “Because the LORD has heard that I am hated, he has given me this son also.” And she called his name Simeon.
verses.kjv: And she conceived again, and bare a son; and said, Because the Lord hath heard that I was hated, he hath therefore given me this son also: and she called his name Simeon.
verses.bsb: Again she conceived and gave birth to a son, and she said, “Because the LORD has heard that I am unloved, He has given me this son as well.” So she named him Simeon.

verses.esv: Again she conceived and bore a son, and said, “Now this time my husband will be attached to me, because I have borne him three sons.” Therefore his name was called Levi.
verses.kjv: And she conceived again, and bare a son; and said, Now this time will my husband be joined unto me, because I have born him three sons: therefore was his name called Levi.
verses.bsb: Once again Leah conceived and gave birth to a son, and she said, “Now at last my husband will become attached to me, because I have borne him three sons.” So he was named Levi.

verses.esv: And she conceived again and bore a son, and said, “This time I will praise the LORD.” Therefore she called his name Judah. Then she ceased bearing.
verses.kjv: And she conceived again, and bare a son: and she said, Now will I praise the Lord: therefore she called his name Judah; and left bearing.
verses.bsb: And once more she conceived and gave birth to a son and said, “This time I will praise the LORD.” So she named him Judah. Then Leah stopped having children.

verses.esv: When Rachel saw that she bore Jacob no children, she envied her sister. She said to Jacob, “Give me children, or I shall die!”
verses.kjv: AND when Rachel saw that she bare Jacob no children, Rachel envied her sister; and said unto Jacob, Give me children, or else I die.
verses.bsb: When Rachel saw that she was not bearing any children for Jacob, she envied her sister. “Give me children, or I will die!” she said to Jacob.

verses.esv: Jacob's anger was kindled against Rachel, and he said, “Am I in the place of God, who has withheld from you the fruit of the womb?”
verses.kjv: And Jacob’s anger was kindled against Rachel: and he said, Am I in God’s stead, who hath withheld from thee the fruit of the womb?
verses.bsb: Jacob became angry with Rachel and said, “Am I in the place of God, who has withheld children from you?”

verses.esv: Then she said, “Here is my servant Bilhah; go in to her, so that she may give birth on my behalf, that even I may have children through her.”
verses.kjv: And she said, Behold my maid Bilhah, go in unto her; and she shall bear upon my knees, that I may also have children by her.
verses.bsb: Then she said, “Here is my maidservant Bilhah. Sleep with her, that she may bear children for me, so that through her I too can build a family.”

verses.esv: So she gave him her servant Bilhah as a wife, and Jacob went in to her.
verses.kjv: And she gave him Bilhah her handmaid to wife: and Jacob went in unto her.
verses.bsb: So Rachel gave Jacob her servant Bilhah as a wife, and he slept with her,

verses.esv: And Bilhah conceived and bore Jacob a son.
verses.kjv: And Bilhah conceived, and bare Jacob a son.
verses.bsb: and Bilhah conceived and bore him a son.

verses.esv: Then Rachel said, “God has judged me, and has also heard my voice and given me a son.” Therefore she called his name Dan.
verses.kjv: And Rachel said, God hath judged me, and hath also heard my voice, and hath given me a son: therefore called she his name Dan.
verses.bsb: Then Rachel said, “God has vindicated me; He has heard my plea and given me a son.” So she named him Dan.

verses.esv: Rachel's servant Bilhah conceived again and bore Jacob a second son.
verses.kjv: And Bilhah Rachel’s maid conceived again, and bare Jacob a second son.
verses.bsb: And Rachel’s servant Bilhah conceived again and bore Jacob a second son.

verses.esv: Then Rachel said, “With mighty wrestlings I have wrestled with my sister and have prevailed.” So she called his name Naphtali.
verses.kjv: And Rachel said, With great wrestlings have I wrestled with my sister, and I have prevailed: and she called his name Naphtali.
verses.bsb: Then Rachel said, “In my great struggles, I have wrestled with my sister and won.” So she named him Naphtali.

verses.esv: When Leah saw that she had ceased bearing children, she took her servant Zilpah and gave her to Jacob as a wife.
verses.kjv: When Leah saw that she had left bearing, she took Zilpah her maid, and gave her Jacob to wife.
verses.bsb: When Leah saw that she had stopped having children, she gave her servant Zilpah to Jacob as a wife.

verses.esv: Then Leah's servant Zilpah bore Jacob a son.
verses.kjv: And Zilpah Leah’s maid bare Jacob a son.
verses.bsb: And Leah’s servant Zilpah bore Jacob a son.

verses.esv: And Leah said, “Good fortune has come!” so she called his name Gad.
verses.kjv: And Leah said, A troop cometh: and she called his name Gad.
verses.bsb: Then Leah said, “How fortunate!” So she named him Gad.

verses.esv: Leah's servant Zilpah bore Jacob a second son.
verses.kjv: And Zilpah Leah’s maid bare Jacob a second son.
verses.bsb: When Leah’s servant Zilpah bore Jacob a second son,

verses.esv: And Leah said, “Happy am I! For women have called me happy.” So she called his name Asher.
verses.kjv: And Leah said, Happy am I, for the daughters will call me blessed: and she called his name Asher.
verses.bsb: Leah said, “How happy I am! For the women call me happy.” So she named him Asher.

verses.esv: In the days of wheat harvest Reuben went and found mandrakes in the field and brought them to his mother Leah. Then Rachel said to Leah, “Please give me some of your son's mandrakes.”
verses.kjv: ¶ And Reuben went in the days of wheat harvest, and found mandrakes in the field, and brought them unto his mother Leah. Then Rachel said to Leah, Give me, I pray thee, of thy son’s mandrakes.
verses.bsb: Now during the wheat harvest, Reuben went out and found some mandrakes in the field. When he brought them to his mother, Rachel begged Leah, “Please give me some of your son’s mandrakes.”

verses.esv: But she said to her, “Is it a small matter that you have taken away my husband? Would you take away my son's mandrakes also?” Rachel said, “Then he may lie with you tonight in exchange for your son's mandrakes.”
verses.kjv: And she said unto her, Is it a small matter that thou hast taken my husband? and wouldest thou take away my son’s mandrakes also? And Rachel said, Therefore he shall lie with thee to night for thy son’s mandrakes.
verses.bsb: But Leah replied, “Is it not enough that you have taken away my husband? Now you want to take my son’s mandrakes as well?”  “Very well,” said Rachel, “he may sleep with you tonight in exchange for your son’s mandrakes.”

verses.esv: When Jacob came from the field in the evening, Leah went out to meet him and said, “You must come in to me, for I have hired you with my son's mandrakes.” So he lay with her that night.
verses.kjv: And Jacob came out of the field in the evening, and Leah went out to meet him, and said, Thou must come in unto me; for surely I have hired thee with my son’s mandrakes. And he lay with her that night.
verses.bsb: When Jacob came in from the field that evening, Leah went out to meet him and said, “You must come with me, for I have hired you with my son’s mandrakes.” So he slept with her that night.

verses.esv: And God listened to Leah, and she conceived and bore Jacob a fifth son.
verses.kjv: And God hearkened unto Leah, and she conceived, and bare Jacob the fifth son.
verses.bsb: And God listened to Leah, and she conceived and bore a fifth son to Jacob.

verses.esv: Leah said, “God has given me my wages because I gave my servant to my husband.” So she called his name Issachar.
verses.kjv: And Leah said, God hath given me my hire, because I have given my maiden to my husband: and she called his name Issachar.
verses.bsb: Then Leah said, “God has rewarded me for giving my maidservant to my husband.” So she named him Issachar.

verses.esv: And Leah conceived again, and she bore Jacob a sixth son.
verses.kjv: And Leah conceived again, and bare Jacob the sixth son.
verses.bsb: Again Leah conceived and bore a sixth son to Jacob.

verses.esv: Then Leah said, “God has endowed me with a good endowment; now my husband will honor me, because I have borne him six sons.” So she called his name Zebulun.
verses.kjv: And Leah said, God hath endued me with a good dowry; now will my husband dwell with me, because I have born him six sons: and she called his name Zebulun.
verses.bsb: “God has given me a good gift,” she said. “This time my husband will honor me, because I have borne him six sons.” And she named him Zebulun.

verses.esv: Afterward she bore a daughter and called her name Dinah.
verses.kjv: And afterwards she bare a daughter, and called her name Dinah.
verses.bsb: After that, Leah gave birth to a daughter and named her Dinah.

verses.esv: Then God remembered Rachel, and God listened to her and opened her womb.
verses.kjv: ¶ And God remembered Rachel, and God hearkened to her, and opened her womb.
verses.bsb: Then God remembered Rachel. He listened to her and opened her womb,

verses.esv: She conceived and bore a son and said, “God has taken away my reproach.”
verses.kjv: And she conceived, and bare a son; and said, God hath taken away my reproach:
verses.bsb: and she conceived and gave birth to a son. “God has taken away my shame,” she said.

verses.esv: And she called his name Joseph, saying, “May the LORD add to me another son!”
verses.kjv: And she called his name Joseph; and said, The Lord shall add to me another son.
verses.bsb: She named him Joseph, and said, “May the LORD add to me another son.”

verses.esv: As soon as Rachel had borne Joseph, Jacob said to Laban, “Send me away, that I may go to my own home and country.
verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass, when Rachel had born Joseph, that Jacob said unto Laban, Send me away, that I may go unto mine own place, and to my country.
verses.bsb: Now after Rachel had given birth to Joseph, Jacob said to Laban, “Send me on my way so I can return to my homeland.

verses.esv: Give me my wives and my children for whom I have served you, that I may go, for you know the service that I have given you.”
verses.kjv: Give me my wives and my children, for whom I have served thee, and let me go: for thou knowest my service which I have done thee.
verses.bsb: Give me my wives and children for whom I have served you, that I may go on my way. You know how hard I have worked for you.”

verses.esv: But Laban said to him, “If I have found favor in your sight, I have learned by divination that the LORD has blessed me because of you.
verses.kjv: And Laban said unto him, I pray thee, if I have found favour in thine eyes, tarry: for I have learned by experience that the Lord hath blessed me for thy sake.
verses.bsb: But Laban replied, “If I have found favor in your eyes, please stay. I have learned by divination that the LORD has blessed me because of you.”

verses.esv: Name your wages, and I will give it.”
verses.kjv: And he said, Appoint me thy wages, and I will give it.
verses.bsb: And he added, “Name your wages, and I will pay them.”

verses.esv: Jacob said to him, “You yourself know how I have served you, and how your livestock has fared with me.
verses.kjv: And he said unto him, Thou knowest how I have served thee, and how thy cattle was with me.
verses.bsb: Then Jacob answered, “You know how I have served you and how your livestock have thrived under my care.

verses.esv: For you had little before I came, and it has increased abundantly, and the LORD has blessed you wherever I turned. But now when shall I provide for my own household also?”
verses.kjv: For it was little which thou hadst before I came, and it is now increased unto a multitude; and the Lord hath blessed thee since my coming: and now when shall I provide for mine own house also?
verses.bsb: Indeed, you had very little before my arrival, but now your wealth has increased many times over. The LORD has blessed you wherever I set foot. But now, when may I also provide for my own household?”

verses.esv: He said, “What shall I give you?” Jacob said, “You shall not give me anything. If you will do this for me, I will again pasture your flock and keep it:
verses.kjv: And he said, What shall I give thee? And Jacob said, Thou shalt not give me any thing: if thou wilt do this thing for me, I will again feed and keep thy flock:
verses.bsb: “What can I give you?” Laban asked.  “You do not need to give me anything,” Jacob replied. “If you do this one thing for me, I will keep on shepherding and keeping your flocks.

verses.esv: let me pass through all your flock today, removing from it every speckled and spotted sheep and every black lamb, and the spotted and speckled among the goats, and they shall be my wages.
verses.kjv: I will pass through all thy flock to day, removing from thence all the speckled and spotted cattle, and all the brown cattle among the sheep, and the spotted and speckled among the goats: and of such shall be my hire.
verses.bsb: Let me go through all your flocks today and remove from them every speckled or spotted sheep, every dark-colored lamb, and every spotted or speckled goat. These will be my wages.

verses.esv: So my honesty will answer for me later, when you come to look into my wages with you. Every one that is not speckled and spotted among the goats and black among the lambs, if found with me, shall be counted stolen.”
verses.kjv: So shall my righteousness answer for me in time to come, when it shall come for my hire before thy face: every one that is not speckled and spotted among the goats, and brown among the sheep, that shall be counted stolen with me.
verses.bsb: So my honesty will testify for me when you come to check on my wages in the future. If I have any goats that are not speckled or spotted, or any lambs that are not dark-colored, they will be considered stolen.”

verses.esv: Laban said, “Good! Let it be as you have said.”
verses.kjv: And Laban said, Behold, I would it might be according to thy word.
verses.bsb: “Agreed,” said Laban. “Let it be as you have said.”

verses.esv: But that day Laban removed the male goats that were striped and spotted, and all the female goats that were speckled and spotted, every one that had white on it, and every lamb that was black, and put them in the charge of his sons.
verses.kjv: And he removed that day the he goats that were ringstraked and spotted, and all the she goats that were speckled and spotted, and every one that had some white in it, and all the brown among the sheep, and gave them into the hand of his sons.
verses.bsb: That very day Laban removed all the streaked or spotted male goats and every speckled or spotted female goat—every one that had any white on it—and every dark-colored lamb, and he placed them under the care of his sons.

verses.esv: And he set a distance of three days' journey between himself and Jacob, and Jacob pastured the rest of Laban's flock.
verses.kjv: And he set three days’ journey betwixt himself and Jacob: and Jacob fed the rest of Laban’s flocks.
verses.bsb: Then he put a three-day journey between himself and Jacob, while Jacob was shepherding the rest of Laban’s flocks.

verses.esv: Then Jacob took fresh sticks of poplar and almond and plane trees, and peeled white streaks in them, exposing the white of the sticks.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Jacob took him rods of green poplar, and of the hazel and chesnut tree; and pilled white strakes in them, and made the white appear which was in the rods.
verses.bsb: Jacob, however, took fresh branches of poplar, almond, and plane trees, and peeled the bark, exposing the white inner wood of the branches.

verses.esv: He set the sticks that he had peeled in front of the flocks in the troughs, that is, the watering places, where the flocks came to drink. And since they bred when they came to drink,
verses.kjv: And he set the rods which he had pilled before the flocks in the gutters in the watering troughs when the flocks came to drink, that they should conceive when they came to drink.
verses.bsb: Then he set the peeled branches in the watering troughs in front of the flocks coming in to drink. So when the flocks were in heat and came to drink,

verses.esv: the flocks bred in front of the sticks and so the flocks brought forth striped, speckled, and spotted.
verses.kjv: And the flocks conceived before the rods, and brought forth cattle ringstraked, speckled, and spotted.
verses.bsb: they mated in front of the branches. And they bore young that were streaked or speckled or spotted.

verses.esv: And Jacob separated the lambs and set the faces of the flocks toward the striped and all the black in the flock of Laban. He put his own droves apart and did not put them with Laban's flock.
verses.kjv: And Jacob did separate the lambs, and set the faces of the flocks toward the ringstraked, and all the brown in the flock of Laban; and he put his own flocks by themselves, and put them not unto Laban’s cattle.
verses.bsb: Jacob set apart the young, but made the rest face the streaked dark-colored sheep in Laban’s flocks. Then he set his own stock apart and did not put them with Laban’s animals.

verses.esv: Whenever the stronger of the flock were breeding, Jacob would lay the sticks in the troughs before the eyes of the flock, that they might breed among the sticks,
verses.kjv: And it came to pass, whensoever the stronger cattle did conceive, that Jacob laid the rods before the eyes of the cattle in the gutters, that they might conceive among the rods.
verses.bsb: Whenever the stronger females of the flock were in heat, Jacob would place the branches in the troughs, in full view of the animals, so that they would breed in front of the branches.

verses.esv: but for the feebler of the flock he would not lay them there. So the feebler would be Laban's, and the stronger Jacob's.
verses.kjv: But when the cattle were feeble, he put them not in: so the feebler were Laban’s, and the stronger Jacob’s.
verses.bsb: But if the animals were weak, he did not set out the branches. So the weaker animals went to Laban and the stronger ones to Jacob.

verses.esv: Thus the man increased greatly and had large flocks, female servants and male servants, and camels and donkeys.
verses.kjv: And the man increased exceedingly, and had much cattle, and maidservants, and menservants, and camels, and asses.
verses.bsb: Thus Jacob became exceedingly prosperous. He owned large flocks, maidservants and menservants, and camels and donkeys.

verses.esv: Now Jacob heard that the sons of Laban were saying, “Jacob has taken all that was our father's, and from what was our father's he has gained all this wealth.”
verses.kjv: AND he heard the words of Laban’s sons, saying, Jacob hath taken away all that was our father’s; and of that which was our father’s hath he gotten all this glory.
verses.bsb: Now Jacob heard that Laban’s sons were saying, “Jacob has taken away all that belonged to our father and built all this wealth at our father’s expense.”

verses.esv: And Jacob saw that Laban did not regard him with favor as before.
verses.kjv: And Jacob beheld the countenance of Laban, and, behold, it was not toward him as before.
verses.bsb: And Jacob saw from the countenance of Laban that his attitude toward him had changed.

verses.esv: Then the LORD said to Jacob, “Return to the land of your fathers and to your kindred, and I will be with you.”
verses.kjv: And the Lord said unto Jacob, Return unto the land of thy fathers, and to thy kindred; and I will be with thee.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Jacob, “Go back to the land of your fathers and to your kindred, and I will be with you.”

verses.esv: So Jacob sent and called Rachel and Leah into the field where his flock was
verses.kjv: And Jacob sent and called Rachel and Leah to the field unto his flock,
verses.bsb: So Jacob sent word and called Rachel and Leah to the field where his flocks were,

verses.esv: and said to them, “I see that your father does not regard me with favor as he did before. But the God of my father has been with me.
verses.kjv: And said unto them, I see your father’s countenance, that it is not toward me as before; but the God of my father hath been with me.
verses.bsb: and he told them, “I can see from your father’s countenance that his attitude toward me has changed; but the God of my father has been with me.

verses.esv: You know that I have served your father with all my strength,
verses.kjv: And ye know that with all my power I have served your father.
verses.bsb: You know that I have served your father with all my strength.

verses.esv: yet your father has cheated me and changed my wages ten times. But God did not permit him to harm me.
verses.kjv: And your father hath deceived me, and changed my wages ten times; but God suffered him not to hurt me.
verses.bsb: And although he has cheated me and changed my wages ten times, God has not allowed him to harm me.

verses.esv: If he said, ‘The spotted shall be your wages,’ then all the flock bore spotted; and if he said, ‘The striped shall be your wages,’ then all the flock bore striped.
verses.kjv: If he said thus, The speckled shall be thy wages; then all the cattle bare speckled: and if he said thus, The ringstraked shall be thy hire; then bare all the cattle ringstraked.
verses.bsb: If he said, ‘The speckled will be your wages,’ then the whole flock bore speckled offspring. If he said, ‘The streaked will be your wages,’ then the whole flock bore streaked offspring.

verses.esv: Thus God has taken away the livestock of your father and given them to me.
verses.kjv: Thus God hath taken away the cattle of your father, and given them to me.
verses.bsb: Thus God has taken away your father’s livestock and given them to me.

verses.esv: In the breeding season of the flock I lifted up my eyes and saw in a dream that the goats that mated with the flock were striped, spotted, and mottled.
verses.kjv: And it came to pass at the time that the cattle conceived, that I lifted up mine eyes, and saw in a dream, and, behold, the rams which leaped upon the cattle were ringstraked, speckled, and grisled.
verses.bsb: When the flocks were breeding, I saw in a dream that the streaked, spotted, and speckled males were mating with the females.

verses.esv: Then the angel of God said to me in the dream, ‘Jacob,’ and I said, ‘Here I am!’
verses.kjv: And the angel of God spake unto me in a dream, saying, Jacob: And I said, Here am I.
verses.bsb: In that dream the angel of God said to me, ‘Jacob!’  And I replied, ‘Here I am.’

verses.esv: And he said, ‘Lift up your eyes and see, all the goats that mate with the flock are striped, spotted, and mottled, for I have seen all that Laban is doing to you.
verses.kjv: And he said, Lift up now thine eyes, and see, all the rams which leap upon the cattle are ringstraked, speckled, and grisled: for I have seen all that Laban doeth unto thee.
verses.bsb: ‘Look up,’ he said, ‘and see that all the males that are mating with the flock are streaked, spotted, or speckled; for I have seen all that Laban has done to you.

verses.esv: I am the God of Bethel, where you anointed a pillar and made a vow to me. Now arise, go out from this land and return to the land of your kindred.’”
verses.kjv: I am the God of Beth-el, where thou anointedst the pillar, and where thou vowedst a vow unto me: now arise, get thee out from this land, and return unto the land of thy kindred.
verses.bsb: I am the God of Bethel, where you anointed the pillar and made a solemn vow to Me. Now get up and leave this land at once, and return to your native land.’”

verses.esv: Then Rachel and Leah answered and said to him, “Is there any portion or inheritance left to us in our father's house?
verses.kjv: And Rachel and Leah answered and said unto him, Is there yet any portion or inheritance for us in our father’s house?
verses.bsb: And Rachel and Leah replied, “Do we have any portion or inheritance left in our father’s house?

verses.esv: Are we not regarded by him as foreigners? For he has sold us, and he has indeed devoured our money.
verses.kjv: Are we not counted of him strangers? for he hath sold us, and hath quite devoured also our money.
verses.bsb: Are we not regarded by him as outsiders? Not only has he sold us, but he has certainly squandered what was paid for us.

verses.esv: All the wealth that God has taken away from our father belongs to us and to our children. Now then, whatever God has said to you, do.”
verses.kjv: For all the riches which God hath taken from our father, that is ours, and our children’s: now then, whatsoever God hath said unto thee, do.
verses.bsb: Surely all the wealth that God has taken away from our father belongs to us and to our children. So do whatever God has told you.”

verses.esv: So Jacob arose and set his sons and his wives on camels.
verses.kjv: ¶ Then Jacob rose up, and set his sons and his wives upon camels;
verses.bsb: Then Jacob got up and put his children and his wives on camels,

verses.esv: He drove away all his livestock, all his property that he had gained, the livestock in his possession that he had acquired in Paddan-aram, to go to the land of Canaan to his father Isaac.
verses.kjv: And he carried away all his cattle, and all his goods which he had gotten, the cattle of his getting, which he had gotten in Padan-aram, for to go to Isaac his father in the land of Canaan.
verses.bsb: and he drove all his livestock before him, along with all the possessions he had acquired in Paddan-aram, to go to his father Isaac in the land in Canaan.

verses.esv: Laban had gone to shear his sheep, and Rachel stole her father's household gods.
verses.kjv: And Laban went to shear his sheep: and Rachel had stolen the images that were her father’s.
verses.bsb: Now while Laban was out shearing his sheep, Rachel stole her father’s household idols.

verses.esv: And Jacob tricked Laban the Aramean, by not telling him that he intended to flee.
verses.kjv: And Jacob stole away unawares to Laban the Syrian, in that he told him not that he fled.
verses.bsb: Moreover, Jacob deceived Laban the Aramean by not telling him that he was running away.

verses.esv: He fled with all that he had and arose and crossed the Euphrates, and set his face toward the hill country of Gilead.
verses.kjv: So he fled with all that he had; and he rose up, and passed over the river, and set his face toward the mount Gilead.
verses.bsb: So he fled with all his possessions, crossed the Euphrates, and headed for the hill country of Gilead.

verses.esv: When it was told Laban on the third day that Jacob had fled,
verses.kjv: And it was told Laban on the third day that Jacob was fled.
verses.bsb: On the third day Laban was informed that Jacob had fled.

verses.esv: he took his kinsmen with him and pursued him for seven days and followed close after him into the hill country of Gilead.
verses.kjv: And he took his brethren with him, and pursued after him seven days’ journey; and they overtook him in the mount Gilead.
verses.bsb: So he took his relatives with him, pursued Jacob for seven days, and overtook him in the hill country of Gilead.

verses.esv: But God came to Laban the Aramean in a dream by night and said to him, “Be careful not to say anything to Jacob, either good or bad.”
verses.kjv: And God came to Laban the Syrian in a dream by night, and said unto him, Take heed that thou speak not to Jacob either good or bad.
verses.bsb: But that night God came to Laban the Aramean in a dream and warned him, “Be careful not to say anything to Jacob, either good or bad.”

verses.esv: And Laban overtook Jacob. Now Jacob had pitched his tent in the hill country, and Laban with his kinsmen pitched tents in the hill country of Gilead.
verses.kjv: ¶ Then Laban overtook Jacob. Now Jacob had pitched his tent in the mount: and Laban with his brethren pitched in the mount of Gilead.
verses.bsb: Now Jacob had pitched his tent in the hill country of Gilead when Laban overtook him, and Laban and his relatives camped there as well.

verses.esv: And Laban said to Jacob, “What have you done, that you have tricked me and driven away my daughters like captives of the sword?
verses.kjv: And Laban said to Jacob, What hast thou done, that thou hast stolen away unawares to me, and carried away my daughters, as captives taken with the sword?
verses.bsb: Then Laban said to Jacob, “What have you done? You have deceived me and carried off my daughters like captives of war!

verses.esv: Why did you flee secretly and trick me, and did not tell me, so that I might have sent you away with mirth and songs, with tambourine and lyre?
verses.kjv: Wherefore didst thou flee away secretly, and steal away from me; and didst not tell me, that I might have sent thee away with mirth, and with songs, with tabret, and with harp?
verses.bsb: Why did you run away secretly and deceive me, without even telling me? I would have sent you away with joy and singing, with tambourines and harps.

verses.esv: And why did you not permit me to kiss my sons and my daughters farewell? Now you have done foolishly.
verses.kjv: And hast not suffered me to kiss my sons and my daughters? thou hast now done foolishly in so doing.
verses.bsb: But you did not even let me kiss my grandchildren and my daughters goodbye. Now you have done a foolish thing.

verses.esv: It is in my power to do you harm. But the God of your father spoke to me last night, saying, ‘Be careful not to say anything to Jacob, either good or bad.’
verses.kjv: It is in the power of my hand to do you hurt: but the God of your father spake unto me yesternight, saying, Take thou heed that thou speak not to Jacob either good or bad.
verses.bsb: I have power to do you great harm, but last night the God of your father said to me, ‘Be careful not to say anything to Jacob, either good or bad.’

verses.esv: And now you have gone away because you longed greatly for your father's house, but why did you steal my gods?”
verses.kjv: And now, though thou wouldest needs be gone, because thou sore longedst after thy father’s house, yet wherefore hast thou stolen my gods?
verses.bsb: Now you have gone off because you long for your father’s house. But why have you stolen my gods?”

verses.esv: Jacob answered and said to Laban, “Because I was afraid, for I thought that you would take your daughters from me by force.
verses.kjv: And Jacob answered and said to Laban, Because I was afraid: for I said, Peradventure thou wouldest take by force thy daughters from me.
verses.bsb: “I was afraid,” Jacob answered, “for I thought you would take your daughters from me by force.

verses.esv: Anyone with whom you find your gods shall not live. In the presence of our kinsmen point out what I have that is yours, and take it.” Now Jacob did not know that Rachel had stolen them.
verses.kjv: With whomsoever thou findest thy gods, let him not live: before our brethren discern thou what is thine with me, and take it to thee. For Jacob knew not that Rachel had stolen them.
verses.bsb: If you find your gods with anyone here, he shall not live! In the presence of our relatives, see for yourself if anything is yours, and take it back.” For Jacob did not know that Rachel had stolen the idols.

verses.esv: So Laban went into Jacob's tent and into Leah's tent and into the tent of the two female servants, but he did not find them. And he went out of Leah's tent and entered Rachel's.
verses.kjv: And Laban went into Jacob’s tent, and into Leah’s tent, and into the two maidservants’ tents; but he found them not. Then went he out of Leah’s tent, and entered into Rachel’s tent.
verses.bsb: So Laban went into Jacob’s tent, then Leah’s tent, and then the tents of the two maidservants, but he found nothing. Then he left Leah’s tent and entered Rachel’s tent.

verses.esv: Now Rachel had taken the household gods and put them in the camel's saddle and sat on them. Laban felt all about the tent, but did not find them.
verses.kjv: Now Rachel had taken the images, and put them in the camel’s furniture, and sat upon them. And Laban searched all the tent, but found them not.
verses.bsb: Now Rachel had taken Laban’s household idols, put them in the saddlebag of her camel, and was sitting on them. And Laban searched everything in the tent but found nothing.

verses.esv: And she said to her father, “Let not my lord be angry that I cannot rise before you, for the way of women is upon me.” So he searched but did not find the household gods.
verses.kjv: And she said to her father, Let it not displease my lord that I cannot rise up before thee; for the custom of women is upon me. And he searched, but found not the images.
verses.bsb: Rachel said to her father, “Sir, do not be angry that I cannot stand up before you; for I am having my period.” So Laban searched, but could not find the household idols.

verses.esv: Then Jacob became angry and berated Laban. Jacob said to Laban, “What is my offense? What is my sin, that you have hotly pursued me?
verses.kjv: ¶ And Jacob was wroth, and chode with Laban: and Jacob answered and said to Laban, What is my trespass? what is my sin, that thou hast so hotly pursued after me?
verses.bsb: Then Jacob became incensed and challenged Laban. “What is my crime?” he said. “For what sin of mine have you so hotly pursued me?

verses.esv: For you have felt through all my goods; what have you found of all your household goods? Set it here before my kinsmen and your kinsmen, that they may decide between us two.
verses.kjv: Whereas thou hast searched all my stuff, what hast thou found of all thy household stuff? set it here before my brethren and thy brethren, that they may judge betwixt us both.
verses.bsb: You have searched all my goods! Have you found anything that belongs to you? Put it here before my brothers and yours, that they may judge between the two of us.

verses.esv: These twenty years I have been with you. Your ewes and your female goats have not miscarried, and I have not eaten the rams of your flocks.
verses.kjv: This twenty years have I been with thee; thy ewes and thy she goats have not cast their young, and the rams of thy flock have I not eaten.
verses.bsb: I have been with you for twenty years now. Your sheep and goats have not miscarried, nor have I eaten the rams of your flock.

verses.esv: What was torn by wild beasts I did not bring to you. I bore the loss of it myself. From my hand you required it, whether stolen by day or stolen by night.
verses.kjv: That which was torn of beasts I brought not unto thee; I bare the loss of it; of my hand didst thou require it, whether stolen by day, or stolen by night.
verses.bsb: I did not bring you anything torn by wild beasts; I bore the loss myself. And you demanded payment from me for what was stolen by day or night.

verses.esv: There I was: by day the heat consumed me, and the cold by night, and my sleep fled from my eyes.
verses.kjv: Thus I was; in the day the drought consumed me, and the frost by night; and my sleep departed from mine eyes.
verses.bsb: As it was, the heat consumed me by day and the frost by night, and sleep fled from my eyes.

verses.esv: These twenty years I have been in your house. I served you fourteen years for your two daughters, and six years for your flock, and you have changed my wages ten times.
verses.kjv: Thus have I been twenty years in thy house; I served thee fourteen years for thy two daughters, and six years for thy cattle: and thou hast changed my wages ten times.
verses.bsb: Thus for twenty years I have served in your household—fourteen years for your two daughters and six years for your flocks—and you have changed my wages ten times!

verses.esv: If the God of my father, the God of Abraham and the Fear of Isaac, had not been on my side, surely now you would have sent me away empty-handed. God saw my affliction and the labor of my hands and rebuked you last night.”
verses.kjv: Except the God of my father, the God of Abraham, and the fear of Isaac, had been with me, surely thou hadst sent me away now empty. God hath seen mine affliction and the labour of my hands, and rebuked thee yesternight.
verses.bsb: If the God of my father, the God of Abraham and the Fear of Isaac, had not been with me, surely by now you would have sent me away empty-handed. But God has seen my affliction and the toil of my hands, and last night He rendered judgment.”

verses.esv: Then Laban answered and said to Jacob, “The daughters are my daughters, the children are my children, the flocks are my flocks, and all that you see is mine. But what can I do this day for these my daughters or for their children whom they have borne?
verses.kjv: ¶ And Laban answered and said unto Jacob, These daughters are my daughters, and these children are my children, and these cattle are my cattle, and all that thou seest is mine: and what can I do this day unto these my daughters, or unto their children which they have born?
verses.bsb: But Laban answered Jacob, “These daughters are my daughters, these sons are my sons, and these flocks are my flocks! Everything you see is mine! Yet what can I do today about these daughters of mine or the children they have borne?

verses.esv: Come now, let us make a covenant, you and I. And let it be a witness between you and me.”
verses.kjv: Now therefore come thou, let us make a covenant, I and thou; and let it be for a witness between me and thee.
verses.bsb: Come now, let us make a covenant, you and I, and let it serve as a witness between you and me.”

verses.esv: So Jacob took a stone and set it up as a pillar.
verses.kjv: And Jacob took a stone, and set it up for a pillar.
verses.bsb: So Jacob picked out a stone and set it up as a pillar,

verses.esv: And Jacob said to his kinsmen, “Gather stones.” And they took stones and made a heap, and they ate there by the heap.
verses.kjv: And Jacob said unto his brethren, Gather stones; and they took stones, and made an heap: and they did eat there upon the heap.
verses.bsb: and he said to his relatives, “Gather some stones.” So they took stones and made a mound, and there by the mound they ate.

verses.esv: Laban called it Jegar-sahadutha, but Jacob called it Galeed.
verses.kjv: And Laban called it Jegar-sahadutha: but Jacob called it Galeed.
verses.bsb: Laban called it Jegar-sahadutha, and Jacob called it Galeed.

verses.esv: Laban said, “This heap is a witness between you and me today.” Therefore he named it Galeed,
verses.kjv: And Laban said, This heap is a witness between me and thee this day. Therefore was the name of it called Galeed;
verses.bsb: Then Laban declared, “This mound is a witness between you and me this day.”  Therefore the place was called Galeed.

verses.esv: and Mizpah, for he said, “The LORD watch between you and me, when we are out of one another's sight.
verses.kjv: And Mizpah; for he said, The Lord watch between me and thee, when we are absent one from another.
verses.bsb: It was also called Mizpah, because Laban said, “May the LORD keep watch between you and me when we are absent from each other.

verses.esv: If you oppress my daughters, or if you take wives besides my daughters, although no one is with us, see, God is witness between you and me.”
verses.kjv: If thou shalt afflict my daughters, or if thou shalt take other wives beside my daughters, no man is with us; see, God is witness betwixt me and thee.
verses.bsb: If you mistreat my daughters or take other wives, although no one is with us, remember that God is a witness between you and me.”

verses.esv: Then Laban said to Jacob, “See this heap and the pillar, which I have set between you and me.
verses.kjv: And Laban said to Jacob, Behold this heap, and behold this pillar, which I have cast betwixt me and thee;
verses.bsb: Laban also said to Jacob, “Here is the mound, and here is the pillar I have set up between you and me.

verses.esv: This heap is a witness, and the pillar is a witness, that I will not pass over this heap to you, and you will not pass over this heap and this pillar to me, to do harm.
verses.kjv: This heap be witness, and this pillar be witness, that I will not pass over this heap to thee, and that thou shalt not pass over this heap and this pillar unto me, for harm.
verses.bsb: This mound is a witness, and this pillar is a witness, that I will not go past this mound to harm you, and you will not go past this mound and pillar to harm me.

verses.esv: The God of Abraham and the God of Nahor, the God of their father, judge between us.” So Jacob swore by the Fear of his father Isaac,
verses.kjv: The God of Abraham, and the God of Nahor, the God of their father, judge betwixt us. And Jacob sware by the fear of his father Isaac.
verses.bsb: May the God of Abraham and the God of Nahor, the God of their father, judge between us.”  So Jacob swore by the Fear of his father Isaac.

verses.esv: and Jacob offered a sacrifice in the hill country and called his kinsmen to eat bread. They ate bread and spent the night in the hill country.
verses.kjv: Then Jacob offered sacrifice upon the mount, and called his brethren to eat bread: and they did eat bread, and tarried all night in the mount.
verses.bsb: Then Jacob offered a sacrifice on the mountain and invited his relatives to eat a meal. And after they had eaten, they spent the night on the mountain.

verses.esv: Early in the morning Laban arose and kissed his grandchildren and his daughters and blessed them. Then Laban departed and returned home.
verses.kjv: And early in the morning Laban rose up, and kissed his sons and his daughters, and blessed them: and Laban departed, and returned unto his place.
verses.bsb: Early the next morning, Laban got up and kissed his grandchildren and daughters and blessed them. Then he left to return home.

verses.esv: Jacob went on his way, and the angels of God met him.
verses.kjv: AND Jacob went on his way, and the angels of God met him.
verses.bsb: Jacob also went on his way, and the angels of God met him.

verses.esv: And when Jacob saw them he said, “This is God's camp!” So he called the name of that place Mahanaim.
verses.kjv: And when Jacob saw them, he said, This is God’s host: and he called the name of that place Mahanaim.
verses.bsb: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is the camp of God.” So he named that place Mahanaim.

verses.esv: And Jacob sent messengers before him to Esau his brother in the land of Seir, the country of Edom,
verses.kjv: And Jacob sent messengers before him to Esau his brother unto the land of Seir, the country of Edom.
verses.bsb: Jacob sent messengers ahead of him to his brother Esau in the land of Seir, the country of Edom.

verses.esv: instructing them, “Thus you shall say to my lord Esau: Thus says your servant Jacob, ‘I have sojourned with Laban and stayed until now.
verses.kjv: And he commanded them, saying, Thus shall ye speak unto my lord Esau; Thy servant Jacob saith thus, I have sojourned with Laban, and stayed there until now:
verses.bsb: He instructed them, “You are to say to my master Esau, ‘Your servant Jacob says: I have been staying with Laban and have remained there until now.

verses.esv: I have oxen, donkeys, flocks, male servants, and female servants. I have sent to tell my lord, in order that I may find favor in your sight.’”
verses.kjv: And I have oxen, and asses, flocks, and menservants, and womenservants: and I have sent to tell my lord, that I may find grace in thy sight.
verses.bsb: I have oxen, donkeys, flocks, menservants, and maidservants. I have sent this message to inform my master, so that I may find favor in your sight.’”

verses.esv: And the messengers returned to Jacob, saying, “We came to your brother Esau, and he is coming to meet you, and there are four hundred men with him.”
verses.kjv: ¶ And the messengers returned to Jacob, saying, We came to thy brother Esau, and also he cometh to meet thee, and four hundred men with him.
verses.bsb: When the messengers returned to Jacob, they said, “We went to your brother Esau, and now he is coming to meet you—he and four hundred men with him.”

verses.esv: Then Jacob was greatly afraid and distressed. He divided the people who were with him, and the flocks and herds and camels, into two camps,
verses.kjv: Then Jacob was greatly afraid and distressed: and he divided the people that was with him, and the flocks, and herds, and the camels, into two bands;
verses.bsb: In great fear and distress, Jacob divided his people into two camps, as well as the flocks and herds and camels.

verses.esv: thinking, “If Esau comes to the one camp and attacks it, then the camp that is left will escape.”
verses.kjv: And said, If Esau come to the one company, and smite it, then the other company which is left shall escape.
verses.bsb: He thought, “If Esau comes and attacks one camp, then the other camp can escape.”

verses.esv: And Jacob said, “O God of my father Abraham and God of my father Isaac, O LORD who said to me, ‘Return to your country and to your kindred, that I may do you good,’
verses.kjv: ¶ And Jacob said, O God of my father Abraham, and God of my father Isaac, the Lord which saidst unto me, Return unto thy country, and to thy kindred, and I will deal well with thee:
verses.bsb: Then Jacob declared, “O God of my father Abraham, God of my father Isaac, the LORD who told me, ‘Go back to your country and to your kindred, and I will make you prosper,’

verses.esv: I am not worthy of the least of all the deeds of steadfast love and all the faithfulness that you have shown to your servant, for with only my staff I crossed this Jordan, and now I have become two camps.
verses.kjv: I am not worthy of the least of all the mercies, and of all the truth, which thou hast shewed unto thy servant; for with my staff I passed over this Jordan; and now I am become two bands.
verses.bsb: I am unworthy of all the kindness and faithfulness You have shown Your servant. Indeed, with only my staff I came across the Jordan, but now I have become two camps.

verses.esv: Please deliver me from the hand of my brother, from the hand of Esau, for I fear him, that he may come and attack me, the mothers with the children.
verses.kjv: Deliver me, I pray thee, from the hand of my brother, from the hand of Esau: for I fear him, lest he will come and smite me, and the mother with the children.
verses.bsb: Please deliver me from the hand of my brother Esau, for I am afraid that he may come and attack me and the mothers and children with me.

verses.esv: But you said, ‘I will surely do you good, and make your offspring as the sand of the sea, which cannot be numbered for multitude.’”
verses.kjv: And thou saidst, I will surely do thee good, and make thy seed as the sand of the sea, which cannot be numbered for multitude.
verses.bsb: But You have said, ‘I will surely make you prosper, and I will make your offspring like the sand of the sea, too numerous to count.’”

verses.esv: So he stayed there that night, and from what he had with him he took a present for his brother Esau,
verses.kjv: ¶ And he lodged there that same night; and took of that which came to his hand a present for Esau his brother;
verses.bsb: Jacob spent the night there, and from what he had brought with him, he selected a gift for his brother Esau:

verses.esv: two hundred female goats and twenty male goats, two hundred ewes and twenty rams,
verses.kjv: Two hundred she goats, and twenty he goats, two hundred ewes, and twenty rams,
verses.bsb: 200 female goats, 20 male goats, 200 ewes, 20 rams,

verses.esv: thirty milking camels and their calves, forty cows and ten bulls, twenty female donkeys and ten male donkeys.
verses.kjv: Thirty milch camels with their colts, forty kine, and ten bulls, twenty she asses, and ten foals.
verses.bsb: 30 milk camels with their young, 40 cows, 10 bulls, 20 female donkeys, and 10 male donkeys.

verses.esv: These he handed over to his servants, every drove by itself, and said to his servants, “Pass on ahead of me and put a space between drove and drove.”
verses.kjv: And he delivered them into the hand of his servants, every drove by themselves; and said unto his servants, Pass over before me, and put a space betwixt drove and drove.
verses.bsb: He entrusted them to his servants in separate herds and told them, “Go on ahead of me, and keep some distance between the herds.”

verses.esv: He instructed the first, “When Esau my brother meets you and asks you, ‘To whom do you belong? Where are you going? And whose are these ahead of you?’
verses.kjv: And he commanded the foremost, saying, When Esau my brother meeteth thee, and asketh thee, saying, Whose art thou? and whither goest thou? and whose are these before thee?
verses.bsb: He instructed the one in the lead, “When my brother Esau meets you and asks, ‘To whom do you belong, where are you going, and whose animals are these before you?’

verses.esv: then you shall say, ‘They belong to your servant Jacob. They are a present sent to my lord Esau. And moreover, he is behind us.’”
verses.kjv: Then thou shalt say, They be thy servant Jacob’s; it is a present sent unto my lord Esau: and, behold, also he is behind us.
verses.bsb: then you are to say, ‘They belong to your servant Jacob. They are a gift, sent to my lord Esau. And behold, Jacob is behind us.’”

verses.esv: He likewise instructed the second and the third and all who followed the droves, “You shall say the same thing to Esau when you find him,
verses.kjv: And so commanded he the second, and the third, and all that followed the droves, saying, On this manner shall ye speak unto Esau, when ye find him.
verses.bsb: He also instructed the second, the third, and all those following behind the herds: “When you meet Esau, you are to say the same thing to him.

verses.esv: and you shall say, ‘Moreover, your servant Jacob is behind us.’” For he thought, “I may appease him with the present that goes ahead of me, and afterward I shall see his face. Perhaps he will accept me.”
verses.kjv: And say ye moreover, Behold, thy servant Jacob is behind us. For he said, I will appease him with the present that goeth before me, and afterward I will see his face; peradventure he will accept of me.
verses.bsb: You are also to say, ‘Look, your servant Jacob is right behind us.’” For he thought, “I will appease Esau with the gift that is going before me. After that I can face him, and perhaps he will accept me.”

verses.esv: So the present passed on ahead of him, and he himself stayed that night in the camp.
verses.kjv: So went the present over before him: and himself lodged that night in the company.
verses.bsb: So Jacob’s gifts went on before him, while he spent the night in the camp.

verses.esv: The same night he arose and took his two wives, his two female servants, and his eleven children, and crossed the ford of the Jabbok.
verses.kjv: And he rose up that night, and took his two wives, and his two womenservants, and his eleven sons, and passed over the ford Jabbok.
verses.bsb: During the night Jacob got up and took his two wives, his two maidservants, and his eleven sons, and crossed the ford of the Jabbok.

verses.esv: He took them and sent them across the stream, and everything else that he had.
verses.kjv: And he took them, and sent them over the brook, and sent over that he had.
verses.bsb: He took them and sent them across the stream, along with all his possessions.

verses.esv: And Jacob was left alone. And a man wrestled with him until the breaking of the day.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Jacob was left alone; and there wrestled a man with him until the breaking of the day.
verses.bsb: So Jacob was left all alone, and there a man wrestled with him until daybreak.

verses.esv: When the man saw that he did not prevail against Jacob, he touched his hip socket, and Jacob's hip was put out of joint as he wrestled with him.
verses.kjv: And when he saw that he prevailed not against him, he touched the hollow of his thigh; and the hollow of Jacob’s thigh was out of joint, as he wrestled with him.
verses.bsb: When the man saw that he could not overpower Jacob, he struck the socket of Jacob’s hip and dislocated it as they wrestled.

verses.esv: Then he said, “Let me go, for the day has broken.” But Jacob said, “I will not let you go unless you bless me.”
verses.kjv: And he said, Let me go, for the day breaketh. And he said, I will not let thee go, except thou bless me.
verses.bsb: Then the man said, “Let me go, for it is daybreak.”  But Jacob replied, “I will not let you go unless you bless me.”

verses.esv: And he said to him, “What is your name?” And he said, “Jacob.”
verses.kjv: And he said unto him, What is thy name? And he said, Jacob.
verses.bsb: “What is your name?” the man asked.  “Jacob,” he replied.

verses.esv: Then he said, “Your name shall no longer be called Jacob, but Israel, for you have striven with God and with men, and have prevailed.”
verses.kjv: And he said, Thy name shall be called no more Jacob, but Israel: for as a prince hast thou power with God and with men, and hast prevailed.
verses.bsb: Then the man said, “Your name will no longer be Jacob, but Israel, because you have struggled with God and with men, and you have prevailed.”

verses.esv: Then Jacob asked him, “Please tell me your name.” But he said, “Why is it that you ask my name?” And there he blessed him.
verses.kjv: And Jacob asked him, and said, Tell me, I pray thee, thy name. And he said, Wherefore is it that thou dost ask after my name? And he blessed him there.
verses.bsb: And Jacob requested, “Please tell me your name.”  But he replied, “Why do you ask my name?” Then he blessed Jacob there.

verses.esv: So Jacob called the name of the place Peniel, saying, “For I have seen God face to face, and yet my life has been delivered.”
verses.kjv: And Jacob called the name of the place Peniel: for I have seen God face to face, and my life is preserved.
verses.bsb: So Jacob named the place Peniel, saying, “Indeed, I have seen God face to face, and yet my life was spared.”

verses.esv: The sun rose upon him as he passed Penuel, limping because of his hip.
verses.kjv: And as he passed over Penuel the sun rose upon him, and he halted upon his thigh.
verses.bsb: The sun rose above him as he passed by Penuel, and he was limping because of his hip.

verses.esv: Therefore to this day the people of Israel do not eat the sinew of the thigh that is on the hip socket, because he touched the socket of Jacob's hip on the sinew of the thigh.
verses.kjv: Therefore the children of Israel eat not of the sinew which shrank, which is upon the hollow of the thigh, unto this day: because he touched the hollow of Jacob’s thigh in the sinew that shrank.
verses.bsb: Therefore to this day the Israelites do not eat the tendon which is at the socket of the hip, because the socket of Jacob’s hip was struck near that tendon.

verses.esv: And Jacob lifted up his eyes and looked, and behold, Esau was coming, and four hundred men with him. So he divided the children among Leah and Rachel and the two female servants.
verses.kjv: AND Jacob lifted up his eyes, and looked, and, behold, Esau came, and with him four hundred men. And he divided the children unto Leah, and unto Rachel, and unto the two handmaids.
verses.bsb: Now Jacob looked up and saw Esau coming toward him with four hundred men. So he divided the children among Leah, Rachel, and the two maidservants.

verses.esv: And he put the servants with their children in front, then Leah with her children, and Rachel and Joseph last of all.
verses.kjv: And he put the handmaids and their children foremost, and Leah and her children after, and Rachel and Joseph hindermost.
verses.bsb: He put the maidservants and their children in front, Leah and her children next, and Rachel and Joseph at the rear.

verses.esv: He himself went on before them, bowing himself to the ground seven times, until he came near to his brother.
verses.kjv: And he passed over before them, and bowed himself to the ground seven times, until he came near to his brother.
verses.bsb: But Jacob himself went on ahead and bowed to the ground seven times as he approached his brother.

verses.esv: But Esau ran to meet him and embraced him and fell on his neck and kissed him, and they wept.
verses.kjv: And Esau ran to meet him, and embraced him, and fell on his neck, and kissed him: and they wept.
verses.bsb: Esau, however, ran to him and embraced him, threw his arms around his neck, and kissed him. And they both wept.

verses.esv: And when Esau lifted up his eyes and saw the women and children, he said, “Who are these with you?” Jacob said, “The children whom God has graciously given your servant.”
verses.kjv: And he lifted up his eyes, and saw the women and the children; and said, Who are those with thee? And he said, The children which God hath graciously given thy servant.
verses.bsb: When Esau looked up and saw the women and children, he asked, “Who are these with you?”  Jacob answered, “These are the children God has graciously given your servant.”

verses.esv: Then the servants drew near, they and their children, and bowed down.
verses.kjv: Then the handmaidens came near, they and their children, and they bowed themselves.
verses.bsb: Then the maidservants and their children approached and bowed down.

verses.esv: Leah likewise and her children drew near and bowed down. And last Joseph and Rachel drew near, and they bowed down.
verses.kjv: And Leah also with her children came near, and bowed themselves: and after came Joseph near and Rachel, and they bowed themselves.
verses.bsb: Leah and her children also approached and bowed down, and then Joseph and Rachel approached and bowed down.

verses.esv: Esau said, “What do you mean by all this company that I met?” Jacob answered, “To find favor in the sight of my lord.”
verses.kjv: And he said, What meanest thou by all this drove which I met? And he said, These are to find grace in the sight of my lord.
verses.bsb: “What do you mean by sending this whole company to meet me?” asked Esau.  “To find favor in your sight, my lord,” Jacob answered.

verses.esv: But Esau said, “I have enough, my brother; keep what you have for yourself.”
verses.kjv: And Esau said, I have enough, my brother; keep that thou hast unto thyself.
verses.bsb: “I already have plenty, my brother,” Esau replied. “Keep what belongs to you.”

verses.esv: Jacob said, “No, please, if I have found favor in your sight, then accept my present from my hand. For I have seen your face, which is like seeing the face of God, and you have accepted me.
verses.kjv: And Jacob said, Nay, I pray thee, if now I have found grace in thy sight, then receive my present at my hand: for therefore I have seen thy face, as though I had seen the face of God, and thou wast pleased with me.
verses.bsb: But Jacob insisted, “No, please! If I have found favor in your sight, then receive this gift from my hand. For indeed, I have seen your face, and it is like seeing the face of God, since you have received me favorably.

verses.esv: Please accept my blessing that is brought to you, because God has dealt graciously with me, and because I have enough.” Thus he urged him, and he took it.
verses.kjv: Take, I pray thee, my blessing that is brought to thee; because God hath dealt graciously with me, and because I have enough. And he urged him, and he took it.
verses.bsb: Please accept my gift that was brought to you, because God has been gracious to me and I have all I need.” So Jacob pressed him until he accepted.

verses.esv: Then Esau said, “Let us journey on our way, and I will go ahead of you.”
verses.kjv: And he said, Let us take our journey, and let us go, and I will go before thee.
verses.bsb: Then Esau said, “Let us be on our way, and I will go ahead of you.”

verses.esv: But Jacob said to him, “My lord knows that the children are frail, and that the nursing flocks and herds are a care to me. If they are driven hard for one day, all the flocks will die.
verses.kjv: And he said unto him, My lord knoweth that the children are tender, and the flocks and herds with young are with me: and if men should overdrive them one day, all the flock will die.
verses.bsb: But Jacob replied, “My lord knows that the children are frail, and I must care for sheep and cattle that are nursing their young. If they are driven hard for even a day, all the animals will die.

verses.esv: Let my lord pass on ahead of his servant, and I will lead on slowly, at the pace of the livestock that are ahead of me and at the pace of the children, until I come to my lord in Seir.”
verses.kjv: Let my lord, I pray thee, pass over before his servant: and I will lead on softly, according as the cattle that goeth before me and the children be able to endure, until I come unto my lord unto Seir.
verses.bsb: Please let my lord go ahead of his servant. I will continue on slowly, at a comfortable pace for the livestock and children, until I come to my lord at Seir.”

verses.esv: So Esau said, “Let me leave with you some of the people who are with me.” But he said, “What need is there? Let me find favor in the sight of my lord.”
verses.kjv: And Esau said, Let me now leave with thee some of the folk that are with me. And he said, What needeth it? let me find grace in the sight of my lord.
verses.bsb: “Let me leave some of my people with you,” Esau said.  But Jacob replied, “Why do that? Let me find favor in the sight of my lord.”

verses.esv: So Esau returned that day on his way to Seir.
verses.kjv: ¶ So Esau returned that day on his way unto Seir.
verses.bsb: So that day Esau started on his way back to Seir,

verses.esv: But Jacob journeyed to Succoth, and built himself a house and made booths for his livestock. Therefore the name of the place is called Succoth.
verses.kjv: And Jacob journeyed to Succoth, and built him an house, and made booths for his cattle: therefore the name of the place is called Succoth.
verses.bsb: but Jacob went on to Succoth, where he built a house for himself and shelters for his livestock; that is why the place was called Succoth.

verses.esv: And Jacob came safely to the city of Shechem, which is in the land of Canaan, on his way from Paddan-aram, and he camped before the city.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Jacob came to Shalem, a city of Shechem, which is in the land of Canaan, when he came from Padan-aram; and pitched his tent before the city.
verses.bsb: After Jacob had come from Paddan-aram, he arrived safely at the city of Shechem in the land of Canaan, and he camped just outside the city.

verses.esv: And from the sons of Hamor, Shechem's father, he bought for a hundred pieces of money the piece of land on which he had pitched his tent.
verses.kjv: And he bought a parcel of a field, where he had spread his tent, at the hand of the children of Hamor, Shechem’s father, for an hundred pieces of money.
verses.bsb: And the plot of ground where he pitched his tent, he purchased from the sons of Hamor, Shechem’s father, for a hundred pieces of silver.

verses.esv: There he erected an altar and called it El-Elohe-Israel.
verses.kjv: And he erected there an altar, and called it El-elohe-Israel.
verses.bsb: There he set up an altar and called it El-Elohe-Israel.

verses.esv: Now Dinah the daughter of Leah, whom she had borne to Jacob, went out to see the women of the land.
verses.kjv: AND Dinah the daughter of Leah, which she bare unto Jacob, went out to see the daughters of the land.
verses.bsb: Now Dinah, the daughter Leah had borne to Jacob, went out to visit the daughters of the land.

verses.esv: And when Shechem the son of Hamor the Hivite, the prince of the land, saw her, he seized her and lay with her and humiliated her.
verses.kjv: And when Shechem the son of Hamor the Hivite, prince of the country, saw her, he took her, and lay with her, and defiled her.
verses.bsb: When Shechem son of Hamor the Hivite, the prince of the region, saw her, he took her and lay with her by force.

verses.esv: And his soul was drawn to Dinah the daughter of Jacob. He loved the young woman and spoke tenderly to her.
verses.kjv: And his soul clave unto Dinah the daughter of Jacob, and he loved the damsel, and spake kindly unto the damsel.
verses.bsb: And his soul was drawn to Dinah, the daughter of Jacob. He loved the young girl and spoke to her tenderly.

verses.esv: So Shechem spoke to his father Hamor, saying, “Get me this girl for my wife.”
verses.kjv: And Shechem spake unto his father Hamor, saying, Get me this damsel to wife.
verses.bsb: So Shechem told his father Hamor, “Get me this girl as a wife.”

verses.esv: Now Jacob heard that he had defiled his daughter Dinah. But his sons were with his livestock in the field, so Jacob held his peace until they came.
verses.kjv: And Jacob heard that he had defiled Dinah his daughter: now his sons were with his cattle in the field: and Jacob held his peace until they were come.
verses.bsb: Jacob heard that Shechem had defiled his daughter Dinah, but since his sons were with his livestock in the field, he remained silent about it until they returned.

verses.esv: And Hamor the father of Shechem went out to Jacob to speak with him.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Hamor the father of Shechem went out unto Jacob to commune with him.
verses.bsb: Meanwhile, Shechem’s father Hamor came to speak with Jacob.

verses.esv: The sons of Jacob had come in from the field as soon as they heard of it, and the men were indignant and very angry, because he had done an outrageous thing in Israel by lying with Jacob's daughter, for such a thing must not be done.
verses.kjv: And the sons of Jacob came out of the field when they heard it: and the men were grieved, and they were very wroth, because he had wrought folly in Israel in lying with Jacob’s daughter; which thing ought not to be done.
verses.bsb: When Jacob’s sons heard what had happened, they returned from the field. They were filled with grief and fury, because Shechem had committed an outrage in Israel by lying with Jacob’s daughter—a thing that should not be done.

verses.esv: But Hamor spoke with them, saying, “The soul of my son Shechem longs for your daughter. Please give her to him to be his wife.
verses.kjv: And Hamor communed with them, saying, The soul of my son Shechem longeth for your daughter: I pray you give her him to wife.
verses.bsb: But Hamor said to them, “My son Shechem longs for your daughter. Please give her to him as his wife.

verses.esv: Make marriages with us. Give your daughters to us, and take our daughters for yourselves.
verses.kjv: And make ye marriages with us, and give your daughters unto us, and take our daughters unto you.
verses.bsb: Intermarry with us; give us your daughters, and take our daughters for yourselves.

verses.esv: You shall dwell with us, and the land shall be open to you. Dwell and trade in it, and get property in it.”
verses.kjv: And ye shall dwell with us: and the land shall be before you; dwell and trade ye therein, and get you possessions therein.
verses.bsb: You may settle among us, and the land will be open to you. Live here, move about freely, and acquire your own property.”

verses.esv: Shechem also said to her father and to her brothers, “Let me find favor in your eyes, and whatever you say to me I will give.
verses.kjv: And Shechem said unto her father and unto her brethren, Let me find grace in your eyes, and what ye shall say unto me I will give.
verses.bsb: Then Shechem said to Dinah’s father and brothers, “Grant me this favor, and I will give you whatever you ask.

verses.esv: Ask me for as great a bride-price and gift as you will, and I will give whatever you say to me. Only give me the young woman to be my wife.”
verses.kjv: Ask me never so much dowry and gift, and I will give according as ye shall say unto me: but give me the damsel to wife.
verses.bsb: Demand a high dowry and an expensive gift, and I will give you whatever you ask. Only give me the girl as my wife!”
