verses.esv: The sons of Jacob answered Shechem and his father Hamor deceitfully, because he had defiled their sister Dinah.
verses.kjv: And the sons of Jacob answered Shechem and Hamor his father deceitfully, and said, because he had defiled Dinah their sister:
verses.bsb: But because Shechem had defiled their sister Dinah, Jacob’s sons answered him and his father Hamor deceitfully.

verses.esv: They said to them, “We cannot do this thing, to give our sister to one who is uncircumcised, for that would be a disgrace to us.
verses.kjv: And they said unto them, We cannot do this thing, to give our sister to one that is uncircumcised; for that were a reproach unto us:
verses.bsb: “We cannot do such a thing,” they said. “To give our sister to an uncircumcised man would be a disgrace to us.

verses.esv: Only on this condition will we agree with you—that you will become as we are by every male among you being circumcised.
verses.kjv: But in this will we consent unto you: If ye will be as we be, that every male of you be circumcised;
verses.bsb: We will consent to this on one condition, that you become circumcised like us—every one of your males.

verses.esv: Then we will give our daughters to you, and we will take your daughters to ourselves, and we will dwell with you and become one people.
verses.kjv: Then will we give our daughters unto you, and we will take your daughters to us, and we will dwell with you, and we will become one people.
verses.bsb: Then we will give you our daughters and take your daughters for ourselves. We will dwell among you and become one people.

verses.esv: But if you will not listen to us and be circumcised, then we will take our daughter, and we will be gone.”
verses.kjv: But if ye will not hearken unto us, to be circumcised; then will we take our daughter, and we will be gone.
verses.bsb: But if you will not agree to be circumcised, then we will take our sister and go.”

verses.esv: Their words pleased Hamor and Hamor's son Shechem.
verses.kjv: And their words pleased Hamor, and Shechem Hamor’s son.
verses.bsb: Their offer seemed good to Hamor and his son Shechem.

verses.esv: And the young man did not delay to do the thing, because he delighted in Jacob's daughter. Now he was the most honored of all his father's house.
verses.kjv: And the young man deferred not to do the thing, because he had delight in Jacob’s daughter: and he was more honourable than all the house of his father.
verses.bsb: The young man, who was the most respected of all his father’s household, did not hesitate to fulfill this request, because he was delighted with Jacob’s daughter.

verses.esv: So Hamor and his son Shechem came to the gate of their city and spoke to the men of their city, saying,
verses.kjv: ¶ And Hamor and Shechem his son came unto the gate of their city, and communed with the men of their city, saying,
verses.bsb: So Hamor and his son Shechem went to the gate of their city and addressed the men of their city:

verses.esv: “These men are at peace with us; let them dwell in the land and trade in it, for behold, the land is large enough for them. Let us take their daughters as wives, and let us give them our daughters.
verses.kjv: These men are peaceable with us; therefore let them dwell in the land, and trade therein; for the land, behold, it is large enough for them; let us take their daughters to us for wives, and let us give them our daughters.
verses.bsb: “These men are at peace with us. Let them live and trade in our land; indeed, it is large enough for them. Let us take their daughters in marriage and give our daughters to them.

verses.esv: Only on this condition will the men agree to dwell with us to become one people—when every male among us is circumcised as they are circumcised.
verses.kjv: Only herein will the men consent unto us for to dwell with us, to be one people, if every male among us be circumcised, as they are circumcised.
verses.bsb: But only on this condition will the men agree to dwell with us and be one people: if all our men are circumcised as they are.

verses.esv: Will not their livestock, their property and all their beasts be ours? Only let us agree with them, and they will dwell with us.”
verses.kjv: Shall not their cattle and their substance and every beast of theirs be ours? only let us consent unto them, and they will dwell with us.
verses.bsb: Will not their livestock, their possessions, and all their animals become ours? Only let us consent to them, and they will dwell among us.”

verses.esv: And all who went out of the gate of his city listened to Hamor and his son Shechem, and every male was circumcised, all who went out of the gate of his city.
verses.kjv: And unto Hamor and unto Shechem his son hearkened all that went out of the gate of his city; and every male was circumcised, all that went out of the gate of his city.
verses.bsb: All the men who went out of the city gate listened to Hamor and his son Shechem, and every male of the city was circumcised.

verses.esv: On the third day, when they were sore, two of the sons of Jacob, Simeon and Levi, Dinah's brothers, took their swords and came against the city while it felt secure and killed all the males.
verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass on the third day, when they were sore, that two of the sons of Jacob, Simeon and Levi, Dinah’s brethren, took each man his sword, and came upon the city boldly, and slew all the males.
verses.bsb: Three days later, while they were still in pain, two of Jacob’s sons (Dinah’s brothers Simeon and Levi) took their swords, went into the unsuspecting city, and slaughtered every male.

verses.esv: They killed Hamor and his son Shechem with the sword and took Dinah out of Shechem's house and went away.
verses.kjv: And they slew Hamor and Shechem his son with the edge of the sword, and took Dinah out of Shechem’s house, and went out.
verses.bsb: They killed Hamor and his son Shechem with their swords, took Dinah out of Shechem’s house, and went away.

verses.esv: The sons of Jacob came upon the slain and plundered the city, because they had defiled their sister.
verses.kjv: The sons of Jacob came upon the slain, and spoiled the city, because they had defiled their sister.
verses.bsb: Jacob’s other sons came upon the slaughter and looted the city, because their sister had been defiled.

verses.esv: They took their flocks and their herds, their donkeys, and whatever was in the city and in the field.
verses.kjv: They took their sheep, and their oxen, and their asses, and that which was in the city, and that which was in the field,
verses.bsb: They took their flocks and herds and donkeys, and everything else in the city or in the field.

verses.esv: All their wealth, all their little ones and their wives, all that was in the houses, they captured and plundered.
verses.kjv: And all their wealth, and all their little ones, and their wives took they captive, and spoiled even all that was in the house.
verses.bsb: They carried off all their possessions and women and children, and they plundered everything in their houses.

verses.esv: Then Jacob said to Simeon and Levi, “You have brought trouble on me by making me stink to the inhabitants of the land, the Canaanites and the Perizzites. My numbers are few, and if they gather themselves against me and attack me, I shall be destroyed, both I and my household.”
verses.kjv: And Jacob said to Simeon and Levi, Ye have troubled me to make me to stink among the inhabitants of the land, among the Canaanites and the Perizzites: and I being few in number, they shall gather themselves together against me, and slay me; and I shall be destroyed, I and my house.
verses.bsb: Then Jacob said to Simeon and Levi, “You have brought trouble upon me by making me a stench to the Canaanites and Perizzites, the people of this land. We are few in number; if they unite against me and attack me, I and my household will be destroyed.”

verses.esv: But they said, “Should he treat our sister like a prostitute?”
verses.kjv: And they said, Should he deal with our sister as with an harlot?
verses.bsb: But they replied, “Should he have treated our sister like a prostitute?”

verses.esv: God said to Jacob, “Arise, go up to Bethel and dwell there. Make an altar there to the God who appeared to you when you fled from your brother Esau.”
verses.kjv: AND God said unto Jacob, Arise, go up to Beth-el, and dwell there: and make there an altar unto God, that appeared unto thee when thou fleddest from the face of Esau thy brother.
verses.bsb: Then God said to Jacob, “Arise, go up to Bethel, and settle there. Build an altar there to the God who appeared to you when you fled from your brother Esau.”

verses.esv: So Jacob said to his household and to all who were with him, “Put away the foreign gods that are among you and purify yourselves and change your garments.
verses.kjv: Then Jacob said unto his household, and to all that were with him, Put away the strange gods that are among you, and be clean, and change your garments:
verses.bsb: So Jacob told his household and all who were with him, “Get rid of the foreign gods that are among you. Purify yourselves and change your garments.

verses.esv: Then let us arise and go up to Bethel, so that I may make there an altar to the God who answers me in the day of my distress and has been with me wherever I have gone.”
verses.kjv: And let us arise, and go up to Beth-el; and I will make there an altar unto God, who answered me in the day of my distress, and was with me in the way which I went.
verses.bsb: Then let us arise and go to Bethel. I will build an altar there to God, who answered me in my day of distress. He has been with me wherever I have gone.”

verses.esv: So they gave to Jacob all the foreign gods that they had, and the rings that were in their ears. Jacob hid them under the terebinth tree that was near Shechem.
verses.kjv: And they gave unto Jacob all the strange gods which were in their hand, and all their earrings which were in their ears; and Jacob hid them under the oak which was by Shechem.
verses.bsb: So they gave Jacob all their foreign gods and all their earrings, and Jacob buried them under the oak near Shechem.

verses.esv: And as they journeyed, a terror from God fell upon the cities that were around them, so that they did not pursue the sons of Jacob.
verses.kjv: And they journeyed: and the terror of God was upon the cities that were round about them, and they did not pursue after the sons of Jacob.
verses.bsb: As they set out, a terror from God fell over the surrounding cities, so that they did not pursue Jacob’s sons.

verses.esv: And Jacob came to Luz (that is, Bethel), which is in the land of Canaan, he and all the people who were with him,
verses.kjv: ¶ So Jacob came to Luz, which is in the land of Canaan, that is, Beth-el, he and all the people that were with him.
verses.bsb: So Jacob and everyone with him arrived in Luz (that is, Bethel) in the land of Canaan.

verses.esv: and there he built an altar and called the place El-bethel, because there God had revealed himself to him when he fled from his brother.
verses.kjv: And he built there an altar, and called the place El-beth-el: because there God appeared unto him, when he fled from the face of his brother.
verses.bsb: There Jacob built an altar, and he called that place El-bethel, because it was there that God had revealed Himself to Jacob as he fled from his brother.

verses.esv: And Deborah, Rebekah's nurse, died, and she was buried under an oak below Bethel. So he called its name Allon-bacuth.
verses.kjv: But Deborah Rebekah’s nurse died, and she was buried beneath Beth-el under an oak: and the name of it was called Allon-bachuth.
verses.bsb: Now Deborah, Rebekah’s nurse, died and was buried under the oak below Bethel. So Jacob named it Allon-bachuth.

verses.esv: God appeared to Jacob again, when he came from Paddan-aram, and blessed him.
verses.kjv: ¶ And God appeared unto Jacob again, when he came out of Padan-aram, and blessed him.
verses.bsb: After Jacob had returned from Paddan-aram, God appeared to him again and blessed him.

verses.esv: And God said to him, “Your name is Jacob; no longer shall your name be called Jacob, but Israel shall be your name.” So he called his name Israel.
verses.kjv: And God said unto him, Thy name is Jacob: thy name shall not be called any more Jacob, but Israel shall be thy name: and he called his name Israel.
verses.bsb: And God said to him, “Though your name is Jacob, you will no longer be called Jacob. Instead, your name will be Israel.” So God named him Israel.

verses.esv: And God said to him, “I am God Almighty: be fruitful and multiply. A nation and a company of nations shall come from you, and kings shall come from your own body.
verses.kjv: And God said unto him, I am God Almighty: be fruitful and multiply; a nation and a company of nations shall be of thee, and kings shall come out of thy loins;
verses.bsb: And God told him, “I am God Almighty. Be fruitful and multiply. A nation—even a company of nations—shall come from you, and kings shall descend from you.

verses.esv: The land that I gave to Abraham and Isaac I will give to you, and I will give the land to your offspring after you.”
verses.kjv: And the land which I gave Abraham and Isaac, to thee I will give it, and to thy seed after thee will I give the land.
verses.bsb: The land that I gave to Abraham and Isaac I will give to you, and I will give this land to your descendants after you.”

verses.esv: Then God went up from him in the place where he had spoken with him.
verses.kjv: And God went up from him in the place where he talked with him.
verses.bsb: Then God went up from the place where He had spoken with him.

verses.esv: And Jacob set up a pillar in the place where he had spoken with him, a pillar of stone. He poured out a drink offering on it and poured oil on it.
verses.kjv: And Jacob set up a pillar in the place where he talked with him, even a pillar of stone: and he poured a drink offering thereon, and he poured oil thereon.
verses.bsb: So Jacob set up a pillar in the place where God had spoken with him—a stone marker—and he poured out a drink offering on it and anointed it with oil.

verses.esv: So Jacob called the name of the place where God had spoken with him Bethel.
verses.kjv: And Jacob called the name of the place where God spake with him, Beth-el.
verses.bsb: Jacob called the place where God had spoken with him Bethel.

verses.esv: Then they journeyed from Bethel. When they were still some distance from Ephrath, Rachel went into labor, and she had hard labor.
verses.kjv: ¶ And they journeyed from Beth-el; and there was but a little way to come to Ephrath: and Rachel travailed, and she had hard labour.
verses.bsb: Later, they set out from Bethel, and while they were still some distance from Ephrath, Rachel began to give birth, and her labor was difficult.

verses.esv: And when her labor was at its hardest, the midwife said to her, “Do not fear, for you have another son.”
verses.kjv: And it came to pass, when she was in hard labour, that the midwife said unto her, Fear not; thou shalt have this son also.
verses.bsb: During her severe labor, the midwife said to her, “Do not be afraid, for you are having another son.”

verses.esv: And as her soul was departing (for she was dying), she called his name Ben-oni; but his father called him Benjamin.
verses.kjv: And it came to pass, as her soul was in departing, (for she died) that she called his name Ben-oni: but his father called him Benjamin.
verses.bsb: And with her last breath—for she was dying—she named him Ben-oni. But his father called him Benjamin.

verses.esv: So Rachel died, and she was buried on the way to Ephrath (that is, Bethlehem),
verses.kjv: And Rachel died, and was buried in the way to Ephrath, which is Beth-lehem.
verses.bsb: So Rachel died and was buried on the way to Ephrath (that is, Bethlehem).

verses.esv: and Jacob set up a pillar over her tomb. It is the pillar of Rachel's tomb, which is there to this day.
verses.kjv: And Jacob set a pillar upon her grave: that is the pillar of Rachel’s grave unto this day.
verses.bsb: Jacob set up a pillar on her grave; it marks Rachel’s tomb to this day.

verses.esv: Israel journeyed on and pitched his tent beyond the tower of Eder.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Israel journeyed, and spread his tent beyond the tower of Edar.
verses.bsb: Israel again set out and pitched his tent beyond the Tower of Eder.

verses.esv: While Israel lived in that land, Reuben went and lay with Bilhah his father's concubine. And Israel heard of it. Now the sons of Jacob were twelve.
verses.kjv: And it came to pass, when Israel dwelt in that land, that Reuben went and lay with Bilhah his father’s concubine: and Israel heard it. Now the sons of Jacob were twelve:
verses.bsb: While Israel was living in that region, Reuben went in and slept with his father’s concubine Bilhah, and Israel heard about it.  Jacob had twelve sons:

verses.esv: The sons of Leah: Reuben (Jacob's firstborn), Simeon, Levi, Judah, Issachar, and Zebulun.
verses.kjv: The sons of Leah; Reuben, Jacob’s firstborn, and Simeon, and Levi, and Judah, and Issachar, and Zebulun:
verses.bsb: The sons of Leah were Reuben the firstborn of Jacob, Simeon, Levi, Judah, Issachar, and Zebulun.

verses.esv: The sons of Rachel: Joseph and Benjamin.
verses.kjv: The sons of Rachel; Joseph, and Benjamin:
verses.bsb: The sons of Rachel were Joseph and Benjamin.

verses.esv: The sons of Bilhah, Rachel's servant: Dan and Naphtali.
verses.kjv: And the sons of Bilhah, Rachel’s handmaid; Dan, and Naphtali:
verses.bsb: The sons of Rachel’s maidservant Bilhah were Dan and Naphtali.

verses.esv: The sons of Zilpah, Leah's servant: Gad and Asher. These were the sons of Jacob who were born to him in Paddan-aram.
verses.kjv: And the sons of Zilpah, Leah’s handmaid; Gad, and Asher: these are the sons of Jacob, which were born to him in Padan-aram.
verses.bsb: And the sons of Leah’s maidservant Zilpah were Gad and Asher.   These are the sons of Jacob, who were born to him in Paddan-aram.

verses.esv: And Jacob came to his father Isaac at Mamre, or Kiriath-arba (that is, Hebron), where Abraham and Isaac had sojourned.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Jacob came unto Isaac his father unto Mamre, unto the city of Arbah, which is Hebron, where Abraham and Isaac sojourned.
verses.bsb: Jacob returned to his father Isaac at Mamre, near Kiriath-arba (that is, Hebron), where Abraham and Isaac had stayed.

verses.esv: Now the days of Isaac were 180 years.
verses.kjv: And the days of Isaac were an hundred and fourscore years.
verses.bsb: And Isaac lived 180 years.

verses.esv: And Isaac breathed his last, and he died and was gathered to his people, old and full of days. And his sons Esau and Jacob buried him.
verses.kjv: And Isaac gave up the ghost, and died, and was gathered unto his people, being old and full of days: and his sons Esau and Jacob buried him.
verses.bsb: Then he breathed his last and died and was gathered to his people, old and full of years. And his sons Esau and Jacob buried him.

verses.esv: These are the generations of Esau (that is, Edom).
verses.kjv: NOW these are the generations of Esau, who is Edom.
verses.bsb: This is the account of Esau (that is, Edom).

verses.esv: Esau took his wives from the Canaanites: Adah the daughter of Elon the Hittite, Oholibamah the daughter of Anah the daughter of Zibeon the Hivite,
verses.kjv: Esau took his wives of the daughters of Canaan; Adah the daughter of Elon the Hittite, and Aholibamah the daughter of Anah the daughter of Zibeon the Hivite;
verses.bsb: Esau took his wives from the daughters of Canaan: Adah daughter of Elon the Hittite, Oholibamah daughter of Anah and granddaughter of Zibeon the Hivite,

verses.esv: and Basemath, Ishmael's daughter, the sister of Nebaioth.
verses.kjv: And Bashemath Ishmael’s daughter, sister of Nebajoth.
verses.bsb: and Basemath daughter of Ishmael and sister of Nebaioth.

verses.esv: And Adah bore to Esau, Eliphaz; Basemath bore Reuel;
verses.kjv: And Adah bare to Esau Eliphaz; and Bashemath bare Reuel;
verses.bsb: And Adah bore Eliphaz to Esau, Basemath gave birth to Reuel,

verses.esv: and Oholibamah bore Jeush, Jalam, and Korah. These are the sons of Esau who were born to him in the land of Canaan.
verses.kjv: And Aholibamah bare Jeush, and Jaalam, and Korah: these are the sons of Esau, which were born unto him in the land of Canaan.
verses.bsb: and Oholibamah gave birth to Jeush, Jalam, and Korah. These were the sons of Esau, who were born to him in the land of Canaan.

verses.esv: Then Esau took his wives, his sons, his daughters, and all the members of his household, his livestock, all his beasts, and all his property that he had acquired in the land of Canaan. He went into a land away from his brother Jacob.
verses.kjv: And Esau took his wives, and his sons, and his daughters, and all the persons of his house, and his cattle, and all his beasts, and all his substance, which he had got in the land of Canaan; and went into the country from the face of his brother Jacob.
verses.bsb: Later, Esau took his wives and sons and daughters and all the people of his household, along with his livestock, all his other animals, and all the property he had acquired in Canaan, and he moved to a land far away from his brother Jacob.

verses.esv: For their possessions were too great for them to dwell together. The land of their sojournings could not support them because of their livestock.
verses.kjv: For their riches were more than that they might dwell together; and the land wherein they were strangers could not bear them because of their cattle.
verses.bsb: For their possessions were too great for them to dwell together; the land where they stayed could not support them because of their livestock.

verses.esv: So Esau settled in the hill country of Seir. (Esau is Edom.)
verses.kjv: Thus dwelt Esau in mount Seir: Esau is Edom.
verses.bsb: So Esau (that is, Edom) settled in the area of Mount Seir.

verses.esv: These are the generations of Esau the father of the Edomites in the hill country of Seir.
verses.kjv: ¶ And these are the generations of Esau the father of the Edomites in mount Seir:
verses.bsb: This is the account of Esau, the father of the Edomites, in the area of Mount Seir.

verses.esv: These are the names of Esau's sons: Eliphaz the son of Adah the wife of Esau, Reuel the son of Basemath the wife of Esau.
verses.kjv: These are the names of Esau’s sons; Eliphaz the son of Adah the wife of Esau, Reuel the son of Bashemath the wife of Esau.
verses.bsb: These are the names of Esau’s sons: Eliphaz son of Esau’s wife Adah, and Reuel son of Esau’s wife Basemath.

verses.esv: The sons of Eliphaz were Teman, Omar, Zepho, Gatam, and Kenaz.
verses.kjv: And the sons of Eliphaz were Teman, Omar, Zepho, and Gatam, and Kenaz.
verses.bsb: The sons of Eliphaz were Teman, Omar, Zepho, Gatam, and Kenaz.

verses.esv: (Timna was a concubine of Eliphaz, Esau's son; she bore Amalek to Eliphaz.) These are the sons of Adah, Esau's wife.
verses.kjv: And Timna was concubine to Eliphaz Esau’s son; and she bare to Eliphaz Amalek: these were the sons of Adah Esau’s wife.
verses.bsb: Additionally, Timna, a concubine of Esau’s son Eliphaz, gave birth to Amalek. These are the grandsons of Esau’s wife Adah.

verses.esv: These are the sons of Reuel: Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, and Mizzah. These are the sons of Basemath, Esau's wife.
verses.kjv: And these are the sons of Reuel; Nahath, and Zerah, Shammah, and Mizzah: these were the sons of Bashemath Esau’s wife.
verses.bsb: These are the sons of Reuel: Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, and Mizzah. They are the grandsons of Esau’s wife Basemath.

verses.esv: These are the sons of Oholibamah the daughter of Anah the daughter of Zibeon, Esau's wife: she bore to Esau Jeush, Jalam, and Korah.
verses.kjv: ¶ And these were the sons of Aholibamah, the daughter of Anah the daughter of Zibeon, Esau’s wife: and she bare to Esau Jeush, and Jaalam, and Korah.
verses.bsb: These are the sons of Esau’s wife Oholibamah (daughter of Anah and granddaughter of Zibeon) whom she bore to Esau: Jeush, Jalam, and Korah.

verses.esv: These are the chiefs of the sons of Esau. The sons of Eliphaz the firstborn of Esau: the chiefs Teman, Omar, Zepho, Kenaz,
verses.kjv: ¶ These were dukes of the sons of Esau: the sons of Eliphaz the firstborn son of Esau; duke Teman, duke Omar, duke Zepho, duke Kenaz,
verses.bsb: These are the chiefs among the sons of Esau. The sons of Eliphaz the firstborn of Esau: Chiefs Teman, Omar, Zepho, Kenaz,

verses.esv: Korah, Gatam, and Amalek; these are the chiefs of Eliphaz in the land of Edom; these are the sons of Adah.
verses.kjv: Duke Korah, duke Gatam, and duke Amalek: these are the dukes that came of Eliphaz in the land of Edom; these were the sons of Adah.
verses.bsb: Korah, Gatam, and Amalek. They are the chiefs of Eliphaz in the land of Edom, and they are the grandsons of Adah.

verses.esv: These are the sons of Reuel, Esau's son: the chiefs Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, and Mizzah; these are the chiefs of Reuel in the land of Edom; these are the sons of Basemath, Esau's wife.
verses.kjv: ¶ And these are the sons of Reuel Esau’s son; duke Nahath, duke Zerah, duke Shammah, duke Mizzah: these are the dukes that came of Reuel in the land of Edom; these are the sons of Bashemath Esau’s wife.
verses.bsb: These are the sons of Esau’s son Reuel: Chiefs Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, and Mizzah. They are the chiefs descended from Reuel in the land of Edom, and they are the grandsons of Esau’s wife Basemath.

verses.esv: These are the sons of Oholibamah, Esau's wife: the chiefs Jeush, Jalam, and Korah; these are the chiefs born of Oholibamah the daughter of Anah, Esau's wife.
verses.kjv: ¶ And these are the sons of Aholibamah Esau’s wife; duke Jeush, duke Jaalam, duke Korah: these were the dukes that came of Aholibamah the daughter of Anah, Esau’s wife.
verses.bsb: These are the sons of Esau’s wife Oholibamah: Chiefs Jeush, Jalam, and Korah. They are the chiefs descended from Esau’s wife Oholibamah, the daughter of Anah.

verses.esv: These are the sons of Esau (that is, Edom), and these are their chiefs.
verses.kjv: These are the sons of Esau, who is Edom, and these are their dukes.
verses.bsb: All these are the sons of Esau (that is, Edom), and they were their chiefs.

verses.esv: These are the sons of Seir the Horite, the inhabitants of the land: Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,
verses.kjv: ¶ These are the sons of Seir the Horite, who inhabited the land; Lotan, and Shobal, and Zibeon, and Anah,
verses.bsb: These are the sons of Seir the Horite, who were living in the land: Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,

verses.esv: Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan; these are the chiefs of the Horites, the sons of Seir in the land of Edom.
verses.kjv: And Dishon, and Ezer, and Dishan: these are the dukes of the Horites, the children of Seir in the land of Edom.
verses.bsb: Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan. They are the chiefs of the Horites, the descendants of Seir in the land of Edom.

verses.esv: The sons of Lotan were Hori and Hemam; and Lotan's sister was Timna.
verses.kjv: And the children of Lotan were Hori and Hemam; and Lotan’s sister was Timna.
verses.bsb: The sons of Lotan were Hori and Hemam. Timna was Lotan’s sister.

verses.esv: These are the sons of Shobal: Alvan, Manahath, Ebal, Shepho, and Onam.
verses.kjv: And the children of Shobal were these; Alvan, and Manahath, and Ebal, Shepho, and Onam.
verses.bsb: These are the sons of Shobal: Alvan, Manahath, Ebal, Shepho, and Onam.

verses.esv: These are the sons of Zibeon: Aiah and Anah; he is the Anah who found the hot springs in the wilderness, as he pastured the donkeys of Zibeon his father.
verses.kjv: And these are the children of Zibeon; both Ajah, and Anah: this was that Anah that found the mules in the wilderness, as he fed the asses of Zibeon his father.
verses.bsb: These are the sons of Zibeon: Aiah and Anah. (This is the Anah who found the hot springs in the wilderness as he was pasturing the donkeys of his father Zibeon.)

verses.esv: These are the children of Anah: Dishon and Oholibamah the daughter of Anah.
verses.kjv: And the children of Anah were these; Dishon, and Aholibamah the daughter of Anah.
verses.bsb: These are the children of Anah: Dishon and Oholibamah daughter of Anah.

verses.esv: These are the sons of Dishon: Hemdan, Eshban, Ithran, and Cheran.
verses.kjv: And these are the children of Dishon; Hemdan, and Eshban, and Ithran, and Cheran.
verses.bsb: These are the sons of Dishon: Hemdan, Eshban, Ithran, and Cheran.

verses.esv: These are the sons of Ezer: Bilhan, Zaavan, and Akan.
verses.kjv: The children of Ezer are these; Bilhan, and Zaavan, and Akan.
verses.bsb: These are the sons of Ezer: Bilhan, Zaavan, and Akan.

verses.esv: These are the sons of Dishan: Uz and Aran.
verses.kjv: The children of Dishan are these; Uz, and Aran.
verses.bsb: These are the sons of Dishan: Uz and Aran.

verses.esv: These are the chiefs of the Horites: the chiefs Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,
verses.kjv: These are the dukes that came of the Horites; duke Lotan, duke Shobal, duke Zibeon, duke Anah,
verses.bsb: These are the chiefs of the Horites: Chiefs Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,

verses.esv: Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan; these are the chiefs of the Horites, chief by chief in the land of Seir.
verses.kjv: Duke Dishon, duke Ezer, duke Dishan: these are the dukes that came of Hori, among their dukes in the land of Seir.
verses.bsb: Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan. They are the chiefs of the Horites, according to their divisions in the land of Seir.

verses.esv: These are the kings who reigned in the land of Edom, before any king reigned over the Israelites.
verses.kjv: ¶ And these are the kings that reigned in the land of Edom, before there reigned any king over the children of Israel.
verses.bsb: These are the kings who reigned in the land of Edom before any king reigned over the Israelites:

verses.esv: Bela the son of Beor reigned in Edom, the name of his city being Dinhabah.
verses.kjv: And Bela the son of Beor reigned in Edom: and the name of his city was Dinhabah.
verses.bsb: Bela son of Beor reigned in Edom; the name of his city was Dinhabah.

verses.esv: Bela died, and Jobab the son of Zerah of Bozrah reigned in his place.
verses.kjv: And Bela died, and Jobab the son of Zerah of Bozrah reigned in his stead.
verses.bsb: When Bela died, Jobab son of Zerah from Bozrah reigned in his place.

verses.esv: Jobab died, and Husham of the land of the Temanites reigned in his place.
verses.kjv: And Jobab died, and Husham of the land of Temani reigned in his stead.
verses.bsb: When Jobab died, Husham from the land of the Temanites reigned in his place.

verses.esv: Husham died, and Hadad the son of Bedad, who defeated Midian in the country of Moab, reigned in his place, the name of his city being Avith.
verses.kjv: And Husham died, and Hadad the son of Bedad, who smote Midian in the field of Moab, reigned in his stead: and the name of his city was Avith.
verses.bsb: When Husham died, Hadad son of Bedad, who defeated Midian in the country of Moab, reigned in his place. And the name of his city was Avith.

verses.esv: Hadad died, and Samlah of Masrekah reigned in his place.
verses.kjv: And Hadad died, and Samlah of Masrekah reigned in his stead.
verses.bsb: When Hadad died, Samlah from Masrekah reigned in his place.

verses.esv: Samlah died, and Shaul of Rehoboth on the Euphrates reigned in his place.
verses.kjv: And Samlah died, and Saul of Rehoboth by the river reigned in his stead.
verses.bsb: When Samlah died, Shaul from Rehoboth on the Euphrates reigned in his place.

verses.esv: Shaul died, and Baal-hanan the son of Achbor reigned in his place.
verses.kjv: And Saul died, and Baal-hanan the son of Achbor reigned in his stead.
verses.bsb: When Shaul died, Baal-hanan son of Achbor reigned in his place.

verses.esv: Baal-hanan the son of Achbor died, and Hadar reigned in his place, the name of his city being Pau; his wife's name was Mehetabel, the daughter of Matred, daughter of Mezahab.
verses.kjv: And Baal-hanan the son of Achbor died, and Hadar reigned in his stead: and the name of his city was Pau; and his wife’s name was Mehetabel, the daughter of Matred, the daughter of Mezahab.
verses.bsb: When Baal-hanan son of Achbor died, Hadad reigned in his place. His city was named Pau, and his wife’s name was Mehetabel daughter of Matred, the daughter of Me-zahab.

verses.esv: These are the names of the chiefs of Esau, according to their clans and their dwelling places, by their names: the chiefs Timna, Alvah, Jetheth,
verses.kjv: And these are the names of the dukes that came of Esau, according to their families, after their places, by their names; duke Timnah, duke Alvah, duke Jetheth,
verses.bsb: These are the names of Esau’s chiefs, according to their families and regions, by their names: Chiefs Timna, Alvah, Jetheth,

verses.esv: Oholibamah, Elah, Pinon,
verses.kjv: Duke Aholibamah, duke Elah, duke Pinon,
verses.bsb: Oholibamah, Elah, Pinon,

verses.esv: Kenaz, Teman, Mibzar,
verses.kjv: Duke Kenaz, duke Teman, duke Mibzar,
verses.bsb: Kenaz, Teman, Mibzar,

verses.esv: Magdiel, and Iram; these are the chiefs of Edom (that is, Esau, the father of Edom), according to their dwelling places in the land of their possession.
verses.kjv: Duke Magdiel, duke Iram: these be the dukes of Edom, according to their habitations in the land of their possession: he is Esau the father of the Edomites.
verses.bsb: Magdiel, and Iram. These were the chiefs of Edom, according to their settlements in the land they possessed. Esau was the father of the Edomites.

verses.esv: Jacob lived in the land of his father's sojournings, in the land of Canaan.
verses.kjv: AND Jacob dwelt in the land wherein his father was a stranger, in the land of Canaan.
verses.bsb: Now Jacob lived in the land where his father had resided, the land of Canaan.

verses.esv: These are the generations of Jacob. Joseph, being seventeen years old, was pasturing the flock with his brothers. He was a boy with the sons of Bilhah and Zilpah, his father's wives. And Joseph brought a bad report of them to their father.
verses.kjv: These are the generations of Jacob. Joseph, being seventeen years old, was feeding the flock with his brethren; and the lad was with the sons of Bilhah, and with the sons of Zilpah, his father’s wives: and Joseph brought unto his father their evil report.
verses.bsb: This is the account of Jacob. When Joseph was seventeen years old, he was tending the flock with his brothers, the sons of his father’s wives Bilhah and Zilpah, and he brought their father a bad report about them.

verses.esv: Now Israel loved Joseph more than any other of his sons, because he was the son of his old age. And he made him a robe of many colors.
verses.kjv: Now Israel loved Joseph more than all his children, because he was the son of his old age: and he made him a coat of many colours.
verses.bsb: Now Israel loved Joseph more than his other sons, because Joseph had been born to him in his old age; so he made him a robe of many colors.

verses.esv: But when his brothers saw that their father loved him more than all his brothers, they hated him and could not speak peacefully to him.
verses.kjv: And when his brethren saw that their father loved him more than all his brethren, they hated him, and could not speak peaceably unto him.
verses.bsb: When Joseph’s brothers saw that their father loved him more than any of them, they hated him and could not speak a kind word to him.

verses.esv: Now Joseph had a dream, and when he told it to his brothers they hated him even more.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Joseph dreamed a dream, and he told it his brethren: and they hated him yet the more.
verses.bsb: Then Joseph had a dream, and when he told it to his brothers, they hated him even more.

verses.esv: He said to them, “Hear this dream that I have dreamed:
verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Hear, I pray you, this dream which I have dreamed:
verses.bsb: He said to them, “Listen to this dream I had:

verses.esv: Behold, we were binding sheaves in the field, and behold, my sheaf arose and stood upright. And behold, your sheaves gathered around it and bowed down to my sheaf.”
verses.kjv: For, behold, we were binding sheaves in the field, and, lo, my sheaf arose, and also stood upright; and, behold, your sheaves stood round about, and made obeisance to my sheaf.
verses.bsb: We were binding sheaves of grain in the field, and suddenly my sheaf rose and stood upright, while your sheaves gathered around and bowed down to mine.”

verses.esv: His brothers said to him, “Are you indeed to reign over us? Or are you indeed to rule over us?” So they hated him even more for his dreams and for his words.
verses.kjv: And his brethren said to him, Shalt thou indeed reign over us? or shalt thou indeed have dominion over us? And they hated him yet the more for his dreams, and for his words.
verses.bsb: “Do you intend to reign over us?” his brothers asked. “Will you actually rule us?” So they hated him even more because of his dream and his statements.

verses.esv: Then he dreamed another dream and told it to his brothers and said, “Behold, I have dreamed another dream. Behold, the sun, the moon, and eleven stars were bowing down to me.”
verses.kjv: ¶ And he dreamed yet another dream, and told it his brethren, and said, Behold, I have dreamed a dream more; and, behold, the sun and the moon and the eleven stars made obeisance to me.
verses.bsb: Then Joseph had another dream and told it to his brothers. “Look,” he said, “I had another dream, and this time the sun and moon and eleven stars were bowing down to me.”

verses.esv: But when he told it to his father and to his brothers, his father rebuked him and said to him, “What is this dream that you have dreamed? Shall I and your mother and your brothers indeed come to bow ourselves to the ground before you?”
verses.kjv: And he told it to his father, and to his brethren: and his father rebuked him, and said unto him, What is this dream that thou hast dreamed? Shall I and thy mother and thy brethren indeed come to bow down ourselves to thee to the earth?
verses.bsb: He told his father and brothers, but his father rebuked him and said, “What is this dream that you have had? Will your mother and brothers and I actually come and bow down to the ground before you?”

verses.esv: And his brothers were jealous of him, but his father kept the saying in mind.
verses.kjv: And his brethren envied him; but his father observed the saying.
verses.bsb: And his brothers were jealous of him, but his father kept in mind what he had said.

verses.esv: Now his brothers went to pasture their father's flock near Shechem.
verses.kjv: ¶ And his brethren went to feed their father’s flock in Shechem.
verses.bsb: Some time later, Joseph’s brothers had gone to pasture their father’s flocks near Shechem.

verses.esv: And Israel said to Joseph, “Are not your brothers pasturing the flock at Shechem? Come, I will send you to them.” And he said to him, “Here I am.”
verses.kjv: And Israel said unto Joseph, Do not thy brethren feed the flock in Shechem? come, and I will send thee unto them. And he said to him, Here am I.
verses.bsb: Israel said to him, “Are not your brothers pasturing the flocks at Shechem? Get ready; I am sending you to them.”  “I am ready,” Joseph replied.

verses.esv: So he said to him, “Go now, see if it is well with your brothers and with the flock, and bring me word.” So he sent him from the Valley of Hebron, and he came to Shechem.
verses.kjv: And he said to him, Go, I pray thee, see whether it be well with thy brethren, and well with the flocks; and bring me word again. So he sent him out of the vale of Hebron, and he came to Shechem.
verses.bsb: Then Israel told him, “Go now and see how your brothers and the flocks are faring, and bring word back to me.”  So he sent him off from the Valley of Hebron. And when Joseph arrived in Shechem,

verses.esv: And a man found him wandering in the fields. And the man asked him, “What are you seeking?”
verses.kjv: ¶ And a certain man found him, and, behold, he was wandering in the field: and the man asked him, saying, What seekest thou?
verses.bsb: a man found him wandering in the field and asked, “What are you looking for?”

verses.esv: “I am seeking my brothers,” he said. “Tell me, please, where they are pasturing the flock.”
verses.kjv: And he said, I seek my brethren: tell me, I pray thee, where they feed their flocks.
verses.bsb: “I am looking for my brothers,” Joseph replied. “Can you please tell me where they are pasturing their flocks?”

verses.esv: And the man said, “They have gone away, for I heard them say, ‘Let us go to Dothan.’” So Joseph went after his brothers and found them at Dothan.
verses.kjv: And the man said, They are departed hence; for I heard them say, Let us go to Dothan. And Joseph went after his brethren, and found them in Dothan.
verses.bsb: “They have moved on from here,” the man answered. “I heard them say, ‘Let us go to Dothan.’” So Joseph set out after his brothers and found them at Dothan.

verses.esv: They saw him from afar, and before he came near to them they conspired against him to kill him.
verses.kjv: And when they saw him afar off, even before he came near unto them, they conspired against him to slay him.
verses.bsb: Now Joseph’s brothers saw him in the distance, and before he arrived, they plotted to kill him.

verses.esv: They said to one another, “Here comes this dreamer.
verses.kjv: And they said one to another, Behold, this dreamer cometh.
verses.bsb: “Here comes that dreamer!” they said to one another.

verses.esv: Come now, let us kill him and throw him into one of the pits. Then we will say that a fierce animal has devoured him, and we will see what will become of his dreams.”
verses.kjv: Come now therefore, and let us slay him, and cast him into some pit, and we will say, Some evil beast hath devoured him: and we shall see what will become of his dreams.
verses.bsb: “Come now, let us kill him and throw him into one of the pits. We can say that a vicious animal has devoured him. Then we shall see what becomes of his dreams!”

verses.esv: But when Reuben heard it, he rescued him out of their hands, saying, “Let us not take his life.”
verses.kjv: And Reuben heard it, and he delivered him out of their hands; and said, Let us not kill him.
verses.bsb: When Reuben heard this, he tried to rescue Joseph from their hands. “Let us not take his life,” he said.

verses.esv: And Reuben said to them, “Shed no blood; throw him into this pit here in the wilderness, but do not lay a hand on him”—that he might rescue him out of their hand to restore him to his father.
verses.kjv: And Reuben said unto them, Shed no blood, but cast him into this pit that is in the wilderness, and lay no hand upon him; that he might rid him out of their hands, to deliver him to his father again.
verses.bsb: “Do not shed his blood. Throw him into this pit in the wilderness, but do not lay a hand on him.” Reuben said this so that he could rescue Joseph from their hands and return him to his father.

verses.esv: So when Joseph came to his brothers, they stripped him of his robe, the robe of many colors that he wore.
verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass, when Joseph was come unto his brethren, that they stript Joseph out of his coat, his coat of many colours that was on him;
verses.bsb: So when Joseph came to his brothers, they stripped him of his robe—the robe of many colors he was wearing—

verses.esv: And they took him and threw him into a pit. The pit was empty; there was no water in it.
verses.kjv: And they took him, and cast him into a pit: and the pit was empty, there was no water in it.
verses.bsb: and they took him and threw him into the pit. Now the pit was empty, with no water in it.

verses.esv: Then they sat down to eat. And looking up they saw a caravan of Ishmaelites coming from Gilead, with their camels bearing gum, balm, and myrrh, on their way to carry it down to Egypt.
verses.kjv: And they sat down to eat bread: and they lifted up their eyes and looked, and, behold, a company of Ishmeelites came from Gilead with their camels bearing spicery and balm and myrrh, going to carry it down to Egypt.
verses.bsb: And as they sat down to eat a meal, they looked up and saw a caravan of Ishmaelites coming from Gilead. Their camels were carrying spices, balm, and myrrh on their way down to Egypt.

verses.esv: Then Judah said to his brothers, “What profit is it if we kill our brother and conceal his blood?
verses.kjv: And Judah said unto his brethren, What profit is it if we slay our brother, and conceal his blood?
verses.bsb: Then Judah said to his brothers, “What profit will we gain if we kill our brother and cover up his blood?

verses.esv: Come, let us sell him to the Ishmaelites, and let not our hand be upon him, for he is our brother, our own flesh.” And his brothers listened to him.
verses.kjv: Come, and let us sell him to the Ishmeelites, and let not our hand be upon him; for he is our brother and our flesh. And his brethren were content.
verses.bsb: Come, let us sell him to the Ishmaelites and not lay a hand on him; for he is our brother, our own flesh.” And they agreed.

verses.esv: Then Midianite traders passed by. And they drew Joseph up and lifted him out of the pit, and sold him to the Ishmaelites for twenty shekels of silver. They took Joseph to Egypt.
verses.kjv: Then there passed by Midianites merchantmen; and they drew and lifted up Joseph out of the pit, and sold Joseph to the Ishmeelites for twenty pieces of silver: and they brought Joseph into Egypt.
verses.bsb: So when the Midianite traders passed by, his brothers pulled Joseph out of the pit and sold him for twenty shekels of silver to the Ishmaelites, who took him to Egypt.

verses.esv: When Reuben returned to the pit and saw that Joseph was not in the pit, he tore his clothes
verses.kjv: ¶ And Reuben returned unto the pit; and, behold, Joseph was not in the pit; and he rent his clothes.
verses.bsb: When Reuben returned to the pit and saw that Joseph was not there, he tore his clothes,

verses.esv: and returned to his brothers and said, “The boy is gone, and I, where shall I go?”
verses.kjv: And he returned unto his brethren, and said, The child is not; and I, whither shall I go?
verses.bsb: returned to his brothers, and said, “The boy is gone! What am I going to do?”

verses.esv: Then they took Joseph's robe and slaughtered a goat and dipped the robe in the blood.
verses.kjv: And they took Joseph’s coat, and killed a kid of the goats, and dipped the coat in the blood;
verses.bsb: Then they took Joseph’s robe, slaughtered a young goat, and dipped the robe in its blood.

verses.esv: And they sent the robe of many colors and brought it to their father and said, “This we have found; please identify whether it is your son's robe or not.”
verses.kjv: And they sent the coat of many colours, and they brought it to their father; and said, This have we found: know now whether it be thy son’s coat or no.
verses.bsb: They sent the robe of many colors to their father and said, “We found this. Examine it to see whether it is your son’s robe or not.”

verses.esv: And he identified it and said, “It is my son's robe. A fierce animal has devoured him. Joseph is without doubt torn to pieces.”
verses.kjv: And he knew it, and said, It is my son’s coat; an evil beast hath devoured him; Joseph is without doubt rent in pieces.
verses.bsb: His father recognized it and said, “It is my son’s robe! A vicious animal has devoured him. Joseph has surely been torn to pieces!”

verses.esv: Then Jacob tore his garments and put sackcloth on his loins and mourned for his son many days.
verses.kjv: And Jacob rent his clothes, and put sackcloth upon his loins, and mourned for his son many days.
verses.bsb: Then Jacob tore his clothes, put sackcloth around his waist, and mourned for his son many days.

verses.esv: All his sons and all his daughters rose up to comfort him, but he refused to be comforted and said, “No, I shall go down to Sheol to my son, mourning.” Thus his father wept for him.
verses.kjv: And all his sons and all his daughters rose up to comfort him; but he refused to be comforted; and he said, For I will go down into the grave unto my son mourning. Thus his father wept for him.
verses.bsb: All his sons and daughters tried to comfort him, but he refused to be comforted. “No,” he said. “I will go down to Sheol mourning for my son.” So his father wept for him.

verses.esv: Meanwhile the Midianites had sold him in Egypt to Potiphar, an officer of Pharaoh, the captain of the guard.
verses.kjv: And the Midianites sold him into Egypt unto Potiphar, an officer of Pharaoh’s, and captain of the guard.
verses.bsb: Meanwhile, the Midianites sold Joseph in Egypt to Potiphar, an officer of Pharaoh and captain of the guard.

verses.esv: It happened at that time that Judah went down from his brothers and turned aside to a certain Adullamite, whose name was Hirah.
verses.kjv: AND it came to pass at that time, that Judah went down from his brethren, and turned in to a certain Adullamite, whose name was Hirah.
verses.bsb: About that time, Judah left his brothers and settled near a man named Hirah, an Adullamite.

verses.esv: There Judah saw the daughter of a certain Canaanite whose name was Shua. He took her and went in to her,
verses.kjv: And Judah saw there a daughter of a certain Canaanite, whose name was Shuah; and he took her, and went in unto her.
verses.bsb: There Judah saw the daughter of a Canaanite man named Shua, and he took her as a wife and slept with her.

verses.esv: and she conceived and bore a son, and he called his name Er.
verses.kjv: And she conceived, and bare a son; and he called his name Er.
verses.bsb: So she conceived and gave birth to a son, and Judah named him Er.

verses.esv: She conceived again and bore a son, and she called his name Onan.
verses.kjv: And she conceived again, and bare a son; and she called his name Onan.
verses.bsb: Again she conceived and gave birth to a son, and she named him Onan.

verses.esv: Yet again she bore a son, and she called his name Shelah. Judah was in Chezib when she bore him.
verses.kjv: And she yet again conceived, and bare a son; and called his name Shelah: and he was at Chezib, when she bare him.
verses.bsb: Then she gave birth to another son and named him Shelah; it was at Chezib that she gave birth to him.

verses.esv: And Judah took a wife for Er his firstborn, and her name was Tamar.
verses.kjv: And Judah took a wife for Er his firstborn, whose name was Tamar.
verses.bsb: Now Judah acquired a wife for Er, his firstborn, and her name was Tamar.

verses.esv: But Er, Judah's firstborn, was wicked in the sight of the LORD, and the LORD put him to death.
verses.kjv: And Er, Judah’s firstborn, was wicked in the sight of the Lord; and the Lord slew him.
verses.bsb: But Er, Judah’s firstborn, was wicked in the sight of the LORD; so the LORD put him to death.

verses.esv: Then Judah said to Onan, “Go in to your brother's wife and perform the duty of a brother-in-law to her, and raise up offspring for your brother.”
verses.kjv: And Judah said unto Onan, Go in unto thy brother’s wife, and marry her, and raise up seed to thy brother.
verses.bsb: Then Judah said to Onan, “Sleep with your brother’s wife. Perform your duty as her brother-in-law and raise up offspring for your brother.”

verses.esv: But Onan knew that the offspring would not be his. So whenever he went in to his brother's wife he would waste the semen on the ground, so as not to give offspring to his brother.
verses.kjv: And Onan knew that the seed should not be his; and it came to pass, when he went in unto his brother’s wife, that he spilled it on the ground, lest that he should give seed to his brother.
verses.bsb: But Onan knew that the offspring would not belong to him; so whenever he would sleep with his brother’s wife, he would spill his seed on the ground so that he would not produce offspring for his brother.

verses.esv: And what he did was wicked in the sight of the LORD, and he put him to death also.
verses.kjv: And the thing which he did displeased the Lord: wherefore he slew him also.
verses.bsb: What he did was wicked in the sight of the LORD, so He put Onan to death as well.

verses.esv: Then Judah said to Tamar his daughter-in-law, “Remain a widow in your father's house, till Shelah my son grows up”—for he feared that he would die, like his brothers. So Tamar went and remained in her father's house.
verses.kjv: Then said Judah to Tamar his daughter in law, Remain a widow at thy father’s house, till Shelah my son be grown: for he said, Lest peradventure he die also, as his brethren did. And Tamar went and dwelt in her father’s house.
verses.bsb: Then Judah said to his daughter-in-law Tamar, “Live as a widow in your father’s house until my son Shelah grows up.” For he thought, “He may die too, like his brothers.” So Tamar went to live in her father’s house.

verses.esv: In the course of time the wife of Judah, Shua's daughter, died. When Judah was comforted, he went up to Timnah to his sheepshearers, he and his friend Hirah the Adullamite.
verses.kjv: ¶ And in process of time the daughter of Shuah Judah’s wife died; and Judah was comforted, and went up unto his sheepshearers to Timnath, he and his friend Hirah the Adullamite.
verses.bsb: After a long time Judah’s wife, the daughter of Shua, died. When Judah had finished mourning, he and his friend Hirah the Adullamite went up to his sheepshearers at Timnah.

verses.esv: And when Tamar was told, “Your father-in-law is going up to Timnah to shear his sheep,”
verses.kjv: And it was told Tamar, saying, Behold thy father in law goeth up to Timnath to shear his sheep.
verses.bsb: When Tamar was told, “Your father-in-law is going up to Timnah to shear his sheep,”

verses.esv: she took off her widow's garments and covered herself with a veil, wrapping herself up, and sat at the entrance to Enaim, which is on the road to Timnah. For she saw that Shelah was grown up, and she had not been given to him in marriage.
verses.kjv: And she put her widow’s garments off from her, and covered her with a vail, and wrapped herself, and sat in an open place, which is by the way to Timnath; for she saw that Shelah was grown, and she was not given unto him to wife.
verses.bsb: she removed her widow’s garments, covered her face with a veil to disguise herself, and sat at the entrance to Enaim, which is on the way to Timnah. For she saw that although Shelah had grown up, she had not been given to him as a wife.

verses.esv: When Judah saw her, he thought she was a prostitute, for she had covered her face.
verses.kjv: When Judah saw her, he thought her to be an harlot; because she had covered her face.
verses.bsb: When Judah saw her, he thought she was a prostitute because she had covered her face.

verses.esv: He turned to her at the roadside and said, “Come, let me come in to you,” for he did not know that she was his daughter-in-law. She said, “What will you give me, that you may come in to me?”
verses.kjv: And he turned unto her by the way, and said, Go to, I pray thee, let me come in unto thee; (for he knew not that she was his daughter in law.) And she said, What wilt thou give me, that thou mayest come in unto me?
verses.bsb: Not realizing that she was his daughter-in-law, he went over to her and said, “Come now, let me sleep with you.”  “What will you give me for sleeping with you?” she inquired.

verses.esv: He answered, “I will send you a young goat from the flock.” And she said, “If you give me a pledge, until you send it—”
verses.kjv: And he said, I will send thee a kid from the flock. And she said, Wilt thou give me a pledge, till thou send it?
verses.bsb: “I will send you a young goat from my flock,” Judah answered.  But she replied, “Only if you leave me something as a pledge until you send it.”

verses.esv: He said, “What pledge shall I give you?” She replied, “Your signet and your cord and your staff that is in your hand.” So he gave them to her and went in to her, and she conceived by him.
verses.kjv: And he said, What pledge shall I give thee? And she said, Thy signet, and thy bracelets, and thy staff that is in thine hand. And he gave it her, and came in unto her, and she conceived by him.
verses.bsb: “What pledge should I give you?” he asked.  She answered, “Your seal and your cord, and the staff in your hand.” So he gave them to her and slept with her, and she became pregnant by him.

verses.esv: Then she arose and went away, and taking off her veil she put on the garments of her widowhood.
verses.kjv: And she arose, and went away, and laid by her vail from her, and put on the garments of her widowhood.
verses.bsb: Then Tamar got up and departed. And she removed her veil and put on her widow’s garments again.

verses.esv: When Judah sent the young goat by his friend the Adullamite to take back the pledge from the woman's hand, he did not find her.
verses.kjv: And Judah sent the kid by the hand of his friend the Adullamite, to receive his pledge from the woman’s hand: but he found her not.
verses.bsb: Now when Judah sent his friend Hirah the Adullamite with the young goat to collect the items he had left with the woman, he could not find her.

verses.esv: And he asked the men of the place, “Where is the cult prostitute who was at Enaim at the roadside?” And they said, “No cult prostitute has been here.”
verses.kjv: Then he asked the men of that place, saying, Where is the harlot, that was openly by the way side? And they said, There was no harlot in this place.
verses.bsb: He asked the men of that place, “Where is the shrine prostitute who was beside the road at Enaim?”  “No shrine prostitute has been here,” they answered.

verses.esv: So he returned to Judah and said, “I have not found her. Also, the men of the place said, ‘No cult prostitute has been here.’”
verses.kjv: And he returned to Judah, and said, I cannot find her; and also the men of the place said, that there was no harlot in this place.
verses.bsb: So Hirah returned to Judah and said, “I could not find her, and furthermore, the men of that place said, ‘No shrine prostitute has been here.’”

verses.esv: And Judah replied, “Let her keep the things as her own, or we shall be laughed at. You see, I sent this young goat, and you did not find her.”
verses.kjv: And Judah said, Let her take it to her, lest we be shamed: behold, I sent this kid, and thou hast not found her.
verses.bsb: “Let her keep the items,” Judah replied. “Otherwise we will become a laughingstock. After all, I did send her this young goat, but you could not find her.”

verses.esv: About three months later Judah was told, “Tamar your daughter-in-law has been immoral. Moreover, she is pregnant by immorality.” And Judah said, “Bring her out, and let her be burned.”
verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass about three months after, that it was told Judah, saying, Tamar thy daughter in law hath played the harlot; and also, behold, she is with child by whoredom. And Judah said, Bring her forth, and let her be burnt.
verses.bsb: About three months later, Judah was told, “Your daughter-in-law Tamar has prostituted herself, and now she is pregnant.”  “Bring her out!” Judah replied. “Let her be burned to death!”

verses.esv: As she was being brought out, she sent word to her father-in-law, “By the man to whom these belong, I am pregnant.” And she said, “Please identify whose these are, the signet and the cord and the staff.”
verses.kjv: When she was brought forth, she sent to her father in law, saying, By the man, whose these are, am I with child: and she said, Discern, I pray thee, whose are these, the signet, and bracelets, and staff.
verses.bsb: As she was being brought out, Tamar sent a message to her father-in-law: “I am pregnant by the man to whom these items belong.” And she added, “Please examine them. Whose seal and cord and staff are these?”

verses.esv: Then Judah identified them and said, “She is more righteous than I, since I did not give her to my son Shelah.” And he did not know her again.
verses.kjv: And Judah acknowledged them, and said, She hath been more righteous than I; because that I gave her not to Shelah my son. And he knew her again no more.
verses.bsb: Judah recognized the items and said, “She is more righteous than I, since I did not give her to my son Shelah.” And he did not have relations with her again.

verses.esv: When the time of her labor came, there were twins in her womb.
verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass in the time of her travail, that, behold, twins were in her womb.
verses.bsb: When the time came for Tamar to give birth, there were twins in her womb.

verses.esv: And when she was in labor, one put out a hand, and the midwife took and tied a scarlet thread on his hand, saying, “This one came out first.”
verses.kjv: And it came to pass, when she travailed, that the one put out his hand: and the midwife took and bound upon his hand a scarlet thread, saying, This came out first.
verses.bsb: And as she was giving birth, one of them put out his hand; so the midwife took a scarlet thread and tied it around his wrist. “This one came out first,” she announced.

verses.esv: But as he drew back his hand, behold, his brother came out. And she said, “What a breach you have made for yourself!” Therefore his name was called Perez.
verses.kjv: And it came to pass, as he drew back his hand, that, behold, his brother came out: and she said, How hast thou broken forth? this breach be upon thee: therefore his name was called Pharez.
verses.bsb: But when he pulled his hand back and his brother came out, she said, “You have broken out first!” So he was named Perez.

verses.esv: Afterward his brother came out with the scarlet thread on his hand, and his name was called Zerah.
verses.kjv: And afterward came out his brother, that had the scarlet thread upon his hand: and his name was called Zarah.
verses.bsb: Then his brother came out with the scarlet thread around his wrist, and he was named Zerah.

verses.esv: Now Joseph had been brought down to Egypt, and Potiphar, an officer of Pharaoh, the captain of the guard, an Egyptian, had bought him from the Ishmaelites who had brought him down there.
verses.kjv: AND Joseph was brought down to Egypt; and Potiphar, an officer of Pharaoh, captain of the guard, an Egyptian, bought him of the hands of the Ishmeelites, which had brought him down thither.
verses.bsb: Meanwhile, Joseph had been taken down to Egypt, where an Egyptian named Potiphar, an officer of Pharaoh and captain of the guard, bought him from the Ishmaelites who had taken him there.

verses.esv: The LORD was with Joseph, and he became a successful man, and he was in the house of his Egyptian master.
verses.kjv: And the Lord was with Joseph, and he was a prosperous man; and he was in the house of his master the Egyptian.
verses.bsb: And the LORD was with Joseph, and he became a successful man, serving in the household of his Egyptian master.

verses.esv: His master saw that the LORD was with him and that the LORD caused all that he did to succeed in his hands.
verses.kjv: And his master saw that the Lord was with him, and that the Lord made all that he did to prosper in his hand.
verses.bsb: When his master saw that the LORD was with him and made him prosper in all he did,

verses.esv: So Joseph found favor in his sight and attended him, and he made him overseer of his house and put him in charge of all that he had.
verses.kjv: And Joseph found grace in his sight, and he served him: and he made him overseer over his house, and all that he had he put into his hand.
verses.bsb: Joseph found favor in his sight and became his personal attendant.  Potiphar put him in charge of his household and entrusted him with everything he owned.

verses.esv: From the time that he made him overseer in his house and over all that he had, the LORD blessed the Egyptian's house for Joseph's sake; the blessing of the LORD was on all that he had, in house and field.
verses.kjv: And it came to pass from the time that he had made him overseer in his house, and over all that he had, that the Lord blessed the Egyptian’s house for Joseph’s sake; and the blessing of the Lord was upon all that he had in the house, and in the field.
verses.bsb: From the time that he put Joseph in charge of his household and all he owned, the LORD blessed the Egyptian’s household on account of him. The LORD’s blessing was on everything he owned, both in his house and in his field.

verses.esv: So he left all that he had in Joseph's charge, and because of him he had no concern about anything but the food he ate. Now Joseph was handsome in form and appearance.
verses.kjv: And he left all that he had in Joseph’s hand; and he knew not ought he had, save the bread which he did eat. And Joseph was a goodly person, and well favoured.
verses.bsb: So Potiphar left all that he owned in Joseph’s care; he did not concern himself with anything except the food he ate.  Now Joseph was well-built and handsome,

verses.esv: And after a time his master's wife cast her eyes on Joseph and said, “Lie with me.”
verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass after these things, that his master’s wife cast her eyes upon Joseph; and she said, Lie with me.
verses.bsb: and after some time his master’s wife cast her eyes upon Joseph and said, “Sleep with me.”

verses.esv: But he refused and said to his master's wife, “Behold, because of me my master has no concern about anything in the house, and he has put everything that he has in my charge.
verses.kjv: But he refused, and said unto his master’s wife, Behold, my master wotteth not what is with me in the house, and he hath committed all that he hath to my hand;
verses.bsb: But he refused. “Look,” he said to his master’s wife, “with me here, my master does not concern himself with anything in his house, and he has entrusted everything he owns to my care.

verses.esv: He is not greater in this house than I am, nor has he kept back anything from me except you, because you are his wife. How then can I do this great wickedness and sin against God?”
verses.kjv: There is none greater in this house than I; neither hath he kept back any thing from me but thee, because thou art his wife: how then can I do this great wickedness, and sin against God?
verses.bsb: No one in this house is greater than I am. He has withheld nothing from me except you, because you are his wife. So how could I do such a great evil and sin against God?”

verses.esv: And as she spoke to Joseph day after day, he would not listen to her, to lie beside her or to be with her.
verses.kjv: And it came to pass, as she spake to Joseph day by day, that he hearkened not unto her, to lie by her, or to be with her.
verses.bsb: Although Potiphar’s wife spoke to Joseph day after day, he refused to go to bed with her or even be near her.

verses.esv: But one day, when he went into the house to do his work and none of the men of the house was there in the house,
verses.kjv: And it came to pass about this time, that Joseph went into the house to do his business; and there was none of the men of the house there within.
verses.bsb: One day, however, Joseph went into the house to attend to his work, and not a single household servant was inside.

verses.esv: she caught him by his garment, saying, “Lie with me.” But he left his garment in her hand and fled and got out of the house.
verses.kjv: And she caught him by his garment, saying, Lie with me: and he left his garment in her hand, and fled, and got him out.
verses.bsb: She grabbed Joseph by his cloak and said, “Sleep with me!” But leaving his cloak in her hand, he escaped and ran outside.

verses.esv: And as soon as she saw that he had left his garment in her hand and had fled out of the house,
verses.kjv: And it came to pass, when she saw that he had left his garment in her hand, and was fled forth,
verses.bsb: When she saw that he had left his cloak in her hand and had run out of the house,

verses.esv: she called to the men of her household and said to them, “See, he has brought among us a Hebrew to laugh at us. He came in to me to lie with me, and I cried out with a loud voice.
verses.kjv: That she called unto the men of her house, and spake unto them, saying, See, he hath brought in an Hebrew unto us to mock us; he came in unto me to lie with me, and I cried with a loud voice:
verses.bsb: she called her household servants. “Look,” she said, “this Hebrew has been brought to us to make sport of us. He came to me so he could sleep with me, but I screamed as loud as I could.

verses.esv: And as soon as he heard that I lifted up my voice and cried out, he left his garment beside me and fled and got out of the house.”
verses.kjv: And it came to pass, when he heard that I lifted up my voice and cried, that he left his garment with me, and fled, and got him out.
verses.bsb: When he heard me scream for help, he left his cloak beside me and ran out of the house.”

verses.esv: Then she laid up his garment by her until his master came home,
verses.kjv: And she laid up his garment by her, until his lord came home.
verses.bsb: So Potiphar’s wife kept Joseph’s cloak beside her until his master came home.

verses.esv: and she told him the same story, saying, “The Hebrew servant, whom you have brought among us, came in to me to laugh at me.
verses.kjv: And she spake unto him according to these words, saying, The Hebrew servant, which thou hast brought unto us, came in unto me to mock me:
verses.bsb: Then she told him the same story: “The Hebrew slave you brought us came to me to make sport of me,

verses.esv: But as soon as I lifted up my voice and cried, he left his garment beside me and fled out of the house.”
verses.kjv: And it came to pass, as I lifted up my voice and cried, that he left his garment with me, and fled out.
verses.bsb: but when I screamed for help, he left his cloak beside me and ran out of the house.”

verses.esv: As soon as his master heard the words that his wife spoke to him, “This is the way your servant treated me,” his anger was kindled.
verses.kjv: And it came to pass, when his master heard the words of his wife, which she spake unto him, saying, After this manner did thy servant to me; that his wrath was kindled.
verses.bsb: When his master heard the story his wife told him, saying, “This is what your slave did to me,” he burned with anger.

verses.esv: And Joseph's master took him and put him into the prison, the place where the king's prisoners were confined, and he was there in prison.
verses.kjv: And Joseph’s master took him, and put him into the prison, a place where the king’s prisoners were bound: and he was there in the prison.
verses.bsb: So Joseph’s master took him and had him thrown into the prison where the king’s prisoners were confined.  While Joseph was there in the prison,

verses.esv: But the LORD was with Joseph and showed him steadfast love and gave him favor in the sight of the keeper of the prison.
verses.kjv: ¶ But the Lord was with Joseph, and shewed him mercy, and gave him favour in the sight of the keeper of the prison.
verses.bsb: the LORD was with him and extended kindness to him, granting him favor in the eyes of the prison warden.

verses.esv: And the keeper of the prison put Joseph in charge of all the prisoners who were in the prison. Whatever was done there, he was the one who did it.
verses.kjv: And the keeper of the prison committed to Joseph’s hand all the prisoners that were in the prison; and whatsoever they did there, he was the doer of it.
verses.bsb: And the warden put all the prisoners under Joseph’s care, so that he was responsible for all that was done in the prison.

verses.esv: The keeper of the prison paid no attention to anything that was in Joseph's charge, because the LORD was with him. And whatever he did, the LORD made it succeed.
verses.kjv: The keeper of the prison looked not to any thing that was under his hand; because the Lord was with him, and that which he did, the Lord made it to prosper.
verses.bsb: The warden did not concern himself with anything under Joseph’s care, because the LORD was with Joseph and gave him success in whatever he did.

verses.esv: Some time after this, the cupbearer of the king of Egypt and his baker committed an offense against their lord the king of Egypt.
verses.kjv: AND it came to pass after these things, that the butler of the king of Egypt and his baker had offended their lord the king of Egypt.
verses.bsb: Some time later, the king’s cupbearer and baker offended their master, the king of Egypt.

verses.esv: And Pharaoh was angry with his two officers, the chief cupbearer and the chief baker,
verses.kjv: And Pharaoh was wroth against two of his officers, against the chief of the butlers, and against the chief of the bakers.
verses.bsb: Pharaoh was angry with his two officers, the chief cupbearer and the chief baker,

verses.esv: and he put them in custody in the house of the captain of the guard, in the prison where Joseph was confined.
verses.kjv: And he put them in ward in the house of the captain of the guard, into the prison, the place where Joseph was bound.
verses.bsb: and imprisoned them in the house of the captain of the guard, the same prison where Joseph was confined.

verses.esv: The captain of the guard appointed Joseph to be with them, and he attended them. They continued for some time in custody.
verses.kjv: And the captain of the guard charged Joseph with them, and he served them: and they continued a season in ward.
verses.bsb: The captain of the guard assigned them to Joseph, and he became their personal attendant.  After they had been in custody for some time,

verses.esv: And one night they both dreamed—the cupbearer and the baker of the king of Egypt, who were confined in the prison—each his own dream, and each dream with its own interpretation.
verses.kjv: ¶ And they dreamed a dream both of them, each man his dream in one night, each man according to the interpretation of his dream, the butler and the baker of the king of Egypt, which were bound in the prison.
verses.bsb: both of these men—the Egyptian king’s cupbearer and baker, who were being held in the prison—had a dream on the same night, and each dream had its own meaning.

verses.esv: When Joseph came to them in the morning, he saw that they were troubled.
verses.kjv: And Joseph came in unto them in the morning, and looked upon them, and, behold, they were sad.
verses.bsb: When Joseph came to them in the morning, he saw that they were distraught.

verses.esv: So he asked Pharaoh's officers who were with him in custody in his master's house, “Why are your faces downcast today?”
verses.kjv: And he asked Pharaoh’s officers that were with him in the ward of his lord’s house, saying, Wherefore look ye so sadly to day?
verses.bsb: So he asked the officials of Pharaoh who were in custody with him in his master’s house, “Why are your faces so downcast today?”

verses.esv: They said to him, “We have had dreams, and there is no one to interpret them.” And Joseph said to them, “Do not interpretations belong to God? Please tell them to me.”
verses.kjv: And they said unto him, We have dreamed a dream, and there is no interpreter of it. And Joseph said unto them, Do not interpretations belong to God? tell me them, I pray you.
verses.bsb: “We both had dreams,” they replied, “but there is no one to interpret them.”  Then Joseph said to them, “Don’t interpretations belong to God? Tell me your dreams.”

verses.esv: So the chief cupbearer told his dream to Joseph and said to him, “In my dream there was a vine before me,
verses.kjv: And the chief butler told his dream to Joseph, and said to him, In my dream, behold, a vine was before me;
verses.bsb: So the chief cupbearer told Joseph his dream: “In my dream there was a vine before me,

verses.esv: and on the vine there were three branches. As soon as it budded, its blossoms shot forth, and the clusters ripened into grapes.
verses.kjv: And in the vine were three branches: and it was as though it budded, and her blossoms shot forth; and the clusters thereof brought forth ripe grapes:
verses.bsb: and on the vine were three branches. As it budded, its blossoms opened and its clusters ripened into grapes.

verses.esv: Pharaoh's cup was in my hand, and I took the grapes and pressed them into Pharaoh's cup and placed the cup in Pharaoh's hand.”
verses.kjv: And Pharaoh’s cup was in my hand: and I took the grapes, and pressed them into Pharaoh’s cup, and I gave the cup into Pharaoh’s hand.
verses.bsb: Pharaoh’s cup was in my hand, and I took the grapes, squeezed them into his cup, and placed the cup in his hand.”

verses.esv: Then Joseph said to him, “This is its interpretation: the three branches are three days.
verses.kjv: And Joseph said unto him, This is the interpretation of it: The three branches are three days:
verses.bsb: Joseph replied, “This is the interpretation: The three branches are three days.

verses.esv: In three days Pharaoh will lift up your head and restore you to your office, and you shall place Pharaoh's cup in his hand as formerly, when you were his cupbearer.
verses.kjv: Yet within three days shall Pharaoh lift up thine head, and restore thee unto thy place: and thou shalt deliver Pharaoh’s cup into his hand, after the former manner when thou wast his butler.
verses.bsb: Within three days Pharaoh will lift up your head and restore your position. You will put Pharaoh’s cup in his hand, just as you did when you were his cupbearer.

verses.esv: Only remember me, when it is well with you, and please do me the kindness to mention me to Pharaoh, and so get me out of this house.
verses.kjv: But think on me when it shall be well with thee, and shew kindness, I pray thee, unto me, and make mention of me unto Pharaoh, and bring me out of this house:
verses.bsb: But when it goes well for you, please remember me and show me kindness by mentioning me to Pharaoh, that he might bring me out of this prison.

verses.esv: For I was indeed stolen out of the land of the Hebrews, and here also I have done nothing that they should put me into the pit.”
verses.kjv: For indeed I was stolen away out of the land of the Hebrews: and here also have I done nothing that they should put me into the dungeon.
verses.bsb: For I was kidnapped from the land of the Hebrews, and even here I have done nothing for which they should have put me in this dungeon.”

verses.esv: When the chief baker saw that the interpretation was favorable, he said to Joseph, “I also had a dream: there were three cake baskets on my head,
verses.kjv: When the chief baker saw that the interpretation was good, he said unto Joseph, I also was in my dream, and, behold, I had three white baskets on my head:
verses.bsb: When the chief baker saw that the interpretation was favorable, he said to Joseph, “I too had a dream: There were three baskets of white bread on my head.

verses.esv: and in the uppermost basket there were all sorts of baked food for Pharaoh, but the birds were eating it out of the basket on my head.”
verses.kjv: And in the uppermost basket there was of all manner of bakemeats for Pharaoh; and the birds did eat them out of the basket upon my head.
verses.bsb: In the top basket were all sorts of baked goods for Pharaoh, but the birds were eating them out of the basket on my head.”

verses.esv: And Joseph answered and said, “This is its interpretation: the three baskets are three days.
verses.kjv: And Joseph answered and said, This is the interpretation thereof: The three baskets are three days:
verses.bsb: Joseph replied, “This is the interpretation: The three baskets are three days.
