verses.esv: In three days Pharaoh will lift up your head—from you!—and hang you on a tree. And the birds will eat the flesh from you.”
verses.kjv: Yet within three days shall Pharaoh lift up thy head from off thee, and shall hang thee on a tree; and the birds shall eat thy flesh from off thee.
verses.bsb: Within three days Pharaoh will lift off your head and hang you on a tree. Then the birds will eat the flesh of your body.”

verses.esv: On the third day, which was Pharaoh's birthday, he made a feast for all his servants and lifted up the head of the chief cupbearer and the head of the chief baker among his servants.
verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass the third day, which was Pharaoh’s birthday, that he made a feast unto all his servants: and he lifted up the head of the chief butler and of the chief baker among his servants.
verses.bsb: On the third day, which was Pharaoh’s birthday, he held a feast for all his officials, and in their presence he lifted up the heads of the chief cupbearer and the chief baker.

verses.esv: He restored the chief cupbearer to his position, and he placed the cup in Pharaoh's hand.
verses.kjv: And he restored the chief butler unto his butlership again; and he gave the cup into Pharaoh’s hand:
verses.bsb: Pharaoh restored the chief cupbearer to his position, so that he once again placed the cup in Pharaoh’s hand.

verses.esv: But he hanged the chief baker, as Joseph had interpreted to them.
verses.kjv: But he hanged the chief baker: as Joseph had interpreted to them.
verses.bsb: But Pharaoh hanged the chief baker, just as Joseph had described to them in his interpretation.

verses.esv: Yet the chief cupbearer did not remember Joseph, but forgot him.
verses.kjv: Yet did not the chief butler remember Joseph, but forgat him.
verses.bsb: The chief cupbearer, however, did not remember Joseph; he forgot all about him.

verses.esv: After two whole years, Pharaoh dreamed that he was standing by the Nile,
verses.kjv: AND it came to pass at the end of two full years, that Pharaoh dreamed: and, behold, he stood by the river.
verses.bsb: After two full years had passed, Pharaoh had a dream: He was standing beside the Nile,

verses.esv: and behold, there came up out of the Nile seven cows, attractive and plump, and they fed in the reed grass.
verses.kjv: And, behold, there came up out of the river seven well favoured kine and fatfleshed; and they fed in a meadow.
verses.bsb: when seven cows, sleek and well-fed, came up from the river and began to graze among the reeds.

verses.esv: And behold, seven other cows, ugly and thin, came up out of the Nile after them, and stood by the other cows on the bank of the Nile.
verses.kjv: And, behold, seven other kine came up after them out of the river, ill favoured and leanfleshed; and stood by the other kine upon the brink of the river.
verses.bsb: After them, seven other cows, sickly and thin, came up from the Nile and stood beside the well-fed cows on the bank of the river.

verses.esv: And the ugly, thin cows ate up the seven attractive, plump cows. And Pharaoh awoke.
verses.kjv: And the ill favoured and leanfleshed kine did eat up the seven well favoured and fat kine. So Pharaoh awoke.
verses.bsb: And the cows that were sickly and thin devoured the seven sleek, well-fed cows.  Then Pharaoh woke up,

verses.esv: And he fell asleep and dreamed a second time. And behold, seven ears of grain, plump and good, were growing on one stalk.
verses.kjv: And he slept and dreamed the second time: and, behold, seven ears of corn came up upon one stalk, rank and good.
verses.bsb: but he fell back asleep and dreamed a second time: Seven heads of grain, plump and ripe, came up on one stalk.

verses.esv: And behold, after them sprouted seven ears, thin and blighted by the east wind.
verses.kjv: And, behold, seven thin ears and blasted with the east wind sprung up after them.
verses.bsb: After them, seven other heads of grain sprouted, thin and scorched by the east wind.

verses.esv: And the thin ears swallowed up the seven plump, full ears. And Pharaoh awoke, and behold, it was a dream.
verses.kjv: And the seven thin ears devoured the seven rank and full ears. And Pharaoh awoke, and, behold, it was a dream.
verses.bsb: And the thin heads of grain swallowed up the seven plump, ripe ones. Then Pharaoh awoke and realized it was a dream.

verses.esv: So in the morning his spirit was troubled, and he sent and called for all the magicians of Egypt and all its wise men. Pharaoh told them his dreams, but there was none who could interpret them to Pharaoh.
verses.kjv: And it came to pass in the morning that his spirit was troubled; and he sent and called for all the magicians of Egypt, and all the wise men thereof: and Pharaoh told them his dream; but there was none that could interpret them unto Pharaoh.
verses.bsb: In the morning his spirit was troubled, so he summoned all the magicians and wise men of Egypt. Pharaoh told them his dreams, but no one could interpret them for him.

verses.esv: Then the chief cupbearer said to Pharaoh, “I remember my offenses today.
verses.kjv: ¶ Then spake the chief butler unto Pharaoh, saying, I do remember my faults this day:
verses.bsb: Then the chief cupbearer said to Pharaoh, “Today I recall my failures.

verses.esv: When Pharaoh was angry with his servants and put me and the chief baker in custody in the house of the captain of the guard,
verses.kjv: Pharaoh was wroth with his servants, and put me in ward in the captain of the guard’s house, both me and the chief baker:
verses.bsb: Pharaoh was once angry with his servants, and he put me and the chief baker in the custody of the captain of the guard.

verses.esv: we dreamed on the same night, he and I, each having a dream with its own interpretation.
verses.kjv: And we dreamed a dream in one night, I and he; we dreamed each man according to the interpretation of his dream.
verses.bsb: One night both the chief baker and I had dreams, and each dream had its own meaning.

verses.esv: A young Hebrew was there with us, a servant of the captain of the guard. When we told him, he interpreted our dreams to us, giving an interpretation to each man according to his dream.
verses.kjv: And there was there with us a young man, an Hebrew, servant to the captain of the guard; and we told him, and he interpreted to us our dreams; to each man according to his dream he did interpret.
verses.bsb: Now a young Hebrew was there with us, a servant of the captain of the guard. We told him our dreams and he interpreted them for us individually.

verses.esv: And as he interpreted to us, so it came about. I was restored to my office, and the baker was hanged.”
verses.kjv: And it came to pass, as he interpreted to us, so it was; me he restored unto mine office, and him he hanged.
verses.bsb: And it happened to us just as he had interpreted: I was restored to my position, and the other man was hanged.”

verses.esv: Then Pharaoh sent and called Joseph, and they quickly brought him out of the pit. And when he had shaved himself and changed his clothes, he came in before Pharaoh.
verses.kjv: ¶ Then Pharaoh sent and called Joseph, and they brought him hastily out of the dungeon: and he shaved himself, and changed his raiment, and came in unto Pharaoh.
verses.bsb: So Pharaoh sent for Joseph, who was quickly brought out of the dungeon. After he had shaved and changed his clothes, he went in before Pharaoh.

verses.esv: And Pharaoh said to Joseph, “I have had a dream, and there is no one who can interpret it. I have heard it said of you that when you hear a dream you can interpret it.”
verses.kjv: And Pharaoh said unto Joseph, I have dreamed a dream, and there is none that can interpret it: and I have heard say of thee, that thou canst understand a dream to interpret it.
verses.bsb: Pharaoh said to Joseph, “I had a dream, and no one can interpret it. But I have heard it said of you that when you hear a dream you can interpret it.”

verses.esv: Joseph answered Pharaoh, “It is not in me; God will give Pharaoh a favorable answer.”
verses.kjv: And Joseph answered Pharaoh, saying, It is not in me: God shall give Pharaoh an answer of peace.
verses.bsb: “I myself cannot do it,” Joseph replied, “but God will give Pharaoh a sound answer.”

verses.esv: Then Pharaoh said to Joseph, “Behold, in my dream I was standing on the banks of the Nile.
verses.kjv: And Pharaoh said unto Joseph, In my dream, behold, I stood upon the bank of the river:
verses.bsb: Then Pharaoh said to Joseph: “In my dream I was standing on the bank of the Nile,

verses.esv: Seven cows, plump and attractive, came up out of the Nile and fed in the reed grass.
verses.kjv: And, behold, there came up out of the river seven kine, fatfleshed and well favoured; and they fed in a meadow:
verses.bsb: when seven cows, well-fed and sleek, came up from the river and began to graze among the reeds.

verses.esv: Seven other cows came up after them, poor and very ugly and thin, such as I had never seen in all the land of Egypt.
verses.kjv: And, behold, seven other kine came up after them, poor and very ill favoured and leanfleshed, such as I never saw in all the land of Egypt for badness:
verses.bsb: After them, seven other cows—sickly, ugly, and thin—came up. I have never seen such ugly cows in all the land of Egypt!

verses.esv: And the thin, ugly cows ate up the first seven plump cows,
verses.kjv: And the lean and the ill favoured kine did eat up the first seven fat kine:
verses.bsb: Then the thin, ugly cows devoured the seven well-fed cows that were there first.

verses.esv: but when they had eaten them no one would have known that they had eaten them, for they were still as ugly as at the beginning. Then I awoke.
verses.kjv: And when they had eaten them up, it could not be known that they had eaten them; but they were still ill favoured, as at the beginning. So I awoke.
verses.bsb: When they had devoured them, however, no one could tell that they had done so; their appearance was as ugly as it had been before. Then I awoke.

verses.esv: I also saw in my dream seven ears growing on one stalk, full and good.
verses.kjv: And I saw in my dream, and, behold, seven ears came up in one stalk, full and good:
verses.bsb: In my dream I also saw seven heads of grain, plump and ripe, growing on a single stalk.

verses.esv: Seven ears, withered, thin, and blighted by the east wind, sprouted after them,
verses.kjv: And, behold, seven ears, withered, thin, and blasted with the east wind, sprung up after them:
verses.bsb: After them, seven other heads of grain sprouted—withered, thin, and scorched by the east wind.

verses.esv: and the thin ears swallowed up the seven good ears. And I told it to the magicians, but there was no one who could explain it to me.”
verses.kjv: And the thin ears devoured the seven good ears: and I told this unto the magicians; but there was none that could declare it to me.
verses.bsb: And the thin heads of grain swallowed the seven plump ones.  I told this dream to the magicians, but no one could explain it to me.”

verses.esv: Then Joseph said to Pharaoh, “The dreams of Pharaoh are one; God has revealed to Pharaoh what he is about to do.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Joseph said unto Pharaoh, The dream of Pharaoh is one: God hath shewed Pharaoh what he is about to do.
verses.bsb: At this, Joseph said to Pharaoh, “The dreams of Pharaoh are one and the same. God has revealed to Pharaoh what He is about to do.

verses.esv: The seven good cows are seven years, and the seven good ears are seven years; the dreams are one.
verses.kjv: The seven good kine are seven years; and the seven good ears are seven years: the dream is one.
verses.bsb: The seven good cows are seven years, and the seven ripe heads of grain are seven years. The dreams have the same meaning.

verses.esv: The seven lean and ugly cows that came up after them are seven years, and the seven empty ears blighted by the east wind are also seven years of famine.
verses.kjv: And the seven thin and ill favoured kine that came up after them are seven years; and the seven empty ears blasted with the east wind shall be seven years of famine.
verses.bsb: Moreover, the seven thin, ugly cows that came up after them are seven years, and so are the seven worthless heads of grain scorched by the east wind—they are seven years of famine.

verses.esv: It is as I told Pharaoh; God has shown to Pharaoh what he is about to do.
verses.kjv: This is the thing which I have spoken unto Pharaoh: What God is about to do he sheweth unto Pharaoh.
verses.bsb: It is just as I said to Pharaoh: God has shown Pharaoh what He is about to do.

verses.esv: There will come seven years of great plenty throughout all the land of Egypt,
verses.kjv: Behold, there come seven years of great plenty throughout all the land of Egypt:
verses.bsb: Behold, seven years of great abundance are coming throughout the land of Egypt,

verses.esv: but after them there will arise seven years of famine, and all the plenty will be forgotten in the land of Egypt. The famine will consume the land,
verses.kjv: And there shall arise after them seven years of famine; and all the plenty shall be forgotten in the land of Egypt; and the famine shall consume the land;
verses.bsb: but seven years of famine will follow them. Then all the abundance in the land of Egypt will be forgotten, and the famine will devastate the land.

verses.esv: and the plenty will be unknown in the land by reason of the famine that will follow, for it will be very severe.
verses.kjv: And the plenty shall not be known in the land by reason of that famine following; for it shall be very grievous.
verses.bsb: The abundance in the land will not be remembered, since the famine that follows it will be so severe.

verses.esv: And the doubling of Pharaoh's dream means that the thing is fixed by God, and God will shortly bring it about.
verses.kjv: And for that the dream was doubled unto Pharaoh twice; it is because the thing is established by God, and God will shortly bring it to pass.
verses.bsb: Moreover, because the dream was given to Pharaoh in two versions, the matter has been decreed by God, and He will carry it out shortly.

verses.esv: Now therefore let Pharaoh select a discerning and wise man, and set him over the land of Egypt.
verses.kjv: Now therefore let Pharaoh look out a man discreet and wise, and set him over the land of Egypt.
verses.bsb: Now, therefore, Pharaoh should look for a discerning and wise man and set him over the land of Egypt.

verses.esv: Let Pharaoh proceed to appoint overseers over the land and take one-fifth of the produce of the land of Egypt during the seven plentiful years.
verses.kjv: Let Pharaoh do this, and let him appoint officers over the land, and take up the fifth part of the land of Egypt in the seven plenteous years.
verses.bsb: Let Pharaoh take action and appoint commissioners over the land to take a fifth of the harvest of Egypt during the seven years of abundance.

verses.esv: And let them gather all the food of these good years that are coming and store up grain under the authority of Pharaoh for food in the cities, and let them keep it.
verses.kjv: And let them gather all the food of those good years that come, and lay up corn under the hand of Pharaoh, and let them keep food in the cities.
verses.bsb: Under the authority of Pharaoh, let them collect all the excess food from these good years, that they may come and lay up the grain to be preserved as food in the cities.

verses.esv: That food shall be a reserve for the land against the seven years of famine that are to occur in the land of Egypt, so that the land may not perish through the famine.”
verses.kjv: And that food shall be for store to the land against the seven years of famine, which shall be in the land of Egypt; that the land perish not through the famine.
verses.bsb: This food will be a reserve for the land during the seven years of famine to come upon the land of Egypt. Then the country will not perish in the famine.”

verses.esv: This proposal pleased Pharaoh and all his servants.
verses.kjv: ¶ And the thing was good in the eyes of Pharaoh, and in the eyes of all his servants.
verses.bsb: This proposal pleased Pharaoh and all his officials.

verses.esv: And Pharaoh said to his servants, “Can we find a man like this, in whom is the Spirit of God?”
verses.kjv: And Pharaoh said unto his servants, Can we find such a one as this is, a man in whom the Spirit of God is?
verses.bsb: So Pharaoh asked them, “Can we find anyone like this man, in whom the Spirit of God abides?”

verses.esv: Then Pharaoh said to Joseph, “Since God has shown you all this, there is none so discerning and wise as you are.
verses.kjv: And Pharaoh said unto Joseph, Forasmuch as God hath shewed thee all this, there is none so discreet and wise as thou art:
verses.bsb: Then Pharaoh said to Joseph, “Since God has made all this known to you, there is no one as discerning and wise as you.

verses.esv: You shall be over my house, and all my people shall order themselves as you command. Only as regards the throne will I be greater than you.”
verses.kjv: Thou shalt be over my house, and according unto thy word shall all my people be ruled: only in the throne will I be greater than thou.
verses.bsb: You shall be in charge of my house, and all my people are to obey your commands. Only with regard to the throne will I be greater than you.”

verses.esv: And Pharaoh said to Joseph, “See, I have set you over all the land of Egypt.”
verses.kjv: And Pharaoh said unto Joseph, See, I have set thee over all the land of Egypt.
verses.bsb: Pharaoh also told Joseph, “I hereby place you over all the land of Egypt.”

verses.esv: Then Pharaoh took his signet ring from his hand and put it on Joseph's hand, and clothed him in garments of fine linen and put a gold chain about his neck.
verses.kjv: And Pharaoh took off his ring from his hand, and put it upon Joseph’s hand, and arrayed him in vestures of fine linen, and put a gold chain about his neck;
verses.bsb: Then Pharaoh removed the signet ring from his finger, put it on Joseph’s finger, clothed him in garments of fine linen, and placed a gold chain around his neck.

verses.esv: And he made him ride in his second chariot. And they called out before him, “Bow the knee!” Thus he set him over all the land of Egypt.
verses.kjv: And he made him to ride in the second chariot which he had; and they cried before him, Bow the knee: and he made him ruler over all the land of Egypt.
verses.bsb: He had Joseph ride in his second chariot, with men calling out before him, “Bow the knee!” So he placed him over all the land of Egypt.

verses.esv: Moreover, Pharaoh said to Joseph, “I am Pharaoh, and without your consent no one shall lift up hand or foot in all the land of Egypt.”
verses.kjv: And Pharaoh said unto Joseph, I am Pharaoh, and without thee shall no man lift up his hand or foot in all the land of Egypt.
verses.bsb: And Pharaoh declared to Joseph, “I am Pharaoh, but without your permission, no one in all the land of Egypt shall lift his hand or foot.”

verses.esv: And Pharaoh called Joseph's name Zaphenath-paneah. And he gave him in marriage Asenath, the daughter of Potiphera priest of On. So Joseph went out over the land of Egypt.
verses.kjv: And Pharaoh called Joseph’s name Zaphnath-paaneah; and he gave him to wife Asenath the daughter of Poti-pherah priest of On. And Joseph went out over all the land of Egypt.
verses.bsb: Pharaoh gave Joseph the name Zaphenath-paneah, and he gave him Asenath daughter of Potiphera, priest of On, to be his wife. And Joseph took charge of all the land of Egypt.

verses.esv: Joseph was thirty years old when he entered the service of Pharaoh king of Egypt. And Joseph went out from the presence of Pharaoh and went through all the land of Egypt.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Joseph was thirty years old when he stood before Pharaoh king of Egypt. And Joseph went out from the presence of Pharaoh, and went throughout all the land of Egypt.
verses.bsb: Now Joseph was thirty years old when he entered the service of Pharaoh king of Egypt. And Joseph left Pharaoh’s presence and traveled throughout the land of Egypt.

verses.esv: During the seven plentiful years the earth produced abundantly,
verses.kjv: And in the seven plenteous years the earth brought forth by handfuls.
verses.bsb: During the seven years of abundance, the land brought forth bountifully.

verses.esv: and he gathered up all the food of these seven years, which occurred in the land of Egypt, and put the food in the cities. He put in every city the food from the fields around it.
verses.kjv: And he gathered up all the food of the seven years, which were in the land of Egypt, and laid up the food in the cities: the food of the field, which was round about every city, laid he up in the same.
verses.bsb: During those seven years, Joseph collected all the excess food in the land of Egypt and stored it in the cities. In every city he laid up the food from the fields around it.

verses.esv: And Joseph stored up grain in great abundance, like the sand of the sea, until he ceased to measure it, for it could not be measured.
verses.kjv: And Joseph gathered corn as the sand of the sea, very much, until he left numbering; for it was without number.
verses.bsb: So Joseph stored up grain in such abundance, like the sand of the sea, that he stopped keeping track of it; for it was beyond measure.

verses.esv: Before the year of famine came, two sons were born to Joseph. Asenath, the daughter of Potiphera priest of On, bore them to him.
verses.kjv: And unto Joseph were born two sons before the years of famine came, which Asenath the daughter of Poti-pherah priest of On bare unto him.
verses.bsb: Before the years of famine arrived, two sons were born to Joseph by Asenath daughter of Potiphera, priest of On.

verses.esv: Joseph called the name of the firstborn Manasseh. “For,” he said, “God has made me forget all my hardship and all my father's house.”
verses.kjv: And Joseph called the name of the firstborn Manasseh: For God, said he, hath made me forget all my toil, and all my father’s house.
verses.bsb: Joseph named the firstborn Manasseh, saying, “God has made me forget all my hardship and all my father’s household.”

verses.esv: The name of the second he called Ephraim, “For God has made me fruitful in the land of my affliction.”
verses.kjv: And the name of the second called he Ephraim: For God hath caused me to be fruitful in the land of my affliction.
verses.bsb: And the second son he named Ephraim, saying, “God has made me fruitful in the land of my affliction.”

verses.esv: The seven years of plenty that occurred in the land of Egypt came to an end,
verses.kjv: ¶ And the seven years of plenteousness, that was in the land of Egypt, were ended.
verses.bsb: When the seven years of abundance in the land of Egypt came to an end,

verses.esv: and the seven years of famine began to come, as Joseph had said. There was famine in all lands, but in all the land of Egypt there was bread.
verses.kjv: And the seven years of dearth began to come, according as Joseph had said: and the dearth was in all lands; but in all the land of Egypt there was bread.
verses.bsb: the seven years of famine began, just as Joseph had said. And although there was famine in every country, there was food throughout the land of Egypt.

verses.esv: When all the land of Egypt was famished, the people cried to Pharaoh for bread. Pharaoh said to all the Egyptians, “Go to Joseph. What he says to you, do.”
verses.kjv: And when all the land of Egypt was famished, the people cried to Pharaoh for bread: and Pharaoh said unto all the Egyptians, Go unto Joseph; what he saith to you, do.
verses.bsb: When extreme hunger came to all the land of Egypt and the people cried out to Pharaoh for food, he told all the Egyptians, “Go to Joseph and do whatever he tells you.”

verses.esv: So when the famine had spread over all the land, Joseph opened all the storehouses and sold to the Egyptians, for the famine was severe in the land of Egypt.
verses.kjv: And the famine was over all the face of the earth: And Joseph opened all the storehouses, and sold unto the Egyptians; and the famine waxed sore in the land of Egypt.
verses.bsb: When the famine had spread over all the land, Joseph opened up all the storehouses and sold grain to the Egyptians; for the famine was severe in the land of Egypt.

verses.esv: Moreover, all the earth came to Egypt to Joseph to buy grain, because the famine was severe over all the earth.
verses.kjv: And all countries came into Egypt to Joseph for to buy corn; because that the famine was so sore in all lands.
verses.bsb: And every nation came to Joseph in Egypt to buy grain, because the famine was severe over all the earth.

verses.esv: When Jacob learned that there was grain for sale in Egypt, he said to his sons, “Why do you look at one another?”
verses.kjv: NOW when Jacob saw that there was corn in Egypt, Jacob said unto his sons, Why do ye look one upon another?
verses.bsb: When Jacob learned that there was grain in Egypt, he said to his sons, “Why are you staring at one another?”

verses.esv: And he said, “Behold, I have heard that there is grain for sale in Egypt. Go down and buy grain for us there, that we may live and not die.”
verses.kjv: And he said, Behold, I have heard that there is corn in Egypt: get you down thither, and buy for us from thence; that we may live, and not die.
verses.bsb: “Look,” he added, “I have heard that there is grain in Egypt. Go down there and buy some for us, so that we may live and not die.”

verses.esv: So ten of Joseph's brothers went down to buy grain in Egypt.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Joseph’s ten brethren went down to buy corn in Egypt.
verses.bsb: So ten of Joseph’s brothers went down to buy grain from Egypt.

verses.esv: But Jacob did not send Benjamin, Joseph's brother, with his brothers, for he feared that harm might happen to him.
verses.kjv: But Benjamin, Joseph’s brother, Jacob sent not with his brethren; for he said, Lest peradventure mischief befall him.
verses.bsb: But Jacob did not send Joseph’s brother Benjamin with his brothers, for he said, “I am afraid that harm might befall him.”

verses.esv: Thus the sons of Israel came to buy among the others who came, for the famine was in the land of Canaan.
verses.kjv: And the sons of Israel came to buy corn among those that came: for the famine was in the land of Canaan.
verses.bsb: So the sons of Israel were among those who came to buy grain, since the famine had also spread to the land of Canaan.

verses.esv: Now Joseph was governor over the land. He was the one who sold to all the people of the land. And Joseph's brothers came and bowed themselves before him with their faces to the ground.
verses.kjv: And Joseph was the governor over the land, and he it was that sold to all the people of the land: and Joseph’s brethren came, and bowed down themselves before him with their faces to the earth.
verses.bsb: Now Joseph was the ruler of the land; he was the one who sold grain to all its people. So when his brothers arrived, they bowed down before him with their faces to the ground.

verses.esv: Joseph saw his brothers and recognized them, but he treated them like strangers and spoke roughly to them. “Where do you come from?” he said. They said, “From the land of Canaan, to buy food.”
verses.kjv: And Joseph saw his brethren, and he knew them, but made himself strange unto them, and spake roughly unto them; and he said unto them, Whence come ye? And they said, From the land of Canaan to buy food.
verses.bsb: And when Joseph saw his brothers, he recognized them, but he treated them as strangers and spoke harshly to them. “Where have you come from?” he asked.  “From the land of Canaan,” they replied. “We are here to buy food.”

verses.esv: And Joseph recognized his brothers, but they did not recognize him.
verses.kjv: And Joseph knew his brethren, but they knew not him.
verses.bsb: Although Joseph recognized his brothers, they did not recognize him.

verses.esv: And Joseph remembered the dreams that he had dreamed of them. And he said to them, “You are spies; you have come to see the nakedness of the land.”
verses.kjv: And Joseph remembered the dreams which he dreamed of them, and said unto them, Ye are spies; to see the nakedness of the land ye are come.
verses.bsb: Joseph remembered his dreams about them and said, “You are spies! You have come to see if our land is vulnerable.”

verses.esv: They said to him, “No, my lord, your servants have come to buy food.
verses.kjv: And they said unto him, Nay, my lord, but to buy food are thy servants come.
verses.bsb: “Not so, my lord,” they replied. “Your servants have come to buy food.

verses.esv: We are all sons of one man. We are honest men. Your servants have never been spies.”
verses.kjv: We are all one man’s sons; we are true men, thy servants are no spies.
verses.bsb: We are all sons of one man. Your servants are honest men, not spies.”

verses.esv: He said to them, “No, it is the nakedness of the land that you have come to see.”
verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Nay, but to see the nakedness of the land ye are come.
verses.bsb: “No,” he told them. “You have come to see if our land is vulnerable.”

verses.esv: And they said, “We, your servants, are twelve brothers, the sons of one man in the land of Canaan, and behold, the youngest is this day with our father, and one is no more.”
verses.kjv: And they said, Thy servants are twelve brethren, the sons of one man in the land of Canaan; and, behold, the youngest is this day with our father, and one is not.
verses.bsb: But they answered, “Your servants are twelve brothers, the sons of one man in the land of Canaan. The youngest is now with our father, and one is no more.”

verses.esv: But Joseph said to them, “It is as I said to you. You are spies.
verses.kjv: And Joseph said unto them, That is it that I spake unto you, saying, Ye are spies:
verses.bsb: Then Joseph declared, “Just as I said, you are spies!

verses.esv: By this you shall be tested: by the life of Pharaoh, you shall not go from this place unless your youngest brother comes here.
verses.kjv: Hereby ye shall be proved: By the life of Pharaoh ye shall not go forth hence, except your youngest brother come hither.
verses.bsb: And this is how you will be tested: As surely as Pharaoh lives, you shall not leave this place unless your youngest brother comes here.

verses.esv: Send one of you, and let him bring your brother, while you remain confined, that your words may be tested, whether there is truth in you. Or else, by the life of Pharaoh, surely you are spies.”
verses.kjv: Send one of you, and let him fetch your brother, and ye shall be kept in prison, that your words may be proved, whether there be any truth in you: or else by the life of Pharaoh surely ye are spies.
verses.bsb: Send one of your number to get your brother; the rest of you will be confined so that the truth of your words may be tested. If they are untrue, then as surely as Pharaoh lives, you are spies!”

verses.esv: And he put them all together in custody for three days.
verses.kjv: And he put them all together into ward three days.
verses.bsb: So Joseph imprisoned them for three days,

verses.esv: On the third day Joseph said to them, “Do this and you will live, for I fear God:
verses.kjv: And Joseph said unto them the third day, This do, and live; for I fear God:
verses.bsb: and on the third day he said to them, “I fear God. So do this and you will live:

verses.esv: if you are honest men, let one of your brothers remain confined where you are in custody, and let the rest go and carry grain for the famine of your households,
verses.kjv: If ye be true men, let one of your brethren be bound in the house of your prison: go ye, carry corn for the famine of your houses:
verses.bsb: If you are honest, leave one of your brothers in custody while the rest of you go and take back grain to relieve the hunger of your households.

verses.esv: and bring your youngest brother to me. So your words will be verified, and you shall not die.” And they did so.
verses.kjv: But bring your youngest brother unto me; so shall your words be verified, and ye shall not die. And they did so.
verses.bsb: Then bring your youngest brother to me so that your words can be verified, that you may not die.”  And to this they consented.

verses.esv: Then they said to one another, “In truth we are guilty concerning our brother, in that we saw the distress of his soul, when he begged us and we did not listen. That is why this distress has come upon us.”
verses.kjv: ¶ And they said one to another, We are verily guilty concerning our brother, in that we saw the anguish of his soul, when he besought us, and we would not hear; therefore is this distress come upon us.
verses.bsb: Then they said to one another, “Surely we are being punished because of our brother. We saw his anguish when he pleaded with us, but we would not listen. That is why this distress has come upon us.”

verses.esv: And Reuben answered them, “Did I not tell you not to sin against the boy? But you did not listen. So now there comes a reckoning for his blood.”
verses.kjv: And Reuben answered them, saying, Spake I not unto you, saying, Do not sin against the child; and ye would not hear? therefore, behold, also his blood is required.
verses.bsb: And Reuben responded, “Didn’t I tell you not to sin against the boy? But you would not listen. Now we must account for his blood!”

verses.esv: They did not know that Joseph understood them, for there was an interpreter between them.
verses.kjv: And they knew not that Joseph understood them; for he spake unto them by an interpreter.
verses.bsb: They did not realize that Joseph understood them, since there was an interpreter between them.

verses.esv: Then he turned away from them and wept. And he returned to them and spoke to them. And he took Simeon from them and bound him before their eyes.
verses.kjv: And he turned himself about from them, and wept; and returned to them again, and communed with them, and took from them Simeon, and bound him before their eyes.
verses.bsb: And he turned away from them and wept. When he turned back and spoke to them, he took Simeon from them and had him bound before their eyes.

verses.esv: And Joseph gave orders to fill their bags with grain, and to replace every man's money in his sack, and to give them provisions for the journey. This was done for them.
verses.kjv: ¶ Then Joseph commanded to fill their sacks with corn, and to restore every man’s money into his sack, and to give them provision for the way: and thus did he unto them.
verses.bsb: Then Joseph gave orders to fill their bags with grain, to return each man’s silver to his sack, and to give them provisions for their journey. This order was carried out,

verses.esv: Then they loaded their donkeys with their grain and departed.
verses.kjv: And they laded their asses with the corn, and departed thence.
verses.bsb: and they loaded the grain on their donkeys and departed.

verses.esv: And as one of them opened his sack to give his donkey fodder at the lodging place, he saw his money in the mouth of his sack.
verses.kjv: And as one of them opened his sack to give his ass provender in the inn, he espied his money; for, behold, it was in his sack’s mouth.
verses.bsb: At the place where they lodged for the night, one of them opened his sack to get feed for his donkey, and he saw his silver in the mouth of the sack.

verses.esv: He said to his brothers, “My money has been put back; here it is in the mouth of my sack!” At this their hearts failed them, and they turned trembling to one another, saying, “What is this that God has done to us?”
verses.kjv: And he said unto his brethren, My money is restored; and, lo, it is even in my sack: and their heart failed them, and they were afraid, saying one to another, What is this that God hath done unto us?
verses.bsb: “My silver has been returned!” he said to his brothers. “It is here in my sack.”  Their hearts sank, and trembling, they turned to one another and said, “What is this that God has done to us?”

verses.esv: When they came to Jacob their father in the land of Canaan, they told him all that had happened to them, saying,
verses.kjv: ¶ And they came unto Jacob their father unto the land of Canaan, and told him all that befell unto them; saying,
verses.bsb: When they reached their father Jacob in the land of Canaan, they described to him all that had happened to them:

verses.esv: “The man, the lord of the land, spoke roughly to us and took us to be spies of the land.
verses.kjv: The man, who is the lord of the land, spake roughly to us, and took us for spies of the country.
verses.bsb: “The man who is lord of the land spoke harshly to us and accused us of spying on the country.

verses.esv: But we said to him, ‘We are honest men; we have never been spies.
verses.kjv: And we said unto him, We are true men; we are no spies:
verses.bsb: But we told him, ‘We are honest men, not spies.

verses.esv: We are twelve brothers, sons of our father. One is no more, and the youngest is this day with our father in the land of Canaan.’
verses.kjv: We be twelve brethren, sons of our father; one is not, and the youngest is this day with our father in the land of Canaan.
verses.bsb: We are twelve brothers, sons of one father. One is no more, and the youngest is now with our father in the land of Canaan.’

verses.esv: Then the man, the lord of the land, said to us, ‘By this I shall know that you are honest men: leave one of your brothers with me, and take grain for the famine of your households, and go your way.
verses.kjv: And the man, the lord of the country, said unto us, Hereby shall I know that ye are true men; leave one of your brethren here with me, and take food for the famine of your households, and be gone:
verses.bsb: Then the man who is lord of the land said to us, ‘This is how I will know whether you are honest: Leave one brother with me, take food to relieve the hunger of your households, and go.

verses.esv: Bring your youngest brother to me. Then I shall know that you are not spies but honest men, and I will deliver your brother to you, and you shall trade in the land.’”
verses.kjv: And bring your youngest brother unto me: then shall I know that ye are no spies, but that ye are true men: so will I deliver you your brother, and ye shall traffick in the land.
verses.bsb: But bring your youngest brother back to me so I will know that you are not spies but honest men. Then I will give your brother back to you, and you can trade in the land.’”

verses.esv: As they emptied their sacks, behold, every man's bundle of money was in his sack. And when they and their father saw their bundles of money, they were afraid.
verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass as they emptied their sacks, that, behold, every man’s bundle of money was in his sack: and when both they and their father saw the bundles of money, they were afraid.
verses.bsb: As they began emptying their sacks, there in each man’s sack was his bag of silver! And when they and their father saw the bags of silver, they were dismayed.

verses.esv: And Jacob their father said to them, “You have bereaved me of my children: Joseph is no more, and Simeon is no more, and now you would take Benjamin. All this has come against me.”
verses.kjv: And Jacob their father said unto them, Me have ye bereaved of my children: Joseph is not, and Simeon is not, and ye will take Benjamin away: all these things are against me.
verses.bsb: Their father Jacob said to them, “You have deprived me of my sons. Joseph is gone and Simeon is no more. Now you want to take Benjamin. Everything is going against me!”

verses.esv: Then Reuben said to his father, “Kill my two sons if I do not bring him back to you. Put him in my hands, and I will bring him back to you.”
verses.kjv: And Reuben spake unto his father, saying, Slay my two sons, if I bring him not to thee: deliver him into my hand, and I will bring him to thee again.
verses.bsb: Then Reuben said to his father, “You may kill my two sons if I fail to bring him back to you. Put him in my care, and I will return him.”

verses.esv: But he said, “My son shall not go down with you, for his brother is dead, and he is the only one left. If harm should happen to him on the journey that you are to make, you would bring down my gray hairs with sorrow to Sheol.”
verses.kjv: And he said, My son shall not go down with you; for his brother is dead, and he is left alone: if mischief befall him by the way in the which ye go, then shall ye bring down my gray hairs with sorrow to the grave.
verses.bsb: But Jacob replied, “My son will not go down there with you, for his brother is dead, and he alone is left. If any harm comes to him on your journey, you will bring my gray hair down to Sheol in sorrow.”

verses.esv: Now the famine was severe in the land.
verses.kjv: AND the famine was sore in the land.
verses.bsb: Now the famine was still severe in the land.

verses.esv: And when they had eaten the grain that they had brought from Egypt, their father said to them, “Go again, buy us a little food.”
verses.kjv: And it came to pass, when they had eaten up the corn which they had brought out of Egypt, their father said unto them, Go again, buy us a little food.
verses.bsb: So when Jacob’s sons had eaten all the grain they had brought from Egypt, their father said to them, “Go back and buy us a little more food.”

verses.esv: But Judah said to him, “The man solemnly warned us, saying, ‘You shall not see my face unless your brother is with you.’
verses.kjv: And Judah spake unto him, saying, The man did solemnly protest unto us, saying, Ye shall not see my face, except your brother be with you.
verses.bsb: But Judah replied, “The man solemnly warned us, ‘You will not see my face again unless your brother is with you.’

verses.esv: If you will send our brother with us, we will go down and buy you food.
verses.kjv: If thou wilt send our brother with us, we will go down and buy thee food:
verses.bsb: If you will send our brother with us, we will go down and buy food for you.

verses.esv: But if you will not send him, we will not go down, for the man said to us, ‘You shall not see my face, unless your brother is with you.’”
verses.kjv: But if thou wilt not send him, we will not go down: for the man said unto us, Ye shall not see my face, except your brother be with you.
verses.bsb: But if you will not send him, we will not go; for the man told us, ‘You will not see my face again unless your brother is with you.’”

verses.esv: Israel said, “Why did you treat me so badly as to tell the man that you had another brother?”
verses.kjv: And Israel said, Wherefore dealt ye so ill with me, as to tell the man whether ye had yet a brother?
verses.bsb: “Why did you bring this trouble upon me?” Israel asked. “Why did you tell the man you had another brother?”

verses.esv: They replied, “The man questioned us carefully about ourselves and our kindred, saying, ‘Is your father still alive? Do you have another brother?’ What we told him was in answer to these questions. Could we in any way know that he would say, ‘Bring your brother down’?”
verses.kjv: And they said, The man asked us straitly of our state, and of our kindred, saying, Is your father yet alive? have ye another brother? and we told him according to the tenor of these words: could we certainly know that he would say, Bring your brother down?
verses.bsb: They replied, “The man questioned us in detail about ourselves and our family: ‘Is your father still alive? Do you have another brother?’ And we answered him accordingly. How could we possibly know that he would say, ‘Bring your brother here’?”

verses.esv: And Judah said to Israel his father, “Send the boy with me, and we will arise and go, that we may live and not die, both we and you and also our little ones.
verses.kjv: And Judah said unto Israel his father, Send the lad with me, and we will arise and go; that we may live, and not die, both we, and thou, and also our little ones.
verses.bsb: And Judah said to his father Israel, “Send the boy with me, and we will go at once, so that we may live and not die—neither we, nor you, nor our children.

verses.esv: I will be a pledge of his safety. From my hand you shall require him. If I do not bring him back to you and set him before you, then let me bear the blame forever.
verses.kjv: I will be surety for him; of my hand shalt thou require him: if I bring him not unto thee, and set him before thee, then let me bear the blame for ever:
verses.bsb: I will guarantee his safety. You may hold me personally responsible. If I do not bring him back and set him before you, then may I bear the guilt before you all my life.

verses.esv: If we had not delayed, we would now have returned twice.”
verses.kjv: For except we had lingered, surely now we had returned this second time.
verses.bsb: If we had not delayed, we could have come and gone twice by now.”

verses.esv: Then their father Israel said to them, “If it must be so, then do this: take some of the choice fruits of the land in your bags, and carry a present down to the man, a little balm and a little honey, gum, myrrh, pistachio nuts, and almonds.
verses.kjv: And their father Israel said unto them, If it must be so now, do this; take of the best fruits in the land in your vessels, and carry down the man a present, a little balm, and a little honey, spices, and myrrh, nuts, and almonds:
verses.bsb: Then their father Israel said to them, “If it must be so, then do this: Put some of the best products of the land in your packs and carry them down as a gift for the man—a little balm and a little honey, spices and myrrh, pistachios and almonds.

verses.esv: Take double the money with you. Carry back with you the money that was returned in the mouth of your sacks. Perhaps it was an oversight.
verses.kjv: And take double money in your hand; and the money that was brought again in the mouth of your sacks, carry it again in your hand; peradventure it was an oversight:
verses.bsb: Take double the silver with you so that you may return the silver that was put back into the mouths of your sacks. Perhaps it was a mistake.

verses.esv: Take also your brother, and arise, go again to the man.
verses.kjv: Take also your brother, and arise, go again unto the man:
verses.bsb: Take your brother as well, and return to the man at once.

verses.esv: May God Almighty grant you mercy before the man, and may he send back your other brother and Benjamin. And as for me, if I am bereaved of my children, I am bereaved.”
verses.kjv: And God Almighty give you mercy before the man, that he may send away your other brother, and Benjamin. If I be bereaved of my children, I am bereaved.
verses.bsb: May God Almighty grant you mercy before the man, that he may release your other brother along with Benjamin. As for me, if I am bereaved, I am bereaved.”

verses.esv: So the men took this present, and they took double the money with them, and Benjamin. They arose and went down to Egypt and stood before Joseph.
verses.kjv: ¶ And the men took that present, and they took double money in their hand, and Benjamin; and rose up, and went down to Egypt, and stood before Joseph.
verses.bsb: So the men took these gifts, along with double the amount of silver, and Benjamin as well. Then they hurried down to Egypt and stood before Joseph.

verses.esv: When Joseph saw Benjamin with them, he said to the steward of his house, “Bring the men into the house, and slaughter an animal and make ready, for the men are to dine with me at noon.”
verses.kjv: And when Joseph saw Benjamin with them, he said to the ruler of his house, Bring these men home, and slay, and make ready; for these men shall dine with me at noon.
verses.bsb: When Joseph saw Benjamin with his brothers, he said to the steward of his house, “Take these men to my house. Slaughter an animal and prepare it, for they shall dine with me at noon.”

verses.esv: The man did as Joseph told him and brought the men to Joseph's house.
verses.kjv: And the man did as Joseph bade; and the man brought the men into Joseph’s house.
verses.bsb: The man did as Joseph had commanded and took the brothers to Joseph’s house.

verses.esv: And the men were afraid because they were brought to Joseph's house, and they said, “It is because of the money, which was replaced in our sacks the first time, that we are brought in, so that he may assault us and fall upon us to make us servants and seize our donkeys.”
verses.kjv: And the men were afraid, because they were brought into Joseph’s house; and they said, Because of the money that was returned in our sacks at the first time are we brought in; that he may seek occasion against us, and fall upon us, and take us for bondmen, and our asses.
verses.bsb: But the brothers were frightened that they had been taken to Joseph’s house. “We have been brought here because of the silver that was returned in our bags the first time,” they said. “They intend to overpower us and take us as slaves, along with our donkeys.”

verses.esv: So they went up to the steward of Joseph's house and spoke with him at the door of the house,
verses.kjv: And they came near to the steward of Joseph’s house, and they communed with him at the door of the house,
verses.bsb: So they approached Joseph’s steward and spoke to him at the entrance to the house.

verses.esv: and said, “Oh, my lord, we came down the first time to buy food.
verses.kjv: And said, O sir, we came indeed down at the first time to buy food:
verses.bsb: “Please, sir,” they said, “we really did come down here the first time to buy food.

verses.esv: And when we came to the lodging place we opened our sacks, and there was each man's money in the mouth of his sack, our money in full weight. So we have brought it again with us,
verses.kjv: And it came to pass, when we came to the inn, that we opened our sacks, and, behold, every man’s money was in the mouth of his sack, our money in full weight: and we have brought it again in our hand.
verses.bsb: But when we came to the place we lodged for the night, we opened our sacks and, behold, each of us found his silver in the mouth of his sack! It was the full amount of our silver, and we have brought it back with us.

verses.esv: and we have brought other money down with us to buy food. We do not know who put our money in our sacks.”
verses.kjv: And other money have we brought down in our hands to buy food: we cannot tell who put our money in our sacks.
verses.bsb: We have brought additional silver with us to buy food. We do not know who put our silver in our sacks.”

verses.esv: He replied, “Peace to you, do not be afraid. Your God and the God of your father has put treasure in your sacks for you. I received your money.” Then he brought Simeon out to them.
verses.kjv: And he said, Peace be to you, fear not: your God, and the God of your father, hath given you treasure in your sacks: I had your money. And he brought Simeon out unto them.
verses.bsb: “It is fine,” said the steward. “Do not be afraid. Your God, the God of your father, gave you the treasure that was in your sacks. I received your silver.” Then he brought Simeon out to them.

verses.esv: And when the man had brought the men into Joseph's house and given them water, and they had washed their feet, and when he had given their donkeys fodder,
verses.kjv: And the man brought the men into Joseph’s house, and gave them water, and they washed their feet; and he gave their asses provender.
verses.bsb: And the steward took the men into Joseph’s house, gave them water to wash their feet, and provided food for their donkeys.

verses.esv: they prepared the present for Joseph's coming at noon, for they heard that they should eat bread there.
verses.kjv: And they made ready the present against Joseph came at noon: for they heard that they should eat bread there.
verses.bsb: Since the brothers had been told that they were going to eat a meal there, they prepared their gift for Joseph’s arrival at noon.

verses.esv: When Joseph came home, they brought into the house to him the present that they had with them and bowed down to him to the ground.
verses.kjv: ¶ And when Joseph came home, they brought him the present which was in their hand into the house, and bowed themselves to him to the earth.
verses.bsb: When Joseph came home, they presented him with the gifts they had brought, and they bowed to the ground before him.

verses.esv: And he inquired about their welfare and said, “Is your father well, the old man of whom you spoke? Is he still alive?”
verses.kjv: And he asked them of their welfare, and said, Is your father well, the old man of whom ye spake? Is he yet alive?
verses.bsb: He asked if they were well, and then he asked, “How is your elderly father you told me about? Is he still alive?”

verses.esv: They said, “Your servant our father is well; he is still alive.” And they bowed their heads and prostrated themselves.
verses.kjv: And they answered, Thy servant our father is in good health, he is yet alive. And they bowed down their heads, and made obeisance.
verses.bsb: “Your servant our father is well,” they answered. “He is still alive.” And they bowed down to honor him.

verses.esv: And he lifted up his eyes and saw his brother Benjamin, his mother's son, and said, “Is this your youngest brother, of whom you spoke to me? God be gracious to you, my son!”
verses.kjv: And he lifted up his eyes, and saw his brother Benjamin, his mother’s son, and said, Is this your younger brother, of whom ye spake unto me? And he said, God be gracious unto thee, my son.
verses.bsb: When Joseph looked up and saw his brother Benjamin, his own mother’s son, he asked, “Is this your youngest brother, the one you told me about?” Then he declared, “May God be gracious to you, my son.”

verses.esv: Then Joseph hurried out, for his compassion grew warm for his brother, and he sought a place to weep. And he entered his chamber and wept there.
verses.kjv: And Joseph made haste; for his bowels did yearn upon his brother: and he sought where to weep; and he entered into his chamber, and wept there.
verses.bsb: Joseph hurried out because he was moved to tears for his brother, and he went to a private room to weep.

verses.esv: Then he washed his face and came out. And controlling himself he said, “Serve the food.”
verses.kjv: And he washed his face, and went out, and refrained himself, and said, Set on bread.
verses.bsb: Then he washed his face and came back out. Regaining his composure, he said, “Serve the meal.”

verses.esv: They served him by himself, and them by themselves, and the Egyptians who ate with him by themselves, because the Egyptians could not eat with the Hebrews, for that is an abomination to the Egyptians.
verses.kjv: And they set on for him by himself, and for them by themselves, and for the Egyptians, which did eat with him, by themselves: because the Egyptians might not eat bread with the Hebrews; for that is an abomination unto the Egyptians.
verses.bsb: They separately served Joseph, his brothers, and the Egyptians. They ate separately because the Egyptians would not eat with the Hebrews, since that was detestable to them.

verses.esv: And they sat before him, the firstborn according to his birthright and the youngest according to his youth. And the men looked at one another in amazement.
verses.kjv: And they sat before him, the firstborn according to his birthright, and the youngest according to his youth: and the men marvelled one at another.
verses.bsb: They were seated before Joseph in order by age, from the firstborn to the youngest, and the men looked at one another in astonishment.

verses.esv: Portions were taken to them from Joseph's table, but Benjamin's portion was five times as much as any of theirs. And they drank and were merry with him.
verses.kjv: And he took and sent messes unto them from before him: but Benjamin’s mess was five times so much as any of theirs. And they drank, and were merry with him.
verses.bsb: When the portions were served to them from Joseph’s table, Benjamin’s portion was five times larger than any of the others. So they feasted and drank freely with Joseph.

verses.esv: Then he commanded the steward of his house, “Fill the men's sacks with food, as much as they can carry, and put each man's money in the mouth of his sack,
verses.kjv: AND he commanded the steward of his house, saying, Fill the men’s sacks with food, as much as they can carry, and put every man’s money in his sack’s mouth.
verses.bsb: Then Joseph instructed his steward: “Fill the men’s sacks with as much food as they can carry, and put each one’s silver in the mouth of his sack.

verses.esv: and put my cup, the silver cup, in the mouth of the sack of the youngest, with his money for the grain.” And he did as Joseph told him.
verses.kjv: And put my cup, the silver cup, in the sack’s mouth of the youngest, and his corn money. And he did according to the word that Joseph had spoken.
verses.bsb: Put my cup, the silver one, in the mouth of the youngest one’s sack, along with the silver for his grain.”  So the steward did as Joseph had instructed.

verses.esv: As soon as the morning was light, the men were sent away with their donkeys.
verses.kjv: As soon as the morning was light, the men were sent away, they and their asses.
verses.bsb: At daybreak, the men were sent on their way with their donkeys.

verses.esv: They had gone only a short distance from the city. Now Joseph said to his steward, “Up, follow after the men, and when you overtake them, say to them, ‘Why have you repaid evil for good?
verses.kjv: And when they were gone out of the city, and not yet far off, Joseph said unto his steward, Up, follow after the men; and when thou dost overtake them, say unto them, Wherefore have ye rewarded evil for good?
verses.bsb: They had not gone far from the city when Joseph told his steward, “Pursue the men at once, and when you overtake them, ask, ‘Why have you repaid good with evil?

verses.esv: Is it not from this that my lord drinks, and by this that he practices divination? You have done evil in doing this.’”
verses.kjv: Is not this it in which my lord drinketh, and whereby indeed he divineth? ye have done evil in so doing.
verses.bsb: Is this not the cup my master drinks from and uses for divination? What you have done is wicked!’”

verses.esv: When he overtook them, he spoke to them these words.
verses.kjv: ¶ And he overtook them, and he spake unto them these same words.
verses.bsb: When the steward overtook them, he relayed these words to them.

verses.esv: They said to him, “Why does my lord speak such words as these? Far be it from your servants to do such a thing!
verses.kjv: And they said unto him, Wherefore saith my lord these words? God forbid that thy servants should do according to this thing:
verses.bsb: “Why does my lord say these things?” they asked. “Your servants could not possibly do such a thing.

verses.esv: Behold, the money that we found in the mouths of our sacks we brought back to you from the land of Canaan. How then could we steal silver or gold from your lord's house?
verses.kjv: Behold, the money, which we found in our sacks’ mouths, we brought again unto thee out of the land of Canaan: how then should we steal out of thy lord’s house silver or gold?
verses.bsb: We even brought back to you from the land of Canaan the silver we found in the mouths of our sacks. Why would we steal silver or gold from your master’s house?

verses.esv: Whichever of your servants is found with it shall die, and we also will be my lord's servants.”
verses.kjv: With whomsoever of thy servants it be found, both let him die, and we also will be my lord’s bondmen.
verses.bsb: If any of your servants is found to have it, he must die, and the rest will become slaves of my lord.”

verses.esv: He said, “Let it be as you say: he who is found with it shall be my servant, and the rest of you shall be innocent.”
verses.kjv: And he said, Now also let it be according unto your words: he with whom it is found shall be my servant; and ye shall be blameless.
verses.bsb: “As you say,” replied the steward. “But only the one who is found with the cup will be my slave, and the rest of you shall be free of blame.”

verses.esv: Then each man quickly lowered his sack to the ground, and each man opened his sack.
verses.kjv: Then they speedily took down every man his sack to the ground, and opened every man his sack.
verses.bsb: So each one quickly lowered his sack to the ground and opened it.

verses.esv: And he searched, beginning with the eldest and ending with the youngest. And the cup was found in Benjamin's sack.
verses.kjv: And he searched, and began at the eldest, and left at the youngest: and the cup was found in Benjamin’s sack.
verses.bsb: The steward searched, beginning with the oldest and ending with the youngest—and the cup was found in Benjamin’s sack.

verses.esv: Then they tore their clothes, and every man loaded his donkey, and they returned to the city.
verses.kjv: Then they rent their clothes, and laded every man his ass, and returned to the city.
verses.bsb: Then they all tore their clothes, loaded their donkeys, and returned to the city.

verses.esv: When Judah and his brothers came to Joseph's house, he was still there. They fell before him to the ground.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Judah and his brethren came to Joseph’s house; for he was yet there: and they fell before him on the ground.
verses.bsb: When Judah and his brothers arrived at Joseph’s house, he was still there, and they fell to the ground before him.

verses.esv: Joseph said to them, “What deed is this that you have done? Do you not know that a man like me can indeed practice divination?”
verses.kjv: And Joseph said unto them, What deed is this that ye have done? wot ye not that such a man as I can certainly divine?
verses.bsb: “What is this deed you have done?” Joseph declared. “Do you not know that a man like me can surely divine the truth?”

verses.esv: And Judah said, “What shall we say to my lord? What shall we speak? Or how can we clear ourselves? God has found out the guilt of your servants; behold, we are my lord's servants, both we and he also in whose hand the cup has been found.”
verses.kjv: And Judah said, What shall we say unto my lord? what shall we speak? or how shall we clear ourselves? God hath found out the iniquity of thy servants: behold, we are my lord’s servants, both we, and he also with whom the cup is found.
verses.bsb: “What can we say to my lord?” Judah replied. “How can we plead? How can we justify ourselves? God has exposed the iniquity of your servants. We are now my lord’s slaves—both we and the one who was found with the cup.”

verses.esv: But he said, “Far be it from me that I should do so! Only the man in whose hand the cup was found shall be my servant. But as for you, go up in peace to your father.”
verses.kjv: And he said, God forbid that I should do so: but the man in whose hand the cup is found, he shall be my servant; and as for you, get you up in peace unto your father.
verses.bsb: But Joseph replied, “Far be it from me to do this. The man who was found with the cup will be my slave. The rest of you may return to your father in peace.”

verses.esv: Then Judah went up to him and said, “Oh, my lord, please let your servant speak a word in my lord's ears, and let not your anger burn against your servant, for you are like Pharaoh himself.
verses.kjv: ¶ Then Judah came near unto him, and said, Oh my lord, let thy servant, I pray thee, speak a word in my lord’s ears, and let not thine anger burn against thy servant: for thou art even as Pharaoh.
verses.bsb: Then Judah approached Joseph and said, “Sir, please let your servant speak personally to my lord. Do not be angry with your servant, for you are equal to Pharaoh himself.

verses.esv: My lord asked his servants, saying, ‘Have you a father, or a brother?’
verses.kjv: My lord asked his servants, saying, Have ye a father, or a brother?
verses.bsb: My lord asked his servants, ‘Do you have a father or a brother?’

verses.esv: And we said to my lord, ‘We have a father, an old man, and a young brother, the child of his old age. His brother is dead, and he alone is left of his mother's children, and his father loves him.’
verses.kjv: And we said unto my lord, We have a father, an old man, and a child of his old age, a little one; and his brother is dead, and he alone is left of his mother, and his father loveth him.
verses.bsb: And we answered, ‘We have an elderly father and a younger brother, the child of his old age. The boy’s brother is dead. He is the only one of his mother’s sons left, and his father loves him.’

verses.esv: Then you said to your servants, ‘Bring him down to me, that I may set my eyes on him.’
verses.kjv: And thou saidst unto thy servants, Bring him down unto me, that I may set mine eyes upon him.
verses.bsb: Then you told your servants, ‘Bring him down to me so that I can see him for myself.’

verses.esv: We said to my lord, ‘The boy cannot leave his father, for if he should leave his father, his father would die.’
verses.kjv: And we said unto my lord, The lad cannot leave his father: for if he should leave his father, his father would die.
verses.bsb: So we said to my lord, ‘The boy cannot leave his father. If he were to leave, his father would die.’

verses.esv: Then you said to your servants, ‘Unless your youngest brother comes down with you, you shall not see my face again.’
verses.kjv: And thou saidst unto thy servants, Except your youngest brother come down with you, ye shall see my face no more.
verses.bsb: But you said to your servants, ‘Unless your younger brother comes down with you, you will not see my face again.’

verses.esv: “When we went back to your servant my father, we told him the words of my lord.
verses.kjv: And it came to pass when we came up unto thy servant my father, we told him the words of my lord.
verses.bsb: Now when we returned to your servant my father, we relayed your words to him.

verses.esv: And when our father said, ‘Go again, buy us a little food,’
verses.kjv: And our father said, Go again, and buy us a little food.
verses.bsb: Then our father said, ‘Go back and buy us some food.’

verses.esv: we said, ‘We cannot go down. If our youngest brother goes with us, then we will go down. For we cannot see the man's face unless our youngest brother is with us.’
verses.kjv: And we said, We cannot go down: if our youngest brother be with us, then will we go down: for we may not see the man’s face, except our youngest brother be with us.
verses.bsb: But we answered, ‘We cannot go down there unless our younger brother goes with us. So if our younger brother is not with us, we cannot see the man.’

verses.esv: Then your servant my father said to us, ‘You know that my wife bore me two sons.
verses.kjv: And thy servant my father said unto us, Ye know that my wife bare me two sons:
verses.bsb: And your servant my father said to us, ‘You know that my wife bore me two sons.

verses.esv: One left me, and I said, “Surely he has been torn to pieces,” and I have never seen him since.
verses.kjv: And the one went out from me, and I said, Surely he is torn in pieces; and I saw him not since:
verses.bsb: When one of them was gone, I said: “Surely he has been torn to pieces.” And I have not seen him since.

verses.esv: If you take this one also from me, and harm happens to him, you will bring down my gray hairs in evil to Sheol.’
verses.kjv: And if ye take this also from me, and mischief befall him, ye shall bring down my gray hairs with sorrow to the grave.
verses.bsb: Now if you also take this one from me and harm comes to him, you will bring my gray hair down to Sheol in sorrow.’

verses.esv: “Now therefore, as soon as I come to your servant my father, and the boy is not with us, then, as his life is bound up in the boy's life,
verses.kjv: Now therefore when I come to thy servant my father, and the lad be not with us; seeing that his life is bound up in the lad’s life;
verses.bsb: So if the boy is not with us when I return to your servant, and if my father, whose life is wrapped up in the boy’s life,

verses.esv: as soon as he sees that the boy is not with us, he will die, and your servants will bring down the gray hairs of your servant our father with sorrow to Sheol.
verses.kjv: It shall come to pass, when he seeth that the lad is not with us, that he will die: and thy servants shall bring down the gray hairs of thy servant our father with sorrow to the grave.
verses.bsb: sees that the boy is not with us, he will die. Then your servants will have brought the gray hair of your servant our father down to Sheol in sorrow.

verses.esv: For your servant became a pledge of safety for the boy to my father, saying, ‘If I do not bring him back to you, then I shall bear the blame before my father all my life.’
verses.kjv: For thy servant became surety for the lad unto my father, saying, If I bring him not unto thee, then I shall bear the blame to my father for ever.
verses.bsb: Indeed, your servant guaranteed the boy’s safety to my father, saying, ‘If I do not return him to you, I will bear the guilt before you, my father, all my life.’

verses.esv: Now therefore, please let your servant remain instead of the boy as a servant to my lord, and let the boy go back with his brothers.
verses.kjv: Now therefore, I pray thee, let thy servant abide instead of the lad a bondman to my lord; and let the lad go up with his brethren.
verses.bsb: Now please let your servant stay here as my lord’s slave in place of the boy. Let him return with his brothers.

verses.esv: For how can I go back to my father if the boy is not with me? I fear to see the evil that would find my father.”
verses.kjv: For how shall I go up to my father, and the lad be not with me? lest peradventure I see the evil that shall come on my father.
verses.bsb: For how can I go back to my father without the boy? I could not bear to see the misery that would overwhelm him.”

verses.esv: Then Joseph could not control himself before all those who stood by him. He cried, “Make everyone go out from me.” So no one stayed with him when Joseph made himself known to his brothers.
verses.kjv: THEN Joseph could not refrain himself before all them that stood by him; and he cried, Cause every man to go out from me. And there stood no man with him, while Joseph made himself known unto his brethren.
verses.bsb: Then Joseph could no longer control himself before all his attendants, and he cried out, “Send everyone away from me!”  So none of them were with Joseph when he made himself known to his brothers.

verses.esv: And he wept aloud, so that the Egyptians heard it, and the household of Pharaoh heard it.
verses.kjv: And he wept aloud: and the Egyptians and the house of Pharaoh heard.
verses.bsb: But he wept so loudly that the Egyptians heard him, and Pharaoh’s household soon heard of it.

verses.esv: And Joseph said to his brothers, “I am Joseph! Is my father still alive?” But his brothers could not answer him, for they were dismayed at his presence.
verses.kjv: And Joseph said unto his brethren, I am Joseph; doth my father yet live? And his brethren could not answer him; for they were troubled at his presence.
verses.bsb: Joseph said to his brothers, “I am Joseph! Is my father still alive?”  But they were unable to answer him, because they were terrified in his presence.

verses.esv: So Joseph said to his brothers, “Come near to me, please.” And they came near. And he said, “I am your brother, Joseph, whom you sold into Egypt.
verses.kjv: And Joseph said unto his brethren, Come near to me, I pray you. And they came near. And he said, I am Joseph your brother, whom ye sold into Egypt.
verses.bsb: Then Joseph said to his brothers, “Please come near me.” And they did so.  “I am Joseph, your brother,” he said, “the one you sold into Egypt!

verses.esv: And now do not be distressed or angry with yourselves because you sold me here, for God sent me before you to preserve life.
verses.kjv: Now therefore be not grieved, nor angry with yourselves, that ye sold me hither: for God did send me before you to preserve life.
verses.bsb: And now, do not be distressed or angry with yourselves that you sold me into this place, because it was to save lives that God sent me before you.

verses.esv: For the famine has been in the land these two years, and there are yet five years in which there will be neither plowing nor harvest.
verses.kjv: For these two years hath the famine been in the land: and yet there are five years, in the which there shall neither be earing nor harvest.
verses.bsb: For the famine has covered the land these two years, and there will be five more years without plowing or harvesting.

verses.esv: And God sent me before you to preserve for you a remnant on earth, and to keep alive for you many survivors.
verses.kjv: And God sent me before you to preserve you a posterity in the earth, and to save your lives by a great deliverance.
verses.bsb: God sent me before you to preserve you as a remnant on the earth and to save your lives by a great deliverance.

verses.esv: So it was not you who sent me here, but God. He has made me a father to Pharaoh, and lord of all his house and ruler over all the land of Egypt.
verses.kjv: So now it was not you that sent me hither, but God: and he hath made me a father to Pharaoh, and lord of all his house, and a ruler throughout all the land of Egypt.
verses.bsb: Therefore it was not you who sent me here, but God, who has made me a father to Pharaoh—lord of all his household and ruler over all the land of Egypt.

verses.esv: Hurry and go up to my father and say to him, ‘Thus says your son Joseph, God has made me lord of all Egypt. Come down to me; do not tarry.
verses.kjv: Haste ye, and go up to my father, and say unto him, Thus saith thy son Joseph, God hath made me lord of all Egypt: come down unto me, tarry not:
verses.bsb: Now return quickly to my father and tell him, ‘This is what your son Joseph says: God has made me lord of all Egypt. Come down to me without delay.

verses.esv: You shall dwell in the land of Goshen, and you shall be near me, you and your children and your children's children, and your flocks, your herds, and all that you have.
verses.kjv: And thou shalt dwell in the land of Goshen, and thou shalt be near unto me, thou, and thy children, and thy children’s children, and thy flocks, and thy herds, and all that thou hast:
verses.bsb: You shall settle in the land of Goshen and be near me—you and your children and grandchildren, your flocks and herds, and everything you own.

verses.esv: There I will provide for you, for there are yet five years of famine to come, so that you and your household, and all that you have, do not come to poverty.’
verses.kjv: And there will I nourish thee; for yet there are five years of famine; lest thou, and thy household, and all that thou hast, come to poverty.
verses.bsb: And there I will provide for you, because there will be five more years of famine. Otherwise, you and your household and everything you own will come to destitution.’

verses.esv: And now your eyes see, and the eyes of my brother Benjamin see, that it is my mouth that speaks to you.
verses.kjv: And, behold, your eyes see, and the eyes of my brother Benjamin, that it is my mouth that speaketh unto you.
verses.bsb: Behold! You and my brother Benjamin can see that I, Joseph, am the one speaking with you.

verses.esv: You must tell my father of all my honor in Egypt, and of all that you have seen. Hurry and bring my father down here.”
verses.kjv: And ye shall tell my father of all my glory in Egypt, and of all that ye have seen; and ye shall haste and bring down my father hither.
verses.bsb: Tell my father about all my splendor in Egypt and everything you have seen. And bring my father down here quickly.”

verses.esv: Then he fell upon his brother Benjamin's neck and wept, and Benjamin wept upon his neck.
verses.kjv: And he fell upon his brother Benjamin’s neck, and wept; and Benjamin wept upon his neck.
verses.bsb: Then Joseph threw his arms around his brother Benjamin and wept, and Benjamin wept as they embraced.

verses.esv: And he kissed all his brothers and wept upon them. After that his brothers talked with him.
verses.kjv: Moreover he kissed all his brethren, and wept upon them: and after that his brethren talked with him.
verses.bsb: Joseph kissed each of his brothers as he wept over them. And afterward his brothers talked with him.

verses.esv: When the report was heard in Pharaoh's house, “Joseph's brothers have come,” it pleased Pharaoh and his servants.
verses.kjv: ¶ And the fame thereof was heard in Pharaoh’s house, saying, Joseph’s brethren are come: and it pleased Pharaoh well, and his servants.
verses.bsb: When the news reached Pharaoh’s house that Joseph’s brothers had come, Pharaoh and his servants were pleased.

verses.esv: And Pharaoh said to Joseph, “Say to your brothers, ‘Do this: load your beasts and go back to the land of Canaan,
verses.kjv: And Pharaoh said unto Joseph, Say unto thy brethren, This do ye; lade your beasts, and go, get you unto the land of Canaan;
verses.bsb: Pharaoh said to Joseph, “Tell your brothers, ‘Do as follows: Load your animals and return to the land of Canaan.

verses.esv: and take your father and your households, and come to me, and I will give you the best of the land of Egypt, and you shall eat the fat of the land.’
verses.kjv: And take your father and your households, and come unto me: and I will give you the good of the land of Egypt, and ye shall eat the fat of the land.
verses.bsb: Then bring your father and your families and return to me. I will give you the best of the land of Egypt, and you shall eat from the fat of the land.’

verses.esv: And you, Joseph, are commanded to say, ‘Do this: take wagons from the land of Egypt for your little ones and for your wives, and bring your father, and come.
verses.kjv: Now thou art commanded, this do ye; take you wagons out of the land of Egypt for your little ones, and for your wives, and bring your father, and come.
verses.bsb: You are also directed to tell them: ‘Take wagons from the land of Egypt for your young children and your wives, and bring your father and come back.

verses.esv: Have no concern for your goods, for the best of all the land of Egypt is yours.’”
verses.kjv: Also regard not your stuff; for the good of all the land of Egypt is yours.
verses.bsb: But pay no regard to your belongings, for the best of all the land of Egypt is yours.’”

verses.esv: The sons of Israel did so: and Joseph gave them wagons, according to the command of Pharaoh, and gave them provisions for the journey.
verses.kjv: And the children of Israel did so: and Joseph gave them wagons, according to the commandment of Pharaoh, and gave them provision for the way.
verses.bsb: So the sons of Israel did as they were told. Joseph gave them wagons as Pharaoh had instructed, and he also gave them provisions for their journey.

verses.esv: To each and all of them he gave a change of clothes, but to Benjamin he gave three hundred shekels of silver and five changes of clothes.
verses.kjv: To all of them he gave each man changes of raiment; but to Benjamin he gave three hundred pieces of silver, and five changes of raiment.
verses.bsb: He gave new garments to each of them, but to Benjamin he gave three hundred shekels of silver and five sets of clothes.

verses.esv: To his father he sent as follows: ten donkeys loaded with the good things of Egypt, and ten female donkeys loaded with grain, bread, and provision for his father on the journey.
